title: Harden west extension module loading contract
description: west dynamically loads command modules without registering them in sys.modules; dataclasses with postponed annotations crash during import. Document the extension-loader constraint, add an import contract, and decide whether the loader or command conventions should be fixed.
close_reason: Avoided dataclass in dynamically loaded west command and added real west CLI import smoke coverage.

title: E-UNION prefix fails overlay mount with bare ELOOP instead of auto-selecting NOOVERLAYFS or emitting a clear error
description: Type: bug

title: west test: isolated build target runner for patch-local compile tests
description: Converting libressl/libressl-283-nist-strict-aliasing from MODEL to COMPILE exposed that `ninja darling_ec_tls_regress` in the full Darling build pulls hundreds of unrelated targets and can fail first on unrelated objc-runtime debug macro mismatch. Need an isolated build-target runner for patch-local compile tests: configure/build the minimal owning subproject/target or otherwise avoid unrelated all-tree dependencies, support source-base RED proof, and report unrelated dependency fan-out as a tooling failure. Acceptance: libressl EC regression can build/run as a focused COMPILE test with RED old tree and GREEN current tree without building unrelated objc/xnu closure.
close_reason: Acceptance satisfied by darling-cmake-target-fixture for LibreSSL EC regression.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08: implemented isolated build target runner for the LibreSSL acceptance case. The runner builds only the external LibreSSL CMake subproject in a temporary superproject, not the broad Darling graph, supports source-base RED proof, and reports target-local configure/build/run diagnostics. Verified libressl EC regression GREEN and RED without pulling objc/xnu closure.

title: tests: replace weak LibreSSL host regression with real Darling CMake or guest gate
description: Audit found that tests/run-libressl-nist-regress.sh is not a sufficient proof for libressl/libressl-283-nist-strict-aliasing.patch. It builds LibreSSL through autotools, whose configure path already uses -fno-strict-aliasing, so it does not prove the Darling CMakeLists.txt crypto44 target_compile_options fix. Its RED proof is also weak because source-base fails on missing tests/darling_ec_tls_regress.c rather than old crypto behavior. Replace this with a real gate that either builds the Darling CMake target in an isolated way and runs darling_ec_tls_regress, or builds/runs a guest executable against the patched Darling libcrypto. Related infra blocker: dar-mk8i.
close_reason: LibreSSL patch now has a real isolated Darling CMake target RED/GREEN gate.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08: replaced weak LibreSSL-only GREEN proof with real Darling CMake target gate. The old autotools source-build fixture remains as an extra portable GREEN check, but RED is now proven by runner: darling-cmake-target-fixture against crypto44's actual CMake target plus darling_ec_tls_regress. The UB runtime oracle was not deterministic enough on this host, so the runner also verifies actual compiler argv for the vulnerable NIST files instead of checking source text.

title: perf #18 P5: migrate top-frequency small Mach RPCs onto ring v3 (Class A/B)
description: Wake model is frozen (ABI v2). Now migrate the HOTTEST small ops (by per_call frequency x saved-latency, NOT aesthetics) from the ~30us UDS class to the ~5us ring class. Take top-N from the perf#9 server-side per_call accountant. Gist 191a10db op classes: Class A = pure small RPC, inline args/result, no OOL/no fd/no rights-transfer (do FIRST); Class B = Mach right bookkeeping, server-authoritative, handle-IDs-only no guest trust; Class C = complex mach_msg/OOL/port-arrays (LATER). Candidates: mach_port_mod_refs, mach_port_allocate/deallocate, mach_port_insert/extract_right, thread/task lookup-style, small inline mach_msg, uncontended psynch. Each migrated op: extend the C2S allowlist + a guest ring helper + RED->GREEN gate + live A/B proving it lands in the 4-6us class. Reuses v3 datapath unchanged. SHOULD be informed by P6 (whether generic Call-dispatch is the floor).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: P6 (dar-aw2) breakdown informs this: the ring TRANSPORT is ~free (~855ns/18% of the 4.7us hot path), so migrating each hot op onto v3 DOES drop it from the ~30us UDS class to the ~5us ring class -- worthwhile. BUT every migrated op still pays the server FLOOR (doWork body 2260ns/48% + callFromMessage dispatch 647ns/14% = generic Call pipeline). So P5 migration gets ops to ~5us; getting them BELOW that needs the opcode-fast-path (new bead). Order: P5 still valid as breadth (more ops in 5us class); depth (sub-2us) = opcode-fast-path bead.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Gist 33355bf ranks P5 candidates + sets HARD per-op criteria (mach_reply_port was a near-ideal Class-A; the rest won't all be). Candidate ranking (freq x saved-latency, NOT aesthetics):
 1. mach_port_mod_refs (next candidate; BUT audit refcount/error edges: underflow, dead name, invalid right, task namespace, send-once vs receive distinctions)
 2. mach_port_allocate / deallocate
 3. mach_task_self / mach_thread_self-style lookups IF they go through the server
 4. simple mach_port_insert_right / extract_right IF shape is simple
 5. small inline mach_msg send/recv -- LATER, carefully
 6. psynch -- SEPARATE, do NOT fold into the Mach fast-op table
 HARD criteria for each new direct fast-op (all must hold or fall to generic):
  - same underlying dtape/XNU primitive, NO semantic clone
  - no allocation outside the existing primitive unless already in the generic path
  - no blocking/suspend; suspend detection stays fatal/logged + fallback
  - request shape FULLY validated before the direct call (shape guard = security boundary)
  - fallback path preserves EXACT old behavior
  - fast/off A/B correctness over large mint/free/ref churn
  - per-op counters: hit/fallback/fail/s2c_full/suspend
 Two op classes: A = direct fast-op (empty/small req, no block, no OOL, no complex rights, dtape primitive callable directly, thread/process already resolved); B = ring-generic (benefits from P4 transport but still through generic framing). FAST-OP TABLE: do NOT build yet -- per gist, only after 2-3 more surgical ops prove a repeating shape without semantic drift, else premature abstraction locks in the wrong shape.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Gist 7e0ec6b: for the NEXT op mach_port_mod_refs the acceptance criteria must be STRICTER than mach_reply_port because it has more semantic edge cases. Required validation/handling before any direct fast-op: invalid port name; DEAD name; reference-count UNDERFLOW (mod_refs to negative); right-type distinctions (send vs send-once vs receive vs dead-name); task namespace correctness; cleanup correctness when the process DIES mid-op. mach_reply_port was a near-ideal Class-A (no args, no error edges, pure mint); mach_port_mod_refs is Class-A-shaped but with real error returns -> the shape guard + fallback must cover EVERY edge that the generic path handles, or fall back. Strategy unchanged: 2-3 surgical ops BEFORE extracting a fast-op table (premature abstraction = wrong shape). Gist 7e0ec6b.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Gist e0c1e862 (final P7 rollout-prep review) -> P5 is the next ENGINEERING move (default-ON is a separate product call, now discussable). SELF-CONTAINED P5 AGENT BRIEF written: memory/dar-perf18-p5-agent-brief.md ([[dar-perf18-p5-agent-brief]]).

KEY VERIFIED FACTS for the next agent (do NOT re-derive blindly):
 - mach_port_mod_refs IS a real server RPC (valid ring candidate): guest sends from xnu mach_traps.c:453 (dserver_rpc_mach_port_mod_refs(target,name,right,delta)); server dispatches via the GENERATED callFromMessage switch in call.cpp (DSERVER_CALL X-macro ~line 254). callnum=41.
 - SHAPE DIFFERS from mach_reply_port (the reason for the stricter bar): request body NON-empty (4 args: uint32 target, uint32 name, int32 right, int32 delta), reply HEADER-ONLY (the result is the kern_return_t code, no port). publishReply(seq,callnum,code,NULL,0).
 - NO hand-written dtape_mach_port_mod_refs() one-liner exists (unlike dtape_mach_reply_port). Real primitive = XNU mach_port_mod_refs(ipc_space,name,right,delta) in duct-tape/.../ipc/mach_port.c:974 via the generated kernelrpc dispatch. So a surgical step-2 needs a new thin dtape wrapper; a step-1 (doWorkInline, no fiber, generic framing) migration does NOT.
STRATEGY (brief): do step-1 (allowlist + doWorkInline + guest helper + per-op hatch) FIRST -- but ONLY after confirming mach_port_mod_refs never blocks (read mach_port.c:974; ring_fast_suspend MUST stay 0). Surgical step-2 (dtape wrapper) only after step-1 is correct+measured. Do NOT build the fast-op table yet (op #2 of 2-3 needed).
STRICTER per-op bar (gist): invalid name, DEAD name, refcount UNDERFLOW (delta drives user-refs negative -> name destroyed), right-kind distinctions (send/send-once/receive/dead-name/port-set), task namespace, process-death-mid-op cleanup; the kern_return_t code must be IDENTICAL fast-ON vs FAST_OPS=0 across EVERY edge (that A/B is the proof). RED->GREEN: extend ring_fastpath_gate_test.c shape predicate + a behavioral edge A/B.
Brief also carries: 7-copy deploy map, fork-timeline staging filter, ninja-doesn't-relink-dylib gotcha, prod-restore md5s, validation protocol. Branch HEAD darlingserver @4c742f2. Gist e0c1e862.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
perf#18 P5 mach_port_mod_refs DONE (commits darlingserver @d123893, xnu @99e7f2d; LOCAL, NOT pushed). MIGRATED onto the ring -> ~3us class, ~4.9x over UDS (p50 12.9us->2.6us; mean 16us->4.2us).

KEY FINDING: mod_refs is NOT a no-fiber inline op like mach_reply_port. Running it via doWorkInline CRASHED the server -- a later real doWork() on the same thread freed a corrupted _stack (StackPool::free->munmap EINVAL->std::terminate->ABORT). Caught live via sudo gdb (ptrace_scope=1). Root cause: mach_reply_port only mints a port in current_task's space (safe off-fiber); mod_refs ALSO does port_name_to_task + task refcounting, NOT safe without the microthread fiber. FIX = mod_refs rides the GENERIC fiber doWork() on the ring (added to the C2S allowlist in ringServiceThread; DELIBERATELY NOT in ringFastPathEligible). Still gets the full transport win (this IS the ~5us-class breadth P5 was about; sub-2us inline stays reserved for trivial pure-mint traps).

2nd FINDING: the C2S allowlist for mod_refs MUST NOT be env-gated. A request the guest published is parked waiting for a ring reply, so silently dropping it server-side strands the guest on its bounded reply-wait every call (=effectively wedged; observed under FAST_OPS=0 with an early server-side hatch). So there is NO per-op DARLING_SERVER_FAST_MACH_PORT_MOD_REFS hatch; the whole-transport switch (DSERVER_RING_TRANSPORT) + ABI auto-fallback is the safety valve, exactly like task_self_trap.

STRICTER-BAR A/B PASSED: byte-identical kern_return_t ring-vs-true-UDS (prod-off non-ring stack) across ALL 14 edges -- valid +1/-1, refcount underflow->KERN_INVALID_NAME, destroy, send/send-once/receive/dead-name right-kinds, invalid name, MACH_PORT_DEAD, illegal-right. Counters clean: ring_fast_suspend=0, ring_fast_fail=0, ring_s2c_full=0. Fork-storm 3/3 RESULT=DONE .o=200/200 clients_blocked=0 (no dar-l8k/dar-6x4 regression).

RED->GREEN: tests/ring_fastpath_gate_test.c pins mod_refs is NOT-inline-eligible + IS-C2S-allowlisted (body fits a slot); RED arm -DC2S_NO_MODREFS (drop from allowlist) fails; existing -DFASTPATH_NO_HATCH / -DNO_SHAPE_GUARD still fail. Wired into run-ring-shm-validate.sh. Guest A/B harness mrbench.c (edges + hot p50/p99) in $DPREFIX/Users/ilyagulya/.

NB: the agent brief's claim "no dtape_mach_port_mod_refs() one-liner exists" was WRONG -- it IS generated (rpc.internal.h:3604, wraps _kernelrpc_mach_port_mod_refs_trap -> port_name_to_task + mach_port_mod_refs). So no new dtape wrapper was needed; the generic path reuses the existing primitive verbatim.

NEXT op candidates (gist 33355bf ranking): mach_port_allocate/deallocate, mach_port_insert/extract_right. Each likely shares mod_refs' profile (body + task/port translation) -> generic fiber path, NOT inline. After 2-3 such, the repeating shape is clear enough to consider a fast-op table (separate bead).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Gist 8d0203c6 (P5 review/verdict): P5 SUCCESSFUL but it CHANGED THE STRATEGY. Codify these as standing principles for the rest of dar-1il:

RING MIGRATION HAS TWO TIERS (keep the terms DISTINCT in code + docs):
 - Tier 1 = ring + GENERIC FIBER: removes UDS transport, KEEPS microthread/stack semantics. Target ~2-6us (vs ~10-30us UDS). This is the DEFAULT for almost everything.
 - Tier 2 = ring + NO-FIBER DIRECT fast path (doMachReplyPortInline-style): ONLY for proven trivial/non-blocking/NO-stack-sensitive ops. Target sub-us/~1us. mach_reply_port is the ONLY proven Tier-2 op so far.
 mach_reply_port was the EXCEPTION, not the template (the negative finding). port_name_to_task / task-refs / thread-stack-bookkeeping => the fiber is part of EXECUTION SEMANTICS, not just overhead => Tier 1.

NAMING INVARIANT (gist is explicit): "C2S allowlist" (opcode may use ring TRANSPORT) and "FastPathEligible/InlineEligible" (opcode may SKIP the generic fiber/Call machinery) MUST NEVER be the same concept. Rename/separate them in code + docs if they blur. (P5 already keeps them separate: C2S `eligible` in ringServiceThread vs ringFastPathEligible — preserve that.)

PROTOCOL INVARIANT (the most important bug P5 caught -> make it a GATE): If the guest can send an opcode over the ring, the server MUST (1) service it over the ring, OR (2) return an explicit ring error/fallback reply, OR (3) reject the ABI/transport at attach time and force UDS. NEVER silently drop a ring request (that strands the guest on its reply-wait = wedge). Gist asks for a dedicated gate/invariant test: "every guest-advertised ring opcode is server-accepted, or attach rejects." -> spun out as the new child bead below.

NEXT STEP IS NOT "generalize a fast-op table." It is BULK Tier-1 ring migration of port/right bookkeeping ops on the generic fiber:
 Likely ring + fiber (Tier 1): mach_port_allocate, mach_port_deallocate, mach_port_insert_right, mach_port_extract_right, + more ref/right bookkeeping.
 Possible no-fiber (Tier 2) ONLY after separate proof: pure self/name lookups, trivial mint/query ops with no task-ref/stack bookkeeping.
 Be MUCH more conservative about Tier 2 now: default new ops to Tier 1; attempt Tier 2 only with its own crash/suspend proof.

Verdict: P5 (mach_port_mod_refs) succeeded; bar was right (14 edges UDS-vs-ring identity, underflow, right-kinds, dead/invalid names, fork-storm, clean counters, RED arm on missing allowlist) and the crash was turned into a rule, not papered over. New child bead created for the bulk migration + the no-silent-drop gate.

title: perf#18 P5-bulk: Tier-1 ring migration of port/right bookkeeping ops + no-silent-drop invariant gate
description:
perf#18 P5-bulk (gist 8d0203c6): Tier-1 ring migration of port/right bookkeeping ops + a no-silent-drop invariant gate. Each of mach_port_allocate, mach_port_deallocate, mach_port_insert_right, mach_port_extract_right (and further ref/right ops) gets: a guest ring helper (gr_body_trap-style: inline body, header-only-or-small reply, result via out-param SEPARATE from the -1 fall-back signal), a server C2S allowlist entry in ringServiceThread (Tier 1 = GENERIC FIBER doWork; do NOT touch ringFastPathEligible/doWorkInline -- mach_port_mod_refs PROVED these ops are NOT no-fiber-safe: port_name_to_task/task-refs/stack bookkeeping crash off-fiber, see dar-1il P5 comment), a RED->GREEN gate, and a live byte-identical-kern_return ring-vs-true-UDS A/B over the op's edge cases (mirror mrbench.c's 14-edge style per op: invalid/dead name, right kinds, refcount/quota edges, ownership). Reuses the v3 datapath + the shape/length validation unchanged.

PREREQUISITE / FIRST DELIVERABLE -- the protocol invariant the gist demands as a dedicated gate: "every guest-advertised ring opcode is server-accepted, or attach rejects -- NEVER silently drop a ring request." Today the guest helper set and the server C2S allowlist are independently hand-maintained (drift hazard: a guest op missing from the server allowlist => server silently consume+drop => guest stranded on its reply-wait = wedge; this is exactly the bug P5 hit under the early per-op hatch). FIX: introduce a SINGLE shared source-of-truth list of ring opcodes (e.g. an X-macro DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES in include/darlingserver/rpc-supplement.h, included by BOTH the guest dserver-ring.c dispatch and the server ringServiceThread `eligible` check), so the two can never drift. Then a hermetic gate asserts (a) every guest-advertised opcode is in the server allowlist, and (b) the server, on receiving an allowlisted-by-guest-but-not-server opcode, either replies-fallback or the attach is rejected -- never a silent consume+drop. RED arm: a guest opcode absent from the server set MUST fail the gate.

NAMING (gist invariant, keep DISTINCT in code+docs): "C2S allowlist" = may use ring TRANSPORT (Tier 1); "FastPathEligible/InlineEligible" = may SKIP the generic fiber/Call machinery (Tier 2). Never merge these concepts. Default every new op to Tier 1; attempt Tier 2 (no-fiber direct) ONLY with its own per-op crash/suspend proof (mach_reply_port is the only proven Tier-2 op).

VALIDATION per op (the P5 stricter bar): identical kern_return_t ring-vs-non-ring-UDS across ALL edges; ring_fast_suspend=0; fork-storm 3/3 .o=200/200 clients_blocked=0; counters clean. Deploy locally (7-copy map), restore prod OFF, NOTHING PUSHED. Branches: darlingserver perf/shmem-ring-abi-validator, xnu perf/shmem-ring-guest. See dar-1il P5 comments + memory dar-perf18-shmem-ring-transport.md (P5 block) + the agent brief dar-perf18-p5-agent-brief.md (deploy map, fork-timeline staging filter, sudo-gdb-for-crashes, validation protocol). After 2-3 ops share the SAME Tier-1 shape with no semantic drift, THEN a fast-op table is discussable (separate future bead) -- NOT before.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
DONE 2026-06-29 (Opus), LOCAL/not pushed. Commits: darlingserver perf/shmem-ring-abi-validator @9f9b24f, xnu perf/shmem-ring-guest @cd62bf3.

DELIVERED:
- NO-SILENT-DROP INVARIANT GATE (the prerequisite): single shared X-macro DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES in rpc-supplement.h, consumed by BOTH the server allowlist (ringServiceThread eligible, now macro-generated) and the guest dispatch. New hermetic RED->GREEN ring_drift_gate_test.c proves guest-set == server-set (RED arm -DDRIFT_GUEST_EXTRA = a guest op the server drops -> fails). All perf#18 ring gates GREEN.
- mach_port_allocate (40) + mach_port_insert_right (43) ring-migrated Tier-1 (generic fiber doWork, NOT inline -- kept ringFastPathEligible untouched). allocate's out-name travels via the guest-memory write the generic Call path already does; reply header-only. Both via existing gr_body_trap.

KEY NEGATIVE FINDING -- mach_port_deallocate is NOT ring-safe and was DROPPED from the allowlist (stays UDS): under launchd's REAL workload it destroys ports backing mapped regions -> vm munmap S2C upcall to the CALLING thread. A thread parked on a ring reply is not in recvmsg and cannot service an S2C -> server microthread blocks on the S2C reply semaphore while the guest blocks in the ring futex-wait = DEADLOCK (launchd wedged before shellspawn). NEW MEMBERSHIP RULE (rpc-supplement.h): a ring-eligible op must NEVER trigger an S2C upcall to the caller. This is the deadlock-class analogue of P5's off-fiber stack-corruption finding: each newly-migrated op needs a real-workload boot, not just an edge A/B.

LATENT FIX surfaced while chasing the wedge: a ring-originated call that performs an S2C upcall DEFERS its reply (_deferReplyForS2C); the deferred-reply flush in _s2cPerform() sent it over UDS unconditionally -> a ring-waiting guest never wakes. Factored Thread::_publishReplyToRingLocked() and call it from BOTH pushCallReply() and the deferred flush.

VALIDATION (ring-ON vs TRUE-UDS prod-off stack): 14-edge byte-IDENTICAL kern_return_t (allocate RECEIVE/PORT_SET/DEAD_NAME=0x0 name_ok, illegal=0x12; insert MAKE_SEND=0x0, invalid=0xf, illegal disp=0x11; all deallocate edges match). allocate p50 ~2.8us vs UDS ~14.4us (~5.1x); insert_right ~2.1us vs ~13.8us (~6.4x). counters ring_fast_fail=0 s2c_full=0 suspend=0 clients_blocked=0. boot+shellspawn OK. fork-storm 3/3 RESULT=DONE .o=200/200.

SCOPE NOTE: mach_port_extract_right is NOT in the RPC spec (generate-rpc-wrappers.py) -> out of scope. Of the 4 bead-named ops, 2 migrated (allocate, insert_right), 1 found unsafe (deallocate -> UDS), 1 nonexistent (extract_right). Prod restored OFF (server a13ec2ae), cmake OFF. NOTHING PUSHED. Harness pbbench.c/pbbench-run.sh in guest home.

title: perf#18 D15a: ring-attach timeline/reclaimability recon (RECON-ONLY; instrument default-OFF; decide A/B/C/D for earlier attach)
description: D15a recon. See full result in commit.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
D15a DONE + committed @038256a (LOCAL/not pushed). RECON-ONLY: default-OFF instrumentation only (env DARLING_SERVER_ATTACH_CENSUS=1). No attach move, no op migration, no arena/duplex/psynch. Lane-1 set unchanged (13 ops).

HEADLINE: the D14 inference ("reclaimable UDS tail is pre-attach -> move attach earlier") is REFUTED by direct measurement. attach_census_total_pre_attach_eligible = 9 -- across all 500 attached processes only NINE ring-eligible UDS calls EVER occurred before the ring attached. Attach is already very early (attach_ordinal p50=4: a process makes ~4 UDS calls -- checkin + ring_attach, NEITHER eligible -- then attaches).

WHERE THE RECLAIMABLE UDS REALLY IS: 2053 POST-attach calls to ring-eligible ops. They ride UDS AFTER the ring exists because the ring is SINGLE-OWNER (P3/P4: only the attaching thread owns it; other threads in the process fall back to UDS). Secondary: 204/704 attach attempts (29%) rejected reason=ABI -- a homebrew-test-prefix dylib-version-skew artifact (the ABI reject is the safety valve degrading to all-UDS; ~0 in a matched prod deploy).

DECISION among A) attach during checkin handshake / B) explicit early mldr-init attach / C) attach-on-first-eligible / D) server-side prewarm: STOP -- all four target the pre-attach window, which holds 9 reclaimable calls. C is ALREADY the status quo and already fires by ordinal ~4. None is worth doing.

REAL NEXT LEVER (a DIFFERENT bead, NOT D15a scope) = dar-1il.11 (D16): PER-THREAD ring ownership (the P4 "full per-thread rings" P3 deferred), so a non-owner thread's eligible ops ride the ring instead of UDS -- that is what moves the 2053-call pool.

INSTRUMENTATION (all default-OFF, hot path byte-identical when disarmed): Metrics D15a block (per-callnum pre/post-attach UDS + eligible subset + attach_ordinal histogram + attempt/reject tallies + reject-by-reason); Process _udsCallOrdinal + _ringAttachedAtOrdinal latch; Call::ringEligibleCallnum classifier; recorded at server.cpp:723 (genuine UDS choke point) + RingAttach handler. Gate tests/attach_census_gate_test.cpp (GREEN + 2 RED arms) wired into run-ring-shm-validate.sh; full perf#18 suite GREEN. Docs tests/PERF18-ATTACH-TIMELINE-D15a.md + -snapshot.json.

LESSON: "op observed on UDS" != "op ran before attach." Measure the window before re-architecting to widen it. call.cpp diff is D15a-only (0 FORK_TIMELINE). Prod binaries restored + md5-verified. Nothing pushed.

title: perf#18 D16: per-thread ring lanes (let every guest thread lazily acquire its own SPSC lane; gate FIRST, then impl; reclaim the 2053 post-attach non-owner-thread UDS pool)
description: D16 per-thread ring lanes. See the full self-contained brief in memory dar-perf18-d16-per-thread-ring-lanes-brief. NOT started; start only on explicit 'ебашь dar-1il.11'.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
D16 DONE — per-thread ring lanes. Commits darlingserver @c714f12 + xnu @76541a7, LOCAL/not pushed.

THE WIN (matched dyld+dylib+server A/B, same warm workload): post_attach_eligible_UDS 1533 -> 231 (-85%).
Per-op heatmap ring-share ~100% on the eligible ops (mach_reply_port 746/0, host_self_trap 491/0,
thread_self_trap 381/0, task_self_trap 372/0, uidgid 284/0, set_thread_handles 261/0, get_tracer 116/0);
only the first-eligible-call-before-lane-attach (started_suspended, vchroot_path) keeps a residual UDS
share. attach_rejects=0, ring_fast_fail/s2c_full/fast_suspend/clients_blocked all 0. fork-storm 3/3.

DESIGN: the SERVER was already per-thread (_ringThreads/_drainRings/per-thread RingAttach+wake-fd
Monitor) -> NO server change. Guest (dserver-ring.c): the six g_ring_* globals -> a fixed-max table
g_lanes[64] keyed by hashed gettid, each with its own {active,generation,owner_tid,map,size,wake_fd,seq}.
gr_lane_for_this_thread() find-or-attach via the EXISTING ring_attach (no new opcode, ABI stays v5);
lane exhaustion -> clean UDS fallback (never block, never share). Storage is a TABLE not __thread (__thread
aborts pre-pthread below libSystem; rpc-sleep-account.h "TLS aborts here", the P3 single-owner-as-TLS-dodge
precedent) -- the brief-authorized proven-safe fallback; per-thread SPSC invariant preserved.

GATE (the dar-my8 contract, done FIRST): tests/ring_multilane_gate_test.c exhaustively enumerates every
{acquire,reader,release,reclaim} interleaving over the active-bit/generation bookkeeping; GREEN=0
violations; RED arms -DRACE_PUBLISH_ACTIVE_BEFORE_INIT (5 found) + -DRACE_REUSE_WITHOUT_GEN_BUMP (1 found)
both fail. Wired into run-ring-shm-validate.sh; full perf#18 suite GREEN. acquire publishes active LAST
(claim CAS 0->2 sentinel); reclaim bumps generation; release clears active FIRST; postfork drops all lanes.

HYGIENE LESSON: the first D16 run had a CONSTANT 116-reject floor (reason=abi(2)) in BOTH arms because the
STALE prod dyld carried ABI v4 -- its early mldr-phase attach was rejected. Deploying the matched-HEAD dyld
(DEPLOY_DYLD=1) dropped rejects to 0 and gave the clean signal. dyld ALSO links the ring-attach code
(emulation_dyld) -- a matched A/B must redeploy dyld, not just the dylib.

NON-GOALS held: no earlier attach, no MPMC ring, no new opcodes, no duplex/msg_overwrite/psynch touched,
no shared lane, ABI v5 unchanged. Prod binaries (server+dylib+dyld, all locations) restored + md5-verified;
prod boots clean. Doc tests/PERF18-PER-THREAD-LANES-D16.md + both snapshots. NOTHING PUSHED.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: RE-MEASURE ON CONSISTENT DYLIB (session ae391f52) — TWO findings, one clean, one needing follow-up. Built a fresh guest libsystem_kernel.dylib (md5 0600ddd0) from the SAME tree as the deployed server (darlingserver@93c7049 / xnu@caddc2b = integration/arch pair; the previously-deployed guest dylib was 07-02, PRE-D16 -> deploy-skew masked the migration). FINDING 1 (clean, POSITIVE): with the consistent dylib, D10/D11/D16 migrations are LIVE on the A0-ARCH pair -- measured ring-share on a light load: set_thread_handles 100%, host_self_trap 76%, thread_self_trap 70%, mach_reply_port 68%, task_self_trap 67%, uidgid 54% (was ring=0 across the board on the skewed dylib). Confirms D16 per-thread lanes work here; the earlier "ring=0" was pure deploy-skew, NOT a regression, and D10/D11/D16 DONE are correct. Residual UDS = first-eligible-call-before-lane-attach (as the D16 brief predicted). FINDING 2 (needs follow-up, NOT concluded): brew reinstall xz FAILS on the 0600ddd0 dylib with "cannot duplicate fd 0 to fd 0: Bad file descriptor" in deep brew subprocesses, REPRODUCIBLY, even on a hard-cleaned workdir + fixed stdin -- while SIMPLE fork chains (seq 1 20; /usr/bin/true) PASS on it, and the OLD 07-02 dylib ran brew fine (first run, msg_total=101k). So there is a real behavior delta between the 07-02 guest dylib and the integration/arch guest dylib in deep fork/exec fd handling. Ruled OUT stand contamination (hard clean: both old+new dylib pass simple load; only new dylib + brew fails). NOT yet root-caused -- do not conclude regression vs build-artifact without a dedicated dylib-diff/bisect. Stand restored to the old working dylib (6bd251c3); server baseline d81b5cf1 untouched. LESSON captured: snapshot before clean on hangs (memory dar-snapshot-before-clean-on-hang).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: REGRESSION found in this D16 impl -> spun out as P0 dar-j7e7. postfork_reset closes fd 0 128x because g_lanes[] is zero-init (wake_fd==0, not -1) and the reset gates on wake_fd>=0. Deterministic brew 'cannot duplicate fd 0'. The D16 gate (ring_multilane_gate_test) covered active/generation races but NOT the wake_fd sentinel across postfork_reset -- that is the coverage gap. Fix + RED test tracked in dar-j7e7.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: CLEAN duplex go/no-go re-measure DONE (#131 finished), unblocked by the dar-j7e7 fix. Consistent fixed guest dylib 1cf2496f + server baseline d81b5cf1, real brew reinstall xz completed rc=0 (msg_total=101067, ~280x the startup-only 362-msg sample the STOP verdict rested on). Lane-1 migrations confirmed LIVE on the consistent dylib: set_thread_handles 100%, mldr_path 100%, host_self_trap 79%, mach_reply_port 74%, task_self_trap 74%, thread_self_trap 73%, uidgid 50%, vchroot_path 33% ring-share. DECISION-CRITICAL: caller_s2c>0 ops = 0, duplex-only verdicts = 0 -> NO live op drives a caller-S2C on a real heavy workload. The STOP verdict HOLDS on real data. Duplex lane = future-proofing for the destroy/memory-op migration stage (KEEP, not cut). Next Lane-1 targets by raw UDS volume: pthread_canceled (total=384158, ring=5), fork_wait_for_child (total=15255, ring=6).

title: perf#18 D17: post-D16 residual-UDS heatmap + classifier (RECON: classify the 231 post-attach eligible UDS by reason + top UDS latency sources; verdict A/B/C/D)

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
D17 DONE — post-D16 residual-UDS classifier + heatmap (RECON). Commits darlingserver @d7573c7 + xnu @a1425a0, LOCAL/not pushed.

VERDICT (the A/B/C/D question): the 231 post-attach eligible-UDS residual is **B/D, NOT A, NOT C**.
- Classifier (default-OFF, DARLING_SERVER_RESIDUAL_CENSUS=1, at the server.cpp:723 UDS choke point) put ALL 231 in `thread_has_ring` (eligible op on UDS DESPITE a live lane); reason-A buckets (thread_no_ring_proc_none/has) are BOTH 0. uds_despite_lane names exactly started_suspended (116) + vchroot_path (115).
- Reason C (exhaustion) conclusively 0: guest lane stats exhausted=0, max 3 ring threads/process vs cap 64; total_ring_threads_registered=150.
- ROOT CAUSE = the PER-IMAGE lane-table split: dserver-ring.c is compiled into BOTH the main dylib (`emulation`) and the dyld image (`emulation_dyld`), each with its OWN g_lanes. started_suspended is VARIANT_DYLD-only (runs in the dyld image's table); vchroot_path also has a structurally-UDS caller in the mldr binary (mldr.c:940, no ring code). So each of these two early-init ops misses the ring ~once per process.

NEXT LEVER: the user's proposed D18 "after lane acquire, send the same current request over the ring" would NOT help — that IS already what gr_*_trap does (attach then publish the same call). These miss for a different reason (image-split / cross-exec lane handoff), not attach-then-UDS. The real (small) next step = per-image lane-handoff investigation; it's a ~2/process tail, tiny vs the 1533→231 D16 win. Worth a bead only if those two ops' startup latency matters.

TOP UDS latency sources (other half of D17): all expected non-ring classes — pthread_canceled (1946, ~2us pthread floor, D9 caller-S2C/duplex-only), ring_attach/checkin/checkout (control-plane handshakes, can't ride the ring they establish), fork_wait_for_child (blocking WAIT not round-trip), set_dyld_info/set_executable_path (pre-attach dyld-image, NO_REPLY). D9's "hot != reclaimable" holds; no new ring lever here.

Instrumentation: residualReason buckets + udsDespiteLaneByCallnum + server-side lane proxies (totalRingThreadsRegistered/maxRingThreadsPerProcess) + guest lane-stats exit dump (DARLING_GUEST_LANE_STATS=1). Gate tests/residual_census_gate_test.cpp (GREEN + 2 RED arms) wired into run-ring-shm-validate.sh; full suite GREEN. Doc tests/PERF18-RESIDUAL-UDS-D17.md + snapshot. No migrations, no duplex/psynch, no lane-ownership change. Prod restored + md5-verified. NOTHING PUSHED.

LESSON: "UDS-despite-a-live-lane ≠ first-call-before-attach" — the natural assumption (from D16 prose) that the residual was unavoidable reason A was overturned by the classifier; it's a per-image coverage gap (B/D).

title: perf#18 D17-rerun: re-run the residual-UDS census on a REAL heavy long-lived multi-thread workload (D17's 231/2-per-process was measured on a startup-dominated true/echo micro-workload; confirm the verdict + get representative residual/top-UDS numbers)

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
NEXT-AGENT TASK (NOT started). Brief: memory/dar-perf18-d17-rerun-heavy-workload-brief.md.

WHY: D17's verdict (residual = B/D per-image lane split, NOT A/C; exhaustion=0) is structural and STANDS,
but its NUMBERS (231 ≈ 2/process, top-UDS table) were measured on a startup-dominated true/echo
micro-workload -> relative shares unrepresentative. User flagged this ("ты же тестил на bin true?").

TWO measurements wanted (user-directed):
(1) HEAVY long-lived workload for representative residual/top-UDS shares. PRIMARY WORKLOAD = a real
    configure + make build in the homebrew-test prefix -- THE canonical perf#18 target (the whole effort
    was to speed up Homebrew make/configure, which spawn hundreds of forked multi-threaded subprocesses:
    sh, cc1/clang, conftest, ld, as... with make -j = a natural fork-storm). Use a tiny package / minimal
    autotools project so it finishes in minutes but still spawns the subprocess swarm. Confirm the residual
    eligible-UDS stays a small ~2/process floor = a <1% tail on a realistic RPC total.
(2) MEANINGFUL fork-storm (D17's was 30x /usr/bin/true = startup+exit only). Children must do real ring
    work; crank concurrency to stress lane acquire/exhaustion/reclaim + postfork drop. KEY exhaustion test
    = ONE process with >64 concurrent eligible-op THREADS (GR_MAX_LANES=64 is per-process); D17 saw max 3.
    make -j partly covers this; an explicit threaded test is better if the prefix has one.

CONSTRAINTS (same as D17): RECON-only, default-OFF, no new migrations, no duplex/psynch, no lane-ownership
change. Binaries ALREADY built+staged in $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp (darlingserver.d17, libsystem_kernel.d17.dylib,
dyld.d17) -- no rebuild unless the tree moved. Reuse tests/run-residual-census-d17.sh skeleton; matched
server+dylib(3 loc)+dyld(2 loc) so attach_rejects=0. Restore prod + md5-verify. NEVER push.

Reuses [[dar-perf18-d17-residual-uds-classifier-brief]] + [[dar-perf18-d16-per-thread-ring-lanes-brief]].

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
D17-rerun DONE — commit @615636c (test-only: doc + 2 snapshots + runner; reused D17 binaries, no source changes), LOCAL/not pushed. Prod restored + md5-verified, boots clean.

Workload: ONE process × 96 concurrent eligible-op threads (task/host/thread_self_trap ×600 iters), run 3× + a 48-child fork-storm. 363,734 RPCs, census ON, ONE warm server (PID constant — no respawn).

FINDING (flips D17 under a WIDE process):
- Residual DOMINATED by reason A/C = LANE EXHAUSTION: thread_no_ring_proc_has = 118,998 (vs D17's 0). A 96-thread process pins EXACTLY 64 lanes (GR_MAX_LANES) and the remaining ~32 threads fall back to UDS every iteration. Guest stats reproducible across all 3 runs: acquired=64 exhausted≈39,330 held_now=64 max=64.
- D17 B/D tail (per-image lane-table split: started_suspended + vchroot_path) CONFIRMED a flat ~2/proc constant: thread_has_ring = 729 @ 363K RPCs (was 717 @ 132K) = <0.2% under load. D17's B/D verdict + mechanism unchanged, now quantified negligible.
- Even under exhaustion, host/thread_self_trap ride the ring ~66% (114,916/174,316). Degradation is a CLEAN UDS fallback: ring_fast_fail / s2c_full / fast_suspend / clients_blocked / attach_rejects ALL 0. No wedge, no corruption, no respawn.

LEVER (NOT this bead — separate if pursued): raise GR_MAX_LANES (currently 64) and/or reclaim lanes on thread-exit (reclaimed=0 observed — lanes held for thread life). Only matters for a single process with >64 concurrently-RPC-active threads (JVM, threadpool server, in-proc -j128). The motivating make/configure swarm = many modest-thread *processes* → hits the B/D floor, not exhaustion.

CAVEAT (env, not transport): a make -j clang build is NOT a usable census leaf in this harness — the darling-shell wrapping it hangs on teardown after objects are built, reproduced IDENTICALLY on the prod (pre-D16) dylib (control: 20/20 objects, then wrapper hung past its own timeout). Wide-thread + fork-storm probes stand in for the swarm.

Doc: tests/PERF18-RESIDUAL-UDS-D17-RERUN.md + snapshots. Brief: dar-perf18-d17-rerun-heavy-workload-brief (DONE). HEADs darlingserver @d7573c7 / xnu @a1425a0 (binaries unchanged from D17).

title: perf#18 mod_refs S2C-safety audit (default-ON BLOCKER) + ABI-tie drift gate + side-effect A/B
description:
perf#18 mod_refs S2C-safety audit (gist 09a2c447). BLOCKS ring default-ON. Reviewer raised dar-1il.1's "residual risk" on mach_port_mod_refs from minor to a DEFAULT-ON BLOCKER, and added two cross-cutting findings. Do NOT flip the ring default-ON (perf#18 P7 checklist) until item 1 below is resolved.

=== WHY (the deadlock class, restated) ===
The ring fast path is NOT just a faster UDS -- it changes wait semantics. A ring caller publishes one request, then PARKS on the s2c futex for EXACTLY ONE reply; it is NOT sitting in a UDS recvmsg and CANNOT service a server-to-client (S2C) upcall. So the load-bearing membership rule from dar-1il.1 is: a ring-eligible C2S op MUST NOT synchronously trigger an S2C upcall to the caller (this is SEPARATE from "non-blocking" / "generic fiber path" -- the fiber does NOT save you if the protocol needs caller-side S2C service while the caller is parked on the ring). This is exactly what sank mach_port_deallocate (destroy of a mapped-region-backed port -> vm munmap S2C -> caller parked on ring -> deadlock; launchd wedged pre-shellspawn).

=== ITEM 1 (the audit -- the bead's core, BLOCKER) ===
mach_port_mod_refs is currently ring-eligible for ALL args. But mod_refs(..., delta<0) that drops the LAST ref is semantically deallocate/destroy-like: if it destroys an object that pulls vm-munmap / cleanup -> S2C upcall to the caller -> SAME deadlock. dar-1il.1's "14-edge A/B destroyed only BARE ports" is insufficient: the bug class is a SIDE EFFECT (destroy->S2C), not a return code, so byte-identical kern_return_t does NOT prove safety.
Pick one, in DESCENDING safety:
  A. IMMEDIATELY remove mach_port_mod_refs from the ring C2S allowlist (DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES) until a proof exists. (Safest; smallest diff; un-blocks default-ON.)
  B. Allow ring ONLY for a proven-safe subset, e.g. positive delta / provably-not-last-ref / no-destroy, with a SERVER-SIDE arg guard AND a guest-side dispatch predicate. CRITICAL: the safety decision MUST be made BEFORE any state mutation -- you may NOT start the op, discover "oops, need S2C", and try to roll back.
  C. Keep as-is ONLY after a test that destroys a MAPPED-REGION-BACKED port via mod_refs(delta<0) reproducing a launchd-like scenario and proving NO deadlock + no wedge (boot + real workload, not just an edge A/B).
Recommend A now (restore default-ON safety), then a separate proof for B/C later if mod_refs latency matters. Either way: deterministic RED->GREEN gate committed with the change; if removed, the drift gate + allowlist test must reflect mod_refs absence (mirror how deallocate was handled in dar-1il.1).

=== ITEM 2 (drift gate must be tied to ABI/handshake, not just source equality) ===
DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES as a shared source-of-truth prevents drift only when guest+server are built from the SAME rpc-supplement.h. An OLD server + NEW guest (or vice versa) built from different headers still drift -> silent-drop wedge. Fix: make the ring C2S opcode SET part of the attach negotiation/ABI -- either bump the ring ABI version when the set changes, OR carry an opcode bitmask/hash in ring_attach and have the server REJECT->UDS (never silent-drop) on mismatch. This generalizes the no-silent-drop invariant across version skew. (Sub-task; not a default-ON blocker by itself but needed before shipping a build whose op-set differs from a deployed one.)

=== ITEM 3 (correctness must cover side-effects/lifecycle, not only kern_return_t) ===
For allocate/insert_right (and any future op), the A/B must assert STATE after the op, not just the return code: right actually present in the correct task namespace; refcounts/right-type match UDS; invalid/dead/send-once/receive edges leave no garbage; process-death/teardown cleanup matches UDS. Add these side-effect/lifecycle assertions to the harness (pbbench.c) for the already-migrated allocate/insert_right as a backfill.

=== STANDING per-op allowlist criteria (apply to every ring op henceforth) ===
1. No synchronous S2C upcall to the caller. 2. No blocking on guest participation. 3. Request shape fully validated. 4. Safety decidable BEFORE mutation. 5. Same XNU/dtape primitive as UDS. 6. Fallback explicit, never silent drop. 7. Ring-vs-UDS correctness covers SIDE EFFECTS, not only kern_return_t. 8. Process death/teardown tested. Tier-2 (no-fiber) adds: 9. no stack/fiber-sensitive bookkeeping; 10. no task/refcount side effects assuming microthread context; 11. proven-trivial body.
Three-tier model (keep distinct): Tier 0 = UDS only (complex/reentrant/destroy/S2C ops); Tier 1 = ring + generic fiber (safe normal-bookkeeping ops); Tier 2 = ring + no-fiber direct (tiny trivial e.g. mach_reply_port). _publishReplyToRingLocked (dar-1il.1) is defense-in-depth, NOT a cure for the S2C deadlock (the caller still can't accept the upcall while parked) -- do NOT use it as grounds to return deallocate/mod_refs to the ring.

=== STATE / POINTERS ===
dar-1il.1 DONE (darlingserver @9f9b24f, xnu @cd62bf3, LOCAL/not pushed; prod restored OFF a13ec2ae, cmake OFF). Ring set today = {task_self_trap, mach_reply_port, mach_port_mod_refs, mach_port_allocate, mach_port_insert_right}. Shared macro DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES in src/external/darlingserver/include/darlingserver/rpc-supplement.h. Drift gate tests/ring_drift_gate_test.c; fastpath gate tests/ring_fastpath_gate_test.c. Guest helpers src/external/xnu/.../linux_premigration/resources/dserver-ring.c. mod_refs guest route in .../xnu_syscall/mach/impl/mach_traps.c:_kernelrpc_mach_port_mod_refs_trap_impl. Harness $DPREFIX/Users/ilyagulya/pbbench.c + mrbench.c. See memory dar-perf18-shmem-ring-transport.md (P5-bulk block) + agent brief dar-perf18-p5-agent-brief.md (deploy map, fork-timeline filter, sudo-gdb, validation protocol). RED-TDD + commit-test-with-fix; NOTHING PUSHED.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
DONE 2026-06-29 (Opus), LOCAL/not pushed. All 3 items + live validation + RED->GREEN gates. darlingserver @6f9cbe8 (perf/shmem-ring-abi-validator), xnu @412e15c (perf/shmem-ring-guest). Prod restored OFF (server md5 a13ec2ae, cmake OFF).

ITEM 1 (BLOCKER) -- mach_port_mod_refs removed from ring (OPTION A). Smallest/safest diff, restores default-ON safety now. mod_refs is destroy-CAPABLE: mod_refs(right, delta<0) dropping the last user-ref of a receive right destroys the port, and destroying a mapped-region-backed port drives the SAME vm-munmap S2C upcall to a ring-parked caller that sank mach_port_deallocate -> deadlock. The hazard is a SIDE EFFECT of certain args (not the return code) and is undecidable before the ipc_space lock is held + refcount known (after mutation begins) -> violates 'decide safety BEFORE mutating'. dar-1il.1's byte-identical kern_return_t A/B destroyed only bare ports, so did NOT prove safety. Now UDS-only: removed from DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES; guest wrapper reverted to UDS; __dserver_ring_mach_port_mod_refs helper removed (decl+impl+stub); call.cpp comment updated; mod_refs in NEITHER C2S nor inline set. Ring set now = {task_self_trap, mach_reply_port, mach_port_allocate, mach_port_insert_right}. Option B (proven-safe positive-delta/not-last-ref subset with a pre-mutation server guard) left as a SEPARATE future bead if mod_refs latency ever matters.

ITEM 2 -- C2S opcode set tied to ring ABI/attach handshake. Chose hash (more precise than a bare ABI bump): 64-bit FNV-1a fold over the C2S callnums, computed identically both sides from the shared DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES macro, carried in the control block as c2s_opcode_hash. Server rejects the ring at attach (new dserver_ring_reject_opcode_set) on mismatch -> the thread uses UDS for EVERYTHING (never a silent per-op drop), so an old-server/new-guest skew degrades cleanly to all-UDS instead of wedging on the first divergent op. Struct grew -> DSERVER_RING_ABI_VERSION 2->3. Hash fn references callnums (generated rpc.h) so it is guarded by _DARLINGSERVER_API_H_; server build always has rpc.h in scope so the validator check is always live in production; the validate gate scripts now thread -I$GEN_RPC in.

ITEM 3 -- side-effect/lifecycle A/B (not just kern_return_t). Added run_sideeffects() to pbbench.c: after each op asserts OBSERVABLE STATE via mach_port_type()+mach_port_get_refs() -- allocate RECEIVE -> type 0x20000 recv_refs==1; insert MAKE_SEND -> +0x30000 send_refs==1; 2nd MAKE_SEND coalesces send_refs->2; mod_refs(-1) -> send_refs->1; deallocate drops send keeps receive; last-ref drop RECLAIMS the name (type_kr==KERN_INVALID_NAME 0xf, no garbage); PORT_SET 0x80000 + DEAD_NAME 0x100000 lifecycles; illegal-allocate leaves NO name. TRUE A/B vs prod-OFF build (ring compiled OUT, real UDS): EVERY EDGE + SE line byte-identical ring-vs-UDS. (NB allocate/insert_right ride the GENERIC fiber so FAST_OPS=0 does not reroute them -- the prod-OFF arm is the real UDS comparison.)

RED->GREEN gates (committed with the change): ring_drift_gate_test.c + ring_fastpath_gate_test.c: mod_refs pinned OUT (sanity asserts absent like deallocate); RED arm -DC2S_READD_MODREFS re-adds it -> 'mod_refs NOT C2S-allowlisted' fails. ring_shm_validate_test.cpp + ring_attach_check_test.cpp: opcode-set hash mismatch -> reject_opcode_set (flip 1 bit); matching hash accepted (non-vacuous). ring_wake_predicates_test.c: ABI assertion -> 3. Full suite run-ring-shm-validate.sh: all perf#18 ring gates OK.

LIVE (ring ON, booted): boot healthy + shellspawn up (NO wedge -- expected, item 1 REMOVES an op). Ring attached (opcode hash accepted). Counters: ring_fast_suspend=0 fail=0 fallback=0 s2c_full=0 clients_blocked=0; serviced_spin=25697. Latency ring p50 alloc ~3.5us / insert ~2.8us vs UDS ~15.4us / ~21.4us (~4.4x / ~7.6x). Fork-storm 3/3 RESULT=DONE .o=200/200 clients_blocked=0 (no dar-l8k/dar-6x4 regression).

DEFAULT-ON: mod_refs blocker RESOLVED (removed). PERF18-RING-DEFAULT-ON-CHECKLIST unblocked w.r.t. this audit; flipping default-ON remains a separate product call.

title: perf#18 Phase C: DESIGN-ONLY spec for the duplex/reentrant ring lane (Lane 2)
description:
perf#18 PHASE C (plan.md §Phase-C) — DESIGN-ONLY bead for the duplex/reentrant ring lane (Lane 2). DO NOT IMPLEMENT broad migration here. This bead produces a SPEC; the prototype is dar-1il.3.1 (Phase D), broad migration is dar-1il.3.2 (Phase E). NOT scheduled; multi-session, high-design — do NOT start unless the user asks.

=== WHY THIS LANE EXISTS ("wrong lane, not bad op") ===
The simple SPSC C2S fast ring (Lane 1) parks the calling guest thread on the s2c futex waiting for EXACTLY ONE reply (dserver-ring.c gr_wait_reply watches only the reply ring). While parked it is NOT in a UDS recvmsg, so it cannot service a server-to-client (S2C) upcall (mmap/munmap/mprotect/msync). Any op that triggers an S2C upcall to the CALLER deadlocks: server microthread blocks on the S2C reply semaphore; guest blocks in the ring futex; neither progresses. This sank mach_port_deallocate (dar-1il.1) and mach_port_mod_refs (dar-1il.2) — both destroy-capable, and destroying a mapped-region-backed port drives a vm-munmap S2C to the caller. These are NOT bad ops; they are WRONG-LANE ops. The duplex lane is their right home.

=== KEEP THE SIMPLE LANE SIMPLE (plan.md §5) ===
Do NOT turn the simple fast ring into a universal/full-duplex protocol. The duplex lane is a SEPARATE protocol: separate queues/offsets/cache-lines/state-words/stats/ABI bits (same memfd + control-block region OK). Fast lane must not pay duplex complexity. Lanes/concepts stay separate: SimpleRingC2SEligible != NoFiberFastEligible != DuplexRingEligible (see Phase-A bead .30.1).

=== DESIGN DELIVERABLES (plan.md §Phase-C §10) — spec only, no broad impl ===
1. Identify the EXACT S2C-upcall path for deallocate of a mapped-region-backed port (the canonical caller-S2C case).
2. Minimal duplex message types: C2S_PARENT_REQUEST, S2C_UPCALL, C2S_UPCALL_REPLY, S2C_FINAL_REPLY (CANCEL/DEATH/ERROR later). IDs: parent_req_id, upcall_id, thread_id, opcode, status, payload off/len. Do NOT reuse the simple-ring slot shape if that would bloat the fast path (plan.md §10.2).
3. Guest wait-pump semantics: while waiting for the final reply, drain the S2C ring, dispatch each upcall ON THE CALLER THREAD, publish C2S_UPCALL_REPLY, then adaptive-spin-then-futex (plan.md §10.3).
4. Server wait-for-upcall-reply semantics: post S2C upcall, wait on the upcall-reply ring (NOT assume caller is in recvmsg); must not wedge the single-threaded loop or starve other clients (plan.md §10.4 / §11.3).
5. Duplex ABI capability bit + opcode/op-set hash extension (mirror the simple-ring dserver_ring_c2s_opcode_hash; ABI bump v3->4). Negotiated at attach; mismatch => no duplex lane (clean fallback), never silent drop.
6. RED->GREEN gate SHAPES (to be implemented in Phase D): the wake-model interleaving gate is the HARD one — see below.
7. Non-goals (plan.md §Phase-C): no broad migration, no general multi-inflight initially, no fast-op table, no helper-thread S2C unless proven, no simple-lane perf changes.

=== HARD GATE, DESIGN IT FIRST (acceptance for THIS bead) ===
Re-derive the WAKE MODEL for bidirectional in-flight on ONE parked thread: its own C2S reply pending AND an S2C request pending simultaneously. The wake model now has TWO waiter relationships, not one — redo the EXHAUSTIVE interleaving enumeration (dar-my8 ring_wake_race_test.cpp style: 0 lost across every interleaving, RED arms that break each recheck). The DESIGN must specify the predicates this test will exercise; the test itself lands in Phase D. plan.md §15.1/§15.2 enumerate the protocol-correctness + RED-arm requirements (no-pump=>deadlock, missing parent recheck / waiter bit => lost wake, wrong correlation id => protocol error, dropped upcall reply => fail).

=== HAZARDS THE DESIGN MUST ADDRESS (plan.md §11) ===
reentrancy (guest lock L re-taken by pumped upcall), thread affinity (S2C may need exact caller current_task/current_thread/dtape/TLS — assume caller-thread pump first), server locks held while waiting for upcall reply, cancellation/death at each stage, ordering vs UDS, fast-lane contamination.

=== POINTERS ===
S2C mechanics: thread.cpp _s2cPerform / _deferReplyForS2C; Thread::_publishReplyToRingLocked (dar-1il.1) republishes a reply but does NOT make a parked caller service an S2C (building block, not a cure); dserver-ring.c gr_wait_reply (the park point). HEADs darlingserver @f997803 / xnu @412e15c. Membership canon: rpc-supplement.h next to DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES. Memory: dar-perf18-dar1il3-duplex-lane-brief.md, dar-perf18-ring-c2s-membership-canon.md, dar-perf18-shmem-ring-transport.md. plan.md = authoritative brief. NOTHING PUSHED policy stands. comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
=== P8 STEP 1 DONE 2026-06-30 (Opus): duplex wake-model DESIGN + exhaustive RED->GREEN gate; commit darlingserver @da13567, LOCAL/not pushed ===

The HARD GATE the whole duplex lane is built on is DONE -- proven BEFORE any transport wiring (dar-my8
discipline). Pure logic, hermetic. Simple Lane-1 fast ring UNTOUCHED.

LANDED (3 files, new test-dir only; no call.cpp -> no FORK_TIMELINE risk):
 - tests/PERF18-DUPLEX-LANE-DESIGN.md: the design grounded in the REAL code. Problem: _s2cPerform
   (thread.cpp ~1351) sends the S2C over Server::sendMessage (UDS) + blocks the microthread on
   _s2cReplySempahore; a guest parked in gr_wait_reply (dserver-ring.c ~233, FUTEX_WAIT on s2c_futex,
   NOT in recvmsg) can't service it -> deadlock (the deallocate/mod_refs wedge class). Lane-2 minimal
   protocol: 4 msg classes (C2S_PARENT_REQUEST / S2C_UPCALL / C2S_UPCALL_REPLY / S2C_FINAL_REPLY),
   one parent in flight/thread, one outstanding upcall, CALLER-THREAD pump, UDS fallback on unexpected
   shape, NO Lane-1 change. Wake model = TWO waiter relationships on one parked thread.
 - tests/ring_duplex_wake_gate_test.c + run-ring-shm-validate.sh: the exhaustive gate. GREEN proves
   DUPLEX-GUEST (parked guest never sleeps through a published S2C upcall; interleaving enum, 0 lost),
   DUPLEX-PUMP (drain ALL upcalls in a burst, while not if), DUPLEX-SERVER (server parked for the
   upcall reply never sleeps through it, 0 lost), correlation (accept only matching upcall_id/parent_id).
   RED arms exit nonzero: -DDUPLEX_NO_GUEST_PUMP, -DDUPLEX_WAITERBIT_AFTER_RECHECK, -DDUPLEX_WRONG_CORRELATION.
 - Full perf#18 ring gate suite GREEN incl. the new ring_duplex_wake gate (all perf#18 ring gates: OK).

WHY STOP HERE FOR THIS STEP: the wake model is the load-bearing correctness artifact and the part most
likely to be subtly wrong; proving it deterministic+lost-wake-free de-risks everything downstream. The
transport WIRING (Phase D proper) is the next, larger chunk.

REMAINING (Phase D = dar-1il.3.1, then Phase E = .3.2):
 2. wire the duplex transport: a server _s2cPerform variant that, for a RING-PARKED caller, posts the
    S2C on a duplex upcall ring + waits on the upcall-reply ring (instead of UDS sendMessage + assuming
    recvmsg); a guest pump in a gr_wait_reply sibling. New duplex ABI (control-block words + ABI bump
    v3->4 + extend the opcode/op-set hash to the S2C op set). Migrate ONE op: mach_port_deallocate.
 3. side-effect A/B vs prod-OFF UDS INCLUDING a real MAPPED-REGION-backed destroy under a launchd-like
    boot (the exact dar-1il.1 wedge) proving NO deadlock; namespace/refs/type/VM-cleanup/errors match.
 4. caller-thread semantics (current_task/current_thread/dtape correct on the pumped upcall); server
    doesn't block the whole dserver (one client waits S2C reply, others live); death/teardown cases
    (client dies parent-pending / after upcall publish / server teardown mid-wait); simple-ring perf
    unchanged (mach_reply_port p50 sub-us); fork-storm 3/3.

If step 2 proves too costly/slow -> ALSO a valid result: fix the architecture as "ring for closed ops,
UDS for reentrant ops" and bulk-migrate only closed ops. The gate stands either way. NOTHING PUSHED.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
=== P8 D1/D2 DONE 2026-06-30 (Opus): minimal duplex mailbox ABI v4 + LIVE synthetic S2C roundtrip; commit darlingserver @9b9e0ae, LOCAL/not pushed ===

D1/D2 scope ONLY (per instruction): the minimal transport to carry ONE synthetic S2C upcall to a
ring-parked caller + one reply, proven live. NO real op on the duplex lane (no deallocate/mod_refs),
NO guest-dispatched duplex allowlist, Lane-1 opcode membership + dispatch UNTOUCHED, mach_reply_port
not regressed, _s2cPerform + UDS S2C path UNCHANGED.

LANDED (4 files, +391; NO call.cpp/thread.cpp -- foreign FORK_TIMELINE excluded, 0 in the diff):
 - rpc-supplement.h ABI v3->v4: DUPLEX MAILBOX in the control block (own cache lines; Lane-1 rings
   byte-identical) = duplex_caps + S2C-upcall slot {ready,op,parent,id,arg} + C2S-upcall-reply slot
   {ready,parent,id,status,arg}; `ready` = release-store linearization (torn-write-safe). Pure helpers
   (publish_upcall / upcall_available / publish_reply / reply_ready[correlated] / consume_reply /
   echo_transform) shared by guest C, server C++, host test. Caps DUPLEX_CAP_SELFTEST, sentinel
   callnum 0xD0DE0001 (outside the generated enum), UPCALL_ECHO. ABI bump => v3<->v4 rejects at attach.
 - tests/ring_duplex_roundtrip_test.c (new) + harness: LIVE 2-thread roundtrip over the real helpers.
 - ring_wake_predicates_test.c: ABI assert 3u->4u.

ACCEPTANCE (the 10 criteria), honest status:
 1. synthetic duplex upcall completes -> YES (live: final == echo(arg)).
 2. wrong parent_id/upcall_id rejected -> YES (live: server flags protocol error, no resume, no final
    reply; RED -DDUPLEX_RT_IGNORE_CORRELATION fails).
 3. burst drained by while-pump -> COVERED ABSTRACTLY by ring_duplex_wake_gate_test.c MODEL 1b. The
    minimal protocol is ONE-outstanding, so a simultaneous burst is vacuous in the live transport; the
    roundtrip test pins a one-outstanding SEQUENCE instead (does NOT fake a burst -- honest).
 4. caller-thread context proven -> YES (live: pump tid == guest tid != server tid, logged).
 5. server doesn't block unrelated clients -> BY DESIGN + partial proof: the synthetic server wait is a
    BOUNDED scope-poll (returns control, yields), not an unbounded block; the host test models it as a
    bounded loop. FULL proof (main loop drains other clients while one waits) lands when the server
    _s2cPerform duplex variant is wired (next step) -- D1/D2 is the mailbox+protocol, not yet the
    server-loop integration. Flagged, not overclaimed.
 6. Lane-1 perf unchanged -> YES (live pbbench under v4: allocate p50 3347ns / insert_right p50 2545ns
    == v3 baseline within noise; bad=0; 14/14 side-effects ok=1; call.cpp/thread.cpp dispatch untouched).
 7. UDS S2C path still works for non-ring callers -> YES by construction (_s2cPerform UNCHANGED).
 8. ABI v4 reject/fallback -> YES (validator reject_abi on abi_version mismatch is live in the gate;
    v4<->v4 boots+attaches clean, NOTICE once, no reject/wedge).
 9. death/teardown safe failure -> PARTIAL: server scope-wait is bounded (times out -> parent fails ->
    would UDS-fall-back) rather than hanging; full lifecycle stress (client dies parent-pending / after
    upcall publish / server teardown mid-wait) is the next step's bar, not D1/D2.
 10. full perf#18 gate suite GREEN -> YES (incl. new ring_duplex_roundtrip + ring_duplex_wake; ABI v4
    struct growth self-adjusts validate/attach/datapath/wake gates off sizeof).

DEPLOYED: dev tree v4 ON (server cd0b5937, dylib d080f2d6); boot clean (1 cold-first-boot refusal then
GREEN -- known flakiness). OFF build clean (duplex gated). OFF rollback binaries still at
$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/prod-bak-stage1/.

DESIGN POINTS HONORED: (a) duplex is NOT "ring enabled -> duplex"; the next wiring step gates it on the
CONJUNCTION (caller in duplex-capable parent wait AND caps say duplex AND upcall shape supported AND
parent_id known AND a free upcall slot), else old UDS -- encoded in the design doc + reply_ready's
correlation. (b) the upcall-reply wait must be SCOPED to the operation, main loop keeps draining --
realized as a bounded scope-poll; server-loop integration is the next step.

NEXT (still D->E, NOT done): wire the server _s2cPerform duplex variant (the conjunction guard) + the
guest pump in a gr_wait_reply sibling + a guest __dserver_ring_duplex_selftest entry point driving it
through the REAL dylib under a boot; THEN migrate mach_port_deallocate of a mapped-region-backed port +
side-effect A/B under a real boot + full death/teardown lifecycle. NOTHING PUSHED.

title: perf#18 P8 D3: real _s2cPerform duplex wiring + real guest pump selftest (end-to-end, NOT deallocate)
description:
perf#18 P8 D3 (gist 28ce03ba): end-to-end duplex integration selftest — wire the REAL server _s2cPerform duplex variant + the REAL guest wait-pump through the ACTUAL booted dylib. This is the LAST mandatory bridge between the synthetic transport (D1/D2 DONE @9b9e0ae) and the real mach_port_deallocate problem. Do NOT migrate deallocate until this is stably GREEN through the real dylib.

FULL SELF-CONTAINED BRIEF: memory/dar-perf18-p8-d3-real-s2cperform-brief.md (read it first).

SCOPE (exactly this): guest duplex wait-pump (sibling of gr_wait_reply) + __dserver_ring_duplex_selftest entry point; server GUARDED duplex variant of Thread::_s2cPerform (thread.cpp ~1351, guard in the 1363-1387 _rwlock block); sentinel parent op recognized in ringServiceThread (call.cpp ~1473); synthetic S2C selftest through the real dylib under boot; OLD UDS _s2cPerform path unchanged unless the guard passes. Env-gated (DARLING_SERVER_DUPLEX_SELFTEST), never on a real op.

IRON CONJUNCTION GUARD (_s2cPerform uses duplex ONLY if ALL hold, else verbatim UDS): (1) caller currently in a duplex parent wait; (2) ring ABI v4+ AND cb.duplex_caps has DUPLEX_SELFTEST; (3) parent_id known+active; (4) upcall shape supported (UPCALL_ECHO); (5) no outstanding upcall slot occupied; (6) correlation ids allocated+nonzero; (7) caller is the SAME client/ring instance. NOT "ring enabled -> duplex".

SECOND DESIGN POINT: the server wait for C2S_UPCALL_REPLY must be SCOPED to the op/microthread; the main loop keeps draining other clients/rings/UDS. Do NOT block the whole dserver (likely a state machine in ringServiceThread: publish upcall + return; a later drain sees the reply + completes the parent).

PITFALLS (each needs a test or argued-safe note): slot cleanup after timeout/error (no stale ready/ids); ABA/correlation reuse; wrong-client isolation; wait-state published BEFORE server reads it; recheck-before-sleep (P7); server wait scope (no starvation); fallback == exactly old UDS; final reply routes to the original ring waiter; teardown on client death = bounded release; Lane-1 perf class unchanged.

ACCEPTANCE (all): real BOOTED guest selftest passes through the actual dylib; a duplex-S2C server counter increments; OLD UDS _s2cPerform still works for a non-duplex caller (prove a real mmap/munmap S2C); wrong parent/upcall id rejected LIVE; timeout clears slot + no wedge; an unrelated client progresses while one duplex parent waits; full perf#18 gates GREEN; Lane-1 pbbench unchanged (allocate p50 ~3.3us/insert ~2.5us/reply_port sub-us); fork-storm 3/3.

NON-GOALS: NO deallocate migration (next bead, only after this is GREEN); NO mod_refs/deallocate in any production allowlist; NO Lane-1 membership change; NO multi-inflight/helper-thread/general duplex/fast-op table. Add a doc line: "live duplex = ONE outstanding upcall per parent; burst-drain is model-level only (ring_duplex_wake_gate_test MODEL 1b) until multi-upcall."

HEADs darlingserver @9b9e0ae / xnu @0c0f0e6. NOTHING PUSHED. call.cpp foreign FORK_TIMELINE diff: stage only your hunks (old-start >= ~1300), verify 0 FORK_TIMELINE staged.

Read dar-perf18-dar1il3-duplex-lane-brief + tests/PERF18-DUPLEX-LANE-DESIGN.md first. Gist: https://gist.github.com/IlyaGulya/28ce03bad73a0d3f6db2c7777e73708a. comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
**P8 D3 (dar-1il.3.1.1) DONE 2026-06-30 (Opus, commit darlingserver @e1d7bfc, xnu @4d9ece8, NOT pushed): REAL-DYLIB duplex selftest GREEN end-to-end + bounded-wait safety proven.** The last bridge between synthetic duplex (D1/D2 @9b9e0ae) and the real deallocate problem is closed: a synthetic S2C ECHO upcall travels server->ring-parked-guest->reply through the ACTUAL booted dylib + real server code. WIRING: (server thread.cpp) `_s2cTryDuplexLocked` = iron 7-condition conjunction guard + duplex-mailbox publish (the SHARED primitive a real op uses in Phase E); `_drainDuplexReply` = main-loop harvest of the correlated reply + parent-final-reply publish (state-machine completion, NOT a fiber park -> the server wait is intrinsically scoped to the op, main loop keeps draining). Lock-free `_duplexInFlightFast` atomic so the hot Lane-1 drain pays one relaxed load/thread (NO rwlock) when nothing is in flight. (server call.cpp) sentinel parent op `DSERVER_RING_DUPLEX_SELFTEST_CALLNUM` recognized in `ringServiceThread` BEFORE the eligible check, env-gated `DARLING_SERVER_DUPLEX_SELFTEST=1` (server env only; no inheritance hazard server-side). (guest dserver-ring.c) `gr_duplex_wait_reply` = gr_wait_reply sibling that pumps the S2C upcall mailbox (handle ECHO on the CALLER thread + publish correlated reply + doorbell) while waiting for the parent final reply, with a BOUNDED slow-path FUTEX_WAIT; `__dserver_ring_duplex_selftest`; one-shot driver `__dserver_ring_maybe_run_duplex_selftest` from task_self_trap_impl. TRIGGER = a MARKER FILE /private/tmp/.dring-duplex-go (NOT an env var): the harness drops it, the FIRST guest to claim (unlink) it runs the selftest exactly once. LIVE on booted H tree: guest prints `[dring-duplex-selftest] PASS rc=0 arg=0xC0FFEE42 result=0x9AA5B418 expect=0x9AA5B418` (echo = arg ^ 0x5A5A5A5A, exact); ring_duplex_s2c increments, ring_duplex_reject=0, s2c_full=0, fast_suspend=0. A NON-duplex process ran fine WITHOUT incrementing ring_duplex_s2c (guard gates duplex to the selftest only; UDS S2C path -- dyld/runtime mmaps -- unchanged). RED->GREEN: tests/run-duplex-real-selftest.sh; RED arm `-DDUPLEX_RED_NO_GUEST_PUMP` (rebuild dserver-ring.c with the guest pump disabled) -> selftest does NOT print PASS (parent reply never arrives) and the bounded wait gives up (~3s) so NO wedge; an unrelated client still runs.

RED PRGON SURFACED + FIXED TWO REAL DEFECTS (the lane's core safety): (1) the duplex wait-pump's slow-path FUTEX_WAIT had a NULL (indefinite) timeout -> a lost/never-serviced upcall parked the guest FOREVER = exactly the deadlock the duplex lane exists to prevent. FIX: bounded slow-path wait (50ms/round * 60 = ~3s cap then give up -> NULL -> caller treats as failure; never trips in GREEN where the reply lands in us). (2) the original env-var trigger was inherited by launchd/shellspawn, so a blocking selftest in those daemons disrupted/wedged boot. FIX: marker-file trigger consumed by exactly one process, no env inheritance. Lesson: a caller-S2C lane MUST bound every park, and a test hook MUST NOT ride inherited env into daemons.

ABI v4 unchanged (no new mailbox words). NON-GOAL held: NO deallocate migration -- dar-1il.3.2 stays blocked until this is the baseline. CAVEAT (doc): live duplex = ONE outstanding upcall per parent; burst-drain is model-level only (ring_duplex_wake_gate_test MODEL 1b) until multi-upcall. New counters ring_duplex_s2c/ring_duplex_reject. Rollback backups $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/prod-bak-d3/.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: CORRECTION to the trigger detail in the prior comment: the FINAL shipped trigger is the env var DARLING_GUEST_DUPLEX_SELFTEST=1 set ONLY on the per-command darling-shell invocation against an ALREADY-WARM server (NOT at boot, NOT a marker file). The marker-file approach was tried and dropped: raw guest open() does reach host /tmp, but 'exactly one claimer' was unreliable (a non-ring claimer consumed the trigger silently) and a marker present during a run let shellspawn run a stalling selftest. The env-per-command discipline is what makes it safe: daemons (launchd/shellspawn) started before the env exists never inherit it, so only the targeted leaf runs the selftest. Everything else in the prior comment stands (commits darlingserver @e1d7bfc / xnu @4d9ece8, GREEN PASS, fork-storm 3/3, bounded-wait fix, lock-free Lane-1).

title: perf#18 Phase E: duplex acceptance + migrate destroy-capable/caller-S2C ops off UDS
description:
perf#18 PHASE E (plan.md §Phase-E) — duplex acceptance + possible broad migration. ONLY after the Phase-D prototype (dar-1il.3.1) is GREEN. This is where destroy-capable / caller-S2C ops actually leave UDS for the duplex lane.

=== CANDIDATES (each proven independently, not en masse) ===
 - mach_port_deallocate
 - destroying mach_port_mod_refs (delta<0 last-ref)
 - other caller-S2C-capable ops surfaced by audit

=== PER-OP BAR (plan.md §Phase-E + §8 + §15) — every op must pass ALL ===
 - side-effect A/B vs UDS: kern_return_t AND port-name validity, mach_port_type bitmask, mach_port_get_refs, namespace state, name reclamation, no garbage on error, cleanup-on-process-death, right-kind distinctions; for destructive ops ALSO last-ref behavior, mapped-region-backed behavior, VM cleanup, S2C/upcall behavior, dead-name behavior, cleanup timing;
 - lifecycle/death tests (client death at each duplex stage; server teardown mid-parent);
 - reentrancy audit (caller-thread context, guest locks, server locks, nested calls, ordering vs UDS);
 - performance A/B vs UDS (p50/p90/p99) — success is NOT sub-µs; success = correct + avoids the simple-ring deadlock class + beats/approaches UDS enough to justify duplex complexity + simple ring unchanged;
 - duplex protocol correctness gates (plan.md §15.1/§15.2): parent final-reply, server S2C-before-final-reply, guest handles S2C while parent pending, guest replies to upcall, server resumes parent, NO lost wake on parent reply / on upcall / on upcall reply; deterministic RED arms (no-pump=>deadlock, missing parent recheck=>lost wake, missing waiter bit=>lost wake, wrong correlation id=>protocol error, server drops upcall reply=>fail).

=== ABI / MEMBERSHIP ===
Duplex membership negotiated like the simple-ring opcode hash but for the DUPLEX op set + an ABI capability bit (extend the dserver_ring_c2s_opcode_hash machinery to the S2C/duplex op set; ABI bump). No silent drop: if the guest can publish a duplex opcode, the server services it or rejects the lane at attach.

=== EXPLICIT NON-GOALS (plan.md §16) ===
Do NOT add deallocate/mod_refs back to the SIMPLE ring. Do NOT build a generic fast-op table here. Do NOT make the simple ring full-duplex. Do NOT route unknown ops through any ring. Do NOT silently drop env-disabled opcodes. Do NOT claim stress covers architecturally-near-zero paths. Do NOT use kern_return_t equality as the only correctness proof.

=== POINTERS ===
Depends on dar-1il.3.1 (Phase-D prototype) GREEN, which depends on dar-1il.3 (Phase-C design). HEADs darlingserver @f997803 / xnu @412e15c. plan.md = authoritative. NOTHING PUSHED.

title: perf#18 D4: migrate EXACTLY mach_port_deallocate to the duplex lane (one op, brutal acceptance)
description:
perf#18 D4/E first real-op migration (gist 6831df7c): migrate EXACTLY mach_port_deallocate to the duplex lane. NOT mod_refs, NOT generic duplex, NOT multi-inflight, NOT a simple-ring/no-fiber change.

FULL SELF-CONTAINED BRIEF: memory/dar-perf18-d4-deallocate-via-duplex-brief.md (read it first, plus dar-perf18-p8-d3-real-s2cperform-brief + plan.md Phase-E/§8/§15/§16).

GOAL: the mapped-region-backed mach_port_deallocate that WEDGED over the simple ring (dar-1il.1: destroying a VM-backed receive right drives a vm-munmap S2C upcall to a ring-parked caller -> deadlock) must complete over the duplex lane, side effects BYTE-IDENTICAL to true UDS.

BUILD ON D3 (DONE @e1d7bfc server / @4d9ece8 xnu): _s2cTryDuplexLocked (guard+publish), _drainDuplexReply (state-machine harvest+final-reply), gr_duplex_wait_reply (bounded caller-thread pump), ABI v4 + DUPLEX_CAP_SELFTEST, counters ring_duplex_s2c/reject. The transport works for a synthetic ECHO; deallocate is the first REAL op with real side effects + a real caller-S2C.

ROUTING (critical): guest sends deallocate on duplex ONLY if ABI/caps allow duplex-deallocate (add DUPLEX_CAP_DEALLOCATE + negotiate like the c2s opcode hash); server STILL validates shape and can DECLINE BEFORE mutation -> UDS fallback. If a clean pre-mutation decline cant be guaranteed for all shapes, first enablement = selftest-only or a VERY NARROW targeted shape.

INVARIANT (permanent duplex policy): fail closed with a BOUNDED timeout. NEVER infinite futex wait. NEVER silent drop. NEVER partial mutation then retry-to-UDS (double-effect).

HARD GATE FIRST (dar-my8/D3 discipline): extend the duplex wake/correlation gate to model the deallocate S2C (munmap) shape with deterministic RED arms (no-pump=>deadlock, missing parent recheck/waiter bit=>lost wake, wrong correlation=>protocol error, drop upcall reply=>fail, partial-mutation-then-UDS=>double-effect). GREEN before wiring the real op.

ACCEPTANCE (brutal, ALL): (1) old bad case reproduced/controlled (mapped-region deallocate over simple ring disallowed/RED); (2) duplex GREEN same op completes; (3) side-effect A/B vs true UDS byte-identical: kern_return_t, port type, refs, namespace, name reuse, VM/mapped-region cleanup, errors on invalid/dead/wrong rights, no garbage on error; (4) S2C proof: ring_duplex_s2c increments, reject understood, caller-thread handling, final reply to original waiter; (5) lifecycle: client death pre/post-S2C, timeout clears mailbox, server teardown bounded; (6) isolation: unrelated client progresses while deallocate waits, fork-storm 3/3, shellspawn clean, boot clean; (7) regression: full gates GREEN, Lane-1 pbbench unchanged, mach_reply_port sub-us-ish p50, deallocate no longer UDS in the targeted safe case but unknown shapes fall back/reject cleanly. Perf bar: NOT sub-us; correct + avoids the deadlock class + justifies duplex complexity + simple ring unchanged.

User explicitly authorized starting this (gist 6831df7c). HEADs darlingserver @e1d7bfc / xnu @4d9ece8. NOTHING PUSHED. If deallocate-via-duplex diverges from UDS or cant guarantee pre-mutation decline -> VALID result: keep deallocate UDS, report exactly which shape/side-effect diverged.
close_reason: D4 DONE; machinery built+GREEN at every reproducible level; live deadlock-cure N/A for deallocate (mach_port_deallocate doesn't drive a munmap S2C in Darling -- make_memory_entry stubbed). Kept default-OFF. Real S2C-cure proof reopened on vm_deallocate as dar-1il.3.2.2. Commits darlingserver @297143f / xnu @bd208c6, not pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: D4 DONE + CLOSED N/A-for-S2C-cure 2026-06-30 (Opus). Commits darlingserver @297143f + xnu @bd208c6, LOCAL/not pushed. Full duplex-deallocate machinery built + GREEN at every reproducible level: HARD GATE FIRST tests/ring_duplex_dealloc_gate_test.c (RED arms -DDUPLEX_PARTIAL_THEN_UDS double-effect + -DDUPLEX_FABRICATE_ON_DROP fake-success both fail; GREEN passes); ABI v4->v5 (DUPLEX_CAP_DEALLOCATE + DUPLEX_UPCALL_MUNMAP + DUPLEX_DECLINE sentinel + typed munmap mailbox payload + publish_munmap_upcall/reply helpers; v4<->v5 rejects at attach); server _s2cTryDuplexMunmapLocked + _s2cPerform munmap-via-mailbox routing + _drainDuplexReply REAL-op fiber resume (synth dserver_s2c_reply_munmap_t -> up _s2cReplySempahore; mis-correlation -> synth FAILED reply, no fabricated success) + ringServiceThread deallocate-as-duplex-parent dispatch w/ pre-mutation DECLINE; guest gr_duplex_pump_once munmap shape (real munmap(2) on caller thread) + __dserver_ring_mach_port_deallocate_duplex (hatch DARLING_GUEST_DUPLEX_DEALLOCATE=1 default OFF + cap) + mach_traps.c try-duplex-then-UDS; new counters ring_duplex_parent/decline. KEY: _pickS2CThread returns currentThread() for the current process => the munmap S2C is delivered to the SAME (parent) thread => mailbox/park design correct. LIVE A/B (warm booted homebrew prefix): deallocate RIDES the duplex lane (ring_duplex_parent=11, decline=0, reject=0), side effects BYTE-IDENTICAL UDS-vs-duplex (VM_ALLOC/VM_DEALLOC kr=0, COMMON refs 2->2, DEALLOC_NULL kr=0, DEALLOC_BOGUS kr=15), both arms exit 0 no wedge. fork-storm 3/3 (200/200 clients_blocked=0); Lane-1 fast path unaffected (ring_fast_hit 42->70); isolation holds (unrelated /bin/echo ran concurrently w/ a duplex deallocate, exit 0); host gate suite GREEN incl new gate + munmap roundtrip; pbbench Lane-1 SE/edge all ok=1. HEADLINE FINDING (honest, brief allows it): mach_port_deallocate does NOT drive a munmap S2C in Darling -- mach_make_memory_entry_64 is a STUB (stub (unsafe): _mach_make_memory_entry), so the port-destroy->named-entry->submap-free->vm_map_remove->munmap-S2C path is unreachable via a port deallocate; the ONLY munmap-S2C trigger is vm_deallocate (different op, not routed to duplex). So ring_duplex_s2c stays 0 and the dar-1il.1 deadlock CANNOT be reproduced via this op => the live deadlock-CURE acceptance (items 1+4 S2C) is N/A for deallocate. Disposition (user direction): close deallocate-via-duplex as N/A for the live cure proof, keep default-OFF, do NOT force a synthetic S2C; OPEN the real S2C-cure proof on vm_deallocate (new bead). Machinery is correct+safe (fail-closed, pre-mutation decline, byte-identical side effects); only the real-world S2C trigger is absent for this op in Darling.

D5 = route vm_deallocate (the op that DOES drive a real caller munmap S2C, unlike mach_port_deallocate which is N/A in Darling) through the EXISTING D4 duplex transport, behind a NEW cap DUPLEX_CAP_VM_DEALLOCATE + per-command hatch DARLING_GUEST_DUPLEX_VM_DEALLOCATE=1 (default OFF). NO new transport.

GOAL (the headline D4 could not get): prove the live caller-S2C cure END-TO-END — vm_deallocate triggers munmap S2C, S2C travels over the duplex mailbox, the caller-thread pump performs the real munmap, the server fiber resumes, the final parent reply returns, side effects match UDS. The proof = ring_duplex_s2c > 0.

HARD GATE FIRST: confirm/extend tests/ring_duplex_dealloc_gate_test.c covers the vm_deallocate munmap S2C flow (shape-identical to D4) GREEN before wiring.

ACCEPTANCE (gist, ALL): (1) routing — ring_duplex_parent>0 AND ring_duplex_s2c>0, reject/timeout/fail explained; (2) live S2C — UPCALL_MUNMAP increments, guest real munmap(2) on caller thread, server fiber resumes, final reply to original waiter; (3) A/B vs UDS STRICTER than D4 (vm_deallocate touches the address space directly) — verify ACTUAL mapping state before/after (region truly unmapped, subsequent access faults), NOT just return codes: kern_return_t, repeated-deallocate, invalid addr/size errors, partial-range if applicable, process cleanup/no garbage; (4) DEADLOCK PROOF — no-pump RED wedges/fails BOUNDEDLY (hazard real + bound prevents wedge), pump GREEN completes (the dar-1il.1 cure, finally live); (5) exactly-once — no double munmap, no partial-then-UDS, no fabricated success on dropped/corrupt reply; (6) isolation — unrelated client progresses while a vm_deallocate parent is pending, fork-storm 3/3, shellspawn/boot clean; (7) regression — full ring gates GREEN, Lane-1 pbbench unchanged, mach_reply_port sub-us-ish p50, default-OFF.

KEY RISK (gist): vm_deallocate is directly tied to the address space + real munmap(2), so the side-effect A/B MUST check actual mapping state, not just kern_return.

NON-GOALS: NO mod_refs, NO deallocate default-ON, NO broad/generic duplex table, NO multi-inflight, NO helper-thread S2C, NO Lane-1 changes.
