comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: RECON COMPLETE 2026-06-30 (Opus). Doc: tests/PERF18-MSG-OVERWRITE-RECON.md (committed darlingserver @8ecd24e, LOCAL/not pushed). RECOMMENDATION: (1) NO-GO Lane-1 migration — blocking receive breaks closed req->reply + variable/complex/descriptor body; (2) NO-GO whole-op migration — receive must stay UDS (real work), OOL needs an mmap-sideband D6 lacks; (3) CONDITIONAL-GO future scoped bead = Lane-2 (duplex) of the SIMPLE SEND-ONLY subset (MACH_SEND_MSG && !MACH_RCV_MSG && rcv_size==0 && !COMPLEX && no descriptors), gated behind (a) extending D6 munmap-sideband to an MMAP-S2C sideband [PREREQUISITE], (b) true side-effect A/B, (c) fork-storm/isolation/Lane-1-no-regress. KEY DANGER FINDINGS: msg_overwrite can drive mmap+munmap+mprotect+msync caller-S2C (D6 covers only munmap); OOL receiver is generally a DIFFERENT process than sender; port-right descriptors mutate receiver namespace/refcounts; blocking receive disqualifies Lane-1; body read via readMemory(/proc/pid/mem) is S2C-free, only descriptor materialization drives S2C. HOTNESS: 19% of all RPC (perf#9-decompose, committed, cited 4 files) — reclaimable prize concentrated in the send-only fraction. CHEAP NEXT MEASUREMENT before any build = add a send-only-vs-receive per-callnum split counter (rcv_size==0 && !MACH_RCV_MSG). D6 recorded as the architectural prerequisite. The conditional Lane-2 build is a SEPARATE future bead; do not start unless asked. Recon work itself is DONE; bead stays OPEN only because parent dar-1il.3.2 (Phase E) is open.

title: perf#18 D9: global RPC heatmap + lane eligibility census — find the next hot CLOSED op by DATA (not guess); pick ONE next migration
description:
perf#18 D9 — global RPC heatmap + lane eligibility census (user-directed 2026-06-30, gist a3cf5280). NEXT actionable perf bead after the D8 STOP on mach_msg_overwrite. PURPOSE: stop guessing the next op to migrate; find the next hot CLOSED op SYSTEMATICALLY by data. The SHM machine is proven (Lane-1 generic fiber + Tier-2 no-fiber + duplex sideband + lane taxonomy + ABI/hash + wake model); the open question is no longer "can SHM work" but "where does it give >1-2% system win".

DELIVERABLE: a default-OFF per-callnum diagnostic + a ranked decision table + ONE recommended next migration bead. NO production behavior change. NOTHING PUSHED.

MEASURE (default-OFF, cheap census + sampling, per callnum): count, transport (uds/ring/duplex/local), sampled latency p50/p90/p99, total_ns, and detectable flags: may_block, did_s2c, did_caller_s2c, did_destroy, used_fiber, complex_payload, bytes_in/out. Reuse the existing perf#9 per-call histogram (Metrics::recordCall + perCallLatency) as the base; add the transport + flag dimensions. Arm via an env hatch like the D8 census (DARLING_SERVER_*_CENSUS), so a warm server measures without rebuild.

RANK by expected_win = call_count * (uds_p50 - expected_ring_p50) * eligibility_confidence (NOT just "op hot" — mach_msg_overwrite was hot but low-eligibility => STOP). 

CLASSIFY each top op into a lane verdict: Lane-1 candidate (closed req->reply, no caller-S2C, no destroy, fixed/simple args, no blocking receive, no fd weirdness) / Tier-2 direct (Lane-1 + trivial body + no fiber-sensitive bookkeeping, like mach_reply_port) / Duplex candidate (caller-S2C exists/possible but parent otherwise worth migrating) / UDS-only (blocking, receive, unknown-complex, OOL/rights-heavy, destroy without duplex proof). Judge against the 5-rule membership canon.

RUN: boot + shellspawn + fork-storm + a representative build-ish workload; produce tables (top by count, top by total time, top Lane-1 candidates, top Tier-2 candidates, UDS-only/no-go list). THEN recommend exactly ONE next migration bead.

EXPLICIT NON-GOALS (user, verbatim): do NOT migrate more ops yet; do NOT touch mach_msg_overwrite; do NOT expand duplex; do NOT build the mmap sideband; do NOT change Lane-1 membership.

SUCCESS = (1) no behavior change (2) diagnostics default-OFF (3) full gates GREEN (4) clean ranked table of top RPCs (5) each top op gets a lane classification (6) ONE clear next target chosen by data.

Build on darlingserver @6e7cbcc / xnu @b412c28. Reuses the D8 census pattern (Call::callFromMessage chokepoint, Metrics counters, stat socket), perf#9 per-call histo, the lane taxonomy / membership canon, and the perf#25.1 wait-vs-service method. Full brief: memory/dar-perf18-d9-rpc-heatmap-eligibility-brief.md.

title: perf#18 D11: bulk closed-fast Lane-1 migration (5 fixed-arg ops: uidgid, set_thread_handles, started_suspended, get_tracer, task_is_64_bit; generic-fiber Tier-1)
description:
perf#18 D11 — bulk closed-fast Lane-1 migration (generic-fiber Tier-1, NOT no-fiber). User-directed 2026-06-30 (gists a6b51a74 + expand-to-all-fixed-arg). EXPANDED from batch-1 to ALL the fixed-arg closed Lane-1 candidates the D9 heatmap surfaced. DIFFERENT CLASS from D10: these ride the GENERIC fiber doWork() path (Lane-1 Tier-1, like dar-1il.1 allocate/insert_right), NOT the no-fiber inline fast path. Do NOT add to ringFastPathEligible().

=== RECON DONE (shapes from generate-rpc-wrappers.py) -- EXPANSION REFINED (don't repeat deallocate/mod_refs/checkout surprises) ===
CLEAN SET (5 ops, migrate all -- fixed shape, no ptr/fd/destroy/S2C/block, normal req->reply):
  - uidgid (line 152):           [int32 new_uid, int32 new_gid] -> [int32 old_uid, int32 old_gid]
  - set_thread_handles (160):    [uint64 pthread_handle, uint64 dispatch_qaddr] -> header-only reply (NOT NO_REPLY; it waits)
  - started_suspended (144):     [] -> [bool suspended]
  - get_tracer (148):            [] -> [int32 tracer]
  - task_is_64_bit (192):        [int32 id] -> [bool is_64_bit]

EXCLUDED by recon (the surprises -- DO NOT migrate in this bead):
  - set_dyld_info (206): [uint64 address, uint64 length] -> [], **NO_REPLY (fire-and-forget)**. The guest wrapper sends and returns WITHOUT waiting for a reply; the server emits NO reply datagram. This is INCOMPATIBLE with the closed request->reply ring (the guest would park on a reply that never comes -> wedge; OR forcing a reply undoes the perf#10 NO_REPLY win). Needs a fire-and-forget RING PUBLISH mode (publish + advance, no reply-wait) = a real ring feature, separate bead. STAYS UDS for now.
  - checkout (127): @fd exec_listener_pipe = FD-PASSING. Ring has no SCM_RIGHTS/ancillary channel -> UDS-only ("wrong lane", future fd-lane).
  - vchroot_path (132) / set_executable_path (242): char* PATH payload (variable-length; set_executable_path is ALSO NO_REPLY). Need arena descriptor + bounds guard -> DEFER (future arena batch).

=> MIGRATE = { uidgid, set_thread_handles, started_suspended, get_tracer, task_is_64_bit } (5 ops). set_dyld_info -> needs ring fire-and-forget (separate). checkout -> UDS (fd). paths -> deferred.

NOTE on started_suspended/get_tracer: empty-request + scalar-reply shape (like the self-traps) so they COULD be Tier-2 no-fiber, BUT per the gist this batch is Tier-1 generic-fiber only (they read process state, not pure-mint) -- keep the batch uniform + low-risk: SIMPLE_C2S only, NOT NOFIBER_FAST.

=== MINI-RECON to re-confirm each before wiring ===
request shape fixed? response shape fixed? pointer/string payload? max length? needs arena? can block? can trigger S2C? can destroy/refcount mutate? depends on fd passing? NO_REPLY? safe before/after attach?

=== WIRING (dar-1il.1 Tier-1 recipe) ===
- DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES += the 5 ops (shared allowlist drives server allowlist + guest dispatch + c2s_opcode_hash; skew auto-rejected at attach -> all-UDS).
- DSERVER_RING_OP_CLASS tag each SIMPLE_C2S ONLY (NOT NOFIBER_FAST). Canon-safe static_assert must still hold (no destroy/S2C bit).
- Do NOT touch ringFastPathEligible().
- Server: generic callFromMessage -> doWork(), identical dtape primitive as UDS. Guest: gr_body_trap-style helper per op (like __dserver_ring_mach_port_allocate) carrying the fixed inline body (empty body for started_suspended/get_tracer -> could reuse gr_port_trap-shape but with the op's scalar reply); ring-then-UDS fallback in each *_impl. Replies travel in the ring reply BODY (uidgid old_uid/old_gid; started_suspended bool; get_tracer int32; task_is_64_bit bool) -- confirm, not via guest-memory write. set_thread_handles is header-only.
- ABI stays v5 (opcode-hash handles the set; no layout change for fixed args).

=== ACCEPTANCE (gist verbatim + recon) ===
1. Each op SIMPLE_C2S only, NOT NOFIBER_FAST. 2. No destroy/caller-S2C/static-guard violation. 3. Shape guard for EVERY payload field (reqlen == sizeof(struct); mismatch -> UDS pre-mutation). 4. UDS fallback on shape mismatch BEFORE mutation. 5. Ring-vs-UDS A/B for normal + malformed/edge, SIDE-EFFECT A/B (assert uid/gid actually set + old returned; thread handles actually set; started_suspended/get_tracer/task_is_64_bit return the SAME value as UDS for the same process) -- STATE not just kern_return. 6. Full perf#18 gates GREEN (lane-class/allowlist/drift updated for all 5, RED->GREEN). 7. Heatmap after shows ring uptake + aggregate impact across all 5. 8. Fork-storm 3/3 + shellspawn + boot clean. 9. D10 self-traps + Tier-2 mach_reply_port UNCHANGED. 10. NO duplex changes.

NON-GOALS: no no-fiber fast-op for these; no fd-passing op on the ring (checkout stays UDS); no NO_REPLY op on the closed ring without fire-and-forget support (set_dyld_info deferred); no variable path payload without arena+bounds-guard (paths deferred); no ABI/duplex churn.

Build on darlingserver @69e1515 / xnu @f3d42a7. Recipe reuses dar-1il.1 (allocate/insert_right Tier-1) + the 5-rule membership canon + the D10 pattern. LIVE-RUN: setuid /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix/bin/darling drives homebrew-test prefix; deploy BOTH server (darling-prefix/bin/darlingserver) + guest dylib (darling-prefix-homebrew-test/usr/lib/system/libsystem_kernel.dylib), backup .prod-bak, restore after. GOTCHAS: DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES is inside #ifdef DSERVER_RING_TRANSPORT (test must -D it); call.cpp foreign FORK_TIMELINE -> patch-extract your hunk only; `darling shutdown` nonzero-when-idle don't chain with &&. NOTHING PUSHED.

title: perf#18 D12: closed-setter / ack-only ring migration (RECON-FIRST; ack-only NOT async; no arena/psynch/duplex)
description:
perf#18 D12: closed-setter / ACK-ONLY ring migration — RECON-FIRST. Follow-on after D11 (dar-1il.6) proved the DECLARATIVE migration recipe (a fixed-shape closed op needs NO server handler).

Full brief: memory dar-perf18-d12-ack-only-setter-recon-brief.

GOAL: bring closed SETTER ops (empty/header-only reply, the result is just a status code) onto Lane-1 the SAFE way. KEY CONSTRAINT: implement ACK-ONLY (request rides ring, server processes via generic fiber, publishes empty/status reply, GUEST STILL WAITS) — NOT true async fire-and-forget. This removes UDS transport while preserving ordering + error semantics. It is the D11 recipe with an empty reply (gr_full_trap with reply_out=NULL,replylen=0 already supports it).

RECON-FIRST. Per candidate decide: does the caller wait today? error observable? reply empty/fixed? ordering guarantee relied on? warm vs pre-attach-only? variable/path payload? caller-S2C? Classify into: ack-only-fixed (MIGRATE via D11 recipe), needs-arena (DEFER), true-async-only (DEFER, separate publish-only bead). Migrate AT MOST one small clean batch; if nothing clean, STOP with the recon doc + deferred lists (valid completion).

Candidates (verify shapes live): set_tracer (213, fixed req, header-only reply — strong), stop_after_exec (211), ptrace_sigexc (224), ptrace_thupdate (229), interrupt_exit (200, check interrupt-sensitivity); set_dyld_info (206, NO_REPLY — recon if ack-only safe); set_executable_path (242, char* path + NO_REPLY — almost certainly DEFER, two blockers). EXCLUDE: char*/path (arena), @fd, destroy/caller-S2C, blocking/ALLOW_INTERRUPTIONS, psynch, duplex.

NON-GOALS: no true async fire-and-forget v1; no arena; no psynch; no duplex; no NOFIBER_FAST (Tier-1 only).

ACCEPTANCE: (1) no async/order semantic change (any NO_REPLY->ack-only move documented+measured); (2) empty-reply/status matches UDS; (3) shape guards + UDS fallback pre-mutation; (4) full gates GREEN (lane-class+allowlist+drift+canon static_assert, +D12 assertions); (5) heatmap before/after + verify used_fiber==total (Tier-1); (6) side-effect A/B for setters (post-op STATE matches UDS, not just return code); (7) fork-storm 3/3 + shellspawn + boot + skew clean; (8) self-trap/D11 ops unchanged, no duplex changes.

WIRING (D11 recipe): += DSERVER_RING_C2S_OPCODES + OP_CLASS(SIMPLE_C2S); gr_full_trap guest wrapper; try-ring-then-UDS at each scattered call site; ABI v5; do NOT touch ringFastPathEligible or call.cpp (foreign FORK_TIMELINE). Live: DPREFIX=darling-prefix-homebrew-test, heatmap armed. NEVER push.

HEADs darlingserver @e82c86c / xnu @e148c87. Reuses dar-perf18-d11-bulk-lane1-batch1-brief + dar-perf18-ring-c2s-membership-canon + dar-perf18-d9-rpc-heatmap-eligibility-brief + dar-perf18-shmem-ring-transport.

title: perf#18 D13: Lane-1.5 string/path arena (RECON-FIRST; inline-vs-arena decision; copy-sealed only; no general allocator/psynch/duplex)
description:
perf#18 D13 (Lane-1.5 string/path arena). RECON-FIRST. Bring the warm char*/path ops
(vchroot_path 248, mldr_path 82, set_executable_path 242; ~413/workload, ~8us p50 UDS)
onto the ring WITHOUT a general variable-payload RPC.

STEP 0 gates everything: the ABI already has a 4096B max slot + reserved arena_off/arena_len
slot fields. PATH_MAX(1024) likely FITS INLINE in one slot. So FIRST measure, per op, whether
the variable payload (request for set_executable_path; REPLY for vchroot_path/mldr_path) fits the
negotiated slot inlineCap. If yes -> migrate INLINE via the D11 recipe, NO arena. Build the arena
ONLY for genuine overflow. "Two inline migrations, no arena" is a valid complete D13.

If an arena IS needed: COPY-SEALED only (server copies out once into a server-owned buffer, never
holds a guest-mutable pointer; TOCTOU/lifetime is the real risk, not the allocator). Fixed
per-ring slabs (N x ~PATH_MAX), SPSC, states FREE/WRITING/READY/CONSUMED. Bounds-check off/len,
oversize/no-slot/bad-shape -> UDS fallback BEFORE mutation. Descriptor reuses existing slot fields;
the arena REGION (cb arena_off/arena_size, currently 0) is the new wire layout -> ABI v5->v6.

PRE-ATTACH SPLIT (D11/D12 lesson): set_executable_path is mldr-only (pre-attach + NO_REPLY ->
likely DEFER); vchroot_path has a pre-attach (mldr) AND a warm (vchroot_userspace) site. Acceptance
heatmap must report total / pre_attach_UDS / ring / oversize_fallback / shape_fallback per op.

NON-GOALS: no general allocator, no zero-copy shared mutable strings, no psynch, no duplex changes,
no NOFIBER_FAST. Acceptance: arena gate (bounds/oversize/no-slot/reuse/mutation-after-publish RED
arms) + correctness A/B ring-vs-UDS (empty/long/oversize/embedded-NUL/invalid-ptr) + no behavior
change + perf p50/p99+uptake + regression (all perf#18 gates GREEN, boot/shellspawn/fork-storm,
Lane-1 fixed ops + D6 duplex unchanged) + ABI bump/hash if layout/caps change.

Full self-contained brief: memory/dar-perf18-d13-lane15-string-arena-brief.md. Don't start unless
asked. HEADs darlingserver @437c2d6 / xnu @e148c87. NEVER push.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
D13 DONE — migrated mldr_path + vchroot_path to Lane-1 ring. Commits darlingserver @88f2727 +
xnu @7981ce8 (LOCAL/not pushed).

STEP-0 KILLED THE ARENA PREMISE: the "char*/path" ops are NOT variable-payload at the RPC layer.
The path bytes ride out-of-band via process_vm_readv/writev (/proc/pid/mem) — the char* in the
body is a guest VA the server reads/writes directly. RPC bodies are fixed+tiny (16B request
{buffer,buffer_size} / 8B reply {length}), same shape class as the D11 batch. So D13 = pure D11
declarative inline migration: NO arena, NO ABI bump (opcode-hash handles the C2S-set change).

Migrated (warm post-attach): mldr_path (sys_execve), vchroot_path (init_vchroot_path). The
mldr.c:940 vchroot_path site is in the mldr binary (no ring) -> stays UDS by construction.
set_executable_path NOT migrated (pre-attach mldr-only + NO_REPLY, D12 verdict stands).

Canon: /proc/mem access is server-side (no caller-S2C), pure read of server config (no destroy)
-> all 5 rules hold; static_assert compiles. Tier-1 generic fiber (used_fiber==total).

LIVE A/B (homebrew-test, 12683 RPCs, fork-storm 3/3 GREEN): both ride the ring, ring health
pristine (fail/fallback/s2c_full/suspend=0), no wedge, blocked=0. Side-effect A/B byte-identical:
uname=Darwin, exec works, vchroot translation correct vs UDS baseline. Lane-1 fixed ops
(self-trap Tier-2 + D11) unchanged. Gates (allowlist + lane-class + canon static_assert + drift)
GREEN with explicit D13 assertions; RED arms still fail.

FINDING worth recording: the per-op heatmap reported mldr_path caller_s2c=3 (verdict auto-flipped
to "duplex-only") — this is a FALSE sticky-latch attribution. The authoritative transport-correct
counter s2c_munmap_ring_parent stayed 0 across 284 ring-routed mldr_path calls (the 5 boot munmaps
all had UDS parents = exec-teardown). The latch froze at 3 while ring calls grew 25->284 — a real
per-op S2C would track volume. Root cause: _heatmapCallDidS2c is a sticky per-thread bool latched
in _s2cPerform; on an exec'ing thread the teardown munmap latches adjacent to mldr_path. Canon
rule 1 holds, MEASURED directly. LESSON: trust s2c_munmap_ring_parent over per-op caller_s2c.

doc: tests/PERF18-LANE15-STRING-ARENA-RECON.md. The copy-sealed arena remains a correctly-scoped
FUTURE bead — not needed for the path tail (Darling sidesteps it via /proc/mem).

title: perf#18 D14: post-D13 heatmap refresh + next-tail pick (RECON-ONLY; fix S2C attribution first; no migrations/arena/duplex)
description:
perf#18 D14 (post-D13 heatmap refresh + pick the next tail). RECON-ONLY: no migrations, no arena,
no duplex, no new transport, no ABI change.

D10 (self-trap) + D11 (5-op batch) + D13 (path ops) removed a big chunk of obvious closed ops, so
the D9 rankings are stale. Re-measure before choosing the next lever.

FIRST (the only code change allowed): fix the S2C attribution. The per-op heatmap caller_s2c is a
STICKY per-thread bool (_heatmapCallDidS2c) that over-attributes an unrelated exec-teardown munmap
S2C to the next op recorded on the same thread (D13: mldr_path showed caller_s2c=3, auto-verdict
"duplex-only", while authoritative s2c_munmap_ring_parent stayed 0). REPLACE with per-call scoped
attribution (cheap -- the active callnum is already in scope at _s2cPerform, next to the D6 block):
add Metrics::recordCallerS2cFor(callnum) bumping perCallDidCallerS2c directly, call it at _s2cPerform
against _activeCall->number(), delete the sticky bool. RED-arm the gate to prove no cross-op leak;
keep s2c_munmap_*_parent as the cross-check. Server-only, no ABI/wire change.

THEN rerun the SAME warm workload (shell boot + exec + fork-storm) with the heatmap armed and produce:
total RPC, ring/UDS split, top remaining UDS by count, top remaining UDS by total time, corrected lane
verdicts, and EXACTLY ONE next migration candidate OR STOP. Rank by the D9 formula; apply the traps
(hot != reclaimable; handshake ops can't self-migrate; blocking latency is real; pre-attach ops won't
touch the ring).

Expected remaining buckets: more fixed-shape closed ops (continue D11-style); pre-attach-heavy ops
(ring can't help until attach is earlier -- if these dominate, the next lever may be EARLIER RING
ATTACH, a different bead, unlocking started_suspended/get_tracer/set_executable_path/mldr-site at
once); blocking/wait ops (not a transport problem); psynch (separate branch, deferred);
mach_msg_overwrite (STOP already proven unless shape mix changed); fd-passing (no ring ancillary).

Acceptance: metric fix per-call scoped (RED arm proves no cross-op leak) + sticky latch removed +
gates GREEN; fresh snapshot + 6 deliverables; mldr_path verdict must flip duplex-only -> already-on-ring
as before/after proof; one candidate or STOP; no migration/arena/duplex/ABI; Lane-1 stays 13 ops; live
run clean + prod restored.

Full self-contained brief: memory/dar-perf18-d14-heatmap-refresh-brief.md. Don't start unless asked.
HEADs darlingserver @88f2727 / xnu @7981ce8. NEVER push.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
D14 DONE + committed @74d8585 (LOCAL/not pushed). RECON-ONLY: no migration/arena/duplex/ABI; Lane-1 stays 13 ops.

METRIC FIX (server-only, the one allowed change): replaced the D13 sticky per-thread caller_s2c latch (_heatmapCallDidS2c — over-attributed exec-teardown munmap S2C to a bystander op) with PER-CALL-scoped Metrics::recordCallerS2cFor(_activeCall->number()) at _s2cPerform; sticky field+sites deleted; the 3 recordCallHeatmap sites pass didCallerS2c=false; s2c_munmap_*_parent counters kept as cross-check. Gate rpc_heatmap_gate_test.cpp INVARIANT 5 + RED arm -DRED_BREAK_PERCALL_LEAK prove no cross-op leak; GREEN + all 3 RED arms fail; full perf#18 suite GREEN. PROOF: mldr_path now reads already-on-ring (was falsely duplex-only in D13).

REFRESH (same warm workload; tests/PERF18-RPC-HEATMAP-D14.md + -snapshot.json): 10621 RPC, ring 36.9% / UDS 63.1%, ring health pristine (fast_fail/fallback/s2c_full/suspend/clients_blocked all 0; fork-storm 3/3). Every op caller_s2c=0; only munmap S2Cs (5) have a UDS parent, s2c_munmap_ring_parent=0.

VERDICT: STOP per-op migration. No clean WARM fixed-shape closed op remains. DECISIVE finding: the reclaimable UDS tail is PRE-ATTACH-dominated — started_suspended/get_tracer are IN the C2S allowlist yet 100% ring=0 (fire before ring_attach); every already-on-ring op's UDS residual is pre-attach too (~1500-1800 pre-attach UDS calls/workload). Rest of the UDS tail is non-migratable: blocking waits (fork_wait_for_child 512us), the ring_attach handshake itself, fd-passing (checkout/kqchan), and pthread_canceled at the 2us noise floor.

NEXT (dar-1il.10 / D15) = a DIFFERENT kind of bead: EARLIER RING ATTACH (move attach ahead of the pre-attach bring-up burst, unlocking the whole pre-attach class at once), NOT another op migration. Ranked table is the STOP evidence if earlier attach proves infeasible.

Conclusion: this proof needs a different runner shape (direct/launch-free dserver RPC harness or another controlled runtime harness) rather than more manifest/source-patch masking. Do not count this as fixed; current west correctly rejects it.

title: west test: launch-free darlingserver runtime proof harness
description:
Context: processcall_uidgid_fault_guest cannot honestly prove RED through the normal guest-c-fixture/darling shell path. After current-minus materialization and reason matching fixes, bad darlingserver builds/deploys, and west correctly rejects the proof because the expected uidgid trace is missing. prepare-fixture-before-deploy can upload/compile on current runtime and run-only under bad artifacts, but this still fails before guest main: old bad darlingserver breaks shell startup with pending-call-overwrite, BAD RECEIVE MESSAGE protocol desync, or missing shellspawn.sock. A RED-only event-loop isolation source patch also failed before oracle.

Need: add a controlled runtime proof harness for server-side regressions that does not require a full darling shell startup under the bad server. Candidate shapes: direct dserver RPC client fixture against a launched bad darlingserver, a launch-free mldr/shellspawn minimal harness, or a west-managed server protocol fixture with explicit startup readiness and trace capture.

Acceptance:
- processcall-exception-guard has a behavioral RED/GREEN proof for the uidgid/processCall throw path, or is explicitly split into subproofs with event-loop vs microthreadWorker coverage.
- The RED proof reaches the intended oracle (test_fault.consume name=processcall.uidgid_throw and call.processcall_throw name=uidgid, or a structured equivalent), not shell upload/startup failure.
- The harness has contract tests proving preparation happens before bad deploy when needed and failure reason matching cannot accept startup/protocol failures as the target oracle.
- No source matching; no upstream push.
close_reason: Implemented and verified processcall direct RED/GREEN runtime proof harness with reason-matched uidgid fault oracle.

comments.text: ingest_process_register_fault_guest is now confirmed in scope too. prepare-fixture-before-deploy proves upload/compile can be moved off the bad runtime, but run-only still requires darling shell startup and fails before the ingest oracle with BAD RECEIVE MESSAGE / missing shellspawn.sock. The launch-free harness should cover both processcall_uidgid_fault_guest and ingest_process_register_fault_guest.

title: HOST-TOOLING: canonical guest-runner that reports completion by process/file state, not stdout buffer, and verifies real work
description: Type: task

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: While adding q95.29.2 runtime gate, two runner hazards reproduced: (1) host-side darling shell process can detach/exit before guest work completes, so runner must poll an explicit guest verdict; (2) guest /tmp verdict may not be visible at /tmp from the host because of overlay/union semantics, even when it exists in the guest. The new run-psynch-kernel-return-contract.sh copies source host-side but polls/cats the verdict guest-side through darling shell. Canonical guest-runner should adopt that pattern: guest-side verdict authority, explicit timeout, and no trust in stdout or host-visible DPREFIX/tmp.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Second runner updated with same lesson: run-getattrlist-shared-packer.sh now copies source into the prefix but polls and reads /tmp verdict inside the guest via darling shell. This avoided false no-verdict caused by guest /tmp overlay files not appearing at /tmp on the host.

comments.text: 2026-07-10: attempted a shared guest state-file completion protocol. Contract RED used a launcher that returned 0 before its background workload completed; the protocol correctly waited for an atomic guest result. Real CTest repro west test --env darling --bead dar-q95.3 --prefix-profile homebrew --ctest-timeout-seconds 300 then timed out twice (bundles 20260710T164917Z and 20260710T165437Z-darling_select_fdset_guest). Guarded tree shows run-darling-c-test.sh blocked in pipe_read/do_wait before the guest state was published. A standalone trace left a live darlingserver plus zombie mldr until explicit shutdown/prefix repair; cleanup then verified no process or mount tail. The speculative helper was reverted in local commit 09c07d3. Conclusion: a file protocol alone is insufficient; implement a launcher-level foreground/completion primitive that can start a real guest worker and query its status without relying on shell background/nohup semantics. Preserve these bundles as RED evidence.

title: DCC6 valid cache hangs after dyld applies cache fixups
description: After fixing west dcc-cache to build against the dyld-visible no-overlay install root, stale inode validation no longer blocks DCC2/DCC6 coverage. Repro: west test --profile perf --patch dyld/dcc2-reader.patch --env darling --prefix existing:/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs with dcc2_valid_cache_guest unblocked. Evidence from 2026-07-09 run: dyld prints 'dyld[DCC2]: enabled', substitutes cached /usr/lib/system/libsystem_blocks.dylib and /usr/lib/system/libunwind.dylib, then prints 'dyld[DCC2]: applied 83 fixups once for whole cache' and /usr/bin/true does not exit before the guest-command watchdog times out after 105s. This is not source matching and not the previous filesystem metadata mismatch; it is a runtime correctness hang after valid cache application. Acceptance: stable repro bundle/log, root cause in DCC reader/cache/fixups or guest process startup, valid-cache guest gate exits 0, stale-cache and missing-path gates are either green or separately blocked with exact repro, no leftover prefix processes/worktrees.
close_reason: Fixed: root cause was west guest-command-fixture inherited stderr fd/PIPE EOF wait, not DCC post-fixup hang. Runner now captures output to temp files and waits for process exit; DCC valid-cache GREEN and --prove-red pass.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-09 root cause correction: this was not a DCC post-fixup hang. The DCC valid-cache process exited, but west guest-command-fixture used stdout/stderr=PIPE and communicate(); Darling runtime children can inherit stderr and keep the pipe open, so Python waited for EOF until the watchdog. Fixed west_commands/test.py to capture guest-command output in temp files and wait for the launcher process exit. Added a regression in tests/run-west-test-runtime-red-contract.sh with a fake Darling launcher that leaves a background child holding stderr. Verified dcc2_valid_cache_guest GREEN and --prove-red after the runner fix.

title: west test materialize-profile must not switch live checkouts
description: Problem: running a metadata test that needs profile materialization can apply the selected profile in temporary worktrees, then later attempt to switch live managed repositories to integration/homebrew. Repro observed from darling-workspace: west test --profile homebrew --patch libressl/libressl-283-nist-strict-aliasing.patch after adding a source-repo CTest fixture. The command first materialized /tmp/west-profile-homebrew-* worktrees, then printed 'temporarily materializing profile homebrew' and ran git switch integration/homebrew in live repos, failed because integration/homebrew was already used by the live libpthread worktree, and left live Darling submodule pointers dirty. Acceptance: west test must keep all profile materialization inside temp worktrees for metadata tests that need it; it must not git switch/reset live checkouts unless the user explicitly requests a live materialization command; failure paths must leave no temp worktrees/processes and no dirty live submodule pointer churn.
close_reason: Fixed nested profile materialization: when selected profile tests are already running inside temp worktree overrides, requires-profile for that same profile no longer falls back to live _profile_checkout. Repro west test --profile homebrew --patch libressl/libressl-283-nist-strict-aliasing.patch now keeps materialization in /tmp worktrees, runs source-repo CTest, and leaves no temp worktrees/processes.

title: dar-6x4: libunistring make check SLOW (not deadlock): Darling thread-create checkin handshake + sched_yield busy-spin starve CPU; tcstorm gate is unfaithful (clickhouse-contaminated). REAL make-check terminate-or-hang UNVERIFIED
notes:
=== РЕАЛЬНЫЙ WORKLOAD ПОДТВЕРДИЛ РЕФРЕЙМ (2026-06-27, когда clickhouse спал ~73% idle) ===

Запустил НАСТОЯЩИЙ `brew reinstall -s libunistring` (из исходников) на пристинном fb027685/5f01b70f. Наблюдение ~18 мин вживую:
- download OK -> extract OK -> CONFIGURE идёт.
- config.log РАСТЁТ бёрстами (174K->225K->318K->372K), проходя десятки разных `checking ...` (strncat, strerror_r, sys/select, putw, pthread_rwlock_t, rdlock-prefers-writer, sched_yield declared, ...).
- Каждый conftest = fork+exec+per-thread checkin RPC; запускаются и ЧИСТО выходят (strace поймал pthread_rwlock conftest: sendmsg/recvmsg=12 туда-сюда, потом exit_group(142), reaped).
- "Залипания" 20-40с на одной `checking...` строке = configure гоняет МНОГО медленных под-conftest'ов до флаша строки, НЕ зависание (conftest-процессы приходят/уходят, config.log растёт нетто-плюсом).

=> РЕАЛЬНЫЙ libunistring configure МЕДЛЕННЫЙ-НО-ИДЁТ, НЕ дедлок, на пристинных бинарях. Прямо подтверждает tcstorm-рефрейм: dar-6x4 = МЕДЛЕННОСТЬ создания потоков/процессов (per-create checkin RPC + sched_yield handshake через однопоточный сервер), не потеря reply/дедлок. Постоянного зависания checkin в реальной сборке НЕ наблюдалось.

ПРОГРЕСС-метрика робастна к контаминации: монотонный рост config.log. (clickhouse-тенант скачет, поэтому абсолютный wall-clock шумит, но прогресс — нет.)

ВЫВОД ПО ЗАДАЧЕ: dar-6x4 в его исходной формулировке ("сервер не отвечает на checkin / reply теряется") — НЕВЕРЕН. Это ПРОИЗВОДИТЕЛЬНОСТЬ. Кандидат-фикс (если оптимизировать): заменить busy-spin threads.c:199-200 sched_yield на futex/eventfd-ожидание (родитель не крутит CPU, отдаёт его ребёнку+серверу, которых ждёт). Это не корректностный баг.

ОТКРЫТО: дать сборке дойти до `make check` и подтвердить, что и он лишь медленный (а не виснет). Идёт в фоне.

СОСТОЯНИЕ: всё пристинно, ничего не собрано/не запушено.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: FIXED 2026-06-28 (Opus), NOT pushed. REFRAME CORRECTED: dar-6x4 is NOT just slowness -- under fork+exec storms it WEDGES the server (real deadlock), reproduced deterministically with a heavy make -jN clang build (wedge-hunt: rpcs_serviced flat >=8s while mldr alive + build unfinished). gdb at the wedge proved it: __cur_writer == MAIN thread tid, the single worker blocked in pthread_rwlock_rdlock (__readers=WRLOCKED|WRPHASE) inside Checkin::processCall -> notifyCheckin -> _notifyListeningKqchannels on the PARENT process lock. ROOT CAUSE: notifyCheckin()'s exec branch holds Process::_rwlock (std::shared_mutex) across the SUSPENDING dtape_task_create/dtape_thread_create/dtape_semaphore_* calls. _rwlock ownership is per-OS-thread, but perf #2b (server-inline-fastpath) runs cheap RPCs INLINE on the MAIN event-loop thread while a suspended microthread resumes on the WORKER. An exec checkin inline-on-MAIN that suspends strands the write lock owned by MAIN; a sibling fork-checkin on the single worker then blocks forever reading that same parent lock -- and only that stuck worker could resume the holder. Pre-#2b impossible (one worker ran all microthreads; lock taken+released on the same OS thread) -> latent hazard perf #2b activated. A/B (heavy load): pristine inline-ON WEDGED attempt 1; DSERVER_NO_INLINE=1 probe 20/20 clean (confirms inline-on-main is the trigger); the fix with inline ON 20/20 + final 10/10 clean. FIX: drop _rwlock around the dtape work in the exec branch and re-acquire to publish new task/thread/semaphore handles (mirrors the existing notifyDead loop pattern). fork/exec/wait/exit-code correctness verified (HELLO_EXEC_OK, fork chain, rc=7). RED->GREEN test committed WITH the fix: tests/inline_suspend_rwlock_deadlock.c (rwlock held across a microthread suspension across 2 OS threads -> RED watchdog deadlock/exit1, GREEN release-before-suspend passes). comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Surfaced 2026-06-27 AFTER the dar-stackmmap-eexist-9j9 stack fix unblocked make check. With the fixed mldr deployed, libunistring 1.4.2 make check now BUILDS and RUNS the whole gnulib test suite: # TOTAL: 674, # PASS: 636, # SKIP: 34, # FAIL: 4, # ERROR: 0. The 4 failures are all file-descriptor semantics tests:
  FAIL: test-cloexec
  FAIL: test-dup2
  FAIL: test-fcntl
  FAIL: test-getdtablesize
These are a SEPARATE class from the (now-fixed) stack EEXIST — they are Darling fd-emulation correctness gaps, not a build blocker. Evidence: ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/diag-1782559867/03.make.log (PASS/FAIL lines ~1961-2030, summary ~2642-2648). test-suite.log not captured by the harness (lives in the ephemeral build dir); rerun with the build tree kept to get per-test assertion detail. Likely culprits: getdtablesize/RLIMIT_NOFILE reporting, dup2 edge cases (same-fd, O_CLOEXEC clearing), fcntl F_DUPFD_CLOEXEC / F_GETFD. Not pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: FIXED + VALIDATED GREEN. Root cause (proven live, fork-isolated fd-probe oracle): mldr socket_bitmap_get (mldr.c:601,625) places the per-thread darlingserver RPC socket at TOP legal Linux fd = RLIMIT_NOFILE-1 = 1048575; mach_driver_init (lkm.c:62) guards it (guard_flag_prevent_close). sys_getrlimit (getrlimit.c:22) intentionally reports NOFILE-1 so getdtablesize()==1048575 == that guarded socket. gnulib test-cloexec/dup2/fcntl/getdtablesize compute bad_fd=getdtablesize() and do dup2(_,bad_fd)/fcntl(bad_fd,..) EXPECTING EBADF, but hit the guard's __simple_abort() in dup2.c -> SIGABRT (Abort trap:6, NOT an assertion). Internal RPC bypasses BSD wrappers (raw LINUX_SYSCALL sendmsg/recvmsg/close via dserver_rpc_hooks_*), so BSD-layer fix is safe. FIX (libsystem_kernel, in-process, no extra syscall): guard_table_check hit -> return -EBADF instead of abort: dup2.c (both endpoints), fcntl.c (new guard), dup.c (new guard). VALIDATED: fd-oracle GREEN; brew reinstall -s libunistring BREW_EXIT=0, make check TOTAL 674/PASS 640/SKIP 34/FAIL 0 (was 4); all 4 tests PASS. Evidence ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/fdfix-1782563338. Deployed md5 2ec0c911. Not pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08 root cause captured with low-noise DSERVER_EXIT_TRACE + last_makecontext TLS. Minimal ring-on A0_SYNTH_FILTER=forkwait-exec repro /tmp/a0gate.dDQ9 failed at t=10-12 with SIGSEGV in libc makecontext. Crash line: last_makecontext branch=worker thread=0x5769db4db990 stack=(nil) size=65536 call=14(dserver_callnum_interrupt_enter). This proves the INCOMPLETE/shellspawn Broken pipe symptom is caused by darlingserver calling makecontext with a null stack returned from StackPool idle state, not guest stdout loss or launcher timeout. WIP fix: harden StackPool so free() never returns invalid Stack handles to the idle list and allocate() skips any pre-existing null idle entries; added A0_SYNTH_FILTER plus host/guest markers/snapshots for stable short reproduction.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08 validation after StackPool/thread fix. Fix details: Thread::doWork now frees a microthread stack only when _stack.isValid(), so restored interrupt frames that moved _stack away do not call StackPool::free() with an empty handle; StackPool itself is hardened to ignore invalid frees and skip any legacy null idle entries before handing a stack to makecontext. Validation: 20/20 A0_SYNTH_FILTER=forkwait-exec ring-on runs green after the fix (same low-noise repro failed on run 1 before fix); 5/5 traced forkwait runs green with no DSERVER_STACKPOOL_TRACE/DSERVER_EXIT_TRACE output; full a0-gate synth /tmp/a0gate.G86m PASS=15 FAIL=0 found old invalid-free trace; after caller fix full a0-gate synth /tmp/a0gate.XfrV PASS=16 FAIL=0 and no DSERVER_STACKPOOL_TRACE invalid-free lines. Final filtered sanity /tmp/a0gate.6ir7 PASS=2 FAIL=0, no trace output, no leftover runtime processes.

title: A0-ARCH: darlingserver wake/wait protocol redesign — interrupt-as-cancellation (closes the #114 SEGV crash class)
description:
LANDED 2026-07-04, deployed d81b5cf1 = doctor baseline (was 7692d9f6). Redesigned the darlingserver wake/wait protocol in small independently-shippable stages to eliminate the #114 storm-on-condvar SEGV crash class. Branch fix/a0-arch-redesign. Detail: tests/A0-ARCH-STAGE{1,2,3}.md + tests/A0-ARCH-REDESIGN-SPEC.md.
STAGES: 0 fuzzer (DSERVER_SCHED_FUZZ); 1 typed wake tokens (kind,gen; arm-before-publish); 2a/2b/2c MicroState machine + one legality-checked transition funnel for all XNU wait-state writes (dtape_thread_entering DELETED); 3 interrupt-as-cancellation -- the interrupted call unwinds via NORMAL dispatch on its own fiber (frame armed {cancellationArmed,replyOwed,enterCall}; enter reply deferred to reply-stash); jumpToResume + syscall-return setcontext detours DELETED -> the SEGV stack-borrowing crash class is gone (zero interrupt SEGVs across every stage-3 run). Stage 4 (single-runner) MEASURED -> NO-GO (WorkQueue worker de-facto unused: workers_busy=0 in 19/20 storm samples, inline fastpath ~100%).
ACCEPTANCE MET: synth 16/16, quick gate 19/19 gating green (nestwait 8/8, brew xz 2/2 strict), zero mstate/xwait violations, zero SEGV; cvstorm2-throttled (#114 grain) 12/12 OK at full storm; perf A/B no regression (nestwait NO_STORM 3590 vs 2c 3606, -0.4% within noise).
SPUN OUT (proven pre-existing, NOT stage-3 regressions): flood dispatch-starvation (-> dar-rpc-correctness-z27x.7) and the -111 flake (-> .6). A follow-up read-only RPC audit surfaced the dar-rpc-correctness epic. Hard-won invariants recorded in the stage docs: consume-resume-permit atomic with the branch; timerfd stale-deadline reset (fix-3); psynch mtx/rw drop-side self-compensates NOT_WAITING (fix-2 revert lesson).

title: Runtime profile: prevent live nested module fallback
description: A rootless bootstrap source forest materialized only declared source-modules and silently symlinked other nested West modules from the live checkout. A profile patch for darling/src/external/libsystem was reported as applied by the profile, yet libSystem.B was built from the unpatched live source. This can produce a false GREEN/RED result. Define typed rootless closure source ownership, validate every required owner is materialized, add a behavioral contract that proves a nested patched source is built rather than symlinked, update rootless runtime profiles, and reject incomplete declarations before build.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 closure evidence: rootless source ownership is now encoded by ROOTLESS_BOOTSTRAP_CLOSURE_SOURCE_MODULES and enforced by load_ctest_runtime_profiles; both homebrew-rootless-no-mount and homebrew-prefix-baseline declare darling/src/external/libsystem. Added a real disposable-Git contract in tests/west_test_contracts/runtime_red_contract.py: it creates a nested libsystem repo with a profile-only patch, materializes the runtime source forest, and proves the nested path is a non-symlink worktree containing the patched content. `tests/run-west-test-runtime-red-contract.sh` passes (including Applying: profile libsystem). title: test-infra: require behavioral RED reasons for guest-runtime-deploy proofs
description:
Context: audit on 2026-07-09 found guest-runtime-deploy RED proofs that were counted as proven on any nonzero rc. Some RED arms failed because the fixture source file was absent in the current-minus source forest, and many darlingserver current-minus runtime proofs unexpectedly passed.

Problem: runtime RED proof must demonstrate the intended bad behavior, not merely rc != 0. The runner and metadata should make the reason explicit and reject common false proofs such as missing fixture files, unrelated build/upload errors, or unexpected bad-runtime pass.

Acceptance:
- guest-runtime-deploy tests either declare stable behavioral expectations (expect-output-contains/lacks or a structured oracle) or are explicitly blocked with a linked task.
- RED failures caused by missing fixture/test files are rejected as infrastructure failures, not accepted as regression proofs.
- Documentation/AGENTS states that fixtures for runtime RED should be stable current-workspace inputs unless the bad source tree is deliberately patched with test assets.
- west patch check or west test --red-audit surfaces guest-runtime-deploy proofs lacking reason matching.
- All unblocked guest-runtime-deploy proofs are re-run with RED and GREEN and have debug bundles confirming the intended cause.
close_reason: fixed: west red-audit now reports 0 guest-runtime-deploy proofs missing RED reason; false rc-only runtime REDs were repaired or replaced with direct launch-free oracles; documentation/manifest rules are enforced by patch check and metadata contract; remaining stale runtime blocker dar-1r72 is closed

title: perf #18 P6: cycle-decompose the hot ring RPC ~4.5us floor (6-phase breakdown)
description: After P4 the hot mach_reply_port path is ~4.4-5.4us with ~0 syscalls -- wakeup is gone; the remaining cost is server-side Mach/dispatch. Gist 191a10db asks for a cycle breakdown of the HOT P4 case across 6 phases: (1) guest submit, (2) server ring-drain, (3) server Call-dispatch, (4) Mach-op body, (5) server publish response, (6) guest observe response. Method: rdtsc brackets on BOTH sides (guest dserver-ring.c gr_port_trap; server ringServiceThread / doWork / publishReply), gated diagnostic (default OFF, byte-identical), dump per-phase mean/p50. Reuse the perf#9 in-guest rdtsc accountant pattern + the server per_call histo. GOAL: identify whether the next win is opcode-specialized fast paths (skip generic Call-dispatch) or port-table/refcount/alloc -- DATA before P5/P6 coding. Non-goal: change the wake model (frozen ABI v2). BLOCKED-BY: none (P4/.33 done).
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
DONE 2026-06-29 (Opus). 6-phase cycle breakdown of the hot ring mach_reply_port. Gated rdtsc instrumentation (DSERVER_RING_PHASE_PROF server / DARLING_RING_PHASE_PROF guest, default OFF byte-identical), live-measured, NOT pushed.

MEASURED (30k hot calls, balanced, TSC 1.9962 GHz, reconciles w/ bench 4.84us):
PHASE / ns / %total:
  guest submit       510  11%  (publish req + conditional doorbell)
  server drain       296   6%  (consumer_begin + rebuild UDS-format Message)
  server dispatch    647  14%  (callFromMessage: registry lookup + decode)
  server BODY       2260  48%  (doWork(): microthread/fiber dispatch around the actual Mach op)
  server publish      32  <1%  (publishReply: claim s2c slot + write + release)
  pure WAKEUP        960  20%  (roundtrip - server work = cross-proc scheduler gap, shared host)
  guest observe       17  <1%  (validate + copy port out)
  TOTAL            ~4724 ns

CONCLUSIONS:
1. TRANSPORT IS ~FREE: submit+drain+publish+observe = ~855ns (18%). Nothing left to win in the ring itself. => migrating MORE ops (P5/dar-1il) is worth it (each gets this cheap transport) but won't shrink THIS floor.
2. FLOOR = SERVER MACH WORK, as the gist predicted. doWork() body 2260ns/48% (generic microthread/fiber Call pipeline wrapped around a trivial port-allocate) + callFromMessage dispatch 647ns/14%. ~2900ns (62%) is generic-pipeline overhead around an op that should cost tens of ns.
3. WAKEUP 960ns/20% is irreducible shared-host scheduler cost (shrinks under pinning / latency mode).

NEXT (P6-followup / new bead): opcode-specialized server fast path -- switch(callnum){case mach_reply_port: fast path that allocates the port directly on the main loop, skipping Message-rebuild + callFromMessage + the microthread doWork() setup; default: generic_rpc_dispatch}. Plausibly cuts floor ~4.7us -> ~1.5-2us (transport 0.85 + wakeup 0.96 + lean body). Requires care: the generic path carries the dar-l8k/dar-6x4/dar-gwn correctness fixes; a fast path must NOT bypass anything that op actually needs (mach_reply_port is a pure self-port mint = safe candidate; validate per-op).

Commits (LOCAL, NOT pushed): darlingserver @7b58a83 (perf/shmem-ring-abi-validator), xnu @51893ca (perf/shmem-ring-guest). Both gated, default OFF, prod restored OFF (a13ec2ae/e5cd6b12/025aa772). TSC calib + harness in job tmp. CLOSING dar-aw2.

Fix must use one composed cleanup handler, preserve guest artifact cleanup, remove runner output and timeout marker on every exit path, and have a regression contract. Implemented locally pending validation; no upstream push.

title: Concurrent ninja/CMake regeneration in one Darling build dir can corrupt/regress build.ninja
description: Observed on 2026-07-07 while rebuilding integration/arch after patchset refresh: three concurrent ninja invocations against /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-build all triggered CMake regeneration; one failed with CMake Generate step failed: ninja -t restat build.ninja failed: ninja: error: failed recompaction: No such file or directory. The other concurrent legs completed. This is a workflow/tooling hazard: Darling build dir operations that may regenerate CMake must be serialized. Add locking to west darling-build and prefer it over parallel raw ninja for deployable builds; investigate whether a broader guard/hook is needed for raw ninja.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Mitigation added in darling-workspace/west_commands/darling_build.py: west darling-build now takes an fcntl lock at <build-dir>/.west-darling-build.lock around doctor/build/deploy. Focused deploy options remain available, so deployable validation should go through the serialized west wrapper instead of concurrent raw ninja. Raw ninja concurrency remains an operator hazard, but the supported workflow is now guarded.

This addresses the stale-DPREFIX failure mode found during dar-li2u runtime validation.

title: brew reinstall -s wget: gettext stage SIGABRT (SI_USER mistag during tar-extract wait)
description:
After dar-6x4.1 + dar-stackmmap-eexist-9j9 fixes (libunistring now GREEN), 'brew reinstall -s wget' dies on the FIRST dependency gettext, during the STAGE/unpack phase (tar-extract of the source tarball), NOT configure/make. The parent brew Ruby aborts while waiting on the extract child:

  formula.rb:3560: [BUG] Aborted at 0x000003e80000571f
  Ruby bt: build.rb install -> stage -> resource.rb unpack -> mktemp.rb:97 chdir -> formula.rb:3560 CFUNC :wait
  C bt: sig_restorer -> sigexc_handler+0x15c -> handler_linux_to_bsd+0x39a -> ruby sig_do_nothing -> rb_bug_for_fatal_signal
  regs: rax=0xfffffffffffffffc (-4 EINTR), rdi/r15=0x527, rip in libsystem_kernel
  abort code 0x000003e8_0000571f: high word 0x3e8 = 1000 = SI_USER (the classic mistag signature, family of dar-gwn.1.5 / dar-gwn.6.4)

This is a SEPARATE bug, NOT a regression from this session's fixes: the dar-gwn.6.4 sigexc self-resend fix (tgkill self, not kill(0)) IS present in the deployed dylib (sigexc.c:382-393), and libunistring (which my fd/stack fixes target) builds + make-check GREEN. Here a fatal signal reaches Ruby during a wait() on a child tar process, passes through Ruby's benign sig_do_nothing (SIGCHLD) handler, and gets escalated to rb_bug_for_fatal_signal -> SIGABRT. Likely an intermittent signal-delivery race (EINTR + SI_USER-tagged signal delivered to the parent during wait), reminiscent of but distinct from the closed dar-gwn.1.5/1.8 reaping/mistag bugs.

NEXT STEPS when picked up: (1) determine determinism - rerun gettext stage a few times (intermittent vs always); (2) capture the FIRST fatal signal + its si_code/sender via the server-side sigprocess trace (DSERVER_DARGWN64SIG) or the debug runner; (3) decide whether sig_do_nothing escalation is the bug (Ruby treats a SIGCHLD/benign signal as fatal because Darling mis-tags si_code) vs a genuine fatal signal from the tar child.

Evidence: ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/wget-e2e-1782565814/wget.out (full Ruby [BUG] dump + maps). Deployed dylib md5 2ec0c911 (has fd-guard + all integration patches). Not pushed.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: REPRO: ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/run-wget-e2e.sh (boots prefix darling-prefix-homebrew-test, runs brew reinstall -s wget). To isolate just gettext stage: brew reinstall -s gettext. Full crash dump preserved at ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/wget-e2e-1782565814/wget.out (lines 27-120 = Ruby [BUG] + control frames + C backtrace).

title: Rootless: diagnose guest bootstrap after dyld main
description: Rootless no-mount bootstrap now reaches dyld main-ready with a dynamically resolved, complete Mach-O runtime closure. Diagnose the remaining deterministic guest crash after dyld handoff, starting at the vchroot-to-launchd transition. Add bounded rootless-only diagnostics that preserve execution semantics, identify the product cause, and carry a committed behavioral RED-to-GREEN proof. Do not extend timeouts, accept source matching as coverage, or mask crashes with fallback behavior.
close_reason: Rootless post-dyld bootstrap cause fixed with deterministic runtime RED-to-GREEN proof.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 correction before adding a dyld hook: the prior missing guest trace was not yet a dyld verdict. The runner exported a host path as DARLING_BOOT_TRACE, while mldr appended the guest path under the same key; getenv selected the host value inside the guest. Root cause is trace-channel collision. Fixed by using DARLING_HOST_BOOT_TRACE for Linux launcher and mldr, leaving DARLING_BOOT_TRACE exclusively for the mldr-injected guest trace. The next full proof will now genuinely distinguish dyld from launchd.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 trace-channel split runtime result: duplicate environment keys are gone, but the guest pathname trace is still absent after mldr enter-target. This is not enough to blame dyld because writing a guest path itself depends on the pre-main VFS path. The next diagnostic must pass a pre-opened host trace FD through the mldr guest stack and have dyld or launchd write that FD directly. That removes guest path resolution from the oracle and keeps startup semantics unchanged.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 FD-backed repro: homebrew-prefix-baseline materialized, built and deployed successfully, then guest smoke timed out after 20s. Host trace reached mldr guest-trace-fd-ready and enter-target. The inherited guest FD trace stayed empty, so launchd main did not execute; this rules out the prior guest pathname/VFS ambiguity. Next diagnostic: add rootless-gated dyld FD markers after dyld rebase and immediately before dyld main, then repeat the same isolated bootstrap. No processes or Darling mounts remained; failed source/build scratch retained at /tmp/west-red-proof-source-cjxdkdma and /tmp/west-runtime-homebrew-prefix-baseline-my5qppdy.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 syscall-trace repro found the actual failure. The new bootstrap trace mode ran strace -ff over the same rootless profile. mldr pid 90435 wrote enter-target and immediately received SIGSEGV SEGV_MAPERR at address 0x18; it was killed one second later. No dyld FD marker can execute because the process faults at or immediately after the mldr-to-target transfer. darlingserver remains in epoll and launcher keeps polling a nonexistent shellspawn socket, which made this look like a 20-second hang. Evidence: /tmp/west-rootless-bootstrap-strace-20260711/bootstrap.90435 and job /tmp/west-job-rootless-strace-20260711. Next: retrieve stack/core or add a transfer-boundary diagnostic, then fix the null/invalid target state; do not treat it as dyld or timeout behavior.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 rootless bootstrap evidence: the materialized homebrew-prefix-baseline runtime now builds fat dyld and reaches dyld mach-init-ready, rebase-ready, and main-ready. Previous RED: old dyld crashed in getenv at PC offset 0xb0df2 immediately after mach-init-enter because libc had no ProgramVars. Fix: rootless dyld owns stable bootstrap ProgramVars and calls _program_vars_init before mach_init; raw Linux write keeps pre-libc trace safe. Full repro: west test --prefix-profile homebrew --bootstrap-runtime-profile homebrew-prefix-baseline --bootstrap-syscall-trace /tmp/west-rootless-bootstrap-owned-vars-202607111230. Current next boundary is a deterministic SIGABRT after dyld main-ready: vchroot cannot load /usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib, reason image not found/no shared cache. No Darling processes or prefix mounts remain. Retained source /tmp/west-red-proof-source-3r0brxpa and build /tmp/west-runtime-homebrew-prefix-baseline-k3dmtnlk.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 rootless bootstrap closure repro: after system-closure deployment, dyld reaches main-ready and loads libSystem.B, then deterministically aborts while loading /usr/lib/system/libdispatch.dylib because /usr/lib/libobjc.A.dylib is absent. Evidence: /tmp/west-rootless-system-closure-optional-202607111340/bootstrap.705509 lines 3482-3498; guest reports No shared cache present and libobjc.A image not found. The runner deployed all names in its static system-closure list but did not build/deploy objc, proving that the list is not a transitive Mach-O runtime closure. No Darling processes or prefix mounts remained. Next: make rootless bootstrap deployment model its complete runtime closure from explicit roots and recursively validated Mach-O dependencies, rather than extending a hand-maintained libsystem name list.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 closure-resolver RED: the first full run with rootless-bootstrap-closure did not execute guest code; it correctly failed setup with exact unresolved Mach-O provider /usr/lib/libresolv.9.dylib required by /usr/lib/system/libsystem_info.dylib. Evidence: /tmp/west-job-rootless-macho-closure-202607111150/log. This proves the new runner rejects incomplete deployment before SIGABRT. CMake inspection shows the final provider is target resolv-darwin (only its firstpass image existed under the prior build roots), so the typed resource now adds resolv-darwin as an explicit build root. No Darling processes or prefix mounts remained.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 full closure result: rootless bootstrap now deploys all transitive Mach-O providers, including libSystem, libdispatch, libobjc, libresolv, libbsm, libc++, and libc++abi. dyld reaches main-ready. The remaining deterministic SIGSEGV is at NULL in libsystem_c _parse_long_options (PC offset 0x68622) after dyld handoff. The initial guest target is vchroot (mldr argv: vchroot, prefix, /sbin/launchd), which does not call getopt; launchd does. This points to the guest exec transition or its argument vector, not a missing dylib. Evidence: /tmp/west-rootless-macho-closure-resolv-202607111210/bootstrap.818613 lines 9, 947, 5365 and build/source scratch retained at /tmp/west-runtime-homebrew-prefix-baseline-aetwlyw4 and /tmp/west-red-proof-source-jo3bc45s. Next diagnostic: rootless-only vchroot markers around execv to distinguish vchroot entry from guest exec handoff. No Darling processes or prefix mounts remained.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 second full typed-closure repro: source and deployed binary both contain vchroot markers, but guest FD trace still ends at dyld main-ready. The complete dynamic closure is present and the crash remains SIGSEGV at NULL in libsystem_c _parse_long_options. Therefore the marker did not prove vchroot was skipped; it proved guest libc cannot read DARLING_BOOT_TRACE_FD after dyld. dyld itself can read that value only by scanning its startup envp directly. The likely contract boundary is guest libSystem initialization, which must call _libc_initializer to establish ProgramVars. Added a rootless-only raw FD trace around libSystem initializer entry and libc completion to distinguish missing initializer from bad ProgramVars. Repro: west test --prefix-profile homebrew --bootstrap-runtime-profile homebrew-prefix-baseline --bootstrap-syscall-trace /tmp/west-rootless-vchroot-trace-202607111140. No live Darling processes or prefix mounts remain.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 libSystem stage repro: the fully materialized typed Mach-O closure built and deployed, dyld reached bootstrap-enter, mach-init-ready, rebase-ready, and main-ready. libSystem then emitted init-enter and immediately received SIGSEGV SEGV_MAPERR at NULL before kernel-ready. This narrows the product fault to the first libSystem initializer call, __libkernel_init, rather than dyld, closure resolution, vchroot, or launchd. Repro: west test --prefix-profile homebrew --bootstrap-runtime-profile homebrew-prefix-baseline --bootstrap-syscall-trace /tmp/west-rootless-libsystem-stages-202607111205. The runner returned rc=1 with the expected crash verdict; no Darling process or prefix mount remained. Evidence was recorded before cleanup of /tmp/west-red-proof-source-2otu7089, /tmp/west-runtime-homebrew-prefix-baseline-hx2hua7h, and /tmp/west-rootless-libsystem-stages-202607111205.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 server-authority identity fix: removed homebrew/xnu/rootless-init-identity.patch. The patch was obsolete because darlingserver/rootless-no-mount-startup already maps the known rootless-init peer to guest nsid 1. Its remaining client hook invoked getenv before _libc_initializer initialized ProgramVars, causing the deterministic NULL SIGSEGV in __libkernel_init. RED evidence: prior run stopped after libsystem init-enter. GREEN boundary evidence: full materialized run /tmp/west-job-rootless-server-identity-202607111225 emitted libsystem kernel-ready, platform-ready, pthread-ready, and libc-ready, then loaded the shellspawn launchd plist. The subsequent bootstrap timeout and orphaned launchd descendants are a distinct lifecycle bug tracked by dar-test-infra-sp5.21; no rootless process or mount remained after bounded cleanup.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 post-libSystem result: after removing the stale client identity hook, libSystem consistently reaches kernel-ready, platform-ready, pthread-ready and libc-ready. launchd then reads org.darlinghq.shellspawn.plist and receives its ProgramArguments, but shellspawn.sock is still absent at the 20s smoke deadline. This is now the next product boundary; the previous NULL crash in __libkernel_init is fixed. The runner timeout/cleanup is bounded and tracked separately in dar-test-infra-sp5.21.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 rootless full repro after materializing the new launchd/shellspawn trace patch: bounded bootstrap again timed out at 20s and cleanup reclaimed six exact DARLING_PREFIX+DARLING_ROOTLESS owners with no residual process or mount. The guest reaches launchd and starts /bin/launchctl bootstrap -S System (mldr PID 1242016); it performs the synchronous mldr_path RPC and then proceeds through plist loading. Syscall trace alone cannot identify the later unreturned RPC. Added runner support so --bootstrap-syscall-trace enables DARLING_SERVER_AUXLOG only for that diagnostic run and exports prefix/private/var/log/dserver-auxlog.txt as darlingserver-rpc.log next to strace. Next repro will use this structured RECV/reply-disposition oracle; no timeout extension or fallback.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 final rootless RPC-trace investigation: runner now passes DARLING_SERVER_AUXLOG=1 only under --bootstrap-syscall-trace and strace -v proved the exact darlingserver exec environment contains it. No dserver-auxlog was produced because the materialized homebrew profile pins darlingserver/test-diagnostics-trace at 6621f77, whose built binary has neither DARLING_SERVER_AUXLOG nor A0 RECV/reply-disposition strings. The local A0 implementation begins at bce051c on a later perf stack, so importing it just for this rootless diagnosis would incorrectly mix unrelated IPC work. Keep dar-d2pm open: the next rootless diagnosis must either extract a minimal profile-compatible RPC trace patch with its own behavior proof or use the existing syscall oracle; do not claim the unavailable A0 trace was captured. Every full run remained bounded at 20s and cleaned exact rootless owners with no residual process/mount.

comments.author: ilyagulya

New darlingserver patch e635652 is based exactly on rootless profile head fd105515 and reuses existing DSERVER_TEST_TRACE_FILE diagnostics. In syscall-trace mode west clears prefix/private/var/log/dserver-rpc-trace.log, passes that path to darlingserver, and exports it as darlingserver-rpc.log. The host contract is GREEN; full materialized rootless runtime is next and must prove the trace is emitted by the deployed binary before any product conclusion.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 full materialized rootless repro after e635652: profile applied rootless-rpc-trace, built/deployed successfully, and bounded smoke timed out after 20s (job /tmp/west-job-rootless-rpc-20260711, syscall bundle /tmp/west-rootless-rpc-20260711). The exported real server trace proves every observed RPC has a terminal reply disposition/send; mldr_path, checkout/checkin, vchroot and launchd child checkins all reply. There is no lost-RPC conclusion. launchd PID 1404249 repeatedly calls pthread_canceled and receives -EINVAL; current documented semantics return EINVAL for action=0 with no pending cancellation, so action must be captured before treating it as a product fault. Cleanup reclaimed exactly six rootless owners; no process/mount tail. Source/build scratch were retained only until this evidence was recorded and are now safe to remove.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 second materialized rootless repro after adding action trace: 86 pthread_canceled calls were all action=0 code=-22. This matches the documented no-pending-cancel result, so cancellation ABI is not the bootstrap cause. The smoke still timed out at 20s; every observed RPC reply is sent. Continue at the launchd/shellspawn boundary, not at darlingserver RPC. Cleanup audit and scratch cleanup follow.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 rootless scheduler trace rerun: completed west test --prefix-profile homebrew --bootstrap-runtime-profile homebrew-prefix-baseline --bootstrap-syscall-trace /tmp/west-rootless-scheduler-20260711 with expected smoke timeout (rc=1). launchd read org.darlinghq.shellspawn.plist and received ProgramArguments=/usr/libexec/shellspawn, but neither guest FD trace nor syscall capture contained any new shellspawn dispatch-start/start-enter/child-enter/spawn markers. The guest trace stopped after dyld/libsystem startup; host boot trace shows mldr entering launchd. Therefore the current marker transport is not reliable for launchd-stage diagnosis (most likely DARLING_BOOT_TRACE_FD is not preserved/usable once launchd starts), so do not infer scheduler non-entry from its absence. RPC trace continued to show normal replies. No prefix-owned processes or mounts remained after cleanup. Next: make launchd-stage diagnostic transport observable independently of the inherited guest FD, then rerun.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 rootless dyld handoff evidence: job /tmp/west-job-rootless-dyld-main-20260711 completed rc=1 (expected guest-smoke timeout). Native guest-fd trace now has dyld main-enter and dyld main-entry-ready for all three bootstrap processes, after libsystem initialization, so dyld _main and mldr handoff to each Mach-O entrypoint complete. vchroot also reaches exec-target. No launchd scheduler markers or shellspawn exec follow. Earlier external trace-provider experiment was removed from the portable archive: launchd imported the provider and libSystem exported it, but no marker appeared; this run narrows the remaining fault to execution after the target entrypoint, not dyld handoff. No lingering rootless processes or mounts after cleanup.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 rootless stack-sample evidence corrected the scheduler hypothesis. Fresh perf run /tmp/west-rootless-perf-stack-fresh-rpc-20260711 completed bounded rc=1 after 20s and cleaned all rootless processes/mounts. Perf mapping proves PID 2039126 is /sbin/launchd and PID 2039134 execs /usr/libexec/shellspawn; addr2line maps its later samples to shellspawn main -> listenForConnections. The prefix contains var/run/shellspawn.sock after the run. Therefore shellspawn is started and listening; the failure is launcher client connectivity. In startup/darling.c, USE_LINUX_4_11_HACK unconditionally creates an abstract socket name prefix + /var/run/shellspawn.sock, while rootless shellspawn binds the filesystem guest path /var/run/shellspawn.sock mapped to the prefix. Rootless no-mount has no mount-namespace reason for the abstract hack, so client and server addresses differ. Next fix: retain abstract addressing only for non-rootless legacy mode and use filesystem path in rootless mode; prove with runtime socket-connect evidence. Also observed stale shellspawn.sock tail after cleanup, to address under lifecycle task dar-test-infra-sp5.21.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 correction from fresh client syscall trace /tmp/west-rootless-client-trace-20260711: the previous abstract-socket diagnosis was wrong. In startup/darling.c, addr.sun_path[0] is immediately overwritten by strcpy(prefix), so the effective client address is the filesystem path. The real deterministic failure is readiness ordering: launcher waits 15 seconds, emits shellspawn-wait-expired, then connect(…/var/run/shellspawn.sock) returns ENOENT and exits at 15:19:27.606. launchd does not receive shellspawn ProgramArguments until 15:19:29.404, after the client has already failed. The fix must remove the silent fixed 15-second fallthrough: rootless startup needs an explicit, bounded readiness/connection contract driven by the caller bootstrap deadline and must report the exact readiness failure; do not change socket addressing or extend a blind timeout.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 runtime RED harness attempt /tmp/west-job-rootless-shellspawn-readiness-redgreen-20260711-rerun: current-minus launcher unexpectedly passed because this particular materialized build reached shellspawn readiness inside the old 15-second window. That confirms the product bug is timing-sensitive and the initial RED oracle is nondeterministic, so it cannot be accepted. Next: introduce an explicit rootless test-only shellspawn readiness delay in a prerequisite instrumentation patch applied to both old and fixed runtimes; then require the old one-shot client to fail with exact ENOENT and the fixed client to reach the guest marker. The failed runner preserved build scratch /tmp/west-red-proof-runtime-o4c8_ifk. Also found framework defect dar-test-infra-sp5.26: RED invocation ran after prefix resource context closed; fix before rerun.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 deterministic final proof passed: prerequisite rootless test hook delayed shellspawn socket readiness 20 seconds in both runtimes. Current-minus launcher failed exactly with Error connecting to shellspawn in the container … No such file or directory (bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260711T154017Z-west-test-rootless_shellspawn_readiness); fixed launcher retried while init remained alive and emitted WEST_ROOTLESS_BOOTSTRAP_OK (bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260711T154509Z-west-test-rootless_shellspawn_readiness). Job /tmp/west-job-rootless-shellspawn-readiness-redgreen-20260711-deterministic rc=0. The remaining stale .darlingserver.sock cleanup issue is separately owned by dar-test-infra-sp5.21.

title: beads: add forgiving aliases for common command syntax misses
description: Agents repeatedly miss Beads command syntax on first attempt. Add or document forgiving aliases/wrappers for common operations used in this repo, especially comments add, dep add/remove, create --description/--body, show/search, and close reason. Acceptance: AGENTS/RTK workflow names the canonical commands, west dw beads exposes or documents short aliases where feasible, and metadata/contract tests cover at least one alias so command drift is caught.

title: Investigate detached west test processes from limited execution transport
description: Long foreground west test invocations launched through the current command transport can report completion while the rtk proxy and child west process remain alive, holding the prefix lock. Repro 2026-07-10: rtk proxy env PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 west test --env darling --prefix-profile homebrew --ctest-timeout-seconds 360 returned to the agent while PID 2141029 continued building and held darling-prefix-homebrew-test/.west-test.lock. Define and document a PID/log based long-run helper or fix the transport integration; add a reproducible contract where feasible, ensure cancellation kills the whole process group, and prevent duplicate prefix runs. No upstream push.
close_reason: Fixed with observable job helper, process-group cancellation, documentation, and regression contract in c7e490d.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented local job tooling in c7e490d. scripts/west-job.sh start/status/wait/cancel records command, PID plus /proc start-time identity, log, and atomic rc, and cancellation signals the entire setsid process group. tests/run-west-job-contract.sh proves green completion, rc propagation, cancellation rc=143, and no live PID. AGENTS.md now requires it for long guest CTest runs in limited transports.

title: dar-6x4-perf: speed up Darling fork/exec/thread-create (libunistring make-check 'hang' = SLOWNESS, not deadlock)
description: REFRAMED 2026-06-27 (live-proven on pristine server fb027685 / mldr 5f01b70f): dar-6x4 is NOT a deadlock and NOT a lost checkin reply — it is PATHOLOGICAL SLOWNESS of process/thread creation under Darling. Each guest fork/exec/pthread_create does a synchronous checkin RPC through the SINGLE-THREADED darlingserver, and the parent BUSY-SPINS sched_yield (mldr threads.c:199-200) until the child clears args.pth. Measured: ~2ms/thread-create, ~73 sched_yield spins per create, ~485 creates/s for one process. configure/make/make-check spawn thousands of tiny fork+exec (conftests, shell pipelines, per-file clang) -> the build LOOKS hung but progresses (config.log grows monotonically; make produced 0->607 .o on the real run; no permanent wedge ever observed on the REAL libunistring build). The tcstorm RED gate is UNFAITHFUL (12 procs x tight pthread_create maximize spin starvation) and all wall-clock verdicts were also contaminated by an unrelated 8-core clickhouse-server tenant. GOAL: make Darling process/thread creation fast enough that libunistring build+check completes in reasonable time. Evidence: ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/reframe-noDeadlock-1782547330/FINDING.txt; supersedes the lost-reply framing in bead dar-6x4. CONSTRAINT: nothing pushed without explicit approval; build server/mldr from darling-build (prefix=darling-prefix).

title: perf #1: replace mldr threads.c sched_yield busy-spin with futex/eventfd wait
description: CHEAPEST, HIGHEST-CONFIDENCE FIX. In mldr __darling_thread_create (darling/src/startup/mldr/elfcalls/threads.c:199-200) the parent does `while (args.pth != NULL) sched_yield();` — a pure busy-spin burning a whole core until the child clears args.pth after its checkin (~73 sched_yield/create measured). Under parallelism (make -j, configure) spinning parents STEAL CPU from the very children + single-threaded server they wait on -> positive-feedback slowdown. FIX: replace spin with a blocking wait — child futex_wake (or write 1 byte to an eventfd) right after `in_args->pth = NULL` (threads.c:247); parent futex_wait/read instead of sched_yield. PRESERVE the register-clobber constraint at threads.c:259-261 (wake BEFORE the no-more-calls region). TDD: guest micro-bench measuring thread-create latency + parent CPU (should drop from ~100% spin to ~0% while waiting); before/after. Build mldr from darling-build, deploy to darling-prefix (kill running server before cp). RED gate = latency/CPU bench; GREEN = same correctness + parent ~0% CPU during wait + faster wall-clock under N-parallel. Risk LOW (local mldr). Do NOT break boot (mldr critical path; cf prior failed unique-bind attempt in dar-6x4). Nothing pushed.
notes: FIXED+packaged+integrated+live-A/B, NOT pushed (Opus 2026-06-28). Implemented as ADAPTIVE bounded-spin-then-FUTEX_WAIT (NOT pure futex): __darling_thread_create spins kSpinIters=4000 (fast path, ~1ms) then FUTEX_WAITs on a new _Atomic int checked_in word (appended LAST in arg_struct -> i386 asm offsets preserved); child stores 1 + FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE at the in_args->pth=NULL point (before the register-clobber asm region, constraint respected). WHY adaptive not pure-futex: live A/B showed pure FUTEX_WAIT REGRESSES the fast warm-server case ~25% (checkin p50=16us < a futex round-trip); the unbounded spin actually wins when the reply is imminent. Adaptive keeps the fast-path spin AND caps CPU so a slow checkin can't monopolise a core. RED->GREEN committed test tests/regression/thread_create_checkin_wait.c + run-thread-create-checkin-wait.sh (RED unbounded spin burns 141-300ms CPU/300ms checkin; GREEN adaptive ~1ms). LIVE single-storm A/B (homebrew-test prefix, fixed-mldr 9e257cbd vs baseline 84cf2fbe): creates/s 1702 vs 1691 (neutral, no regression), checkin p50/p95/p99 64/128/256us identical. Multistorm aggregate too noisy on this host (clickhouse; baseline alone 5.4k-8.3k/s) to resolve. Branch fix/mldr-thread-create-futex-wait @d87489f87, patch darling/mldr-thread-create-futex-wait.patch sha256 0b006aae, west verify=60, status--strict 0-missing, integration 03e1c9980, canonical mldr 9e257cbd deployed. HARNESS: real guest prefix is DPREFIX=darling-prefix-homebrew-test via darling-prefix/bin/darling launcher (see memory darling-prefix-nooverlayfs-boot).
close_reason: done

title: perf #2d: narrow dtape kernel locks (fine-grained per-task/per-waitq/per-port)
description: DESIGN C from the perf #2 redesign analysis (HIGH risk, biggest ceiling -- BACKLOG until #2a/#2b prove it's needed). The reason naive MT regresses: dtape kernel state is effectively under a big-lock set, so MT workers serialize. Make the locks fine-grained (per-task, per-waitq, per-port) so workers run truly in parallel for INDEPENDENT guest processes (a build = many independent processes -> linear scaling potential, the real make -j win). This is a rewrite of the XNU-port lock model = EXACTLY the 'bugs specific to multi-threaded darlingserver' upstream commit 2b0ae0e backed off from. Weeks of work + subtle deadlock risk. Do a FOCUSED spike on ONE lock first (the top contender from perf #2a), prove correctness + speedup on that alone, before broadening. Requires DSERVER_SINGLE_THREADED=0. RED/GREEN multistorm + full brew/libunistring e2e + stress for deadlocks. Build from darling-build. Risk HIGH. Nothing pushed. Blocked-by perf #2a.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: DEFERRED by perf #2a profiling (2026-06-28): no dtape-lock contention exists in the make-j/checkin workload -- waitq/ipc/sched/libsimple_lock do NOT appear in the perf profile (real kernel-emulation work <1% CPU). Narrowing dtape locks would optimize a non-bottleneck. Keep as backlog ONLY if a future heavier workload shows real dtape contention. The actual MT bottleneck is the main-loop<->worker condvar handoff -> see #2b.

title: perf #2e: shard darlingserver by guest process (multiple event-loops/dtape instances)
description: DESIGN D from the perf #2 redesign analysis (VERY HIGH risk, most architectural -- BACKLOG / last resort). Instead of making the locks scale, run N event-loops / N dtape instances, each owning a disjoint subset of guest processes via sharded listener sockets. Within a shard: single-threaded (preserves dtape's single-thread invariant, safe). Parallelism BETWEEN shards. Hard part: cross-process Mach IPC, shared ports, the global registries and kernel_task are shared -> need replication or a cross-shard protocol. Effectively splitting the emulated kernel. Only pursue if #2b+#2d cannot deliver. Build from darling-build. Risk VERY HIGH. Nothing pushed.

title: perf #3a: fuse thread checkin + thread_self_trap into ONE RPC round-trip
description: Measured (perf #3): a new guest thread does TWO back-to-back SYNCHRONOUS RPC round-trips in darling_thread_entry (threads.c) -- dserver_rpc_explicit_checkin (registration, ~205us) immediately followed by thread_self_trap -> dserver_rpc_thread_self_trap (fetch the thread's Mach port, needed synchronously: written to TSD before the jump to the entry point). The single remaining round-trip can't be removed (out-of-process kernel: registration must reach the server), but the TWO can be FUSED into one: have the checkin reply ALSO carry the thread-self Mach port, so the new thread gets both registration-ack and its port in a single send+recv. Expected ~-50% of the per-thread RPC cost (the part perf #3's recv-spin only shaved ~23% off). Scope: extend the checkin reply wire format (dserver_rpc_reply_checkin_t) + server Checkin::processCall to compute+return dtape_thread_self_trap (already done server-side at call.cpp:526 for the self-trap call), + mldr darling_thread_entry to consume the port from the checkin reply and SKIP the separate thread_self_trap RPC.
