title: perf #18 P4-spike: isolated host A/B of wake-model variants (conditional doorbell + adaptive poll + guest spin)
description:
GOAL: pick the P4 wake-model by DATA before touching darlingserver. Build a standalone Linux host harness (two pinned processes sharing a real shm SPSC ring, NO Mach/dispatch/darlingserver) that isolates the WAKEUP variable -- the thing the live A/B (perf#18 P3) proved is ~90% of cost (ring removed only ~12% socket marshalling; wakeup model untouched).

Source: gist https://gist.github.com/IlyaGulya/5e274d4b231ac89910bab20eb4d31880 (confirms diagnosis; gives the P4 model). Key idea: doorbell ONLY on transition-to-sleep, not per request; adaptive server poll (ACTIVE_POLLING/IDLE_SPINNING/SLEEP_ARMED/SLEEPING_EPOLL with critical recheck before epoll); guest spin-before-futex.

VARIANTS to A/B (focused 3-4):
  V0 baseline = current syscall-per-call (eventfd_write + epoll + FUTEX_WAKE + FUTEX_WAIT) -- reproduce the ~26us number as the control.
  V1 conditional doorbell + adaptive server poll (server spins the ring; client writes eventfd ONLY if server is SLEEP_ARMED/SLEEPING_EPOLL).
  V2 guest spin-before-futex on the reply (short spin on seq/status before FUTEX_WAIT; server FUTEX_WAKE only if waiter-bit set).
  V3 = V1+V2 combined (the gist's target: hot path 0 syscalls, 0 forced ctx switches).

MEASURE per variant (gist's checklist): p50/p90/p99/max latency; syscalls/call (strace -c sampling); voluntary ctx-switches/call (/proc or getrusage); hot (both runnable, pinned via taskset) AND cold (server idle/sleeping) cases. Success criterion (NOT 270ns immediately): hot null RPC -> syscalls/call ~0, vctxsw/call ~0, latency well under 10us. If it stalls at 15-20us after that, the remaining cost is dispatch/locking/Mach/cacheline, not wakeup.

DELIVERABLE: harness committed under tests/ (RED->GREEN where applicable), a results table, and a recommendation for which variant to port. Host-only, nothing deployed. Reference ready SPSC/IPC libs as an etalon (see sibling survey bead) but keep prod libc-free.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
SPIKE DONE (2026-06-29, Opus). Host harness committed darlingserver @9907e18 (tests/ring_wake_model_bench.c + run-ring-wake-model-bench.sh). Two pinned processes, real shm SPSC ring, no Mach/dispatch. 4 variants A/B'd hot+cold, N=100k, pinned cores.

RESULT (cold p50 / vctxsw-per-call / syscalls-per-call):
  v0 baseline syscall-per-call ........ ~58000 ns / 0.94 / ~2.2   (== current P3 prod model)
  v1 conditional doorbell + adaptive poll ~240 ns / 0.01 / -
  v2 guest spin-before-futex ........... ~180 ns / 0.00 / -
  v3 v1+v2 (gist target) ............... ~160 ns / 0.00 / ~0   (p99 ~200ns)
strace confirms v3 = 4 syscalls TOTAL for a 20k-call run (vs 43k for v0). HOT case all variants ~150-210ns.

DECISION (by data): PORT v3 for P4 (.33). v2 = guest spin-before-futex is the dominant single lever (alone ~180ns, 0 ctxsw) if a minimal first step is wanted; v1 (server adaptive poll + conditional doorbell) handles the c2s/idle side and is the io_uring SQPOLL pattern. Combined = hot path 0 syscalls / 0 forced ctx switches = the gist's ~270ns category reproduced.

CONFIRMS the live-A/B diagnosis: the WAKEUP model, not the ring transport, was ~90% of cost (~330x between v0 and v3). Lib survey (.32, CLOSED): no ready SPSC lib is a guest-side drop-in (all libc + trusted-peer); keep the hand-rolled libc-free ring, adopt the io_uring SQPOLL conditional-doorbell PATTERN. .33 (P4 integration) is now unblocked & fully specified by this data. Host-only, nothing deployed/pushed.

title: perf #18: survey ready SPSC/lock-free IPC libs as reference/etalon for the wake-model bench
description:
Survey existing SPSC ring / lock-free queue / shared-mem IPC libraries to (a) use as a correctness+latency ETALON in the P4 wake-model harness (perf#18 .31), and (b) decide -- BY DATA, not default -- whether any is worth integrating instead of the hand-rolled ring.

CANDIDATES to look at: rigtorp/SPSCQueue (header-only C++), boost::lockfree::spsc_queue, folly ProducerConsumerQueue, moodycamel readerwriterqueue, dpdk rte_ring (overkill), iceoryx (too heavy), liburing (different model but relevant for the doorbell idea), raw futex2/FUTEX_WAIT_MULTIPLE.

CONSTRAINTS that rule most out for PROD (but not as a bench etalon): the guest side is libc-free raw-LINUX_SYSCALL, very early-boot, fragile TLS, must be byte-identical when the feature flag is OFF, untrusted-peer trust boundary (offset-not-pointer, bounds-checked) -- a typical C++ header-only queue assumes libc + trusts both ends. So default expectation = libs serve as a reference implementation / latency target to benchmark our ring against, NOT a drop-in.

DELIVERABLE: short comparison (license, header-only?, libc deps, trust model, wake mechanism, claimed latency) + a verdict per lib: bench-etalon-only vs integration-candidate. Feeds .31.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
SURVEY DONE (2026-06-29, Opus). Verdict up front: NO ready lib is a prod drop-in for the guest side; all assume libc + trust both ends. They're useful as a bench ETALON / design reference only — and the .31 host harness already reproduced the gist's ~270ns category with a hand-rolled ring, so the ring DATA STRUCTURE was never the gap. The gap was the WAKE MODEL, which no SPSC-queue lib addresses (they're queues, not doorbell/sleep protocols).

Per-candidate:
- rigtorp/SPSCQueue (C++17, header-only, MIT): excellent cache-line-padded SPSC, the de-facto latency reference. BUT C++ + assumes trusted peer (no offset-not-pointer / bounds-check of an untrusted writer) + no wake protocol. = bench-etalon only.
- boost::lockfree::spsc_queue: heavy Boost dep, C++, trusted-peer. Not for libc-free early-boot guest. = skip.
- folly ProducerConsumerQueue (Apache2): single-header-ish but pulls folly conventions; C++; trusted. = etalon-only.
- moodycamel readerwriterqueue (BSD, header-only C++): great SPSC, dynamic resize (allocates! unacceptable in the guest hot path / under-lock). trusted-peer. = etalon-only.
- DPDK rte_ring: kernel-bypass scale, massive dep, NIC-oriented. = overkill, skip.
- iceoryx (Eclipse, zero-copy shm IPC, C++): closest in PURPOSE (untrusted-ish shm IPC) but huge runtime (RouDi daemon), C++17, not embeddable in libsystem_kernel. = skip; worth reading their wake/doorbell design as prior art.
- liburing / io_uring: DIFFERENT model but the MOST relevant prior art for the wake side — SQ/CQ rings with IORING_SETUP_SQPOLL = exactly the gist's "doorbell only when kernel thread is asleep" idea (the kernel polls; userspace skips the syscall unless the poller slept). Our v1 adaptive-poll mirrors this. Reference, not integratable (it's the kernel's ring, not ours).
- futex2 / FUTEX_WAIT_MULTIPLE: kernel-side, not a queue; relevant only if we later wait on many client rings at once (P5+).

CONCLUSION: keep the hand-rolled libc-free ring (offset-addressed, bounds-checked, byte-identical-when-OFF — none of the libs satisfy these). Adopt the io_uring SQPOLL conditional-doorbell PATTERN (validated as .31 v1/v3), not a library. Close as reference-complete; no integration candidate.

title: perf #18 P4: integrate winning wake-model into darlingserver+guest (conditional doorbell + adaptive poll), live A/B
description:
After .31 picks a wake-model variant by data, port ONLY the winner into the real darlingserver main-loop + guest ring path, behind the existing DSERVER_RING_TRANSPORT / DARLING_RING_TRANSPORT flags (default OFF, byte-identical off).

SCOPE (from gist 5e274d4b...): server main loop gains adaptive states ACTIVE_POLLING/IDLE_SPINNING/SLEEP_ARMED/SLEEPING_EPOLL with the critical recheck (drain rings AFTER arming, before epoll_wait, to avoid the publish/sleep race). Guest reply path gains spin-before-FUTEX_WAIT; server FUTEX_WAKE only when the guest waiter-bit is set; guest eventfd doorbell only when server is sleeping/armed. Adaptive spin budget + env knobs (DARLING_SERVER_SPIN_US, modes low-power/balanced/latency) so idle desktop workload doesn't burn a core.

HARD: the server main loop is single-threaded and also services UDS + all other Monitors -- a spin budget must NOT starve them; integrate the poll as a bounded pre-epoll phase, not an infinite loop. Must not regress fork-storm/build correctness (dar-l8k / dar-6x4 / dar-gwn). RED gate: extend the host harness assertion; live gate: re-run the mach_reply_port A/B + the gist's syscalls/ctxsw-per-call measurement, target hot null RPC well under 10us with ~0 syscalls/call. BLOCKED-BY .31.

Reuses the P3 datapath + the nsid/setPID fix (perf#18 P3, darlingserver @1418bee) -- only the WAKE model changes.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
DONE 2026-06-29 (Opus). v3 wake model integrated + live-validated; NOT pushed. DATAPATH UNCHANGED from P3; only the WAKE model changed. Reused nsid/setPID fix (darlingserver @1418bee).

DELIVERED:
- ABI v2 (rpc-supplement.h): control-block words server_state (ACTIVE_POLLING/SLEEP_ARMED/SLEEPING_EPOLL, server-written) + s2c_waiters (guest waiter-bit), each own cache line; pure shared predicates dserver_ring_guest_should_doorbell / dserver_ring_server_should_wake. ABI bumped 1->2 so a mismatched peer rejects cleanly.
- Server (server.cpp): bounded pre-epoll spin drains all rings while publishing ACTIVE_POLLING, polls epoll(timeout 0) each iter so UDS/Monitors not starved; idle -> SLEEP_ARMED -> critical recheck -> SLEEPING_EPOLL -> epoll_wait(-1). Adaptive DARLING_SERVER_SPIN_US + DARLING_SERVER_MODE=low-power/balanced/latency (balanced=20us). weak_ptr ring registry; servicing OUTSIDE registry lock (dar-6x4 hazard). wakeGuest() FUTEX_WAKE only when s2c_waiters set.
- Guest (dserver-ring.c): doorbell only when !ACTIVE_POLLING; gr_wait_reply sets s2c_waiters only on slow path before FUTEX_WAIT (hot spin advertises NO waiter -> server skips wake too).
- Metrics: ring_serviced_spin/_doorbell, _doorbells_received, ring_wakes_issued/_skipped in stat JSON.

RED->GREEN GATE: tests/ring_wake_predicates_test.c + run-ring-wake-model-bench.sh; -DWAKE_MODEL_OLD (v0 always-wake) arm FAILS; committed WITH fix. Host bench gate: v3 cold 336x faster than v0.

LIVE A/B (ring ON balanced, all 7 locations, booted): mach_reply_port 60k calls ~4.4-5.4us/call (vs ~29.7us UDS prod, ~26us P3-ring) = ~6x. Counters: spin 57958 vs doorbell 3062 (95% hot-path), wakes_skipped 59115 vs issued 1905 (97% no FUTEX_WAKE), doorbells 1855 (~3%). => hot path ~0 syscalls/call BOTH sides = gist P4 target met (hot null RPC <10us, ~0 syscalls). Remaining ~4.5us = gist's predicted dispatch/Mach floor, NOT wakeup.
CORRECTNESS: fork-storm build 3/3 DONE (200/200 .o, no crash/wedge); dar-l8k/dar-6x4 unregressed. Boot clean.

GOTCHA: 'ninja emulation' rebuilds only .o/archive, NOT libsystem_kernel.dylib -- build the dylib target path + dyld explicitly or the deployed dylib is STALE and guest silently UDS-falls-back (~12.8us fake-ring = real UDS). Confirmed RING HIT via temp GR_DIAG __simple_fprintf (removed before commit).

Commits (LOCAL, NOT pushed): darlingserver @19b334f (perf/shmem-ring-abi-validator), xnu @5b2248a (perf/shmem-ring-guest). Prod restored OFF: server a13ec2ae, dylib e5cd6b12 x3, dyld 025aa772 x2, mldr ebd247fd. Build flags reset OFF.

NEXT: ~4.5us floor is now dispatch/Mach. Migrate more ops onto the ring (worth it on v3, unlike P3) or attack server Call dispatch / port-table.

title: perf#21c: strict guest NOFILE virtual cap (boot-compat investigation)
description: Session tasklist #75. perf#21c: investigate a strict guest RLIMIT_NOFILE virtual cap (follow-on to perf#21b compact-fd band). Open question is boot-compatibility: too-low a cap breaks boot/launchd; needs the boot-compat envelope measured before enforcing. Recon/measurement task under the dar-6x4-perf epic. Reference: memory perf#21b compact-fd band.

title: perf #4: build a FAITHFUL benchmark + retire the unfaithful tcstorm gate
description: METHODOLOGY. tcstorm (12 procs x tight pthread_create loop) ARTIFICIALLY maximizes the sched_yield-spin starvation and gave misleading permanent-hang verdicts; also all wall-clock runs were contaminated by an external clickhouse-server (8 cores). Build a faithful, contention-robust benchmark: (a) measure thread-create + fork+exec latency in isolation (single + few-parallel), reporting parent CPU and per-op latency, NOT just wall-clock; (b) a config/make-shaped micro-bench (many small fork+exec like a configure run) tracking THROUGHPUT (ops/s) and forward-progress (monotonic counter), robust to external CPU hogs; (c) a real end-to-end timer on `brew reinstall -s libunistring` to completion on a quiet box. Use these as the before/after gate for #1/#2/#3. Document that tcstorm is deprecated as a correctness gate. Nothing pushed.

title: perf #5: confirm REAL libunistring make check completes (no permanent wedge), measure end-to-end
description: VALIDATION / OPEN VERIFICATION. On pristine binaries the real `brew reinstall -s libunistring` was observed progressing through download->extract->configure (config.log monotonic)->make (0->607 .o). REMAINING: let it reach and pass `make check` (the named dar-6x4 phase) and the install, to CONFIRM end-to-end that it COMPLETES (slow, not hung) on a quiet box. Capture total wall-clock as the baseline to beat with #1/#2/#3. If — contrary to current evidence — a genuine permanent wedge appears in make check, re-open a correctness investigation on the REAL workload (not tcstorm). Build pristine. Nothing pushed.

title: perf #0: darlingserver progress+timing metrics over UNIX socket (throughput, per-RPC latency, slow-vs-wedged)
description:
PREREQUISITE for all other perf work: we cannot tell 'progressing slowly' from 'genuinely wedged', and we cannot measure the effect of any perf fix, without an in-server progress signal. (dar-l3a was misdiagnosed as a stall precisely because the external proxy 'log file size frozen' conflates slow with hung.)

DESIGN (chosen with user 2026-06-27): a dedicated listening UNIX socket beside the main one (path e.g. $DPREFIX/.darlingserver.stat.sock, or env DSERVER_STAT_SOCK). On connect, server writes a one-shot JSON snapshot of MONOTONIC counters then closes. Counters are plain std::atomic<uint64_t> incremented on the hot paths; no locks on the fast path; registered in the existing epoll loop as a Monitor so it adds no thread.

SNAPSHOT FIELDS (initial):
  uptime_s
  rpcs_serviced        (monotonic; ++ when a Call::processCall completes)
  replies_sent         (++ in MessageQueue::sendMany per datagram sent)
  checkins             (++ in Call::Checkin::processCall)
  forks                (++ on fork checkin path)
  microthread_switches (++ per microthread schedule, if cheap)
  workqueue_depth      (current _workItems.size())
  workers_busy / workers_total
  clients_blocked_in_rpc (guests currently awaiting a reply; from pending-call bookkeeping)
  last_reply_age_ms    (now - timestamp of last replies_sent)

DECISION RULE the tooling applies:
  progressing := rpcs_serviced strictly increased between two samples
  WEDGED      := counters flat for N samples AND clients_blocked_in_rpc>0 AND last_reply_age_ms large
  (a flat counter with zero blocked clients = idle, NOT wedged)

DELIVERABLES:
  1. server: atomic counters + stat socket + JSON writer (libsystem_kernel/darlingserver side).
  2. client: 'darling-stat' helper (connect+print) and a 'darling-progress-watch.sh' that samples every Ns and prints PROGRESSING / IDLE / WEDGED with the deciding fields — replaces the unreliable l3a-catch.sh 'log frozen' heuristic.
  3. RED->GREEN test (per the standing TDD rule): a fixture that (a) runs a known-progressing workload and asserts the watcher reports PROGRESSING and never WEDGED, and (b) simulates a wedge (hold the microthread worker / drop a reply) and asserts the watcher reports WEDGED within the grace window. Commit the test with the fix.

MUST run BEFORE perf #1..#4 so each of those can be measured against a faithful throughput number (supersedes the unfaithful tcstorm gate, cf perf #4).
notes: IMPLEMENTED+VALIDATED+PACKAGED+INTEGRATED 2026-06-28 (Opus), NOT pushed. Server: Metrics singleton (metrics.{hpp,cpp}) = lock-free atomic counters (rpcs_serviced/replies_sent/messages_received/checkins/forks) + log2 latency histograms (rpc/checkin/fork) -> p50/p95/p99/max. Exposed via ABSTRACT-namespace SOCK_STREAM stat socket (KEY: darlingserver runs in a private MOUNT ns so a pathname socket is host-unreachable; the NET ns IS shared, so abstract name darlingserver-stat:<prefix> works host-side). One-shot JSON snapshot on the main epoll loop. Instrumented inbox loop / sendMany / microthreadWorker(processCall) / Checkin(forks+fork_latency); WorkQueue::stats() gauges. Tools darling-stat + darling-progress-watch (PROGRESSING/SLOW/WEDGED/IDLE rule). TDD tests committed WITH fix: tests/metrics_histogram_test.cpp + tests/progress_classifier_test.py (proven RED when reverted to flat==wedged). LIVE: abstract socket reachable host-side across mnt ns; darling-stat full snapshot; darling-progress-watch=PROGRESSING through fork-heavy guest (+~4000 rpc/iv, fork p50 16us). Packaged patch darlingserver/progress-metrics.patch sha256 12532bb3 branch fix/darlingserver-progress-metrics @33c0257 base b0f99486 publication-status blocked. west patch verify=59, apply regenerated integration 9701495a1, status --strict 44 applied/0 missing. Canonical integration build darlingserver md5 c9184cd6 deployed. UNBLOCKS perf #1..#4 (measurable vs rpc/checkin/fork pXX).
close_reason: perf #0 DONE: in-server progress+timing metrics over an abstract UNIX stat socket, tools (darling-stat/darling-progress-watch), RED->GREEN tests committed with the fix, west verify=59 + status --strict 0-missing, integration regenerated, canonical build deployed. Live-validated PROGRESSING under a fork-heavy guest. Nothing pushed. Unblocks perf #1..#4.

title: perf #2a: profile WHICH dtape lock serializes MT workers (contention trace)
description: DIAGNOSTIC SPIKE feeding perf #2b/#2c. The perf #2 scope-spike proved naive DSERVER_SINGLE_THREADED=0 is ~32% SLOWER (live A/B: ST ~3830 vs MT ~2594 checkin/s) because ~all RPC work runs inside dtape big-locks and workers serialize on them (mid-storm workers_busy~0 => blocked on locks). Before committing to a lock redesign, MEASURE which lock and what fraction of time. Build the MT server (cmake -DDSERVER_SINGLE_THREADED=OFF in ~/work/darling-build; binary md5 0d64e45a already built), run perf1-multistorm.sh under it, and capture: (a) perf record -g on the darlingserver PID during the storm -> flamegraph of where worker time goes; (b) /proc/<srv>/task/*/stack or gdb thread apply all bt to see what the 10 workers block on; (c) optionally a lightweight in-server contention counter on dtape_mutex_lock/libsimple_lock (count + wait-time per named lock). Deliverable: ranked list of the top contended dtape locks (waitq vs sched vs registry vs IPC vs the lock-without-active-thread spin locks.c:59=task#53) with %time. Decides whether perf #2c (narrow that lock) is worth it or whether perf #2b (fast-path off the lock) suffices. Risk LOW (measurement only). Nothing pushed.
close_reason: Diagnostic spike done: root cause = main-loop<->worker condvar handoff (~70% CPU futex wake/wait), NOT dtape locks; redirects to #2b inline fast-path

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
DONE 2026-06-28 (Opus), NOT pushed. Profiled the MT server (0d64e45a) under 8-proc tcstorm multistorm (perf record -g, 51659 samples + gdb thread-apply-all-bt mid-storm). RESULT OVERTURNS the spike's "dtape big-lock" hypothesis.

perf self-time (top):
  48.72%  _raw_spin_unlock_irqrestore  (kernel) -- of which 25.81% = try_to_wake_up->futex_wake, and 20.47% of THAT comes straight from Server::start() via pthread_cond_signal
  ~22%    __wake_up_sync_key (more futex/socket wakeups)
   2.94%  finish_task_switch (__schedule)
   1.5%/1.0% malloc/_int_malloc
   0.66%  Call::callFromMessage    <-- actual dispatch work
   0.51%  Thread::doWork           <-- actual worker work
  dtape symbols (waitq/ipc/sched/libsimple_lock): NONE in the top -- real kernel-emulation work is <1%.

gdb snapshot (mid-storm): main thread in sendMany/__sendmmsg; ALL 8 workers parked in WorkQueue::worker -> pthread_cond_wait on the SAME _cv/_queueMutex (idle, waiting for work). NOT blocked on any dtape lock.

ROOT CAUSE (real): the bottleneck is the MAIN-LOOP <-> WORKER HANDOFF via condvar, not dtape locks. Per inbound message the single main loop does push()->pthread_cond_signal -> kernel futex_wake -> wakes a parked worker (cross-CPU, IPI, ctx-switch). The worker does sub-microsecond work then re-parks on the condvar. So ~70% of server CPU is spent in futex wake/wait + scheduling ping-pong for work that is <1% of CPU. That is why MT is ~32% SLOWER than ST: ST has NO handoff (main loop does the light work inline); MT pays the wakeup tax for nothing.

CONSEQUENCES for the other perf #2 children:
- #2d (narrow dtape locks): WRONG TARGET -- no dtape lock contention exists in this workload. DEFER/close unless a future heavier workload shows it.
- #2c (parallel dispatch / parse off main loop): parsing is only 0.66%; moving it won't help. The expensive thing the main loop does is WAKING WORKERS, not parsing.
- #2b (fast-path cheap RPCs): RIGHT DIRECTION, refined -> handle light calls (checkin etc.) INLINE on the main loop with NO worker handoff (no cond_signal/futex), so they never pay the wakeup tax. Only calls that actually block/suspend a microthread need the worker pool. This is the real perf #2 fix.

Artifacts: scratchpad/perf-mt.data, gdb-mt-bt.txt. Build reverted-to-ST + cleanup pending.

title: perf #2b: fast-path cheap RPCs (checkin/fork-checkin) off the dtape big-lock
description: DESIGN A from the perf #2 redesign analysis (recommended, LOW risk). Checkin::processCall (call.cpp:372) is itself light (notifyCheckin + _sendReply); the heavy serialization is the per-RPC trip through workqueue->microthread->dtape kernel-lock. Idea: handle a whitelist of CHEAP RPCs (checkin, esp. fork-checkin, task/host-self-trap) DIRECTLY on a path that does NOT enter the shared dtape kernel critical section / does not need a microthread+scheduler, replying immediately. PRECONDITION: prove notifyCheckin + parent-notify (fork coordination) really do not touch waitq/sched under a lock (they may, via the fork parent handshake) -- if they do, scope what minimal state they need and protect just that. Heavily overlaps perf #3 (lighten per-checkin cost). RED gate = perf1 multistorm checkin p50/p99 + aggregate rate; GREEN = lower latency, no correctness regression, brew/libunistring still builds. Live A/B vs ST baseline c9184cd6 (do not regress single-process case, cf perf #1 lesson). Build from darling-build. Risk LOW. Nothing pushed. Likely depends on perf #2a (profile) to confirm the lock is actually on this path.
close_reason: Inline fast-path: +22% throughput, ~8x p99; ships safe at default ST. Packaged e164f293, integrated 4cbf9ba5b, deployed a98c3870. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: PROMOTED to THE perf #2 fix by #2a profiling (2026-06-28), and REFINED: root cause is the main-loop<->worker condvar handoff (futex wake/wait ~70% CPU; Server::start's pthread_cond_signal alone = 20% of all server CPU). Plan: handle LIGHT calls (checkin, fork-checkin, self-traps, anything whose processCall does not block/suspend a microthread) DIRECTLY on the main event loop -- no _workQueue.push, no cond_signal, no worker wakeup. Reserve the worker pool only for calls that actually suspend (waitq/psynch/blocking IPC). Net effect: kill the wakeup tax that made MT slower than ST, and the main loop services cheap RPCs at ST-or-better latency while genuinely-blocking calls still parallelize. RED gate = multistorm checkin p50/aggregate; GREEN = beats ST baseline c9184cd6 (>3830/s) with no single-process regression + brew/libunistring intact. Build from darling-build, DSERVER_SINGLE_THREADED=0. Depends on #2a (DONE).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
DONE 2026-06-28 (Opus), NOT pushed. Implemented + RED->GREEN tested + live A/B validated + packaged + integrated + deployed.

FIX: in Server::start() the event loop now runs the call's microthread INLINE via Thread::doWork() instead of always _workQueue.push() to the worker pool. doWork() never blocks its caller (a non-blocking call runs to completion on the loop; a blocking call suspends its microthread via setcontext and resumes on the pool), so inlining is safe in both cases and only genuinely-blocking calls ever pay a worker wakeup. Added a Thread::isCurrentlySuspended() accessor + inline_handled/queued_to_pool metrics.

LIVE A/B (8-proc tcstorm multistorm, 22.6s; clean prefix; deployed to local prefix lowerdir):
  ST baseline (no fix):  ~3830 checkin/s, p50 16-32us, p95/p99 512us
  naive MT (no fix):     ~2594 checkin/s, p50 64us,    p95/p99 512us   (the regression)
  inline + ST (default): 4671 checkin/s,  p50 8us,     p95/p99 64us
  inline + MT:           4676-7295,       p50 8us,     p95/p99 64us
=> +22% throughput over ST and ~8x better p99 latency. CRUCIAL FINDING: the win comes from the inline fast-path, NOT from multithreading -- inline+ST (the DEFAULT ship config, DSERVER_SINGLE_THREADED unchanged=ON) already gets the full win; MT adds ~nothing because ~100% of calls go inline (pool nearly unused). So the patch ships SAFE with the single-threaded flag untouched, no risky MT flip needed.

Under load 99.998% of RPCs handled inline (241 of 11.27M to the pool). Boot clean (2s); 200x fork/exec/wait correctness (true=0/false=1/1000-line pipeline) PASS; server stable across many storms + 290s+ uptime, no crash/panic/deadlock.

RED->GREEN test committed WITH the fix: tests/server_inline_fastpath.c + run-server-inline-fastpath.sh -- models the two dispatch strategies over an identical tiny RPC, gates on ctx-switches/item: handoff path 2.05/item (RED fails), inline 0.00/item (GREEN passes).

Packaging: branch fix/server-inline-fastpath @6144a4bb, base 33c0257 (stacks on perf #0), patch darlingserver/server-inline-fastpath.patch sha256 e164f293, module darling/src/external/darlingserver, publication-status blocked. west patch verify=61, status --strict 0-missing. Integration regenerated 4cbf9ba5b. Canonical darlingserver md5 a98c3870 built from integration (DSERVER_SINGLE_THREADED=ON default) and deployed to darling-prefix/bin/darlingserver. Nothing pushed.

This RESOLVES the perf #2 epic goal (server dispatch no longer the bottleneck) via the cheap+safe route; the HIGH-risk #2d (narrow dtape locks) stays deferred (unneeded), #2c (parallel parse) stays P3, #2e backlog.

title: perf #2c: parallelize dispatch (callFromMessage/registration) off the main loop
description: DESIGN B from the perf #2 redesign analysis (LOW-MED risk). server.cpp:653 literally says '// TODO: this could be done concurrently'. Today the main event loop alone runs callFromMessage (parse + Call construction + process/thread registration) before pushing thread() to the workqueue -- that is the FIRST of the two serialization layers (the second is the dtape big-lock, addressed by #2c-narrow/#2b). registerIfAbsent / threadRegistry / processRegistry are already shared_mutex-guarded, so parsing+registration can plausibly move to the pool while keeping kernel-emulation single-threaded. Audit: Thread::setPendingCall race ('pending call overwritten while active', dar-gwn.6.2), _replyAddress routing, ordering guarantees per-thread (a thread's calls must stay ordered). Deliverable: dispatch concurrency without touching dtape. Measure win -- may be small if the dtape lock dominates (perf #2a tells us). RED/GREEN via multistorm; live A/B. Build from darling-build. Risk LOW-MED. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: RE-SCOPED by perf #2a profiling (2026-06-28): parsing (callFromMessage) is only 0.66% CPU, so moving dispatch off the main loop won't help. The expensive thing the main loop does is WAKING WORKERS (pthread_cond_signal -> futex_wake ~70% CPU), not parsing. The fix is to AVOID the handoff for light calls (#2b inline fast-path), not to parallelize parsing. Lower priority; revisit only if #2b leaves residual main-loop cost.

title: Stabilize repeated guest CLT compilation in combined CTest runtime
description: Full composed CTest run is not yet stable. Repro 2026-07-10: scripts/west-job.sh start --state-dir /tmp/west-job-ctest-full-20260710 -- env PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 west test --env darling --prefix-profile homebrew --ctest-timeout-seconds 420. The composed homebrew+homebrew-libplatform+homebrew-darlingserver lifecycle built/deployed system_kernel, libsystem_platform, and darlingserver. First 8 guest CTests passed; vchroot_pathnull_guard_guest then hung in WEST_GUEST_STAGE=compile for 30s and failed after 36.01s. Bundle: /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T141739Z-darling_vchroot_pathnull_guard_guest. It records only upload+compile; inner guest CLT command was killed by timeout, and runtime hashes were captured. West restored artifacts, removed source/build scratch, unmounted proc, and left no Darling processes. Investigate deterministic cause: repeated guest compiler launches, profile composition, or prefix lifecycle. Add a minimal reproducible sequence, a behavioral regression/tooling fix, and do not close dar-test-infra-sp5.3 or dar-test-infra-sp5.15 until the full composed run is GREEN.
close_reason: Profile-aware CTest lifecycles reproduce and pass the former 120-run timeout sequence with no runtime or cleanup leak.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 isolation result: the added west test --with-runtime-profile selector reproduced the same three-artifact deployment for only vchroot. Command: scripts/west-job.sh start --state-dir /tmp/west-job-vchroot-composed-20260710 -- env PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 west test --bead dar-gwn.1.6 --env darling --prefix-profile homebrew --with-runtime-profile homebrew-libplatform --with-runtime-profile homebrew-darlingserver --ctest-timeout-seconds 420. It passed in 3.00s. Therefore artifact composition alone is insufficient; the failing full run needs prior guest compiler launches. Focus on repeated guest CLT lifecycle/state, not libplatform or darlingserver deployment. The selector is covered by ctest_lifecycle_contract.py and committed in 0a53fc0.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 sequence control: an exact 9-case prefix ending in vchroot also passed under the same composed providers. Command state /tmp/west-job-vchroot-sequence-20260710 ran abort, select, darwin priority, socket, bzero, sigexc restart/default, ulock, then vchroot via CTest -R; result 9/9 GREEN, each 3s. Together with the single-case control, this excludes deterministic artifact composition and this minimal prior-compile sequence. Current classification is intermittent guest CLT/runtime flake. Next tooling work: capture guest compiler/process state before the inner timeout; do not mask it with blind retries.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 tooling note: attempted pre-kill process-group capture by replacing the guest shell watchdog path; real CTest immediately exposed that it changed transport semantics and caused empty stage failures. The experiment was removed before finalizing; only the independent output-temp cleanup fix remains in e7bc0e0. Future capture must preserve the existing darling_guest_shell transport exactly and be introduced behind a dedicated tested execution primitive.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 repeat control: standard west test --bead dar-gwn.1.6 --env darling --prefix-profile homebrew --ctest-timeout-seconds 420 --repeat until-fail:10 completed rc=0. vchroot_pathnull_guard_guest passed 10 consecutive guest upload/compile/run cycles (30.04s total, about 3s each); lifecycle restored artifacts, unmounted proc, and left no source/build scratch or runtime processes. This rules out a simple per-case repeated compiler-launch leak. The original full-suite timeout remains an intermittent failure without a deterministic reproducer.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 full mixed-queue control: scripts/west-job.sh state /tmp/west-job-ctest-full-repeat-20260710 ran env PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 west test --env darling --prefix-profile homebrew --ctest-timeout-seconds 600 --repeat until-fail:3. One composed homebrew+homebrew-libplatform+homebrew-darlingserver lifecycle built/deployed all artifacts; each of the 12 guest CTests completed upload/compile/run three times, 36/36 GREEN (108.17s). The formerly failing vchroot case was ninth in every round and passed 3.00s, 3.00s, 3.01s. Prefix was restored/unmounted and post-run checks found no runtime processes, CTest source/build scratch, or guest runner output files. This substantially weakens the suspected sequence dependency but does not explain the original timeout; keep the task open pending a stronger mixed-queue repeat control or a reproducer.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 stronger mixed-queue control failed reproducibly enough to narrow the symptom. Command state /tmp/west-job-ctest-full-repeat10-20260710 ran the same 12-case composed runtime with --repeat until-fail:10. The first 11 tests completed all 10 guest cycles (110 GREEN runs), including vchroot 10/10 in its original ninth position. The first fork_checkin_signal_storm_guest run then timed out after 51.01s. Bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T150218Z-darling_fork_checkin_signal_storm_guest: upload completed, compile emitted ORACLE_RC=0, run began but emitted no verdict; GNU timeout killed the guest transport (exit 137) at DARLING_GUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=45. Deployed libsystem_kernel hashes were 849a9436... for both locations. Lifecycle still unmounted/shut down and removed source/build scratch. This disproves the vchroot-only theory: after a long mixed queue, a later guest runtime can hang. Investigate fork checkin/runtime state and preserve an equivalent process-state capture without changing darling_guest_shell transport.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 timeout diagnostics architecture fixed. Root cause of the missing snapshot was deadline inversion: run-darling-c-test's inner guest timeout fired before darling-debug-runner's guarded deadline, so the runner only observed exit 137. add_compat_test now gives the guarded executor ACT_TIMEOUT, gives CTest cleanup grace, and gives the inner guest stage that grace only when an executor exists; bare guest execution remains self-bounded. The strengthened guarded timeout contract creates a real timed-out process and requires timeout.txt, guarded-tree, cleanup-status.txt, and a reaped PID. CMake contract asserts the generated deadline order; focused fork run confirmed generated fork values executor=45, guest stage=55, CTest=55 and passed at 4096 signal-storm iterations. This makes the next real timeout inspectable without changing darling_guest_shell transport.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 diagnostic repeat reproduced the fork hang with evidence. Full 12-case --repeat until-fail:10 reached 110 GREEN executions; fork_checkin_signal_storm_guest first run passed, second timed out at 45.23s. Bundle 20260710T152258Z-darling_fork_checkin_signal_storm_guest has guarded-tree: runner bash PID 2481798 blocked in do_wait; timeout PID 2481827 waiting in sigsuspend; darling launcher PID 2481828 blocked in poll. The guest run command had begun but stdout/stderr contain no stage/verdict because the runner process was blocked waiting for the guest transport before it could flush. cleanup-status confirms SIGTERM reaped the process group; lifecycle then unmounted prefix and removed scratch. This is a reproducible sequence-dependent runtime launch/run hang, now captured without transport changes. Next investigation should inspect the launcher/darlingserver RPC side for the poll wait and add targeted process/RPC trace evidence before changing product code.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 follow-up tooling: guarded child-tree capture proved launcher was blocked in poll but does not include detached darlingserver. Added a Darling-only debug-runner capture hook that, before terminate, snapshots prefix_processes.txt plus every process rooted at darlingserver <prefix> (ps, cmdline, status, wchan, stack, syscall). It records an explicit no-root result too. CMake contract proves registration only for guarded Darling cases; guarded timeout contract remains green. The next reproduction will expose the daemon side of the RPC/poll wait.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 daemon snapshot result: bundle 20260710T153546Z-darling_fork_checkin_signal_storm_guest captured the full prefix process tree. The guest test process (host PID 2530186) was blocked in __skb_wait_for_more_packets; its direct host child mldr PID 2530221 was already a zombie. The guest shellspawn/bash chain was still alive, while launcher timeout waited. This is therefore a missing guest RPC/socket reply after child exit, not compilation, test cleanup, or a live child. Existing dar-gwn.6.5 direct dserverdbg oracle proves the sticky checkin contract on its targeted path, but this combined-profile smoke demonstrates an unhandled runtime path still leaves a zombie plus a blocked parent. Follow-up must compare its runtime diagnostic events against the direct oracle and extend the behavioral test to this sequence before modifying product code.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 capture hygiene refinement: prefix timeout hook now uses a temporary full ps snapshot only for discovery, then stores only the selected darlingserver-rooted process lines in prefix-processes.txt. This keeps bundles focused and bounded while retaining per-PID status/wchan/stack/syscall evidence. CMake registration contract plus no-server hook smoke passed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10: reproduced a distinct CTest provider build-contract failure while moving fork_checkin_signal_storm_guest to perf-darlingserver. The disposable perf source forest compiled metrics.cpp with DSERVER_RING_TRANSPORT forced OFF by the generic runtime builder; perf metrics references the ring classification API outside that feature guard, so the build failed before deployment. Fixed test infrastructure by adding typed runtime-profile cmake-defines, conflict validation during profile composition, and declaring DSERVER_RING_TRANSPORT=true on perf-darlingserver. Focused real run now materializes perf, builds/deploys darlingserver, runs the guest test (1/1 Passed), restores the prefix, and cleans its proc mount. Next: serial combined repeat validates profile partitioning and the original timeout signature.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 resolution: replaced the invalid single-source-profile CTest lifecycle with profile-aware isolated groups. The 11 ordinary guest cases run from homebrew/homebrew-libplatform; fork_checkin_signal_storm_guest runs from the explicit perf-darlingserver provider, which materializes the A0-hang-fix stack and declares its required DSERVER_RING_TRANSPORT build setting. Full controlled command: scripts/west-job.sh start --state-dir /tmp/west-job-ctest-full-profile-partition-20260710 -- west test --env darling --prefix-profile homebrew --ctest-timeout-seconds 600 --repeat until-fail:10. Result rc=0: 110 homebrew guest executions and 10 perf fork executions, all GREEN; each group deployed/restored independently, proc was unmounted after each group, and final ps/mount/scratch audit was clean. The old timeout signature does not reproduce on the correct runtime contract.

title: Darling broad deploy/build gate fails in dyld/objc4 before focused regression gates
description: While validating q95.29.1/q95.29.2/dar-rpc-correctness-z27x.8 on 2026-07-07, focused targets in /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-build passed, but broad deploy/install gates failed outside the touched code. west darling-build --deploy --force failed linking src/external/dyld/dyld with: ld: chained binds not implemented yet in l_reltable._ZN9libunwind12UnwindCursor... from src/external/libunwind/libunwind_static.a(libunwind.cpp.o). A broad ninja install/strip also reached objc4 and failed at src/external/objc4/runtime/objc-runtime.mm:128: #error mismatch in debug-ness macros. Need a stable deploy/repro path that can update only touched closure dylibs and avoid unrelated broad rebuild failures, or fix the broad gate so it is deterministic.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Follow-up from the same validation pass: west darling-build now has focused deploy support: --deploy-closure-names, --no-deploy-dyld, and --skip-post-doctor. This made q95 validation possible without broad dyld/objc4 rebuild. The broad deploy failures remain real and still need root-cause/fix.

title: west test: bound Darling shutdown during E-UNION prefix checks
description: During E-UNION prefix self-check work, west test could hang after cleanup because _shutdown_runtime_prefix invoked darling shutdown without a timeout. Repro observed while running: west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/eunion-7-activation.patch --env darling --prefix existing:/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs. No darling/darlingserver process or mount remained visible, but the west/rtk command did not return until interrupted. Fix: bound darling shutdown with WEST_TEST_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_SECONDS defaulting to 15s, then continue forced process/mount cleanup.
close_reason: Fixed and verified. _shutdown_runtime_prefix now bounds darling shutdown with WEST_TEST_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, and E-UNION runtime self-check no longer uses capture_output pipes that darlingserver can inherit and hold open. Re-running west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/eunion-7-activation.patch --env darling --prefix existing:/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs now returns quickly with the expected E-UNION self-check failure instead of hanging.

title: Darling getattrlist missing ATTR_CMN_NAME -> Ruby Dir.glob returns empty (breaks Homebrew empty_installation? + all glob)
description:
Cross-process directory-entry coherence bug surfaced as the final blocker for Homebrew source builds (dar-pk4).

REPRO (via brew, but it's a pure VFS issue):
- A long-lived process (brew/ruby) creates an empty dir D (formula.prefix / keg) and stats it.
- A forked+exec'd child (make install) writes files into D/{bin,lib,include,...}.
- The child exits; the parent re-reads D (Pathname.glob('D/*')) and sees it STILL EMPTY.
- A FRESH process (find/ls run after) AND the host filesystem both see all the files correctly.

EVIDENCE: with Homebrew's failure-rollback disabled (HOMEBREW_KEEP_FAILED_KEG), the lz4 keg on disk is fully populated (bin/lz4 238KB, lib/liblz4.1.10.0.dylib 153KB, headers, man, pkgconfig) and visible to a fresh process + host — yet brew's parent raised 'Empty installation'. A simpler fs-probe showed that child/exec'd/nested writes to /usr/local/Cellar DO persist and are visible to the SAME process tree's later ls; the bug specifically affects a process that cached/stat'd the directory as empty BEFORE another process populated it (stale per-process dir cache, no invalidation on external modification).

IMPACT: blocks every Homebrew --build-from-source install at the post-make-install emptiness check. Worked around in the test prefix with HOMEBREW_FRESH_KEG_CHECK=1 (re-check keg via fresh subprocess) to get a green install, but the proper fix is VFS-level dir-cache invalidation/coherence in darlingserver.

NEXT: locate where Darling caches directory contents per-process (vfs/dcache in darlingserver or libsystem) and invalidate on external writes / disable stale caching for bind-mounted prefix paths.
notes: Surfaced while proving dar-pk4 Option B (from-source brew install). Final blocker after SDKSettings.json + oso_prefix gate + NO_SANDBOX fixes. Belongs under epic dar-gwn (Make Homebrew usable under Darling).
close_reason: Fixed: implemented getattrlist ATTR_CMN_NAME + ATTR_CMN_OBJTYPE in libsystem_kernel (xnu d157c3f, darling 7df268d2d). Ruby Dir.glob + Homebrew empty_installation? now correct; full from-source brew install green with no workaround.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
REPRO NUANCE — minimal C repro does NOT reproduce. A single process that does opendir/readdir on an empty dir, then a child (system()) writes a file + subdir, then the SAME process opendir/readdir again → SEES the 2 new entries correctly (both /tmp and /usr/local/Cellar). parent stat nlink also updates 2->3. So the bug is NARROWER than 'a process that cached a dir can't see another process's writes' — plain opendir/readdir is coherent.

So the trigger is something more specific in the Homebrew/Ruby path. Candidates to test next:
- Ruby Dir.glob/Pathname.glob (may use a different syscall path / fts / caching) vs raw readdir.
- The keg dir is created by parent via mkpath then the BUILD child is a deep fork+exec chain (sh -> make -> install); maybe depth or an exec boundary matters.
- A retained open DIR*/fd or fts handle from an earlier glob in the long-lived ruby process.
- darlingserver microthread/vchroot state specific to the long-lived process.
Repro harness: /tmp/vfs-repro.sh (C). Next: write a Ruby repro using Dir.glob/Pathname.glob mirroring formula_installer empty_installation? exactly, and an fts-based C repro.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE FOUND — dar-e1j is MISDIAGNOSED. This is NOT a VFS cross-process coherence bug. It is a missing getattrlist attribute in Darling's libsystem_kernel emulation.

CHAIN OF EVIDENCE (all via darling-debug-runner against darling-prefix-homebrew-test):
1. Multi-scenario C readdir test (pre-existing/negative-lookup/keg-subtree/retained-DIR*): ALL coherent. readdir/getdirentries works perfectly cross-process. So no coherence bug.
2. Ruby repro of empty_installation?: Dir.entries(keg)=full (readdir OK) but Pathname.glob/Dir.glob(keg/*)=[] EMPTY. Same process, same instant. => Dir.glob is broken, not coherence.
3. Dir.glob returns 0 for EVERY dir (/usr/bin=534 entries, /bin=47) regardless of freshness. Confirms general breakage.
4. Both portable-ruby 4.0.5 AND system ruby 2.6.3 fail identically. Not version-specific.
5. Pattern: Dir.glob("/*")=14 OK; Dir.glob("*",base:"/usr/bin")=534 OK; but Dir.glob("/usr/*")=0, Dir.glob("/bin/bash") literal=0 while File.exist?=true. => fails on every LITERAL path component, works when only "*" / direct base.
6. Ruby's dir.c replace_real_basename() calls getattrlist(path, ATTR_CMN_NAME, FSOPT_NOFOLLOW) on each literal component (Darwin case-preservation path, taken because Darling reports volume case-insensitive). On failure it treats the component as not-found -> glob returns [].
7. C test: getattrlist(path, ATTR_CMN_NAME) => EINVAL for every path. getattrlistbulk => ENOTSUP. fdopendir/openat/fstatat/chained-relative-openat ALL work.

EXACT DEFECT: src/external/xnu/darling/src/libsystem_kernel/emulation/include/xnu_syscall/bsd/helper/xattr/getattrlist_generic.c
  line 18: #define COMMON_SUPPORTED (ATTR_CMN_FNDRINFO | ATTR_CMN_OBJTAG)   <-- no ATTR_CMN_NAME
  line 77-78: if ((commonattr & COMMON_SUPPORTED) != commonattr) return -EINVAL;  <-- rejects ATTR_CMN_NAME
Template is used by sys_getattrlist / sys_getattrlistat (HAS_PATH=1) and sys_fgetattrlist (HAS_PATH=0). Userland shim, not kernel/duct-tape.

IMPACT: GLOBAL, far beyond Homebrew. Any macOS program using Dir.glob / glob(3)-with-realcase / getattrlistbulk / NSDirectoryEnumerator is affected. The Homebrew "Empty installation" was just one symptom; the HOMEBREW_FRESH_KEG_CHECK workaround masked it. Fixing ATTR_CMN_NAME removes the need for that hack.

FIX: implement ATTR_CMN_NAME in getattrlist_generic.c — pack an attrreference_t + the basename string (basename of the supplied path for HAS_PATH; readlink /proc/self/fd/N for the fd variant). Also fixes the precondition for un-stubbing getattrlistbulk (which additionally needs ATTR_CMN_RETURNED_ATTRS). Volume case-sensitivity (ATTR_VOL_CAPABILITIES, _PC_CASE_SENSITIVE=11) is also unsupported (returns EINVAL) — secondary.

Repro scripts: /tmp/run-vfsr2.sh, /tmp/run-rbvfs.sh, /tmp/run-galb.sh (getattrlistbulk ENOTSUP), /tmp/run-fdo.sh, /tmp/run-at.sh, /tmp/run-gal.sh (getattrlist ATTR_CMN_NAME EINVAL).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
RESOLVED + FIXED + VALIDATED.

Final root cause (supersedes the earlier "VFS coherence" and "ATTR_CMN_NAME-only" theories):
Darling's getattrlist emulation (libsystem_kernel getattrlist_generic.c) supported only
ATTR_CMN_FNDRINFO|ATTR_CMN_OBJTAG and returned EINVAL for any request containing another
attribute. The Darwin build of Ruby's dir.c globber (USE_NAME_ON_FS_REAL_BASENAME, always on
for macOS) resolves every LITERAL path component through replace_real_basename(), which calls
  getattrlist(path, {ATTR_CMN_NAME | ATTR_CMN_OBJTYPE}, ..., FSOPT_NOFOLLOW)
and on failure marks the component path_noent with NO lstat fallback. So EINVAL made
Dir.glob/Pathname.glob return [] for any pattern with a literal component (/usr/*, /bin/bash,
glob("bin") in cwd), while pure-"*" or base:-relative globs worked. Keg#empty_installation?
uses Pathname.glob, so a freshly-built keg always looked empty -> "Empty installation" abort.

Why the earlier fix attempt (ATTR_CMN_NAME only) didn't work: Ruby requests NAME *and* OBJTYPE
in the same attrlist; the combined request still failed the COMMON_SUPPORTED mask check.
Confirmed via strace: the failing /usr/* glob made only one visible syscall, lstat("/usr")=dir
(unrelated); the getattrlist EINVAL is invisible because it returns before issuing any Linux
syscall. Working /* differs because its only component is the magic "*" (no literal component).

FIX (committed): implement both attributes in getattrlist_generic.c.
- xnu submodule: d157c3f "libsystem_kernel: implement getattrlist ATTR_CMN_NAME + ATTR_CMN_OBJTYPE"
- darling parent: 7df268d2d "Update xnu: ..."
ATTR_CMN_NAME packs an attrreference_t + basename (path basename, or /proc/self/fd/<fd> for the
fd variant). ATTR_CMN_OBJTYPE stats the entry and returns the fsobj_type_t. Buffer field order
(name-ref, objtype, then name string in the variable area) matches replace_real_basename.

VALIDATION (libsystem_kernel.dylib rebuilt + deployed to homebrew-test prefix + libexec):
- C getattrlist(path, ATTR_CMN_NAME) now returns "usr"/"bin"/"clang" (was EINVAL).
- Ruby Dir.glob == Dir.entries for /usr/bin (534), /bin (47), Homebrew (225); literal /bin/bash
  => ["/bin/bash"]; /usr/* => 9. Both ruby 4.0.5 and 2.6.3.
- empty_installation? mirror => false (was true) with Pathname.glob listing bin/include/lib.
- FULL from-source build green WITHOUT HOMEBREW_FRESH_KEG_CHECK:
  "brew install --build-from-source lz4" -> lz4 1.10.0, 24 files, built in 28s, binary runs,
  compress/decompress roundtrip OK. (Also moved Homebrew from Tier 3 to Tier 2 - SDK now detected.)

FOLLOW-UPS (separate, not blocking):
- getattrlistbulk still stubbed ENOTSUP; with ATTR_CMN_NAME done it can now be implemented
  (also needs ATTR_CMN_RETURNED_ATTRS). Affects NSDirectoryEnumerator/bulk dir reads.
- Volume case-sensitivity attrs (ATTR_VOL_CAPABILITIES, _PC_CASE_SENSITIVE=11) still EINVAL.
- The glob fix EXPOSED a stale test-prefix SDK symlink (MacOSX10.13.sdk -> MacOSX.sdk while
  SDKSettings.json says 11.3) that tripped Homebrew's check_broken_sdks once all_sdks could be
  globbed. Fixed in the test prefix by replacing it with MacOSX11.sdk. If Darling ships a CLT
  SDK layout, the bundled versioned symlink name must match the SDK's SDKSettings version.

Repro scripts: /tmp/run-gal.sh (getattrlist), /tmp/run-rbvfs.sh (empty_installation?),
/tmp/trace-glob3.sh (strace working-vs-failing diff), /tmp/run-lz4-nohack.sh (end-to-end).

The homebrew profile exports 10 clean topic branches with source commits, Bead/PR provenance, SHA256 checksums, deterministic git-am integration commits, top-level gitlinks, and a frozen profile lock. title: DCC6 guest cache builder must use dyld-visible filesystem metadata
description: DCC2/DCC6 valid-cache guest gate currently builds the cache on the host from prefix/libexec/darling, but dyld validates staleness with dyld3::stat('/usr/lib/...') inside Darling. On 2026-07-09 the cache opened successfully but the reader hard-failed immediately: stale source '/usr/lib/system/libsystem_blocks.dylib' (inode/mtime/size changed). Need a reproducible tool path that builds or normalizes DCC source metadata against the exact guest-visible filesystem view used by dyld (likely prefix root selection, no-overlay/union semantics, or a guest-side stat/build helper), then rerun valid-cache + stale-cache RED/GREEN. Do not count dyld/dcc2-reader.patch as fully covered until this valid-cache gate is green.
close_reason: fixed

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Evidence from west test run: after deploying profile dyld and building smoke2 DCC cache into prefix/libexec/darling/private/var/tmp, guest dyld opened the cache but aborted with: stale source '/usr/lib/system/libsystem_blocks.dylib' (inode/mtime/size changed), then DCC2: cache invalid/stale. This proves path visibility is fixed but staleness metadata is built from the wrong filesystem view. Next investigation should compare host stat of prefix/libexec/darling/usr/lib/system/libsystem_blocks.dylib vs guest dyld3::stat('/usr/lib/system/libsystem_blocks.dylib') and decide whether the builder must run in guest, consume a guest-stat manifest, or normalize metadata for no-overlay/union roots.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Fixed the filesystem-view root cause in west dcc-cache: the cache builder now uses install-root=guest-visible, which resolves to DPREFIX when DARLING_NOOVERLAYFS=1 and DPREFIX/libexec/darling otherwise. Focused DCC run showed the reader no longer rejects the cache as stale: dyld enabled DCC2, substituted cached libsystem_blocks and libunwind, and applied 83 fixups. The next blocker is a different runtime hang after cache fixups, tracked as dar-6hri.

title: west test: E-UNION upper fixtures were deleted by Darling preinit
description: Root cause found while validating dar-r7fc.4.1: west prepared E-UNION upper fixtures under prefix/private/var/tmp before starting Darling, but Darling preinit wipes /var/tmp on server boot. That made the guest smoke falsely fail as if E-UNION upper-over-lower was inactive. Fix: for darling-eunion-prefix, west now creates the .union-work marker, shuts down stale runtime, boots the prefix once so preinit runs, prepares lower/upper fixtures after that cleanup, verifies the live E-UNION probe, keeps the same server alive through the actual guest test, and shuts down/cleans fixtures after the test.
close_reason: Implemented and verified active E-UNION runtime prefix support, west fixture lifecycle fix, guest path/direnum/exitpath gates, and cleanup audit. Checks: py_compile, run-west-test-metadata-contract, west patch check, west patch export --check, E-UNION guest selectors green.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Fixed in west_commands/test.py: E-UNION fixtures are now created after Darling preinit, not before it can wipe /var/tmp. Verification: py_compile, metadata contract, west patch check/export --check, eunion activation/path/exitpath guest selectors, and cleanup audit all pass.

title: libunwind/dyld: define static-runtime no-jump-table codegen policy
description: Architectural follow-up from dar-rpc-correctness-z27x.9. The libunwind static no-jump-tables workaround has patch-local red metadata, but the upstream-quality architectural target is a documented dyld/static-runtime codegen policy rather than a one-off flag. Acceptance: identify which dyld/static-runtime objects require no jump tables and why; encode that as a target-specific build policy; keep libunwind/static-no-jump-tables-for-dyld.patch red/build gate green; add a small policy regression if another static-runtime target can regress the same relocation class.

title: west test: source-base guest runtime RED proof runner
description: Need a west test runner mode that can prove RED for guest/runtime tests against a bad source-base tree, not just host/source fixtures. Current runtime tests can run GREEN in a Darling prefix, but --prove-red cannot build/deploy or otherwise select the old libsystem_kernel/darlingserver bits for the same guest test. This blocks replacing remaining XNU syscall/signal MODEL tests with honest RUNTIME coverage. Acceptance: a metadata test can declare a guest/runtime source-base proof; west materializes/builds/deploys or otherwise isolates the bad runtime, runs the current test asset and observes RED, then restores/runs GREEN on current runtime; cleanup must leave no darlingserver/prefix/worktree processes behind.
close_reason: guest-runtime-deploy runner is implemented and proven by real bad-runtime RED/current-runtime GREEN canary for xnu/getattrlist-name-objtype.patch.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08 partial infra step: added explicit red-proof.mode=guest-runtime-deploy metadata model for runner: guest-c-fixture. west patch check now validates the intended deploy-artifact declaration via runtime-artifacts, and west test --prove-red fails before profile materialization/prefix use with a clear not-implemented message pointing back to dar-facb. This does not implement bad/fixed runtime deploy yet; it prevents agents from encoding deployed guest RED proof as fake source-base proof while the real runner is still pending.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08 partial infra step: tightened guest-runtime-deploy runtime-artifacts schema so each artifact must declare module, build-targets, and deploy paths, and west test --prove-red --list now prints the deploy plan. This keeps future bad/fixed runtime RED metadata explicit while the actual isolated build/deploy runner remains pending.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08 implementation step: west test now has a guest-runtime-deploy runner path instead of the previous not-implemented failure. It creates a temporary bad source forest, configures/builds declared targets in a temporary CMake/Ninja build dir with the selected prefix as CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX, backs up declared deploy paths, deploys bad artifacts, requires the guest fixture to fail, restores artifacts, then runs GREEN on the current prefix. Added contract coverage for deploy/restore ordering and symlink-parent safety. Remaining before closing: run at least one real guest-runtime RED proof against an actual Darling prefix/build to validate the source forest + CMake build path outside mocks.

comments.author: ilyagulya
