Runner successfully passed source-base compile RED/GREEN, configured a temporary Darling source forest, built system_kernel+dyld in a temporary CMake/Ninja build dir, and deployed bad libsystem_kernel.dylib+dyld into both prefix runtime copies. Bad guest run timed out as RED. After restore, GREEN guest also timed out; a separate normal GREEN guest run on the current prefix also fails. Bundle stderr shows prefix boot/ns failure (overlay symlink loop without --no-overlayfs, then mnt namespace missing with --no-overlayfs). Created blocker dar-vqr9 and made dar-facb depend on it; do not close dar-facb until a real RED/GREEN guest canary passes.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08 acceptance evidence: guest-runtime-deploy runner implemented and real canary passed after fixing prefix blockers. Command: west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/getattrlist-name-objtype.patch --red-only --prove-red --prefix existing:/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs. Runner materialized temp profile/source forest, built bad system_kernel + dyld, deployed both declared artifacts to prefix root and base tree, observed RED rc=1 for the guest fixture, restored artifacts, then ran current runtime GREEN. Cleanup audit after run: no darlingserver/mldr/launchd/shellspawn processes, no prefix mounts, no west-red-proof/west-profile temp dirs, stale .init.pid removed by updated cleanup helper.

title: build drift: move policy into west workspace tooling with optional CMake guard
description: Architectural follow-up from dar-rpc-correctness-z27x.9. The build-drift gate currently lives in CMake as a workspace-aware policy guard. Target shape: west/workspace tooling owns the primary policy, while CMake remains an opt-in or defensive guard for local builds. Acceptance: design the ownership split; keep west patch status/export/check gates authoritative; add or update a west contract proving drift is detected before deploy/export/build workflows; document when CMake should warn/fail and how packagers can opt out intentionally.

title: Fix guest-runtime-deploy for host-install darlingserver artifacts
description: guest-runtime-deploy currently deploys bin/darlingserver into the data prefix and, after adding launcher-sibling deployment, can make RED fail for an infrastructure reason: Cannot open mnt namespace file / missing /proc/<init>/ns/mnt before the guest fixture runs. The root issue is that darlingserver is a host-install artifact launched from INSTALL_PREFIX/bin/darlingserver, not an ordinary prefix payload. The runner needs an architecture-correct deploy model for host-install artifacts, probably building with the installed launcher prefix or installing into an isolated host-install worktree, then proving that RED/GREEN failures are behavior-level and not prefix bootstrap failures. Acceptance: stable repro command for microthread runtime proof; RED matcher fails if namespace/bootstrap fails instead of target symptom; GREEN passes after host-install artifact deploy; no leftover processes or modified global install binary after failure.
close_reason: Fixed by the host-install runtime deploy repairs already landed: bin/darlingserver is deployed to launcher-sibling install targets, mldr/libsystem_kernel host-install deploy targets are covered, NO_REPLY stale reply desync was fixed, and focused pushreply guest-runtime-deploy RED/GREEN passed with intended ORACLE_RC=1/PUSHREPLY_BAD_POINTER_OK evidence. Verified with runtime_red_contract.py, patch export --check, patch check, red-audit, patch verify, and cleanup audit; west no longer accepts namespace/bootstrap failure as this RED reason.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-09 update: narrowed host-install runtime proof failure. Added runner stale .init.pid cleanup after namespace failure and host-install mldr deploy targets for guest-runtime-deploy. pushreply proof now materializes matched dserver+mldr+libsystem_kernel and deploys mldr to test prefix plus host install (/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix/usr/libexec/darling/mldr and libexec/darling/usr/libexec/darling/mldr). This fixes the earlier namespace-only failure and wrong mldr deploy path. Remaining blocker: RED still dies during upload with NO_REPLY RPC skew: guest receives stale replies number=25/17 for set_executable_path/set_dyld_info before task_self_trap. Preserved scratch: /tmp/west-red-proof-runtime-p5oaw6gb. In that scratch, generated dserver rpc.internal.h has NO_REPLY no-op for SetDyldInfo and SetExecutablePath, so the next investigation is to find which actually-used artifact/path is still stale or whether deploy/restore is not replacing the dserver/mldr used by the launched guest. Do not accept WEST_GUEST_SHELL_RC=137 or BAD RECEIVE MESSAGE as RED for pushreply; it is still pre-oracle.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Update: pushreply runtime RED/GREEN is now valid after fixing two infrastructure issues. First, the old invalid RED was caused by a NO_REPLY RPC desync: Call::callFromMessage used a generic sendErrorReplyFromHeader(-EAGAIN) when setPendingCall detected an active pending call, which emitted a short reply even for generated NO_REPLY setter calls. That stale reply was then consumed by the next task_self/mach_reply_port call, so west correctly rejected the RED as the wrong failure reason. Fixed in the no-reply patch by replying through result->sendBasicReply(-EAGAIN), which is a no-op for NO_REPLY classes and still replies for normal calls. Second, exported darlingserver patch metadata had stale source-base values after branch rewrite; psynch-rpc-arg-trace, checkin-rwlock-across-suspend, and pot-guest-lifetime-binding were exporting unrelated earlier commits into their patch files, which broke GREEN current-profile materialization. Repaired source-base metadata, re-exported, and added west patch export preflight coverage so a linear stack stale source-base now fails before writing patch files. Verified: focused west test --profile homebrew --patch darlingserver/pushreply-pipe-leak.patch --prefix-profile homebrew --prove-red passed; RED bundle contained ORACLE_RC=1 and 'push_reply pipe did not close after bad pointer'; GREEN bundle contained PUSHREPLY_BAD_POINTER_OK and ORACLE_RC=0. Also passed runtime_red_contract.py, run-west-patch-export-preflight-contract.sh, west patch export --profile homebrew --check, west patch check --profile homebrew, west test --profile homebrew --red-audit, and west patch verify --profile homebrew. Cleanup audit after proof found no Darling processes, mounts, or west-red/green proof scratch tails.

title: Make handoff bundle packing idempotent
description: Repeated west dw handoff rewrites tracked handoff bundle files even when exported branch heads did not change. Repro: run west dw handoff, commit or amend the bundle updates, then run west dw handoff again; root, darlingserver, and xnu bundles become dirty again. Root cause: scripts/handoff.py deletes the output directory and recreates git bundle files; git bundle output is not byte-stable enough to use as an idempotence signal. Fix pack to keep an existing bundle when git bundle list-heads matches the branch heads being exported, and only replace bundles when exported heads actually change.
close_reason: Fixed scripts/handoff.py pack to preserve existing bundles when exported heads are unchanged; verified west dw handoff no longer rewrites bundle files in a dirty workspace.

title: Capture dar-gwn.1.11 fix-v2 intermittent server crash via in-server fault tracer
description: dar-gwn.1.11 fix v2 (psynch_mtxcontinue grant-stash) introduces an INTERMITTENT server crash (~2 CRASH / 4 valid storm runs; canonical c9ef337b = 0/6). Server goes defunct, dserver.log empty => raw SIGSEGV/abort, NOT a duct-tape panic. gdb-attach PERTURBS timing so it will not reproduce under gdb. Need an IN-SERVER fault tracer (instrument darlingserver Thread::processSignal / dtape_thread_process_signal to dump the faulting host-thread backtrace + the duct-tape thread/kwq state at fault time, written to dserver.log) to capture the crash bt without perturbing timing. Then root-cause the likely turnstile/owner bookkeeping conflict (original grant set kw_owner + turnstile_update_inheritor; psynch_wait_complete tears it down; retry re-sets owner) and land a non-crashing fix. See memory darling-storm-oracle-harness, darling-debug-runner. Parent dar-gwn.1.11. Nothing pushed.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Fault tracer BUILT + deployed (darlingserver.cpp: async-signal-safe SIGSEGV/ABRT/BUS/FPE/ILL handler, DSERVER_FAULT_TRACE=1 -> dumps host backtrace via backtrace_symbols_fd to $DPREFIX/private/var/log/dserver-fault.log + stderr, on altstack, chains to SIG_DFL for core. Dropped -rdynamic: it breaks the duct-tape link (hidden section$end$__DATA$__percpu); will resolve offsets with addr2line against the unstripped build binary instead). Build 0f92c20abb50 = fix-v2 + waitq diag + tracer.

CAPTURE RESULT (catch-fwl.sh, 12 iters, ROUNDS=250 storms, fault tracer armed): 12/12 NO CRASH. Storms genuinely ran the fork/exec load each iter (ROUND 0->~200 printed) but were truncated at the ~220s watch cap (never reached STORM_DONE). So fix-v2's earlier '~2 CRASH/4 runs' did NOT reproduce in 12 real storm runs. The earlier tally was n=4 (very noisy) and/or tied to prior build/teardown state. Re-running a proper A/B with COMPLETING storms (ROUNDS=200, WATCH=320s): canonical c9ef337b control first (must be 0-crash/all-complete to validate harness), then fix-v2. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
CRASH CAUGHT + ROOT-CAUSED via the in-server fault tracer (no gdb, no timing perturbation). A/B: canonical c9ef337b = 0/6 crash; fix-v2 (0f92c20abb50) = 1/8 crash (7 clean + 1 SIGSEGV on iter 8).

CAPTURED BACKTRACE (signal=11), resolved with addr2line vs the unstripped build binary:
  microthreadWorker
   -> Call::PsynchMutexwait::processCall
    -> dtape_psynch_mutexwait -> _psynch_mutexwait -> ksyn_wait (kern_synch.c)
     -> waitq_assert_wait64_leeway -> waitq_assert_wait64_locked  <-- SIGSEGV
Faulting instruction (objdump): 0xc4118 'push 0x8(%r11)' inside the dtape_log_error("DARGWN-WAITQPANIC ...") varargs setup, where __builtin_return_address(1) walks *rbp->0x8(%r11).

VERDICT: the SIGSEGV itself is a SELF-INFLICTED ARTIFACT of my own waitq.c:2835 diagnostic — __builtin_return_address(1) is unsafe on the ucontext microthread stack (shallow/broken FP chain). FIXED: dropped the 2nd-level unwind from the diagnostic (ret0 only).

THE REAL BUG (what the artifact exposed) = fix-v2 is INCOMPLETE. dserver.log right before the fault:
  DARGWN111-STASH kwq=0x703b1c14bef0 lockseq=0x2c000 retval=0x2c003 intr.count=0
  PSYNCH: psynch_mutexwait: returning 0 lseq in mutexwait with no EBIT
  -> next ksyn_wait trips waitq_assert_wait64_locked 'thread->waitq != NULL' (leftover-waitq) invariant.
So fix-v2's grant-stash hands the grant back, but (a) the retry mishandles it ('returning 0 lseq with no EBIT') and (b) the thread carries a STALE thread->waitq into the next assert_wait (the duct-tape thread_unblock quirk: it sets wait_result + resumes but does NOT clear TH_WAIT/waitq the way XNU does). This CONFIRMS the waitq.c:2835 panic, the ETIMEDOUT cvwait, and the recvmsg deadlock are all the SAME race, and that the earlier '~2/4 fix-v2 crash' was this exact diagnostic-induced SEGV on the leftover-waitq path, NOT a turnstile/owner bug.

NEXT (dar-gwn.1.11 fix work, separate from this capture bead): in psynch_mtxcontinue's grant-landed branch, also clear the thread's stale waitq/TH_WAIT (or fix _kwq_handle_interrupted_wakeup so the retry consumes the stash instead of re-asserting wait). Capture goal of dar-fwl = DONE. Nothing pushed.

title: Darling teardown not robust to non-graceful death: leaves mount-ns / overlay / procfs mounts that poison the next boot
description: Type: bug

title: west patch status reports false APPLIED -> patchset drift not caught (caused dar-gwn.6 + dar-gwn.1.9/1.11 regressions)
description: ROOT CAUSE of why we keep silently regressing fixes (asked by user 2026-06-25). west patch status reported 'APPLIED  darlingserver/assert-wait-cancel-stale-timer.patch [dar-gwn.1.9]' and 'status: 37 applied, 0 missing', BUT the patch FORWARD-APPLIES CLEANLY to integration/homebrew HEAD 1ace361 (proven via git apply --check), i.e. its content (the 'leftover from a PRIOR wait' cancel block in duct-tape/xnu/osfmk/kern/waitq.c waitq_assert_wait64_locked) is ABSENT from the integration branch we actually build+deploy from. So 'APPLIED' is a FALSE POSITIVE: the detector does not verify the patch content is present in the checked-out integration tree; it trusts patches.yml registration / tracked state / clean-apply-to-upstream instead. Consequence: deployed darlingserver 437407b2 (built from integration/homebrew) silently regressed dar-gwn.1.9 -> stale wait_timer fires THREAD_TIMED_OUT on a later untimed wait -> bogus ETIMEDOUT (ruby [BUG] crash) AND stranded psynch_mutexwait handoff (the dar-gwn.1.11 deadlock). Same drift CLASS as dar-gwn.6 (xnu ulock-wait-eintr-retry 9d5c222 was missing from integration too). WHY ABSENT: integration/homebrew HEAD 1ace361 (=exc-fatal-reply-bound) predates assert-wait-cancel-stale-timer.patch (patch+commit 7344b17 dated Jun 24 15:16); the integration branch was NOT regenerated after that patch was added to patches.yml line 329 -> west never re-applied it, and patch status did not flag the gap. FIX NEEDED: (1) make 'west patch status' verify content presence in the integration tree (forward-apply --check == NOT applied), not just registration; (2) regenerate integration/homebrew so it actually contains every registered patch; (3) a pre-deploy guard that greps the built binary / source tree for each fix's signature. Evidence: git apply --check forward-applies; git show integration/homebrew:duct-tape/.../waitq.c has 0 occurrences of the cancel block. Nothing pushed.
close_reason: Resolved: patch status was root-caused as process drift, unconditional west patch status --strict build gate shipped, and current workspace verifies 0 missing patches.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: CORRECTION + EXACT cause (proven 2026-06-25): west patch status is NOT lying — _patch_state (west_commands/patch.py:303) classifies each patch against the CURRENT WORKING TREE of the repo (reverse-applies clean => APPLIED present; forward-applies clean => MISSING absent). Proven: with the assert-wait fix in the worktree -> 'APPLIED'; stash it (= the exact state integration/homebrew @1ace361 and the deployed binary 437407b2 were in) -> flips to 'MISSING, status: 36 applied, 1 missing ... build tree drifted from patches.yml'. So the detector WOULD have caught it. The real failure is PROCESS: nobody ran 'west patch status [--strict]' against the integration tree BEFORE building+deploying darlingserver 437407b2 (and likewise before building libsystem_kernel for dar-gwn.6). The integration branch was checked out/generated missing the patch, the binary built from it, deployed, and the drift gate was never run. SAME for both regressions. FIX = make the drift check a MANDATORY pre-build/pre-deploy gate (run 'west patch status --strict' in the build+deploy recipe; fail the build on any MISSING/NOFILE). Secondary hardening: regenerate integration/homebrew so its committed tree actually contains every registered patch (right now @1ace361 the committed tree is MISSING assert-wait-cancel-stale-timer even though patches.yml line 329 registers it), so a fresh checkout isn't born drifted. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: RESOLVED (drift closed at source) 2026-06-25, with user approval, NOTHING PUSHED: regenerated integration/homebrew locally via 'west patch apply --roll-back' (after 'west patch verify' = 49/49 clean, and after stashing the two manual worktree edits so _ensure_clean passed). New committed tips: darlingserver 1ace361 -> 448d96b, xnu ac4e340 -> 8886216, darling 904eaf17c -> 3217657a1; wrote patches/homebrew/west.lock.yml. VERIFIED the two previously-drifted fixes are now in the COMMITTED integration trees (not just worktree): darlingserver HEAD waitq.c has the assert-wait cancel block (7344b17), xnu HEAD ulock_wait.c has the EINTR-retry (dar-gwn.6). 'west patch status' = 37 applied / 0 missing against the now-clean worktrees (== committed branches). Rebuilt darlingserver from the clean branch -> md5 c9ef337b, BYTE-IDENTICAL to the manual-edit build, proving the branch reproduces the fix; deployed canonical. Pre-regen tips saved at ~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/pre-regen-tips.txt for rollback. REMAINING (process gate, task #60): wire 'west patch status --strict' into the build/deploy recipe so a drifted tree fails the build before deploy. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: UNCONDITIONAL GATE SHIPPED 2026-06-25 (nothing pushed). User: 'он должен безусловно запускаться, а не надеяться на твою память.' Wired the drift check INTO the build so it cannot be bypassed: edited darling/CMakeLists.txt to add (1) configure-time execute_process(west patch status --strict) -> message(FATAL_ERROR) on drift; (2) an always-out-of-date 'drift_gate' custom target in ALL; (3) add_dependencies(darlingserver system_kernel drift_gate) so even a TARGETED 'ninja darlingserver'/'ninja system_kernel' runs the gate first. PROVEN both ways: reverse-applied the assert-wait patch in the worktree to create a clean MISSING -> 'ninja darlingserver' => 'FATAL ERROR: 1 missing ... build tree drifted from patches.yml', build stopped exit 1; restored -> build links exit 0. Opt-out -DDARLING_SKIP_DRIFT_GATE=ON for packaging/CI; if west/patches.yml absent it warns + proceeds. Made the gate ITSELF drift-proof: canonical fix branch fix/build-drift-gate @ fc45343a2 (base 5f2d7401), patch patches/homebrew/darling/build-drift-gate.patch sha256 5ae8fae2, registered in patches.yml [bead dar-gax, publication-status blocked]. west patch verify=50 (was 49). Regenerated integration/homebrew so the gate is in the COMMITTED darling tree (tip 84622ef20); west patch status=38 applied/0 missing. Deployed server c9ef337b. Also added a standalone helper predeploy-drift-gate.sh. dar-gax effectively RESOLVED (root-caused + unconditional guard shipped); leave open only until #60 housekeeping closed. Nothing pushed.

title: Implement getattrlistbulk (was ENOTSUP)
description: Follow-up to dar-e1j. Darling's getattrlistbulk (BSD syscall 461) returned -ENOTSUP, so fts-based tools and NSDirectoryEnumerator silently fell back (or failed). Now implemented in libsystem_kernel.
close_reason:
Implemented in xnu 6159147 / darling 4b504ae86.

getattrlistbulk now iterates the directory via getdents64 and packs each entry in the canonical layout: u32 length, optional ATTR_CMN_RETURNED_ATTRS attribute_set_t, then requested attrs in ascending-bit order. Supported: ATTR_CMN_NAME, OBJTYPE, OBJTAG, FNDRINFO, ERROR, ATTR_DIR_ENTRYCOUNT, ATTR_FILE_RSRCLENGTH. Object type from dirent d_type with fstatat fallback for DT_UNKNOWN. '.'/'..' skipped. Entries that don't fit are left for the next call by rewinding to the last packed entry's d_off cookie via lseek, so iteration spans multiple calls losslessly.

When the caller requests ATTR_CMN_RETURNED_ATTRS (mandatory per man page; fts/NSDirectoryEnumerator do), unsupported attrs are omitted and reported via the returned set instead of EINVAL. Without it, strict fixed-layout + EINVAL on unsupported.

VALIDATED (darling-debug-runner, both prefixes): bulk count == readdir count for /usr/bin (534 across 4 calls -> proves lseek-rewind has no dup/miss), /bin (47), /usr (9), /usr/lib (161/2 calls), /etc (51), / (16), empty dir (0). Object types REG/DIR/LNK correct. Fixed-layout (no RETURNED_ATTRS) path OK. Graceful-omit path: requesting unsupported ATTR_CMN_CRTIME succeeds, bit not set in returned set. Repro: /tmp/run-galbulk.sh, /tmp/run-galbulk2.sh. LOCAL COMMITS ONLY (not pushed).

title: West: add patch selector to quality check
description: west patch export supports selecting one patch, but west patch check rejects the same selector and only audits the full profile. Add a clear single-patch selector or an explicit helpful error with the supported filter syntax; cover both forms with a command contract so focused metadata validation is discoverable and reproducible.

title: Build a DETERMINISTIC RED test for dar-gwn.1.11 grant-vs-interrupt race + run full metrics storm
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
GOAL: replace the stochastic fork-storm oracle with a DETERMINISTIC RED test that triggers the dar-gwn.1.11 grant-vs-interrupt race on demand (no random waiting), AND run the full metrics storm to quantify/capture the residual crash. Two deliverables below.

=========================================================================
THE BUG (root-caused, confirmed via in-server fault tracer dar-fwl):
=========================================================================
A guest thread blocked in psynch_mutexwait is GRANTED the mutex by a concurrent
psynch_mutexdrop -> ksyn_signal -> waitq_wakeup64_thread(THREAD_AWAKENED). If a signal
(SIGCHLD from the fork storm) is delivered in the exact window AFTER the grant made the
thread runnable but BEFORE its psynch_mtxcontinue continuation runs, dtape_thread_sigexc_enter
clobbers wait_result THREAD_AWAKENED -> THREAD_INTERRUPTED. Consequences cascade into THREE
faces of the SAME race (all confirmed same root):
  1. lost-RPC recvmsg deadlock (grant lost, userspace re-blocks on an unlocked mutex)
  2. waitq.c 'thread already waiting' panic (stale thread->waitq on the retry's ksyn_wait)
  3. ETIMEDOUT cvwait [BUG] (stale wait_timer)

=========================================================================
EXACT CODE LOCATIONS (all in darling/src/external/darlingserver, worktree-only, NOT pushed):
=========================================================================
* duct-tape/pthread/kern_synch.c:
  - psynch_mtxcontinue ~L811-846: fix-v2 grant-stash. error!=0 + kwe NOT on queue => grant
    landed; converts consumed grant to a kw_intr stash via _kwq_mark_interruped_wakeup (L834).
    Diagnostic DARGWN111-STASH at L831.
* duct-tape/src/thread.c:
  - dtape_thread_sigexc_enter L452+: STALE-WAITQ FIX (after clear_wait_internal, if
    thread->waitq still !=NULL, waitq_pull_thread_locked it, else direct field-clear). This
    ELIMINATED crash-face #2 (0 panics across ~22 storms). Metrics: dtape_dargwn_* counters +
    DARGWN111-RACE (logs when sigexc clobbers AWAKENED) + DARGWN111-LEFTOVER (logs the stale-waitq
    clear). Gated by env DSERVER_DARGWN_TRACE=1.
* src/thread.cpp:
  - doWork L516-528: throws 'Thread has both a pending call and a pending continuation' =
    the RESIDUAL crash (SIGABRT) the stale-waitq fix EXPOSED. Diagnostic DARGWN111-CALLCONT at
    L519 dumps pendingCall#, pendingCallOverride, handlingInterruptedCall, interrupts.size.
    The interrupt re-home block is L506-514 (only fires when pendingCall IS InterruptEnter &&
    !pendingCallOverride). Interrupt machinery: _handleInterruptEnterForCurrentThread L1603.
* duct-tape/xnu/osfmk/kern/waitq.c:
  - waitq_assert_wait64_locked L2834 'thread already waiting' panic; DARGWN-WAITQPANIC diag
    L2843. NOTE removed __builtin_return_address(1) (it SEGV'd on the ucontext microthread
    stack -> that was the 'fix-v2 crash' artifact, see dar-fwl).
* src/darlingserver.cpp:
  - install_fault_tracer L575 / dserver_fault_handler L559: async-signal-safe SIGSEGV/ABRT
    backtrace dumper -> $DPREFIX/private/var/log/dserver-fault.log. Gated by DSERVER_FAULT_TRACE=1.

CURRENT BUILD: 7b674434e3a9 (all of the above + metrics) at darling-build/src/external/darlingserver/darlingserver, deployed to darling-prefix/bin/darlingserver. Resolve fault offsets with: addr2line -f -C -e <that binary> 0xOFFSET. Do NOT add -rdynamic (breaks duct-tape link: hidden section$end$__DATA$__percpu).

=========================================================================
DELIVERABLE 1: run the FULL metrics storm (currently NOT run — stopped before launch)
=========================================================================
cd ~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro
SRVBIN=~/work/darling-build/src/external/darlingserver/darlingserver \
OUT=~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/FULL-metrics.out \
ITERS=10 ROUNDS=250 NWORK=12 WATCH=200 bash catch-fwl.sh    # catch-fwl.sh already passes DSERVER_FAULT_TRACE=1 DSERVER_DARGWN_TRACE=1
Then grep dserver.log for DARGWN111-RACE / DARGWN111-LEFTOVER / DARGWN111-STASH / DARGWN111-CALLCONT
counts to quantify race frequency, and if a fault is caught, addr2line the dserver-fault.log bt.
Each iter ~3min (full storm, fresh boot/iter). This QUANTIFIES the residual call-cont crash rate
and confirms the stale-waitq fix holds (expect 0 DARGWN-WAITQPANIC).

=========================================================================
DELIVERABLE 2: build the DETERMINISTIC RED test (the real ask)
=========================================================================
WHY the stochastic repro is bad: the fork-storm (sleep_stress2.rb) hits the race by luck
(~1 call-cont / ~22 storms; STASH ~6/run). FAILED fast-repro attempts (see dar-gwn.1.11
comments): synthetic Ruby Mutex contention (red_psynch_race.rb v1/v2/v3) NEVER reached the
kernel psynch path (0 STASH, 647M userspace os_unfair_lock fast-path increments) because Ruby
Mutex stays in userspace unless the hold is long + a real waiter queues in-kernel. The HYBRID
(hybrid111.sh: persistent server + sleep_stress2.rb at ROUNDS~70) got iter time to ~70s but
is still stochastic.

APPROACH for a TRUE deterministic RED (no random): drive the race from INSIDE darlingserver
with an in-server unit/integration harness that does NOT need a guest boot:
  (a) Set up a duct-tape ksyn_wait_queue + two duct-tape microthreads. Thread A blocks in
      ksyn_wait (KSYN_QUEUE_WRITE). Thread B calls ksyn_mtxsignal/ksyn_signal to GRANT A
      (this runs waitq_wakeup64_thread -> A becomes THREAD_AWAKENED, dequeued).
  (b) BEFORE A's psynch_mtxcontinue continuation is scheduled, synchronously call
      dtape_thread_sigexc_enter(A) -> deterministically clobbers AWAKENED->INTERRUPTED.
      This is the EXACT window; no timing luck. Assert: A's grant is stashed as kw_intr
      (DARGWN111-STASH path), A->waitq is cleared (no leftover), and A does NOT reach
      doWork's L516 throw. RED (pre-fix) = waitq panic / stale waitq / lost grant; GREEN = stash
      hands the grant back on retry.
  (c) For the call-cont (crash-face that survives the waitq fix): reproduce the
      _continuationCallback + _pendingCall coexistence by injecting a non-InterruptEnter
      pendingCall on a thread that still holds a continuation (the L506 re-home only handles
      InterruptEnter). Pin via DARGWN111-CALLCONT which path sets it.
  Look at existing in-server test scaffolding: duct-tape/xnu/osfmk/kern/test_lock.c,
  test_mpsc_queue.c (patterns for driving duct-tape kernel primitives directly). The microthread
  driver is DarlingServer::Thread::doWork / microthreadWorker (src/thread.cpp); kernelAsync()
  schedules a kernel microthread. psynch shim hooks: duct-tape/src/psynch.c
  (shim_psynch_wait_wakeup L597 = the grant primitive). 
  ALTERNATIVE if in-server proves too invasive: a minimal C guest program (NOT ruby) that
  pins 2 threads to one pthread_mutex with a controlled hold + raises SIGCHLD via a self-pipe
  at a deterministic point relative to the unlock — tighter control than ruby, may force the
  kernel path the synthetic ruby couldn't. Verify STASH fires (DSERVER_DARGWN_TRACE=1) as the
  acceptance gate: a RED test that does not produce >0 STASH is NOT exercising the bug.

ACCEPTANCE: the RED test must (1) fail deterministically on canonical c9ef337b /
pre-stale-waitq-fix builds, (2) pass on the fixed build, (3) run in seconds, (4) require zero
retries. Wire it as the dar-gwn.1.11 regression oracle.

HARNESS/TRAP NOTES: see memories darling-storm-oracle-harness (pkill corrupts mount-ns; teardown
via 'darling shutdown'; track guest via pgrep mldr; the catch-fwl.sh recover()) and
dar-fwl-fault-tracer-and-finding (fault tracer usage, addr2line not -rdynamic). Env clean now
(0 srv/0 mldr). NOTHING PUSHED — all fixes worktree-only.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
DONE (2026-06-26). Both deliverables completed; conclusion supersedes the original framing.

DELIVERABLE 1 (full metrics storm): ran catch-fwl.sh ITERS=10 ROUNDS=70 NWORK=12 on build 7b674434e3a9 (fix + DARGWN111-RACE/LEFTOVER/STASH/CALLCONT metrics). Result: 9/10 iters clean +1 watch-timeout, NO crash. RACE=6646 LEFTOVER=0 STASH=0 WAITQPANIC=0 ETIMEDOUT=0 CALLCONT=0.

DELIVERABLE 2 (deterministic RED): the user rejected the in-server approach as not faithful (it bypasses real microthread scheduling + RPC + signal timing). Built instead a guest-side C RED: red111.c (N threads on a DEFAULT pthread_mutex -> real kernel __psynch_mutexwait + each thread forks short children = SIGCHLD storm on contending threads) + red111.sh harness (acceptance gate RACE>0 || STASH>0; RED=crash/wedge, GREEN=clean+race-exercised). Host-syntax-clean. Ruby Mutex was confirmed unusable (os_unfair_lock fast path, STASH=0).

But the A/B control on dar-gwn.1.11 made the RED moot: built DSERVER_DARGWN_FIXOFF gate (binary 3c2d7c7f6af6), ran 10-iter storm in BOTH arms -> IDENTICALLY clean (fix-ON RACE=6646, fix-OFF RACE=7073, 0 crashes, LEFTOVER=0 both). Plus source proof: the grant nulls thread->waitq atomically (waitq_select_thread_locked->thread_clear_waitq_state), so the stale-waitq face cannot arise. => dar-gwn.1.11 is NOT a residual bug on the current build; the patch was dropped as redundant (user decision). A RED targeting this race therefore has nothing to catch.

Artifacts kept in ~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/: red111.c, red111.sh, catch-fwl.sh, catch-fwl-fixoff.sh (the A/B variant). Nothing pushed.

title: Make Homebrew usable under Darling (nhs)
description: Umbrella for getting Homebrew (portable Ruby 4.0.5) working under Darling on host nhs. Several root fixes already landed on branch fix/homebrew-psynch-ruby-hang (psynch cvwait, xnu psynch args, select/pselect fdset, CLT Perl bzero, LibreSSL ECC). Remaining: a flaky deadlock during 'brew install --force-bottle wget'.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
2026-06-10 Homebrew status update after re-running on nhs against the fixed darlingserver (suspend/resume permit, md5 dd95758b):
- brew --version: OK.
- 'brew install --force-bottle wget': COMPLETES cleanly, no hang. Fetches API source wget.rb (network OK), then exits with 'no bottle' (macOS 11 Tier 3). The earlier download stall is no longer reached on this path.
- 'brew fetch wget': still HANGS. Reproduced the ORIGINAL idle-server lost-RPC-reply signature (darlingserver idle = epoll + parked worker; ruby blocked in recvmsg on .darlingserver.sock; sibling thread busy-spins; no live curl). So a RESIDUAL server-side lost-reply hang remains; the suspend/resume fix reduced but did not eliminate it. Tracked in dar-gwn.2 (full repro details there). NOT a network stall.
Net: Homebrew is much closer (psynch/timer/suspend-resume hangs fixed, network works) but 'brew install/fetch' is still not reliably completable until dar-gwn.2 is resolved.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Tooling: added 'darling-debug-runner --attach-strace <pgrep-pattern>' — host-side poller attaches strace -f (sendmsg/recvmsg) to a target the instant it appears, and detaches (SIGINT flush) before any gdb capture (single-tracer constraint). Needs kernel.yama.ptrace_scope=0 for same-uid attach to container procs (mldr runs as uid 1000); wrapper toggles+restores it. This is what pinned the orphan RPC for dar-gwn.2.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-10 end-to-end validation after dar-gwn.2 fixes: brew fetch --retry jq completes successfully under Darling (FETCH_RC=0), bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260610T165718Z-brew-fetch-jq-validation. The remaining wget timeout was isolated to ftpmirror.gnu.org redirecting to externally unresponsive mirror.accum.se; identical host curl times out. Closed dar-gwn.3 as false positive. Homebrew fetch path is now usable; wget-specific validation must wait for a responsive GNU mirror or use ftp.gnu.org directly.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
STATUS 2026-06-11 — Homebrew source builds essentially working under Darling.

POURED bottles: 'brew install ca-certificates' green end-to-end (dar-pk4 Option A).
FROM-SOURCE: 'brew install --build-from-source lz4' green end-to-end (xz also compiles+links+installs). Tier moved 3 -> 2 (SDK now detected).

Darling-side root fixes landed this session (local commits on fix/homebrew-psynch-ruby-hang, NOT pushed):
- getattrlist ATTR_CMN_NAME + ATTR_CMN_OBJTYPE — fixes Ruby Dir.glob / Homebrew empty_installation? (dar-e1j, closed). xnu d157c3f / darling 7df268d2d.
- getattrlistbulk implemented (was ENOTSUP) — fts/NSDirectoryEnumerator (dar-getattrlistbulk-xjz, closed). xnu 6159147 / darling 4b504ae86.
- SDK: SDKSettings.json (75437f43b) + versioned symlink MacOSX10.13.sdk -> MacOSX11.sdk (3c79b03fa) so MacOS.sdk detects + check_broken_sdks passes.
- sandbox: tracked sandbox-exec(1) pass-through (f85f220f0) — removes the HOMEBREW_NO_SANDBOX workaround; stock Homebrew sandbox path verified green.

REMAINING for out-of-box source builds: just the toolchain -oso_prefix issue (CLT ld64-305 too old). Tracked in dar-gwn.4 (CLT upgrade) and dar-pk4 (full analysis + option A: use Darling cctools ld). This is an environment/CLT-version matter, not a Darling source bug.

Parent commit chain (this session, local): 7df268d2d, 4b504ae86, 3c79b03fa, f85f220f0 on top of 75437f43b.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
STATUS 2026-06-11 (later) — CLT toolchain blocker RESOLVED; remaining blocker re-scoped to the flaky psynch hang.

CLT UPGRADE DONE (dar-gwn.4, closed): installed CLT 13.2 (clang-1300/ld64-711) into darling-prefix-homebrew-test via Darling's OWN native installer (installer -pkg <flat .pkg> -target /; host / visible inside as /Volumes/SystemRoot; no 7z/pbzx hacks). Validated: hello-world compile+link+run green, -oso_prefix accepted by ld64-711, Homebrew version-floor passed (floor keys on emulated MacOS.version=11 -> needs CLT>=12.5; 13.2 clears it), lz4 --build-from-source green. => the -oso_prefix gate is now a NO-OP on ld64-711 and can be dropped; it was never a Darling source bug. CLT 11.5 ruled out (supports -oso_prefix but fails the macOS-11 >=12.5 floor, reports as 11.7 'too outdated').

RE-SCOPED REMAINING BLOCKER: out-of-box from-source builds are blocked NOT by toolchain but by the intermittent psynch/kwq lost-wakeup deadlock = dar-gwn.1 (IN_PROGRESS). Characterized on CLT 13.2: 6 clean lz4 rebuilds in one session -> 5 PASS / 1 HANG (~17%); hang point floats (after checksum-verify / at 'API Source lz4.rb'), forked build child wedges before spawning any subprocess. Confirmed deployment current (darlingserver 5bd7a59b, libsystem_kernel eea332e6, libsystem_pthread c5f3541d all md5-identical build==both prefixes; no shared-cache shadow), so dar-gwn.2's 3 psynch layers REDUCED but did not eliminate it. Root cause + fix locus already documented in dar-gwn.1 (ksyn_wqfind kwq identity/lifecycle in kern_synch.c; pr-drafts/darling-psynch-kwq-lost-wakeup-findings.md). Repro: /tmp/run-lz4-flaky.sh.

NET: poured + from-source builds SUCCEED when the flaky hang doesn't trigger; toolchain/SDK/sandbox/getattrlist all done. The ONE thing between here and reliable 'brew install --build-from-source' is dar-gwn.1. No new Darling source commits this round (CLT work was prefix-side only); prior session commit chain unchanged (7df268d2d, 4b504ae86, 3c79b03fa, f85f220f0 on 75437f43b).

comments.text: dar-gwn.5 (the flaky lz4/brew --build-from-source hang = raw-fork glibc stack-cache-lock deadlock): candidate fix IMPLEMENTED + DEPLOYED but UNCOMMITTED (src/startup/mldr/mldr.c, child-side reset of GL(_dl_stack_cache_lock) in __mldr_postfork_child). lz4 round1 5/5 clean, 0 hang; round2 (14 trials) validating in background.
