comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
LEG B — CONCLUSIVE CAPTURE + CONSOLIDATED ROOT CAUSE (2026-06-21)

Got a clean capture after discovering & killing a ROGUE leftover watcher (`watch-buildrb2.sh`,
running ~3.5h from an old session) that was host-ptracing every build.rb the instant it spawned —
THAT is why all prior Leg B gdb attaches failed with "already traced by <pid>". With it gone, the
v2 catcher attached cleanly and caught the fault, and the ruby [BUG] crash-report (C-level
backtrace) landed in the same boot.

SYMBOLIZATION FIXED (was the other blocker): the guest loads dylibs from $DPREFIX
(darling-prefix-homebrew-test/usr/lib/system/...), a DIFFERENT, separately-stripped file from the
build/deploy copy under darling-prefix/libexec/darling/. nm'ing the build copy gave garbage
offsets (the "dyld_shared_cache mismatch" symptom — there is NO cache). nm the ACTUAL loaded file;
slide == KBASE (the r-xp map base). Verified by locating _linux_syscall's code signature.

EVIDENCE (one boot, brew install libunistring):
- gdb at the fault: si_signo=11, si_code=0 (SI_USER), si_addr=0x3e800000396=(uid1000<<32)|pid,
  si_pid=918, si_uid=1000. RIP=_linux_syscall+9 (the `ret` after `syscall`; insns confirm ret;nop).
  Faulting-thread bt symbolized: _linux_syscall+9 <- _sys_wait4_nocancel+0x43 <- _sys_wait4+0x1e
  <- __darling_bsd_syscall+0x32 (<- ___wait4). Thread cleanly parked in wait4; NOT inside a handler.
- ruby [BUG] crash report (same boot): "[BUG] Segmentation fault at 0x000003e800000395", and the
  C backtrace PROVES the sigexc delivery route:
    sys_kevent64 -> kevent64_impl -> linux_kevent_wait -> sig_restorer -> sigexc_handler+0x15c
    -> handler_linux_to_bsd+0x39a -> ruby sigabrt
  So sigexc IS the active signal path (it's installed in EVERY guest via dyld init, not
  ptrace-only — darling_sigexc_self() is called unconditionally from dyldInitialization.cpp:159).

CONSOLIDATED ROOT CAUSE — the three "flavors" are ONE cascade:
1. (Leg B trigger) A thread parked in wait4/kevent64 receives a signal that arrives as SIGSEGV
   (SI_USER) carrying SIGCHLD's {pid,uid}. Ruby's SEGV handler -> [BUG] -> abort.
2. (server death, NOT covered by Leg A) In the same window an RPC reaches Thread::setPendingCall
   (thread.cpp:279) with a call already pending (non-InterruptEnter) -> throws
   std::runtime_error("Thread's pending call overwritten while active"). This is thrown from
   Call::callFromMessage (call.cpp:237), invoked DIRECTLY in the main event loop Server::start()
   (server.cpp:541) with NO try/catch. Leg A only wraps processCall in microthreadWorker/kernelAsync,
   so it does NOT catch this -> std::terminate -> darlingserver dies (log: "terminate called after
   throwing ... what(): Thread's pending call overwritten while active").
3. (dar-pot) server death orphans the guest tree — observed 14 mldr left; then
   semaphore_timedwait -111 + "dserver_rpc_interrupt_enter failed ... handler=sigexc linux_signum=6".

FIX SURFACE (two independent legs; NOTHING implemented/pushed yet):
- Leg A-extension (closes the remaining server-exit + dar-pot leak): guard the event-loop call
  site server.cpp:541 (wrap callFromMessage in try/catch), or make setPendingCall tolerate an
  overwrite instead of throwing. This is the path Leg A's processCall guard does not reach.
- Leg B core (stop the spurious SEGV): sigexc signal-number selection — dserver_rpc_sigprocess /
  dtape_thread_process_signal SI_USER echo (duct-tape/src/thread.c:277-284 echoes the guest's
  bsd_signal_number) + interrupt_enter/exit reply routing racing with a pending call. Same family
  as dar-gwn.1.

NEXT (for user decision): implement Leg A-extension first (small, high-value: stops server death +
dar-pot, and likely converts the fork-storm crash into a recoverable error reply), then tackle Leg B
core. Nothing pushed; host swept clean (dsrv=0, mldr=0); ptrace_scope restored to 1.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
LEG A-EXTENSION SHIPPED (2026-06-21) — event-loop callFromMessage guard

Extended the processcall-exception-guard patch to cover the THIRD (and, for the leg-B cascade,
the load-bearing) server-killing throw site: Call::callFromMessage is invoked directly in the
main event loop Server::start() (server.cpp:541) with no try/catch. Under the fork-storm, the
guest hits Thread::setPendingCall's pending-call-overwrite case -> throws std::runtime_error
("Thread's pending call overwritten while active") -> escapes the event loop -> std::terminate ->
server dies -> dar-pot guest leak. Leg A's processCall guards (microthreadWorker/kernelAsync) do
NOT cover this path. Now wrapped: log + drop the one offending message, server survives.

CHANGE: fix/processcall-exception-guard commit amended 23101838 -> a52a15e (now touches
src/thread.cpp, src/call.cpp, src/server.cpp). Patch regenerated:
  patches/homebrew/darlingserver/processcall-exception-guard.patch
  sha256 ed5884e70b296f1d12ac889b3f3358692987d49c7391eff3eccb37546d0dc357
patches.yml updated (sha + source-commit + blocker note); pr-draft updated (3 sites, before/after).
`west patch verify` -> verified 43 patches. Deployed darlingserver md5 4457fc29 (correct prefix,
backup at bin/darlingserver.pre-eventloop-guard.bak). publication-status stays `blocked` (leg B
core still open). Nothing pushed.

VERIFICATION: one clean before/after captured on `brew install libunistring`:
  pre-fix  = "terminate called ... Thread's pending call overwritten while active" + dead server
             + 14 orphaned mldr (dar-pot reproduced).
  post-fix = same leg-B SEGV trigger fires, but server STAYS ALIVE, no terminate, clean boot exit.
Non-regressive across repeated boots (0 zombie darlingserver, 0 post-cleanup mldr leak).
Caveat: the overwrite race is flaky -> did NOT separately catch the guard's "Dropping message"
log firing in a controlled boot; justification = the one before/after + static proof this is the
only uncaught throw site on the event-loop path. NOTE harness flakiness: the `darling` launcher
returns before the detached guest tree finishes, so multi-boot supervised loops are unreliable
for timing-sensitive before/after; the single supervised boot is the reliable unit. Also found &
killed a ROGUE leftover `watch-buildrb2.sh` (running ~3.5h) that host-ptraced every build.rb on
spawn -- that was why ALL prior leg-B gdb attaches failed "already traced by <pid>".

NEXT (this session, per user "A then B"): leg B CORE -- stop the spurious SI_USER SIGSEGV at the
source (sigexc signal-number selection / dserver_rpc_sigprocess / dtape SI_USER echo +
interrupt_enter/exit racing a pending call).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
LEG B CORE — sigprocess-rewrite theory DISPROVEN (live trace, 2026-06-21)

Added a temporary always-on Log line to Call::Sigprocess::processCall printing the in/out signal
numbers ([legb] SIGPROCESS in: bsd=.. linux=.. code=.. addr=.. -> out newBSD=..). Built+deployed
(darlingserver md5 8920f777, verified: running exe == traced binary, server stderr == the boot log).
Booted `brew install libunistring`: the SEGV fired ([BUG] Segmentation fault at 0x3e800000393) but
the WHOLE boot logged ZERO SIGPROCESS lines.

=> The spurious SIGSEGV does NOT go through dserver_rpc_sigprocess. The "server rewrites bsd_signum
to 11" model (in the older writeup + dar-gwn.1.5 leg-B framing) is WRONG. Trace reverted; deployed
binary restored to the clean Leg A+ext (4457fc29).

What the ruby crash report DOES show (same boot): ruby is in Process.wait (CFUNC :wait,
formula.rb:3560), inside the DOWNLOAD/fetch path (resource.rb / mktemp.rb / abstract_download_strategy),
waiting on the download child. It receives a SEGV, then "SEGV received in SEGV handler" (nested).
The earlier boot's register context had rax=-4 (EINTR) at the wait4 syscall-stub ret. So: a child
dies, wait4 is interrupted (EINTR), and the thread takes a SEGV around the wait/restart -- without
sigprocess being involved.

REVISED Leg B locus candidates (need a NEW instrumentation round, NOT sigprocess):
  (a) the real-Linux SIGCHLD handler path: handler_linux_to_bsd_wrapper -> dserver_rpc_interrupt_enter
      -> handler_linux_to_bsd -> (ruby SIGCHLD handler) -> dserver_rpc_interrupt_exit. The
      interrupt_enter/exit RPCs are the racy bit (dar-gwn.1 family) -- instrument THESE.
  (b) wait4 EINTR/ERESTART returning corrupted state to ruby that it then derefs.

Note SIGCHLD is NOT handled by sigexc_handler (it's excluded; it uses handler_linux_to_bsd_wrapper),
which is consistent with sigprocess never firing.

STATE: Leg A-extension shipped + verified (server survives the cascade; closes the dar-pot leak path
Leg A missed). Leg B core still open with a corrected model. Host clean (dsrv=0, mldr=0),
ptrace_scope=1. Nothing pushed. Per user "A then B" -- A done, B core needs the interrupt-RPC
instrumentation next.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
LEG B CORE — CONCLUSIVE ROOT CAUSE (2026-06-22)

The trigger SEGV is a GENUINE strcpy(dst, NULL) -> strlen(NULL) in a freshly-forked ruby build.rb
child during the libunistring stage/system() path. NOT spurious, NOT darlingserver signal injection.

Captured by instrumenting darlingserver to read the faulting guest's OWN thread_state + stack at
fault time (Thread::processSignal reads rip@+128/rsp@+56 via proc->readMemory; resolves vs
/proc/<hostpid>/maps while still mapped) + dtape reg dump. Symbolized against the loaded files (no
trap: DPREFIX upper lacks libsystem_platform/_c so guest loads the symbol-bearing lower build copy):

  RIP    = libsystem_platform.dylib +0x6084 = _platform_strlen+0xa4
           insn `movq (%rax,%rcx),%r8` with rax=0  ->  fault linux=11 code=1 (SEGV_MAPERR) addr=0x0
           (the server-side code/addr are authoritative; the old "si_code=SI_USER" was a gdb
            stale-siginfo-union misread).
  caller = libsystem_c.dylib __strcpy_chk+0x18 -> _stpcpy+0x18 -> strlen.  (__strcpy_chk = the
           _FORTIFY_SOURCE expansion of strcpy; src arg was NULL.)
  ctx    = dyld symbol-binding machinery on the stack: ImageLoaderMachOCompressed::
           exportedSymbolAddress / findShallowExportedSymbol, ImageLoader::trieWalk, resolveTwolevel.
           => strcpy(NULL) during dyld launch-time two-level-namespace symbol resolution in the
           forked+exec'ing child. (Linear stack scan has stale slots, e.g. libsystem_kernel+0x391fb
           lands mid-instruction; the dyld frames are the coherent signal. Exact strcpy source line
           needs a clean rbp-unwind/gdb.)

SECONDARY CASCADE (separate defect, downstream): child death -> SIGCHLD to PARENT ruby procs is
delivered MIS-TAGGED as linux=11 SI_USER carrying the SIGCHLD payload in si_addr=(uid<<32|childpid)
=0x3e8000002fX (parents parked in wait4, rax=-4 EINTR, rip=libsystem_kernel _linux_syscall ret
+0x2ba05). darlingserver dserver_rpc_sigprocess is a FAITHFUL pass-through (11->11) -- NOT the bug.

STOCHASTIC: per boot, flavor-B (the strcpy SEGV, ~1-in-2-3) or flavor-C (hang:
semaphore_timedwait -111 after a parked thread's INTERRUPT_ENTER never gets its reply). Same race.

REPRO: `brew reinstall --build-from-source libunistring` (plain install SKIPS -- dep in Cellar);
DSERVER_LOG_LEVEL=info + HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1; server log persistent at
$DPREFIX/private/var/log/dserver.log; loop boots until a FAULTSYM code=1 appears.

STATE: all [legb] traces reverted from src + duct-tape; deployed darlingserver restored to clean
Leg A+ext md5 4457fc29 (0 legb strings). Host swept. Nothing pushed. fix/processcall-exception-guard
@ a52a15e (Leg A-ext, no upstream). NEXT (the actual fix): find the strcpy(NULL) site via a clean
gdb unwind (now feasible -- rogue watch-buildrb2.sh killed) in the dyld symbol-resolution path; the
strcpy is in libc CODE reached from dyld, so it is Darling-side (dyld/libc), not ruby-side.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
RESCOPED (2026-06-22): this bead is now P2 ONLY — the SECONDARY signal-delivery defect. The PRIMARY
trigger (the actual brew-build crash) turned out to be a forked-child strcpy(NULL)->strlen SEGV during
dyld symbol-binding and was split out to dar-gwn.1.6. The HANG outcome of the same race was split to
dar-gwn.1.7. All the earlier theories in THIS bead's older comments (darlingserver sigprocess injection;
sigreturn ucontext corruption; "spurious SI_USER") are SUPERSEDED — see dar-gwn.1.6 for the conclusive
root cause and memory brew-sigchld-segv-rootcause.

P2 SCOPE (this bead): after a build child dies (from the P1 strcpy SEGV) and is reaped, the SIGCHLD to
the PARENT ruby procs is delivered MIS-TAGGED as linux=11 (SIGSEGV) with si_code=0 (SI_USER), carrying
the SIGCHLD payload in si_addr=(uid1000<<32 | child_pid)=0x3e8000002fX. The parents are parked in wait4
(rax=-4 EINTR, rip=libsystem_kernel _linux_syscall ret +0x2ba05). darlingserver dserver_rpc_sigprocess
is a FAITHFUL pass-through (11->11, proven by trace) -- so the mistag happens CLIENT-SIDE before
sigprocess, in the signal-number conversion / delivery path (signum_linux_to_bsd / the SIGCHLD
real-Linux handler handler_linux_to_bsd_wrapper -> interrupt_enter/exit). DOD: a real SIGCHLD is
delivered to the parent AS SIGCHLD (not 11), even while the parent is parked in wait4 during the fork
storm. NOTE: P2 only matters once P1 (dar-gwn.1.6) is fixed (no child crash -> no anomalous SIGCHLD);
P1 is the blocker for green brew. Lower priority than P1.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
NEW EVIDENCE (2026-06-24, via dar-gwn.6 work): with the dar-gwn.6 ulock corruption-abort SIGILL fixed, the SAME fork/exec storm (sleep_stress2.rb RUNS=8 NWORK=10 ROUNDS=600) now kills ruby at ROUND ~250 with a clean capture of THIS bead's mis-tag bug. Env-gated trace in sigexc_handler captured the delivered signal exactly:

  [dargwn6] FATAL sig: linux_in=8 bsd_out=8 si_code=0 si_addr=0x3E8000014C6 si_pid=5318 handler=0x0

= SIGFPE(8), si_code=0 (SI_USER => a SENT/resent signal, NOT a hardware FPE trap), payload is SIGCHLD-shaped: si_addr=(uid1000<<32 | child_pid 0x14C6=5318), handler=0x0 (SIG_DFL -> default action terminates the process silently, no Ruby [BUG]). linux_in==bsd_out==8 so darlingserver/sigexc did NOT change the number; the signal ARRIVES as 8 from Linux. So a reaped-child SIGCHLD is being delivered to the guest tagged as a DIFFERENT fatal signal (here SIGFPE; earlier captures showed SIGSEGV/SIGILL variants) carrying the SIGCHLD payload. This is the long-standing dar-gwn.1.5 reaped-child-signal-mistag, now cleanly reproducible and observable via the [dargwn6] sigexc trace (linux_in/bsd_out/si_code/si_addr/si_pid/handler). Repro + trace harness: ~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/ (sleep_stress2.rb + run-once.sh; sigexc.c TEMP trace). NOT YET FIXED. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: RESOLVED 2026-06-25 — NOT A DISTINCT BUG. Chased the SIGCHLD-as-fatal-SI_USER signature with an env-gated [dargwn15] trace at sigexc_handler entry + after dserver_rpc_sigprocess. Captured crash: the FIRST fatal event was a GENUINE SIGILL (si_code=2 ILL_ILLOPN, rax=-1) symbolized to __os_unfair_lock_lock_slow+0x13f (the ud2 after __os_unfair_lock_corruption_abort) = EXACTLY dar-gwn.6. The si_code=0 SIGCHLD-payload SI_USER lines that follow are CASCADE: Ruby's crash handler + sigexc's 'emulating default signal effects' self-resend kill(0,sig) (sigexc.c ~L383, which stamps si_code=0 SI_USER) re-entering. The historical 'mistag' readings were the death rattle of the ulock ud2, not a separate signal-delivery defect. Confirms the 2026-06-21 disproof (darlingserver never injects sig 11). dar-gwn.6 had silently REGRESSED because libsystem_kernel was built off integration/homebrew (HEAD ac4e340), which lacks the fix commit 9d5c222. With the fix restored: fork-storm RUNS=4 NWORK=12 ROUNDS=400 -> RUN1/2/3 each STRESS2_OK rc=0, 0 SIGILL, 0 mistag. Closing as resolved-by-dar-gwn.6. Separate finding exposed = a lost-RPC startup stall (new bead under dar-gwn). Nothing pushed. Memory dar-gwn15-was-dargwn6-regression.

title: Fix strcpy(NULL) in Darling vchroot path handler (lkm 0x103d -> strcpy -> vchroot_unexpand) — the brew-build SEGV trigger, reached via dyld bind in a forked child
description:
THE PRIMARY TRIGGER (P1) of the brew-under-Darling build crash. CONCLUSIVE root cause (2026-06-22,
server-side fault-state + caller-chain trace; full evidence in memory brew-sigchld-segv-rootcause):

A freshly-forked ruby build.rb child takes a GENUINE strcpy(dst, NULL) -> strlen(NULL) SEGV during
the libunistring (any source build) stage/system() path. NOT spurious, NOT darlingserver injection.

  RIP    = libsystem_platform.dylib +0x6084 = _platform_strlen+0xa4
           insn `movq (%rax,%rcx),%r8` with rax=0  ->  fault linux=11 code=1 (SEGV_MAPERR) addr=0x0
  caller = libsystem_c.dylib __strcpy_chk+0x18 -> _stpcpy+0x18 -> strlen
           (__strcpy_chk = the _FORTIFY_SOURCE expansion of strcpy; the src arg was NULL)
  ctx    = dyld symbol-binding machinery on the stack: ImageLoaderMachOCompressed::
           exportedSymbolAddress / findShallowExportedSymbol, ImageLoader::trieWalk, resolveTwolevel.
  => strcpy(NULL) during dyld launch-time two-level-namespace symbol resolution in the forked+exec'ing
     child. The strcpy is in libc CODE reached from dyld => Darling-side (dyld/libc), NOT ruby-side.

SCOPE / DEFINITION OF DONE:
1. Pin the EXACT strcpy(NULL) source line via a CLEAN unwind (use the darling-debug-runner:
   `capture --tree --gdb --gdb-namespace` -- it gives a proper rbp unwind; the manual linear stack
   scan had stale slots, e.g. libsystem_kernel+0x391fb landed mid-instruction).
2. Fix the NULL-source (likely a getenv()/confstr()/symbol-name lookup returning NULL that is strcpy'd
   without a check in the dyld/libc symbol-binding path).
3. Capture as a proper workspace patch (patches/homebrew/<module>/<name>.patch + patches.yml + pr-draft).
4. Deploy + verify: a from-source formula build no longer SEGVs; then brew install end-to-end green.

REPRO: `brew reinstall --build-from-source libunistring` (plain install SKIPS -- dep already in
Cellar). DSERVER_LOG_LEVEL=info + HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1. Server log persistent at
$DPREFIX/private/var/log/dserver.log. STOCHASTIC: each boot lands on flavor-B (this SEGV, ~1-in-2-3)
or flavor-C (the hang -- see the sibling bead). See memory: brew-sigchld-segv-rootcause,
darling-debug-runner, homebrew-run-harness. Constraint: nothing pushed without explicit per-action OK.
close_reason: FIXED + live-verified. Root cause: sys_lstat64() (and 14 sibling vchroot path syscalls) did strcpy(vc.path, path) with no NULL check that sys_lstat has. Fix: added 'if (!path) return -EFAULT;' guard to 15 files (linkat guards path+link), branch fix/vchroot-pathnull-guard (5f26a4c..aad6215), integrated Variant A before the eunion stack (west patch verify RC0, 44/44). Live-verified on the deployed guarded dylib: a forked guest child calling lstat/stat/chdir/truncate/readlink/unlink with NULL now returns -1/EFAULT (errno 14) instead of SIGSEGV; negative control (old dylib) SEGVs in build.rb; zero strcpy(NULL) SEGVs across 6+ boots. Disasm confirms testq %rdi/je->-0xe guard before strcpy. The 'brew install end-to-end green' remainder is blocked ONLY by the orthogonal psynch download hang dar-gwn.1.7, not by this SEGV. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
UPDATE 2026-06-22 — strcpy(NULL) caller PINNED TO SOURCE (DoD step 1 DONE).

Method: extended the server-side fault tracer (NOT the darling-debug-runner — its gdb capture only
fires on stall/timeout, so it cannot catch this clean-exit transient forked-child crash; the right
tool was the in-server fault tracer + a CALL-SITE-VALIDATED stack scan). Reproduced 3/3 boots.

VERIFIED CHAIN (call-site-validated — keep only return addrs immediately preceded by a real `call`):
  _platform_strlen+0xa4 (rax=0, NULL deref, code=1 addr=0x0)
   <- _stpcpy+0x18 [libsystem_c]
   <- ___strcpy_chk+0x18 [libsystem_c]              == strcpy(dst, src) with src == NULL
   <- <Darling libsystem_kernel VCHROOT path-handler>   <-- THE BUG
   <- dyld ImageLoaderMachOCompressed::doBind/eachBind / instantiateFromCache / sniffLoadCommands
   <- libdyld start

CORRECTION to the prior note: the outer context is dyld TWO-LEVEL SYMBOL BINDING (doBind/eachBind),
NOT bsdtar/libarchive. The earlier rbp-walk that suggested bsdtar/libarchive derailed at the asm
strcpy_chk frame; those frames were stale and are call-site-INVALIDATED. dyld-bind confirms the
original root cause.

BUG SITE (byte-exact disasm of the loaded libsystem_kernel):
  lea <pathbuf>(%rbp),%rsi ; mov $0x1000,%edx ; mov %rbx,%rdi ; call ___strcpy_chk ; ... ; call _vchroot_unexpand
  i.e. a Darling vchroot path handler does _lkm_call(opcode 0x103d) to fetch a path, then
  strcpy(dst, pathResult) WITHOUT a NULL check, then vchroot_unexpand(dst). When the lkm/path lookup
  returns NULL (error / unmapped fd), strcpy(dst, NULL) faults. This is a GENUINE NULL-deref in
  DARLING'S OWN emulation code (the vchroot path layer), reached because a forked child is loading/
  binding a dylib via dyld during the brew build.

  File: src/external/xnu/darling/src/libsystem_kernel/emulation/src/linux_premigration/
        vchroot_userspace.c  (vchroot path layer; see also fdpath.c, fcntl.c F_GETPATH).
  Exact C function = the lkm(0x103d)->strcpy->vchroot_unexpand handler (NOT the readlink-based
  vchroot_fdpath; its link[] buffer is never NULL). Files referencing vchroot_unexpand to inspect:
  vchroot_userspace.c, xnu_syscall/bsd/impl/misc/proc_info.c, linux_premigration/ext/file_handle.c.

REMAINING (DoD steps 2-4): name the exact handler (grep the 0x103d lkm opcode -> NR_ -> handler),
add `if (!src) return -EINVAL;` (or skip strcpy) guard, capture as workspace patch
(patches/homebrew/<module>/*.patch + patches.yml), deploy, verify from-source brew build no longer
SEGVs across N boots.

Bug-site evidence: faultwalk-HIT.log (nsid 774) + -run1/-run2, /tmp/legb-maps-774.txt, upkernel-disasm.txt.
All [legb] traces reverted; src/thread.cpp = Leg A-ext only; deployed dserver = clean 4457fc29. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Variant A integration COMPLETE (steps 1-3 of DoD done; step 4 live-boot verify still pending).

Fix: sys_lstat64() (and 14 sibling vchroot path syscalls) did strcpy(vc.path, path) with no NULL check; sys_lstat had the guard, the rest didn't. Added 'if (!path) return -EFAULT;' (linkat guards path AND link) to: lstat64, statfs64, utimes, chdir, chmod_extended, fchmodat, fchownat, lchown, linkat, mknod, readlinkat, truncate, unlinkat, getxattr, listxattr. 8 files also gained #include <sys/errno.h>. Baseline bug (not E-UNION); applies on preupstream/main 5f26a4c.

Patch: patches/homebrew/xnu/vchroot-pathnull-guard.patch (branch fix/vchroot-pathnull-guard, 5f26a4c..aad6215). PR-draft: pr-drafts/xnu-vchroot-pathnull-guard.md.

Integration (Variant A): guard placed BEFORE the eunion stack in patches.yml; rebased all 22 fix/e-union-N-* branches --onto aad6215. Two conflict points, both trivial keep-both (guard before EUNION copy-up block): eunion-5-writeop-wiring (mknod/utimes/chmod_extended/fchmodat/truncate) and eunion-15-xattr-isolation (listxattr). All 22 eunion patch files regenerated with chained bases; patches.yml source-base/commit/sha refreshed. west patch verify --profile homebrew = RC 0, 44/44 verified, full git am --3way stack clean. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
LIVE VERIFICATION PASSED (DoD step 4 done).

Built guarded libsystem_kernel.dylib in darling-build (sha 68a0fda0, _sys_lstat64 disasm confirms 'testq %rdi,%rdi; je ->movq $-0xe' = early NULL->-EFAULT guard before strcpy). Deployed to BOTH overlay layers: lower (darling-prefix/libexec/darling/usr/lib/system/) AND — critically — the DPREFIX UPPER layer (darling-prefix-homebrew-test/usr/lib/system/libsystem_kernel.dylib), which is the copy the guest actually loads. (First build runs silently exercised the OLD unguarded upper dylib until this was caught: the upper override's _sys_lstat64 went straight to strcpy with NO testq/je guard, and its offset 0x391a0 matches the libsystem_kernel+0x391fb crash address from the root-cause investigation.)

NEGATIVE CONTROL: a from-source brew build child (build.rb under sandbox-exec) on the OLD unguarded dylib reproduced 'terminated by uncaught signal SEGV'.

POSITIVE: with the guarded dylib deployed, a direct guest reproducer (clang-compiled in-guest, a forked child that does dyld two-level bind on launch then calls the guarded syscalls with NULL) returns -1/EFAULT for ALL of lstat/stat/chdir/truncate/readlink/unlink instead of crashing:
  lstat(NULL) -> r=-1 errno=14 (Bad address); ... NULLPATH_TEST_DONE fails=0; TEST_EXIT=0.
errno 14 = EFAULT = the guard's -0xe return. Across 6+ guarded boots: ZERO strcpy(NULL)/lstat64 SEGV signatures.

CAVEAT: could not drive a full guarded brew-from-source build to completion because brew's download_queue (Concurrent::FixedThreadPool future + cond_wait, download_queue.rb:184) deadlocks/ETIMEDOUTs on nearly every boot under Darling psynch — the SIBLING hang (dar-gwn.1.7 family), orthogonal to this SEGV. So the build-completion path is blocked by the hang, not by this crash. The direct NULL-path reproducer is a cleaner, deterministic proof of THIS fix than the flaky brew build. dar-gwn.1.6 crash site is verified fixed.

Nothing pushed.

title: Fix flavor-C build hang: interrupt_enter while parked never gets its reply -> guest recvmsg blocks forever (semaphore_timedwait -111)
description:
FLAVOR-C: the HANG outcome of the same brew-build fork-storm race (the SEGV in the P1 bead is
flavor-B; same root race, different stochastic outcome -- ~1-in-2-3 boots hang instead of SEGV).

SIGNATURE (2026-06-22): darlingserver goes SILENT mid-build. Last activity on the wedged guest thread
is an INTERRUPT_ENTER while it is parked (state=1, after MARKWAIT/BLOCK) -- and the matching
INTERRUPT_EXIT / RPC reply NEVER arrives. The guest thread then blocks forever in recvmsg on its
per-thread darlingserver RPC socket; stranded siblings time out with `semaphore_timedwait code=-111`.
No terminate/throw in the server (the Leg A-ext guard holds); the server process stays alive but idle.
This is the dar-gwn.1 interrupt_enter/exit reply-deferral family (PUSH_UNKNOWN_REPLIES), surfacing as
a lost reply when a signal hits a thread mid-RPC during the fork storm.

SCOPE / DOD: make the interrupt_enter/exit + push-reply protocol not lose/misroute the reply when a
signal interrupts a parked thread mid-RPC, so the guest recvmsg always gets its reply (or a detectable
EOF/error) instead of blocking forever. Verify the hang no longer reproduces across repeated
from-source builds. Likely shares a fix with dar-gwn.1; coordinate.

TOOLING: use darling-debug-runner `run ... --capture-gdb --capture-tree` (auto-captures the tree on
detected stall) -- see memory darling-debug-runner. NOTE flavor-C also leaves orphaned guests => feeds
dar-pot. Constraint: nothing pushed without explicit per-action OK.
close_reason: Completed: push_reply pipe leak/lost-reply hang was root-caused, fixed, packaged, and validated; later libunistring/configure failures are tracked separately in dar-gwn.1.10/dar-gwn.6.4.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIXED (2026-06-23). The flavor-C hang is a LEAKED SYNCHRONIZATION-PIPE FD in the push_reply handler.

MECHANISM: when a signal interrupts a thread waiting for an RPC reply, the interrupt protocol may hand the client the interrupted call's reply before the interrupt_enter reply; the client pushes it back via push_reply (callnum 0xbadca11). The client push hook (libsystem_kernel __dserver_rpc_hooks_push_reply, dserver-rpc-defs.c) creates a pipe, sends the WRITE end to the server, and BLOCKS in read() on the read end until the server writes a handshake byte or closes its write end (EOF). Server-side, Call::callFromMessage() push_reply branch (call.cpp:164) extracts that write fd as a RAW int (extractDescriptorAtIndex removes it from the Message, ownership -> local), then does readMemory + invariant checks that CAN THROW, and only at the end write()s+close()s the fd. Any throw between extraction and close LEAKS the raw fd -> never written, never closed -> client read() blocks FOREVER -> interrupted call reply never re-delivered -> guest recvmsg hangs (siblings: semaphore_timedwait -111), server alive+idle = EXACT 1.7 signature. dar-gwn.1.8 event-loop guard turns the throw into a silently-dropped message -> clean one-guest hang instead of server crash. Realistic recurring trigger under fork-storm: the _interrupts.empty() race (push_reply arrives AFTER interrupt_exit popped the interrupt -> old code threw + dropped the reply).

FIX (fix/dar-gwn-1-7-pushreply-pipe-leak @ 0c190dd, stacked on fix/processcall-exception-guard a52a15e; patch darlingserver/pushreply-pipe-leak.patch sha256 4c8ad4c1, registered in patches.yml, pr-draft written): (1) bind extracted fd to RAII FD immediately -> dtor closes on EVERY exit path incl exception unwind -> client read() always returns (byte on success / EOF on failure), never strands guest. (2) in _interrupts.empty() race DON'T drop the pushed reply -- it IS the interrupted call reply, send it straight back so the call completes.

VERIFIED: (a) standalone deterministic pipe-protocol test (dar-gwn17-verify/pipe_mechanism_test.cpp): OLD raw-fd HANGS forever, NEW FD-RAII unblocks via EOF -> RESULT PASS. (b) IN-BINARY live fault injection (DSERVER_INJECT_PUSHREPLY_FAULT [legb17], reverted+rebuilt byte-identical clean md5 000b3da1 after): forced REAL darlingserver to throw on a genuine push_reply from a brew fork-storm (log: push_reply received thread 3917458 -> INJECTING forced throw -> Dropping message injected push_reply fault); server stayed ALIVE (0 terminate) and thread 3917458 KEPT RUNNING (sigexc/PH 79s later) instead of wedging in recvmsg. west patch verify --profile homebrew = 45/45 RC0. Clean binary built+deployed, boots clean, host swept 0/0.

REMAINING (orthogonal): full from-source brew build still blocked by brew download_queue psynch cond_wait deadlock on nearly every boot (separate defect) -- injection+mechanism tests are the deterministic proof for THIS fix. NOTHING PUSHED.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
DOWNLOAD HANG (the orthogonal blocker of this bead's DoD) RESOLVED via tooling drift fix, NOT new code (2026-06-23).

ROOT CAUSE OF THE DRIFT: builds were made from the EUNION branch (xnu exp/route2-nomount tip 95366ed / fix/e-union-22), NOT from the tooling-generated integration/homebrew. The EUNION branch forked before two xnu psynch fixes were added to patches.yml and never carried them, so the deployed libsystem_kernel was MISSING:
  - xnu/psynch-cvsignal-args (dar-q95.2): cvsignal/cvbroad passed cvlsgen/mugen as uint32_t (truncating 64-bit gen-count words) and cvbroad passed mugen where the mutex ptr belongs -> condvar wakeups dropped/misrouted.
  - xnu/psynch-negative-errno (dar-q95.19): psynch wait errors returned positive -> libpthread mistook an interrupted cvwait for success, never cleared the orphaned prepost -> stranded condvar wakeup.
These are EXACTLY the brew download_queue (Concurrent::FixedThreadPool + cond_wait) primitives. Added a drift detector (west patch status); it showed 9 MISSING xnu patches in the build tree incl. these two.

FIX: migrated all modules to integration/homebrew via west patch apply (upstream + all 45 patches). west patch status -> 0 MISSING. Rebuilt darlingserver (86cdfb89) + libsystem_kernel (4be98f61), deployed to both overlay layers.

VERIFIED (live before/after, brew fetch --deps wget = 7 parallel bottle downloads through download_queue):
  - OLD libsystem_kernel 0e036820 (no 2 psynch fixes): HANG on ITER 1 -- darlingserver idle (state=S ep_poll, 2 threads), 5 mldr threads blocked in recvmsg/__skb_wait_for_more_packets, 0 formulae fetched in 2+ min. Canonical dar-gwn.1 idle-server lost-reply signature.
  - NEW libsystem_kernel 4be98f61 (integration/homebrew): 3/3 ITERS OK (74s/65s/70s), all 7 formulae fetched each run, server state=R. No hang.

So dar-gwn.1.7's DoD blocker (download hang) is cleared by delivering already-tracked fixes; the pushreply-pipe-leak fix (this bead) + the download psynch fixes together unblock the download/fetch path. Host swept 0/0. NOTHING PUSHED.

title: Leg A-ext: contain event-loop callFromMessage throw so a bad RPC can't terminate darlingserver (DONE, publication blocked)
description:
DONE (work complete), publication BLOCKED on dar-gwn.1.6 (P1) + dar-gwn.1.7 (flavor-C).

Containment fix: a single bad RPC race could throw an exception that escapes the darlingserver dispatch
and terminates the WHOLE server (-> every in-flight guest stranded in recvmsg, whole guest tree
orphaned = dar-pot). Wrapped all THREE uncaught throw sites:
  1. Thread::microthreadWorker (fiber stack) — existing TODO.
  2. Call::callFromMessage kernelAsync path.
  3. Call::callFromMessage invoked DIRECTLY in the main event loop Server::start() (the load-bearing
     site for the brew cascade; Leg A's processCall guards did NOT cover it).
Now: log + drop/error-reply the one offending message; the server SURVIVES.

This does NOT fix the crash (dar-gwn.1.6) or the hang (dar-gwn.1.7) — it stops the cascade from KILLING
the server and leaking the guest tree (the dar-pot path Leg A missed).

DELIVERED: branch fix/processcall-exception-guard @ a52a15e (src/thread.cpp + src/call.cpp +
src/server.cpp). Patch patches/homebrew/darlingserver/processcall-exception-guard.patch
sha256 ed5884e70b296f1d12ac889b3f3358692987d49c7391eff3eccb37546d0dc357, source-commit a52a15e,
patches.yml updated, pr-draft pr-drafts/darlingserver-processcall-exception-guard.md. `west patch
verify` -> 43 patches. Deployed darlingserver md5 4457fc29 (clean, correct prefix). publication-status
`blocked`. Verified: ONE clean before/after on brew install libunistring (pre-fix: terminate + dead
server + ~14 orphaned mldr; post-fix: same SEGV fires, server stays alive, clean exit, no leak).

NOT pushed (standing constraint). Flip publication-status off `blocked` and close once dar-gwn.1.6 +
dar-gwn.1.7 land and brew install is green.

title: RPC log flood: sigexc DEBUG_SIGEXC kprintf storm collapses guest throughput (brew install)
description: ROOT CAUSE of the brew-install throughput collapse / download stall. sigexc.c hardcoded `#define DEBUG_SIGEXC`, routing every kern_printf() in the signal-delivery + thread state save/restore path through __simple_kprintf() = a synchronous RPC round-trip to darlingserver. The sigexc handler + helpers emit dozens of lines per signal (dump_gregs loops 23 regs; float/thread state save+restore add more). Under a fork/exec storm (brew download workers spawn+reap many curl children) this floods thousands of blocking RPCs (measured 4995 kprintf RPCs in ~5s on one ruby worker), collapsing guest throughput: downloads stall, curl children pile up as unreaped zombies, DownloadQueue spins, and Ruby aborts with [BUG] pthread_cond_wait ETIMEDOUT at download_queue.rb:184. Evidence: dump-kprintf-strings.sh recovered the flood strings ('sigexc: have RIP', 'sigexc: gregs', 'sigexc: saving to kernel'). FIX: branch fix/sigexc-debug-flood @ 7268517 (remove the unconditional define; gate the per-S2C line). Patch patches/homebrew/xnu/sigexc-debug-flood.patch. LIVE: with fix deployed, brew install wget gets PAST the download stall into the dependency configure/conftest build phase with zero zombies (previously hard-stalled at download). CVT19 instrumentation disproved the untimed-cvwait-spurious-timeout theory (0/197 timeouts on untimed waits). Family: ties dar-gwn.1.8 (kprintf was the real culprit, not the condvar), dar-pot (zombie leak). Nothing pushed.
close_reason: Completed: unconditional sigexc DEBUG_SIGEXC kprintf flood was fixed and verified to move brew past the download stall; remaining configure/conftest failure is tracked in dar-gwn.1.10/dar-gwn.6.4.

title: brew install mutex handoff deadlock after cvwait EINTR recovery
description: After the darlingserver suspend/resume lost-wakeup fix (dar-gwn.1.4) the prior psynch/timer hang is gone, but 'brew install' still does not complete end-to-end. It reaches 'Fetching downloads', fetches some files (e.g. *.big_sur.jws.json), then Ruby threads block in socket receive and the run times out at 300s while darlingserver stays healthy (continues servicing timed-wait cycles). Characterized in dar-gwn.1.4 / dar-gwn.1.3 final comments as a NEW network/download stall, distinct from the lost-wakeup hang. NOT yet diagnosed. Next: fresh 'brew install --force-bottle wget' on nhs against current darlingserver 26f50b5 to capture the exact stall point (socket fd, server-side state).
design:
ROOT CAUSE (confirmed 2026-06-10, repro 20260610T115553Z-brew-fetch-rpcattach):
brew-fetch hang = LOST psynch condition-variable wakeup, NOT network / suspend-resume / SIOCGIFCONF.

WHAT IS CLEAR:
- Orphan RPC behind the client recvmsg hang = psynch_cvwait (callnum 71). ruby main thread sends cvwait, server parks it in the ksyn waitq, and thread_unblock is NEVER called for it => no RPC reply => recvmsg blocks forever.
- Proven by host strace (--attach-strace) of ruby pid 430228 correlated with dserver.log [PH] trace: main thread dt=0x70f5c4033a90 final event BLOCK cont=psynch_cvcontinue @736.305; zero subsequent UNBLOCK for that dt in the whole 1.7GB log. A 2nd worker (tid 248) also stuck in cvwait.
- cv 0x79c56a6048e0 is producer/consumer: worker threads cvsignal (callnum 70) to wake main. Last cvsignal @722.195, BEFORE cvwait #1 (722.289) and #2 (736.304). cvwait #1 escaped only via SIGCHLD (ERESTARTSYS); cvwait #2 had no signal and hung.
- The lost wakeup stands immediately after a SIGCHLD-interrupted (ALLOW_INTERRUPTIONS) cvwait #1 => suspect: prepost/sequence (sword/lword/cgen/csgen) mis-accounted when a cvwait leaves the queue via interrupt rather than via signal, so a later signal is absorbed / the final waiter is left unmatched.
- NOT dar-q95.8 (suspend/resume): no UNBLOCK was ever issued, nothing lost at resume level. q95.1 only relaxed cvwait validation (is_seqhigher_eq->is_seqhigher); does not fix this.

PLAN (option B): add targeted [CVDBG] logging (cv, sword/lword, cgen/csgen, nwaiters, kwe_state, interrupted-vs-signaled) in __psynch_cvsignal / ksyn_cvsignal / ksyn_handle_cvbroad / ksyn_wait / psynch_cvcontinue / _kwq_mark_interruped_wakeup / _kwq_handle_interrupted_wakeup; disable the [PH] firehose; reproduce once and pin the exact sequence desync, then targeted fix.

Repro: darling-debug-runner darling --attach-strace 'brew\.rb fetch' -- bash -c 'brew fetch wget' (needs ptrace_scope=0). File: src/external/darlingserver/duct-tape/pthread/kern_synch.c.
close_reason: All 3 psynch deadlock layers fixed and validated (brew fetch wget 13/13; brew install jq no longer hangs). Layer 3 (libpthread negative-error decode for cond/mutex/rwlock) committed: libpthread 3de6464, xnu 0a10e19, parent f510f71. Remaining jq failure is an unrelated CLT/bottle env limitation.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
2026-06-10 reproduction on nhs against current fixed darlingserver (installed bin md5 dd95758b == fixed build with suspend/resume permit, Jun 10 06:36; DPREFIX=~/work/darling-prefix-homebrew-test). Two runs via tools/darling-debug-run.sh:

(1) 'brew install --force-bottle wget' -> COMPLETES, no hang (RC=1). Fetched API source wget.rb successfully (network OK), then exited cleanly with 'Error: --force-bottle passed but wget has no bottle!' (macOS 11 Tier 3). So the --force-bottle path no longer reaches the download stall described earlier.

(2) 'brew fetch wget' -> HANGS, timed out at 300s. Bundle: ~/work/darling-debug/20260610T102808Z-brew-fetch-wget. Signature is the ORIGINAL dar-gwn.1 idle-server lost-reply, NOT a network/HTTP stall:
  - darlingserver (pid 3871908) has exactly 2 threads: main in ep_poll (epoll_wait) + one WorkQueue worker parked in futex_wait_queue => server IDLE / empty work queue.
  - ruby 'brew.rb fetch wget' (pid 3871951): main mldr thread blocked in recvmsg on fd to .darlingserver.sock (wchan __skb_wait_for_more_packets), awaiting an RPC reply that never arrives; a sibling thread busy-spins (Rl + tight sched_yield/epoll_wait(10ms)=0/clock_gettime/sysinfo loop); 'system_command.' thread also in skb_wait.
  - NO live curl/git/download child; brew only printed START_FETCH (hung during Ruby startup/formula phase, BEFORE any network fetch).
  - stdout also showed two earlier helper processes aborting: 'semaphore_timedwait failed (internally): code=-111' = ECONNREFUSED on the darlingserver RPC socket (pid 5 tid 11; pid 9 tid 16) — a client RPC connection was refused/closed.

CONCLUSION: dar-gwn.2 is a RESIDUAL server-side lost-RPC-reply hang (same class as dar-gwn.1), not a network stall. The suspend/resume permit fix (dar-gwn.1.4) reduced but did not eliminate it. 'brew fetch wget' is a faster, earlier-firing reproducer than the full install.

NOTES/CAVEATS:
  - The installed darlingserver is the [PH]-traced debug build: dserver.log grew to ~1.7 GB (every thread transition logged at Error). Need a clean (untraced) build for production, but the trace is useful here.
  - gdb backtraces in the bundle are unsymbolized (gdb attached from host PID namespace; warning 'connect to gdbserver inside the container'). For a symbolized server-side backtrace, attach gdbserver inside the container or correlate the stuck guest tid against the server log.

NEXT STEP candidates: (a) identify the un-replied RPC — decode ruby's last sendmsg callnum on fd 1048575 (strace from BEFORE the block, not just at timeout) and find the matching server-side microthread that suspended and never resumed/replied; (b) targeted instrumentation around interrupt_enter/exit + push-reply and resume() for the fetch path, instead of the 1.7GB [PH] firehose; (c) check whether the ECONNREFUSED child-RPC failure is what strands the parent's wait.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE LOCALIZED (strace+dserver [PH] correlation, repro 20260610T115553Z-brew-fetch-rpcattach).

Orphan RPC = psynch_cvwait (callnum 71, 0x47 'G'). Verified callnum map from generate-rpc-wrappers.py: 14=interrupt_enter,15=interrupt_exit,31=pthread_canceled,70=psynch_cvsignal,71=psynch_cvwait,72=psynch_mutexdrop.

Evidence chain:
- strace (host-attached to ruby pid 430228 from process start via new darling-debug-runner --attach-strace): main thread fd 0xFFFFF last op = sendmsg(callnum 71)=80B at 736.304765, then recvmsg(<unfinished>) NEVER returns. The earlier cvwait #1 (722.289) blocked 14s and was released only by SIGCHLD (ERESTARTSYS), not by a signal.
- dserver.log [PH] trace (same run, pid 430228): main thread dt=0x70f5c4033a90 final event = BLOCK cont=psynch_cvcontinue at 736.305006; ZERO subsequent 'UNBLOCK dt=0x70f5c4033a90' anywhere in the 1.7GB log (any executor). A second worker (tid 248/500382) is likewise stuck: BLOCK cont=psynch_cvcontinue at 736.309, never unblocked.
- cv 0x79c56a6048e0 history: producer/consumer (main G=cvwait woken by worker F=cvsignal 70). LAST cvsignal for this cv = 722.195, BEFORE both cvwait #1 (722.289) and #2 (736.304). After the download child (pid 245) exits (SIGCHLD 736.300) the signaling workers (tid 174/175/220/221) go idle (ENTERING/EXITING, not waiting); nobody signals again.

CONCLUSION: lost psynch condvar wakeup. Waiter is correctly parked in the ksyn waitq but thread_unblock is never called for it => no RPC reply => client recvmsg hangs forever. This is a signal/wait sequence (prepost) accounting bug in duct-tape/pthread/kern_synch.c. It is NOT the microthread suspend/resume race (dar-q95.8 — that path is fine: no UNBLOCK was ever issued, so nothing was lost at resume level), NOT network, NOT SIOCGIFCONF (dar-q95.11). dar-q95.1 'balanced sequences' only relaxed is_seqhigher_eq->is_seqhigher validation and does not address the wakeup loss.

NEXT: pin the exact off-by-one in the signal/wait sequence accounting around interrupted (ALLOW_INTERRUPTIONS) cvwait — the SIGCHLD interrupt of cvwait #1 immediately precedes the lost wakeup, suggesting a prepost/sequence is mis-accounted when a cvwait returns via interrupt rather than via signal.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Option B instrumentation added (local, uncommitted): disabled the [PH] firehose in duct-tape/src/thread.c (6 dtape_log_error lines commented) and added targeted [CVDBG] dtape_log_error tracing across the cv path in duct-tape/pthread/kern_synch.c (cvwait_enter/lookup/block, cvcontinue+exit, cvsignal_enter/balanced_skip/exit, ksyn_cvsignal+stale_th, ksyn_signal, cvbroad_enter/exit, ksyn_wait_inserted) plus shim_wait_prepare/shim_wait_wakeup in duct-tape/src/psynch.c. Each line carries cv addr + lword/sword/inqueue + kwe state; dtape_log auto-prefixes pid(nspid):tid(nstid). Built darlingserver in darling-build (links clean). Reproducing brew fetch wget via darling-debug-runner --install-server + --attach-strace to capture the sword/lword desync on the stuck cv.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE PINNED via [CVDBG] (stall reproduced 1/6 runs without strace, dserver-stall-iter1.log, 223 lines). Heisenbug: disabling the [PH] firehose narrowed but did NOT remove the race. Stuck cv=0x7a9908209470 (consumer tid nstid=23; producers 174/175/220/221). Sequence (PTHRW_INC=0x100, low byte=flags):

gen 0x100/0x200: healthy cvwait->cvsignal->cvcontinue(err=0).
gen 0x300: cvwait INTERRUPTED -> cvcontinue result=2(THREAD_INTERRUPTED) error=4(EINTR). error-branch: removes own kwe, sword 0x201+=PTHRW_INC=0x301, (lword 0x300 & MASK)==(sword 0x301 & MASK)=0x300 -> ECVCLEARED -> ZEROES cv (lword=uword=sword=0, KSYN_KWF_ZEROEDOUT). final_error=260=EINTR|ECVCLEARED.
In-flight signal tid220 (cgen=0x300,csgen=0x200) hits the zeroed cv: UPDATE_CVKWQ(ZEROEDOUT) reinits sword=rw_wc=csgen=0x200 -- REGRESSES sword from 0x300 to 0x200. No waiter present -> becomes PREPOST@0x300 (fakecount=1,inqueue=1), updatebits=0, sword stays 0x200.
gen 0x400: tid23 re-waits lockseq=0x400. find_cvpreposeq(0x400) returns NULL because it requires kwe_lockseq>=cgen and the prepost is at 0x300<0x400 -> prepost stranded forever. tid23 BLOCKS (inqueue=2). A real signal (tid221, uptoseq 0x400) wakes it, sword 0x200->0x300, prepost@0x300 remains.
gen 0x500: interrupted again -> EINTR|ECVPREPOST(516); fakecount==inqueue.
gen 0x600: tid23 blocks (csgen==lockseq==0x600, allowed by q95.1 relaxation). NO producer signals 0x600 -> permanent block. Whole process quiesces (all worker threads idle/blocked). Same shape as the original cv 0x79c56a6048e0 evidence.

MECHANISM: a Unix-signal (SIGCHLD from the fetch child) interrupting cvwait (THREAD_ABORTSAFE) at generation N races with the producer's in-flight cvsignal for N. The ECVCLEARED zero-out + the signal's UPDATE_CVKWQ reinit-from-csgen regresses kw_sword by one PTHRW_INC, losing one signal generation. The orphaned prepost can never be consumed (find_cvpreposeq seq>=cgen). NOT suspend/resume (q95.8): the dtape wakeup primitives all returned kr=0; this is pure psynch sequence-accounting corruption in kern_synch.c. NEXT: compare cvcontinue interrupt path + UPDATE_CVKWQ ZEROEDOUT against upstream Apple libpthread to locate the exact deviation.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Round-2 instrumentation: closed the block_hint=11 (mutex/rwlock) blind spot by adding [CVDBG] entry logs to psynch_mtxcontinue and _psynch_rw_continue (cv continuations already covered by cvcontinue). Rationale: ksyn_signal + shim_wait_wakeup (kr=0) already prove the wakeup primitive fired and thread_unblock requested a resume for ALL psynch types; the only remaining unknown for a stuck thread is whether its continuation actually executed. So: signaled (kr=0) + NO continuation logged => microthread resume was lost (suspend/resume defect, dar-q95.8 territory); signaled + continuation logged => pure psynch sequence-accounting (kern_synch.c). Rebuilt, re-running loop to classify the stuck thread definitively.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Round-2 result (dserver-stall-iter1.log, 143 lines, stall again on iter 1): RULED OUT lost microthread resume / suspend-resume (dar-q95.8). The ONLY stuck thread was nstid=23 in cvwait (tstore=nil, block_hint=14); every other thread completed its mtxcontinue (resume delivered fine) or returned to userspace. So this is PURE psynch sequence-accounting in kern_synch.c, not the scheduler.

Clean full trace of stuck cv=0x7e48c7f093d0 (consumer tid23) shows orphan-prepost ACCUMULATION:
- gen 0x100 cvwait INTERRUPTED (EINTR) before any signal -> waiter removes self, ECVCLEARED, cv zeroed. Late signal for 0x100 lands on zeroed cv -> orphan PREPOST@0x100 (fakecount=1).
- gen 0x200: real signal wakes it (err=0), but orphan@0x100 stays (inqueue=1).
- gen 0x300 INTERRUPTED again -> EINTR|ECVPREPOST(516); now 1 orphan. sword lags lword.
- gen 0x400: cvwait_lookup shows inqueue=2 fakecount=2 (TWO orphan preposts) -> blocks; no further signal arrives -> permanent block. find_cvpreposeq never matches orphans (requires kwe_lockseq>=cgen; orphans are at lower seqs).

ERROR-RETURN PLUMBING: psynch_cvcontinue returns the AUGMENTED error via unix_syscall_return -> thread_syscall_return -> Thread::syscallReturn -> Call::sendBSDReply(resultCode=error, returnValue=_bsdReturnValue). For the interrupt case error=516=EINTR(4)|ECVPREPOST(0x200) (ECVCLEARED=0x100, ECVPREPOST=0x200 per kern_internal.h). If the client maps resultCode straight to errno, errno=516 != EINTR(4) -> libpthread EINTR/prepost-cleanup logic may not fire. Observed: after EINTR the consumer goes straight to the next cvwait ~1ms later with NO _psynch_cvclrprepost call in between -> orphan preposts are never cleaned. _psynch_cvclrprepost IS wired (kern_init + RPC callnum + cvar branch ksyn_queue_free_items) but appears uninvoked. Round-3 (cvclrprepost_enter instrumentation) running to confirm whether libpthread ever calls it.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Round-3 CONFIRMED: _psynch_cvclrprepost is called ZERO times during the entire hang (added cvclrprepost_enter [CVDBG], stall reproduced again on iter 1, grep count = 0). So userspace libpthread never cleans the orphan preposts. The starvation is real: the producer signaled N times; each signal that landed on an interrupted (absent) waiter became an orphan prepost (a genuinely LOST wakeup, not just leaked memory), so later legitimate waiters get fewer signals than waits and one starves forever. Note: condvars do NOT use the kw_intr interrupted-wakeup redirection mechanism that mutex/rwlock use (_kwq_mark/_handle_interrupted_wakeup are KWQ_INTR_WRITE only) -- so an interrupt-vs-signal race on a cv has no redirection safety net. Next: diff darling kern_synch.c (_psynch_cvwait prepost scan, psynch_cvcontinue interrupt path, ksyn_queue_find_cvpreposeq) against upstream Apple libpthread to find the exact port deviation before choosing a fix.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIX APPLIED.

Upstream diff: darling's duct-tape/pthread/kern_synch.c (_psynch_cvwait, psynch_cvcontinue, ksyn_queue_find_cvpreposeq, kw_intr) is a FAITHFUL byte-for-byte port of Apple libpthread-454.120.2 kern/kern_synch.c -- no kernel-side deviation. Real macOS doesn't deadlock because userspace libpthread (_pthread_psynch_cond_wait in src/pthread_cond.c) handles the cvwait error: 'if (updateval == (uint32_t)-1) { err=errno; switch(err & 0xff){case EINTR:...} _pthread_cond_updateval(cond,mutex,err,0); }' where _pthread_cond_updateval decodes err & ECVCLEARED / err & ECVPREPOST and triggers __psynch_cvclrprepost. The trigger REQUIRES the syscall to return -1 with errno = the augmented error.

THE BUG (darling client glue, NOT the kernel): src/external/xnu/darling/src/libsystem_kernel/emulation/src/xnu_syscall/bsd/impl/psynch/psynch_cvwait.c, sys_psynch_cvwait did 'return (ret) ? ret : retval;' where ret = dserver_rpc header.code = the BSD error from psynch_cvcontinue (e.g. 516 = EINTR|ECVPREPOST, 260 = EINTR|ECVCLEARED). Returning it POSITIVELY made the macOS syscall look SUCCESSFUL with updateval=516, so libpthread took the success branch: never saw EINTR, never called _pthread_cond_updateval, never called psynch_cvclrprepost. The orphan prepost from the interrupted cvwait was thus never cleared -> accumulated -> sword lagged lword -> a later legitimate waiter starved -> permanent condvar block. Intermittent because cvwait only returns an error on the rare SIGCHLD-interrupt race.

FIX: 'return (ret) ? -ret : retval;' -- propagate the cv error as a negative value so cerror sets errno (full 516, high bits intact) and the syscall returns -1; libpthread then masks errno&0xff=EINTR and inspects ECVCLEARED/ECVPREPOST -> calls cvclrprepost. ret is the header.code which is non-zero ONLY on error (cv updateval bits travel via the separate retval/uthread_set_returnval), so -ret is always correct. Built libsystem_kernel.dylib, installed into darling-prefix/libexec/darling/usr/lib/system/. Validating with 6x brew fetch wget loop (was hanging on iter 1 every prior round). NOTE: sibling waiters psynch_mutexwait.c / psynch_rw_wrlock.c / psynch_rw_rdlock.c have the identical 'return (ret)?ret:retval' pattern and the same latent bug for their EINTR/ETIMEDOUT returns -- candidates for the same one-line fix (mutex/rwlock are partly shielded by the kw_intr redirection mechanism that condvars lack).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: FIX VALIDATION round 1 (cv-only fix, 6x loop): iters 1,2,3 PASSED (FETCH_RC=0), iter 4 HUNG. Before fix: hung on iter 1 every single round (3/3). So the cvwait fix dramatically reduces the hang but a RESIDUAL remains. Analyzed iter-4 stall: orphan-prepost mechanism is GONE (the interrupted cvwait at gen 0x100 re-waited cleanly, inqueue=0 fakecount=0, no orphan) -- cv fix confirmed working. Residual: stuck thread tid23 waits cv 0x78ef9f409450 gen 0x400 with a CLEAN cv (sword=0x301 lword=0x400, zero orphans); producers 174/175 signaled gen 0x200/0x300 then returned to userspace; no signal for 0x400 arrives. No interrupted mutex/rw in that run (so not a mutex-path interrupt). Looks like a cross-thread userspace dependency (producer waiting on consumer or vice versa). Applied the same one-line -ret fix to the sibling waiters psynch_mutexwait.c / psynch_rw_wrlock.c / psynch_rw_rdlock.c (identical bug class; an interrupted mutex/rwlock wait must also return -1/errno so libpthread retries and consumes the kw_intr-redirected wakeup). Rebuilt+installed dylib (cv+mutex+rw). Now running a 12x loop WITH gdb capture to get userspace backtraces of all threads on the residual hang.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
FIX VALIDATION round 2 + RESIDUAL IS A DIFFERENT BUG.

Confirmed the errno plumbing is correct: darling SYS.h x86_64 DARLING macro does 'call __darling_bsd_syscall; cmpq $-4095,%rax; (in error range) negq %rdi; call _cerror' -- the negative return becomes errno = -rax with NO truncation and NO Linux->BSD table conversion, so the full augmented value (516/316 incl. ECVCLEARED/ECVPREPOST bits) reaches libpthread. cvclrprepost staying at 0 is NOT a bug: libpthread only calls it when its 'needclearpre' drain condition triggers, which is rare. The psynch -ret fix is therefore correct and complete for its bug class (orphan prepost from interrupted cvwait): fixed runs show NO orphan accumulation.

The psynch fix dramatically reduced hangs (was 100% on iter 1; now passes multiple iters) but a RESIDUAL hang remains, and gdb + [CVDBG] prove it is a SEPARATE bug:
- gdb of the hung brew process: main thread blocked in recvmsg on the dserver RPC socket (a stuck psynch wait); a sibling thread in wait4(child) and another in select() -- i.e. brew is running an external download command (curl) and the main Ruby thread waits on a condvar for it.
- dserver [CVDBG]: stuck cv 0x773643409450, consumer tid23. gen 0x300: cvwait_block with abstime=0 / deadline=0 (a plain pthread_cond_WAIT, no timeout, sec=0 nsec=0). After ~29s it received cvcontinue result=1 (THREAD_TIMED_OUT) error=60(ETIMEDOUT) -- a SPURIOUS timeout on a no-deadline wait. That zeroed the cv (ECVCLEARED), the consumer re-waited at gen 0x400, the producer never signals it -> permanent block.

ROOT of residual = a spurious THREAD_TIMED_OUT delivered to a deadline=0 (wait-forever) psynch wait in duct-tape, NOT psynch sequence accounting. This matches the in-tree 'timer lost-wakeup reproducer' work (commits 7b266b7f7 / 7288e6305 / 6b682e086) and is dar-q95.8 (suspend/resume / timer) territory, a distinct issue from dar-gwn.2's psynch error-return bug. RECOMMENDATION: ship the psynch -ret fix (cvwait + mutexwait + rw_wrlock + rw_rdlock) as the dar-gwn.2 resolution; track the spurious-timeout as the timer bug.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: RESIDUAL ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIX (darlingserver, separate from the psynch errno fix). The spurious THREAD_TIMED_OUT on a deadline=0 wait is a MISSING WAIT-TIMER CANCELLATION in duct-tape. waitq_assert_wait64_locked (duct-tape/xnu/osfmk/kern/waitq.c:2926-2928) arms thread->wait_timer (wait_timer_is_set=TRUE, wait_timer_active++) for a timed wait. XNU's thread_unblock() (osfmk/kern/sched_prim.c:631-635) cancels it on wakeup, but duct-tape's thread_unblock (duct-tape/src/thread.c) did NOT -- it only cancelled in dtape_thread_destroy(). So a timed wait woken EARLY (by signal or a normal condvar/mutex wakeup before its deadline) leaks an armed wait_timer; it fires later in thread_timer_expire() -> clear_wait_internal(THREAD_TIMED_OUT), delivering a spurious timeout to whatever the thread is doing then. Observed: tid23 main brew thread, gen 0x300 plain pthread_cond_WAIT (abstime=0, deadline=0) received cvcontinue result=1 (THREAD_TIMED_OUT) error=60 ~29s after blocking -> ECVCLEARED zeroed the cv -> re-wait gen 0x400 never signalled -> permanent hang while a child download (wait4/select threads) was in flight. Intermittent because it needs a timed wait woken early followed by a critical untimed wait, and most condvar waits are untimed (no timer). FIX: add the XNU wait_timer cancel block to duct-tape thread_unblock (timer_call_cancel + wait_timer_active-- + wait_timer_is_set=FALSE). Safe re-entrancy: thread_timer_expire sets wait_timer_is_set=FALSE before clear_wait_internal->thread_go->thread_unblock, so the timer-expiry path skips the cancel. Distinct from dar-gwn.1.3 (lost timer DELIVERY) and dar-q95.8 (resume race); this is spurious timer DELIVERY-after-cancel-miss. Rebuilding + validating.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
MULTI-LAYER reality. Validation after the wait_timer-cancel fix (12x loop): iter1 PASSED, iter2 HUNG but with a DIFFERENT signature and NO spurious THREAD_TIMED_OUT (zero result=1 in the log) -> the timer-cancel fix removed the spurious-timeout class. New residual in iter2 is a MUTEX deadlock: tid23 and tid221 both block in psynch_mutexwait (block_hint=11, turnstile) on mutex 0x71dad4109590 (the mutex paired with cv 0x71dad4109450). Sequence: tid23 cvcontinue_exit final_error=516 (EINTR|ECVPREPOST) -> re-acquires mutex (mtxcontinue 0x503) -> immediately re-waits mutex at lockseq 0x602 (stuck); tid221 hands off the mutex then re-waits at 0x702 (stuck); no owner ever drops it to wake either. This ECVPREPOST->mutex-reacquire handoff path is only now exercised because the psynch errno fix made libpthread actually process the 516 return (previously swallowed). Also saw nstid=175 cvsignal_exit lword=0x200 sword=0x100 inqueue=1 desync on cv 0x71dad4109450.

STATE: three distinct psynch/scheduler issues identified under the SIGCHLD-storm + microthread timing of brew fetch: (1) psynch wrappers returned errors positively [FIXED+COMMITTED: xnu f3c8d94 / parent f38d2a146]; (2) wait_timer not cancelled in thread_unblock -> spurious THREAD_TIMED_OUT [FIX APPLIED in duct-tape/src/thread.c, validated to remove result=1; NOT yet committed, lives alongside [CVDBG] debug in darlingserver working tree]; (3) mutex hand-off desync in the cvwait-EINTR|ECVPREPOST -> mutex-reacquire path [OPEN]. Each fix peels back a layer; hang rate is dropping but not yet zero. Recommend committing (2) and continuing on (3).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: COMMITTED (local only, no push/PR per standing instruction): fix #2 wait_timer-cancel. darlingserver 32af4d5 'duct-tape: cancel wait_timer in thread_unblock'; parent f6f5fba31 bumps the submodule pointer. Combined with fix #1 (psynch errno, xnu f3c8d94 / parent f38d2a146) the brew-fetch hang is substantially reduced (orphan-prepost class + spurious-timeout class both eliminated). The [CVDBG] psynch instrumentation in darlingserver duct-tape/pthread/kern_synch.c + duct-tape/src/psynch.c is intentionally left UNCOMMITTED in the working tree for continued investigation of the remaining layer #3 (mutex hand-off desync in the cvwait-EINTR|ECVPREPOST -> mutex-reacquire path). The committed thread.c restores the [PH] tracing to its prior (committed) state so the timer-fix commit is single-purpose.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
=== HANDOFF (for a fresh agent picking up layer #3) ===

STATUS: 3 layers found under the SIGCHLD-storm + microthread timing of 'brew fetch wget'. Layers 1 & 2 FIXED + COMMITTED LOCALLY; layer 3 OPEN.
  (1) psynch wait wrappers returned BSD errors POSITIVELY -> libpthread skipped EINTR/ECVPREPOST recovery -> orphan preposts. FIX: 'return (ret)?-ret:retval'. COMMITTED: xnu f3c8d94 / parent f38d2a146. Files: src/external/xnu/.../psynch/psynch_{cvwait,mutexwait,rw_wrlock,rw_rdlock}.c.
  (2) duct-tape thread_unblock did NOT cancel wait_timer (XNU divergence) -> spurious THREAD_TIMED_OUT on a later deadline=0 wait. FIX: add timer_call_cancel block to thread_unblock. COMMITTED: darlingserver 32af4d5 / parent f6f5fba31. File: src/external/darlingserver/duct-tape/src/thread.c.
