comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
2026-06-10 layer-3 correction after targeted mutex instrumentation and fresh post-fix reproductions:

The alleged mutex hand-off deadlock is DISPROVEN. Added [CVDBG] around _psynch_mutexdrop_internal, _psynch_mutexwait, ksyn_mtxsignal, and psynch_mtxcontinue. In both fresh timeout traces, every mutex waiter received a matching drop/grant and mtxcontinue; no mutex owner or waiter was stranded. The earlier two-waiter terminal snapshot was transient and was incorrectly classified without drop/handoff visibility.

Fresh post-L1+L2 runs also show clean condition-variable accounting: no orphan preposts, no spurious THREAD_TIMED_OUT, and no balanced cgen==csgen entry. The only balanced entry remains in the old pre-fix SAVED trace and arose downstream of the already-fixed error/timer corruption. A speculative balanced-wait immediate-return patch was tested locally, did not trigger, and was REMOVED rather than masking an invalid state.

Current 120s timeout signature is a separate real child-command stall: live process tree shows brew Ruby waiting while /usr/bin/curl --silent --head https://ftpmirror.gnu.org/gnu/wget/wget-1.25.0.tar.gz remains blocked in do_select for the full timeout. Host curl to the same URL returns HTTP 302 in ~2s. darlingserver remains healthy, mutex handoffs complete, and the final Ruby condvar wait is clean/unsignaled because the curl child has not exited. Tracked separately as a child of dar-gwn.

Conclusion: dar-gwn.2 psynch/timer race is resolved by committed layers 1 and 2: xnu f3c8d94 / parent f38d2a146 and darlingserver 32af4d5 / parent f6f5fba31. The expanded [CVDBG] instrumentation remains intentionally uncommitted in darlingserver for future diagnosis.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-10 E2E REPRO CONFIRMS LAYER 3 IS REAL. Command: brew install jq under current L1+L2 fixed build. Bundle in progress: /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260610T170420Z-brew-install-jq-e2e. After fetching, no live curl/compiler remains; Ruby main + system_command threads block in dserver RPC recvmsg; darlingserver is idle (epoll + empty worker). Final [CVDBG] sequence on mutex 0x7699d15095d0: thread225 drops mutex from cvwait and grants thread226 (0x403); thread226 later drops and grants main tid23 (0x503); main mtxcontinue succeeds, then immediately mutexwaits at 0x602 while owner hint resolves to thread225; thread226 then mutexwaits at 0x702 with same owner thread225. Thread225 is itself sleeping in cvwait 0x7699798adc10 after having dropped this mutex. No owner drops again, so both waiters strand. This directly restores the previously suspected cvwait-EINTR|ECVPREPOST -> mutex reacquire/handoff layer. Earlier fresh brew-fetch traces only disproved it for those particular fetch-only runs; closing dar-gwn.2 was premature. Next: determine why userspace owner TID points back to cv-waiting thread225 after grants to thread226/main.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Reopened: Reopened after true end-to-end brew install jq validation reproduced the layer-3 mutex deadlock. Earlier fetch-only runs did not exercise this path.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-10 live E2E diagnosis update (brew install jq): corrected the initial mutex-deadlock interpretation. Mutex handoffs themselves complete: thread225 cvwait drops/grants thread226; thread226 drops/grants main; both mtxcontinue callbacks run. The apparent owner thread225 is not asleep: its cvwait is signaled and cvcontinue completes, then it reacquires the mutex. It subsequently busy-spins while holding the mutex, so main/system_command correctly block behind it. Host strace over 5s: thread225 performs ~34,000 sched_yield calls; companion workqueue thread cycles epoll_wait(10ms)/clock_gettime/sysinfo. Live memory pins the busy-loop to condvar 0x7699d1509490: L=0x300, U=0x400, S=0x200 (U > L, invalid). libpthread _pthread_psynch_cond_signal explicitly loops with sched_yield while U > L, explaining the spin and retained mutex. This invalid state appears immediately after main cvwait returns EINTR|ECVPREPOST (final_error=516): trace before terminal state has orphan fake prepost and L/S lag. Therefore layer 3 is not a kernel mutex handoff failure; it is userspace condvar U/S accounting corruption after interrupted cvwait recovery, causing pthread_cond_signal to spin forever while owning the associated mutex. Artifacts: /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/brew-install-jq-live-gdb.txt, brew-install-jq-cv-memory.txt, brew-install-jq-busy-thread-strace.log. Next: trace _pthread_cond_updateval / signal-side U transitions or compare installed libpthread behavior to expected error recovery to identify why U advances past L.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: CORRECTION to preceding live-memory interpretation: COND_GETSEQ_ADDR for aligned condvars maps c_seq[0]=L, c_seq[1]=S, c_seq[2]=U. Terminal memory at 0x7699d1509490 is therefore L=0x300, S=0x400, U=0x200, not U=0x400/S=0x200. The busy-loop is _pthread_psynch_cond_signal first validation branch (S > L), not U > L. This points to DOUBLE/EXCESS accounting of the interrupted waiter on userspace S after final_error=516. At cvwait entry userspace passed csgen=0x202 (S count 0x200); one correct EINTR|ECVPREPOST _pthread_cond_updateval should advance S only to 0x300, balanced with L. Actual S=0x400 means the same wait was accounted at least twice. Next instrumentation target: userspace _pthread_cond_updateval call count/error/updateval and cancellation cleanup path.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-10 CONDDBG E2E update: bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260610T171818Z-brew-install-jq-conddbg timed out, but it disproves double userspace updateval accounting. For cv 0x7bfd69e09460, _pthread_cond_updateval is called ONCE at the failing transition with error=0/updateval=0x204; it advances S 0x400 -> 0x600 while L=0x500, producing S>L and the signal-side sched_yield spin. Server CVDBG for the same wait reports cvcontinue_exit final_error=516 (0x204 = EINTR|ECVPREPOST). Therefore the immediate cause is that this run sees 0x204 as a successful syscall retval instead of errno, exactly the pre-L1 positive-BSD-error behavior. Strong suspicion: test DPREFIX has a stale libsystem_kernel.dylib shadowing the fixed main-prefix dylib. Next: compare/install fixed L1 dylib into DPREFIX and rerun true E2E.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-10 stale-DPREFIX confirmation: md5(test DPREFIX usr/lib/system/libsystem_kernel.dylib)=cc9a08ece274b5a334eb91c69d9cad48, while fixed L1 main-prefix dylib md5=6c1d2acd82bf4577de0a32105b97b5b2. The test DPREFIX was shadowing the committed L1 fix, so the latest apparent layer-3 S>L failure was not a new bug: final_error 0x204 was still returned positively by the stale dylib. Installed the fixed main-prefix libsystem_kernel.dylib into the Homebrew test DPREFIX. Control true E2E brew install jq is running now (bundle name brew-install-jq-fixed-kernel-e2e). Keep dar-gwn.2 open until that run completes.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-10 control E2E after installing fixed-L1 dylib into test DPREFIX: STILL TIMEOUT, bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260610T172330Z-brew-install-jq-fixed-kernel-e2e. Important correction: stale-positive 0x204 is gone, but the fixed wrapper now exposes another ABI mismatch. CONDDBG repeatedly sees error=0 with updateval=0xfffffffc (-EINTR), 0xfffffdfc (-516), etc. _pthread_psynch_cond_wait only treats updateval==(uint32_t)-1 as syscall error and otherwise feeds any nonzero value into _pthread_cond_updateval as a successful wake; thus negative BSD errors corrupt S in the opposite direction and the install still hangs. Server logs final_error=516, while userspace receives updateval=-516 rather than -1 with errno=516. Conclusion: L1 change from positive ret to -ret is insufficient/wrong at this libpthread ABI boundary; wrapper must produce libc syscall-error semantics (-1 + errno), or libpthread must explicitly decode negative returns. brew does NOT work E2E yet. Next: inspect generated psynch syscall wrapper/error conversion and fix the boundary, then rerun.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-10 root cause of failed L1 confirmed in source: sys_psynch_* implementations are called directly from the BSD syscall table; returning -ret does NOT invoke cerror automatically. Patched cvwait/mutexwait/rw_rdlock/rw_wrlock wrappers to call cerror(ret) explicitly, which should yield the ABI libpthread expects: return -1 and errno=BSD error/status bits. Build of libsystem_kernel.dylib succeeds. Installing into main + Homebrew test prefixes, then rerunning E2E.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-10 explicit-cerror trial result: build succeeded but brew install jq failed fast with Ruby pthread_cond_wait EINVAL; bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260610T173038Z-brew-install-jq-cerror-e2e. CONDDBG shows the failing cvwait reaches updateval with error=0x1, not the expected composite 0x104/0x204. So a plain C call cerror(ret) is also not preserving the composite psynch status errno correctly in this emulation path. This trial is diagnostic only; brew still does not work. Investigating cerror implementation/calling convention before choosing the boundary fix.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
LAYER 3 ROOT CAUSE FOUND (static + CONDDBG evidence) + FIX IN PROGRESS.

The neighboring agent's discovery overturns the 14:02 beads conclusion that 'errno plumbing is correct': for these EMULATED BSD psynch syscalls the negative BSD-error return does NOT pass through cerror/errno. Proven by [CONDDBG] in libpthread _pthread_cond_updateval: the committed -ret build showed updateval=0xffffffc4 (=-60=-ETIMEDOUT) arriving DIRECTLY as updateval with error=0x0 -> libpthread misread the negative error as a giant successful updateval. The neighboring agent's libpthread decode change ((int32_t)updateval<0 -> err=-updateval) in _pthread_psynch_cond_wait fixes the CONDVAR path: negative-decode run shows error=0x13c (ETIMEDOUT|ECVCLEARED) decoded correctly. That decode fix is CONFIRMED GOOD and must be kept.

LAYER 3 = SAME bug class, unpatched on the MUTEX and RWLOCK consumer paths:
- src/pthread_mutex.c:773 (fairshare) and :1415 (firstfit): 'while (updateval == (uint32_t)-1)'. With emulation returning -EINTR (0xfffffffc) directly, != 0xffffffff -> loop EXITS treating interrupted mutex wait as SUCCESSFUL acquisition -> stuck handoff. Exactly the 14:57 layer-3 signature (tid23+tid221 parked in psynch_mutexwait, no owner drops it). Kernel kw_intr redirection only works if userspace RE-ENTERS the wait; broken ==-1 loop never retries.
- src/pthread_rwlock.c:644: relies on errno (never set here) -> same failure; PTHREAD_INTERNAL_CRASHes on other decoded errors.

FIX: mutex/rwlock retry loops treat negative BSD-error return as 'interrupted, retry' (mirror validated cond decode (int32_t)updateval<0). Implementing now; rebuild + validate via brew install jq e2e and cvdbg-loop.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
BREAKTHROUGH — LAYER 3 FIX VALIDATED, psynch deadlock RESOLVED.

Applied the layer-3 fix in libpthread (mutex/rwlock consumer paths must treat the directly-returned negative BSD error as 'interrupted -> retry the kernel wait', mirroring the validated cond decode):
- src/pthread_mutex.c:773 (fairshare) and the firstfit loop: 'while (updateval == (uint32_t)-1)' -> 'while ((int32_t)updateval < 0)'.
- src/pthread_rwlock.c:644: added an '(int32_t)updateval < 0 -> res = -updateval' branch before the success branch so EINTR retries instead of being mistaken for a successful rwlock updateval.

VALIDATION (libsystem_pthread.dylib rebuilt 8530bfbb, installed to darling-prefix + darling-prefix-homebrew-test):
- brew fetch wget cvdbg-loop: 8/8 iters RESULT=exited. Before any layer-3 fix this stalled on iter 1-2 with 100% reliability.
- brew install jq e2e (bundle 20260610T192658Z-brew-install-jq-layer3-fix-e2e): RESULT=failed (NOT timeout). The process now gets PAST 'Fetching downloads' (previously the permanent hang point) — it downloaded the automake/libtool/oniguruma dependency formulae — then terminated normally at a Homebrew toolchain error: 'Your Command Line Tools (CLT) does not support macOS 11' (jq has no bottle on macOS 11 Tier 3 -> brew attempted a source build needing CLT). That CLT/bottle limitation is an UNRELATED Homebrew environment issue, not a psynch hang.

CONCLUSION: all 3 layers of dar-gwn.2 are fixed:
  (1) psynch wait wrappers return BSD errors as -ret [xnu f3c8d94 / parent f38d2a146].
  (2) duct-tape thread_unblock cancels wait_timer (spurious THREAD_TIMED_OUT) [darlingserver 32af4d5 / parent f6f5fba31].
  (3) libpthread cond/mutex/rwlock consumers DECODE the directly-returned negative BSD error (cerror/errno does NOT fire for these emulated BSD syscalls) so EINTR retries / prepost cleanup fires [libpthread, this change — cond decode from the neighboring agent + mutex/rwlock decode added now].
Finalizing: stripping [CONDDBG] debug, correcting the now-inaccurate 'cerror sets errno' wrapper comments, rebuild+commit locally.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
COMMITTED (local only, per standing no-push/no-PR instruction). Clean build (no [CONDDBG]) re-validated: brew fetch wget 5/5 RESULT=exited.

Commits:
- libpthread 3de6464 'pthread: decode negative BSD-error returns from emulated psynch waits' (pthread_cond.c + pthread_mutex.c x2 loops + pthread_rwlock.c).
- xnu 0a10e19 'libsystem_kernel: correct psynch wrapper comments' (comment-only; return logic already -ret in f3c8d94).
- parent f510f71 bumps both submodule pointers.

Total validation across the layer-3 fix: brew fetch wget 13/13 (8/8 debug build + 5/5 clean build); brew install jq e2e no longer hangs at 'Fetching downloads' (downloads dep formulae, then exits at an unrelated Homebrew CLT/bottle limitation: 'Command Line Tools does not support macOS 11' — jq has no macOS 11 Tier 3 bottle so brew attempts a source build).

dar-gwn.2 (all 3 psynch layers) RESOLVED. Remaining 'brew install jq' failure is NOT psynch: it is the missing/unsupported CLT for source builds + no bottle on this macOS-11 prefix — a separate Homebrew-environment concern, out of scope for this bug.

Working-tree note: src/external/darlingserver still carries the uncommitted [CVDBG] logging in duct-tape/pthread/kern_synch.c + duct-tape/src/psynch.c (verified debug-only, no behavioral change). Left as-is; can be reverted to get a clean/quiet server build.

title: brew fetch: Darling curl HEAD stalls in select
description: After dar-gwn.2 psynch/timer fixes, brew fetch wget can still exceed the runner timeout for a separate reason. Live process inspection shows Ruby legitimately waiting for child /usr/bin/curl --silent --head https://ftpmirror.gnu.org/gnu/wget/wget-1.25.0.tar.gz, which remains blocked in do_select for at least 120s. Host curl -I to the same URL returns HTTP 302 in about 2s. Fresh dserver [CVDBG] traces show clean mutex handoffs, no orphan preposts, no spurious timeout, and a clean final condvar wait with no producer signal because curl has not exited. Bundle: /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260610T163351Z-brew-fetch-childinspect. Next: run the exact curl command directly inside Darling with socket/select tracing and compare redirects/DNS/TLS against host.
close_reason: False positive: random GNU redirect target mirror.accum.se is externally unresponsive on both host and Darling; select correctly waits. brew fetch jq validates Darling fetch path.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-10 ROOT CAUSE / FALSE POSITIVE: no Darling select/network bug. A direct 20-run curl loop inside Darling reproduced the timeout immediately with verbose output. TCP connect and TLS handshake completed, curl sent HEAD to the random redirect target mirror.accum.se (194.71.11.165), then the remote server sent zero response bytes until timeout. The identical direct host curl to mirror.accum.se also timed out, and host curl -L to ftpmirror.gnu.org timed out after being redirected there. Therefore the observed do_select wait is correct socket behavior against an unresponsive external GNU mirror. No code change warranted. Independent Darling validation: brew fetch --retry jq completed successfully (FETCH_RC=0), bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260610T165718Z-brew-fetch-jq-validation. wget remains externally blocked while ftpmirror.gnu.org selects mirror.accum.se.

title: Upgrade CLT to ld64>=507 so -oso_prefix works (drop Homebrew workaround)
description:
Replace the Xcode-9-era CLT (ld64-305, no -oso_prefix) in the Homebrew test prefix with one new enough to accept -oso_prefix, removing the need for the 3-edit Homebrew oso_prefix gate (a local workaround, NOT an upstream candidate — see dar-pk4 analysis).

MINIMUM: CLT 11.0 / Xcode 11.0 = ld64-507 (first release with -oso_prefix). Version map: Xcode9=ld64-305 (current,broken), Xcode10=351, Xcode11.0=507, Xcode12=609, Xcode13.2=711. Tradeoff: newer clears -oso_prefix more safely but raises emulation risk (newer clang/ld need newer dyld/libsystem features Darling may not fully emulate); CLT 11 is closest-to-working-old while still >=507.

ALREADY ON DISK (host nhs, /home/ilyagulya/work):
- Command_Line_Tools_for_Xcode_13.2.pkg (591MB) — ld64-711, exactly the version Homebrew's own build warning requests ('download the Command Line Tools for Xcode 13.2.1').
- darling-prefix-homebrew-clt13/ + .workdir — a PRIOR attempt with CLT 13.2 already extracted; its ld is verified ld64-711. But the working test prefix STILL uses ld64-305, strongly implying the clt13 attempt hit emulation gaps and was not adopted (this is why we remained on the old CLT).
- darling-clt-pkgs/ — the current old CLT (CLTools_Executables.pkg + CLTools_SDK_macOS1013.pkg).
- clt-{deep-trace,sequence-case,version-plain}.rb — version-probing scripts from the prior attempt.

NEXT STEP (cheap before any reinstall): diagnose why darling-prefix-homebrew-clt13 was not adopted — build lz4 from source in it on ld64-711/clang-1300 and capture where it stalls. If it hits a specific emulation hole, decide fix-vs-fallback-to-CLT-11. Alternative track: option (A) in dar-pk4 — use Darling's own cctools ld64 with CLT clang via -fuse-ld, avoiding a CLT swap entirely.

NOTE: dar-e1j (getattrlist), getattrlistbulk, SDK symlink rename (3c79b03fa), and sandbox-exec stub (f85f220f0) are all DONE; -oso_prefix is the last remaining build-toolchain blocker for out-of-box source builds.
close_reason: CLT 13.2 (clang-1300/ld64-711) installed into homebrew-test via Darling's native installer; toolchain + Homebrew version-floor + -oso_prefix all validated; lz4 --build-from-source green (5/6). CLT-upgrade variable closed-success and the -oso_prefix gate is now a no-op on ld64-711. CLT 11.5 ruled out (fails Homebrew's macOS-11 floor >=12.5). Residual blocker for RELIABLE out-of-box builds is the intermittent (~17%) psynch/kwq lost-wakeup flake -> tracked in dar-gwn.1 (not a CLT issue).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
CLT 13.2 UPGRADE: DONE & VALIDATED (2026-06-11).

Installed CLT 13.2 into darling-prefix-homebrew-test via Darling's OWN native installer (no 7z/manual pbzx hacks needed -- Darling ships installer/hdiutil/pkgutil/xar):
  installer -verbose -pkg '/Volumes/SystemRoot/home/ilyagulya/work/Command_Line_Tools_for_Xcode_13.2.pkg' -target /   (RC=0)
(host / is visible inside Darling at /Volumes/SystemRoot; flat distribution .pkg fed directly, installer handled all component sub-pkgs + uninstalled prior CLT.)

RESULT: clang-1300.0.29.30, ld64-711, SDKs MacOSX11/11.3/12/12.1. Old CLTs preserved as CommandLineTools.clt305-bak / .clt11-bak.

VERIFICATION:
- Toolchain works: hello-world compile+link+run GREEN (printed output, rc=0); ld64-711 ACCEPTS -oso_prefix (no 'unknown option').
- Homebrew version-floor PASSES: floor is keyed on emulated MacOS.version (=11, darwin20) -> minimum CLT 12.5.0 / Xcode 12.2; CLT 13.2 clears it. (The 'update to Xcode 13.2.1' text is latest_version, not the floor.)
- lz4 --build-from-source GREEN: 'built in 30 seconds', real compile + codesign + dylib-id change.

CLT 11.5 was tried first (ld64-556, supports -oso_prefix) but REJECTED by Homebrew floor: emulated macOS 11 requires CLT>=12.5, and 11.5 reports as 11.7 -> 'too outdated', aborts before compiling. So 11.5 is a dead end despite supporting -oso_prefix; 13.2 is the right target.

RESIDUAL BLOCKER (NOT the CLT): intermittent deadlock. Flakiness run = 6 clean from-source rebuilds in one session -> 5 PASS / 1 HANG (~17%). Hang point FLOATS (one run stalled after checksum-verify, another at '✔︎ API Source lz4.rb' -- both early brew/Ruby stages, forked build child wedged before spawning any tar/clang subprocess). Floating point + Ruby's heavy pthread_cond_timedwait->psynch_cvwait usage => this is the psynch-class race targeted by dar-gwn.2 / branch fix/homebrew-psynch-ruby-hang. Deployed psynch fixes (libsystem_kernel eea332e6, libsystem_pthread c5f3541d, darlingserver 5bd7a59b -- all confirmed md5-identical build==prefix==homebrew-test, no dyld_shared_cache shadowing) REDUCED but did not ELIMINATE it.

CONCLUSION: CLT-upgrade variable is CLOSED-SUCCESS. -oso_prefix gate can be dropped on CLT 13.2 (ld64-711 supports it natively). The remaining obstacle to reliable out-of-box source builds is the residual flaky psynch deadlock -> hand off to dar-gwn.2.

title: Fix mldr raw-fork child deadlock on inherited glibc _rtld_global lock
description: After the SA_RESTART/Ruby blocking-read hang was fixed, full lz4 reproduction exposed a separate pre-exec child deadlock. Capture: /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260611T091302Z-lz4-sarestart-fix, child PID 3144244. GDB stack: futex_wait(_rtld_global+4344) -> __lll_lock_wait_private -> get_cached_stack -> allocate_stack -> __pthread_create_2_1 -> __darling_thread_create (src/startup/mldr/elfcalls/threads.c:196). Darling raw Linux fork bypasses glibc child lock fixups; child later creates a native mldr thread and deadlocks on inherited glibc stack-cache/loader state. Calling full native glibc fork was tested and rejected: it runs native callbacks and breaks launchd/bootstrap (bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260611T091902Z-fork-native-regressions). Find a fix that preserves Darling fork semantics while preventing/recovering inherited mldr/glibc locks.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Investigated glibc _Fork as a no-atfork replacement. Rejected before deployment: host _Fork@@GLIBC_2.34 disassembly is essentially raw clone plus robust-list reset, and glibc source shows only full __libc_fork calls reclaim_stacks()/resets loader locks. Therefore _Fork cannot repair the captured inherited dl_stack_cache_lock. Testing a narrower mldr fork lock that serializes raw fork with native pthread_create without running callbacks.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Narrow mldr fork-lock experiment rejected and reverted. It serialized raw fork against native pthread_attr/pthread_create and reset its own lock in the child, preserving bootstrap and passing a 20-task fork smoke. However the main lz4 series still reproduced: /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260611T093042Z-lz4flaky, runs 1-2 PASS, run 3 HANG after Formula lz4 before make. Therefore the inherited glibc lock window is broader than pthread_create alone (thread exit/stack reclaim and/or loader state); do not use this narrow serialization as the fix.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
=== dar-gwn.5 FULL STATE + HANDOFF (consolidated; earlier comments were lost to a br syntax error) ===

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed via gdb capture, child 3144244): after Darling's raw Linux fork, the child deadlocks in pthread_create -> allocate_stack -> get_cached_stack, blocked on glibc's GL(_dl_stack_cache_lock) (= _rtld_global+4344 on host glibc 2.39), inherited HELD because a parent native thread held it at fork time. __darling_thread_create backs every Darwin thread with a native pthread, so the child wedges before spawning any subprocess. This IS the flaky lz4/brew --build-from-source hang.

OPTIONS EVALUATED (all empirically tested with a RELIABLE shared-lib harness; do not re-tread):
- A METHODOLOGY TRAP: referencing _rtld_global with `extern` from an EXECUTABLE emits R_X86_64_COPY that snapshots ld.so's live loader state into BSS and desyncs it -> breaks ALL threading (even baseline). Must use a shared lib (PIC) OR dlsym at runtime.
- B (user-provided native stacks via pthread_attr_setstack to bypass get_cached_stack): INVALID. glibc takes _dl_stack_cache_lock to list_add the new thread to _dl_stack_used on the user-stack path too. Proven: poke lock held -> user-stack pthread_create still hangs.
- C (full native fork()/_Fork so glibc resets it): INVALID on glibc 2.34+. Proven: with lock held, BOTH fork() and _Fork() children still hang -> neither resets _dl_stack_cache_lock. Also full fork() separately breaks Darling's dserver child-checkin (bundle 20260611T091902Z: pid=1 launchd aborts on dserver_rpc code=-32). The atfork fear was unfounded (mldr links only -lrt -ldl -lm; Darwin atfork handlers run in emulated libc, not native fork scope).
- A (explicit child-side reset): WORKS. Proven: child sets lock=0 -> OK, +20 threads after -> OK. Discovery of the offset via startup layout signature finds 4344 reliably.

FIX IMPLEMENTED (Option A) - UNCOMMITTED working-tree change in src/startup/mldr/mldr.c (+77 lines):
- __mldr_detect_stack_cache_lock(): base = dlsym(RTLD_DEFAULT,"_rtld_global") (NOT extern - copy-reloc trap). Scan rtld_global for the stack-list signature: list_t _dl_stack_used (empty self-ref), list_t _dl_stack_user (1 elem = main thread), list_t _dl_stack_cache (empty self-ref), size_t actsize=0, uintptr in_flight=0, int lock=0. Store &lock and &cache. No hardcoded glibc offset; safe no-op if signature not found. Called once near top of main() while single-threaded.
- __mldr_postfork_child(): if located, empty _dl_stack_cache (self-ref), zero actsize + in_flight, then __atomic_store_n(lock,0) LAST. Runs in fork child before any thread create (sys_fork child path calls it via elfcalls vtable).

BUILD/DEPLOY:
- cd ~/work/darling-build && ninja src/startup/mldr/all   (mldr + mldr32, OK; only pre-existing stack.c warnings)
- cp built mldr,mldr32 -> ~/work/darling-prefix/libexec/darling/usr/libexec/darling/   (DEPLOYED; mldr md5 86ab0f85...)
- SAFETY: readelf -r on built mldr shows NO R_X86_64_COPY for _rtld_global (dlsym-only). Smoke: darling shell boot + fork/exec OK.

VALIDATION:
- lz4 round1 (MAKE_JOBS=1, bundle 20260611T104715Z-lz4flaky): runs 1-5 PASS, 0 HANG; run6 cut by the 800s runner timeout (not a failure).
- lz4 round2 IN PROGRESS in background (MAKE_JOBS=4, N=14, bundle 20260611T105328Z-lz4flaky2, script /tmp/run-lz4-flaky2.sh, log /tmp/lz4-validation-fix2.log): early - run1 FAIL rc=1 (Homebrew BUILD ERROR 'Read the above document', NOT a hang; likely MAKE_JOBS=4 flake), run2 PASS.
- Micro-harnesses saved: /tmp/fork_lib.c+driver2, /tmp/discover_lib.c+driver3 (reliable, run with sandbox disabled).

NEXT STEPS TO CLOSE (for the continuing session):
1. Check round2 result: success = ZERO 'HANG' lines across cumulative ~19+ builds. FAIL rc=1 build errors are a SEPARATE concern (fork deadlock = HANG only). If many FAILs, re-run a round at MAKE_JOBS=1 to rule out a parallel-build flake.
2. If 0 hangs: git add src/startup/mldr/mldr.c; commit on branch fix/homebrew-psynch-ruby-hang (e.g. 'mldr: reset glibc stack-cache lock in fork child to fix raw-fork pthread_create deadlock (dar-gwn.5)'); push. Then close dar-gwn.5.
3. Optional: deterministic regression under tests/regression (fork + native-thread-create under churn); pr-draft note.

DO NOT TOUCH: src/external/darlingserver (uncommitted submodule content) and pr-drafts/ - they belong to parallel dar-gwn.1/.2 work.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: RESOLVED. Validation conclusive: lz4 --build-from-source under Homebrew = 17 PASS / 0 HANG across 19 completed builds (round1 5/5 @ MAKE_JOBS=1; round2 12 pass/0 hang/2 fail @ MAKE_JOBS=4). The 2 fails were unrelated brew errors (run1 Tier-3 macOS-11 build failure; run5 'Failed to parse curl version' fetch flake), NOT hangs. title: os_unfair_lock SIGILL: sys_ulock_wait -EINTR cerror'd to -1 hits corruption_abort (ud2) under fork storm
description:
ORTHOGONAL to dar-gwn.1.8/.1.9 (the ETIMEDOUT, fixed). Surfaced while GREEN-verifying the assert-wait-cancel-stale-timer fix at HIGH fork/exec density.

SYMPTOM: ruby aborts with '[BUG] Illegal instruction at 0x...' at the backtick child-spawn line (sleep_stress2.rb:16), NOT the ETIMEDOUT line.

BACKTRACE (green-final.log, fix build c9ef337b):
  free_tiny (libsystem_malloc) +0x85
  <- sig_restorer (libsystem_kernel) +0x39
  <- sigexc_handler (libsystem_kernel) +0x15c
  <- handler_linux_to_bsd (libsystem_kernel) +0x39a
  ^ a Mach-exception/signal is delivered to a ruby IO thread while it is INSIDE free_tiny
    (rb_f_backquote -> io_fread -> rb_thread_io_blocking_call -> ... -> rb_gc_impl_free -> free_tiny).
  The Ruby (sigsegv/rb_bug_for_fatal_signal) frames are its own fatal-signal handler firing
  afterward, reporting the corruption.

ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis): sigexc_handler / handler_linux_to_bsd path runs heavy, async-signal-UNSAFE
work (state save/restore, possibly malloc) re-entrant on a thread that holds the malloc tiny-region
lock. Same dar-gwn SIGILL/SIGSEGV-cascade family as brew-sigchld-segv-rootcause. Distinct from the
DEBUG_SIGEXC kprintf flood (dar-gwn.1.9, fixed) and the stale-wait-timer ETIMEDOUT (dar-gwn.1.9 #2, fixed).

REPRO: ~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/sleep_stress2.rb at high ROUNDS (fork/exec storm). Evidence
log green-final.log line 16/42-48. Real brew spawns curl far LESS densely than the stressor, so
lower live risk -- but it is a genuine latent signal-safety bug.

NOT STARTED. publication-status would be: not-yet-investigated. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
STATIC ANALYSIS (2026-06-24, from green-final.log, fix build c9ef337b):

Resolved the topmost real C frame precisely against the LOADED libsystem_malloc.dylib (llvm-objdump): free_tiny+0x85 = 0x11625 = the instruction IMMEDIATELY AFTER 'callq _os_unfair_lock_lock_with_options' at 0x11620. So at signal time the faulting ruby IO thread had JUST returned from acquiring the tiny-region malloc MAGAZINE os_unfair_lock. NEW, sharper than dar-gwn.1.5's prior captures (which all showed the thread PARKED IN wait4/recvmsg at linux_syscall+9). Here the thread is in CPU-bound malloc lock code, NOT a syscall.

Top frames (sigsegv <- handler_linux_to_bsd+0x39a <- sigexc_handler+0x15c <- sig_restorer+0x39 <- free_tiny+0x85) = the sigexc delivery path forwarding a fatal signal to Ruby's generic fatal-signal handler 'sigsegv' (registered for SEGV/ILL/BUS/FPE). A fatal signal was DELIVERED while the thread held the malloc magazine lock.

OPEN: reported crash RIP 0x73e7eae1bf7f does NOT map into libsystem_kernel (base 0x73e7ea0d1000) nor malloc (base 0x73e7e9f58000); ~0xd4af7f above kernel base = unidentified higher module (likely dyld_shared_cache). Need a LIVE handler-side capture (catch fault INSIDE sigexc_handler, cache-aware symbols) to name the real fault module/insn at 0x...bf7f and read the ACTUAL si_signo/si_code. Exactly the missing tool dar-gwn.1.5 ended on.

SAME CLASS as dar-gwn.1.5. dar-gwn.6 = malloc-lock variant; dar-gwn.1.5 = parked-in-syscall variant. Likely one root fix. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE PINNED + FIXED (2026-06-24, live capture + TDD RED):

LIVE CAPTURE (env-gated [dargwn6] trace added to sigexc_handler; high-density fork storm, RUNS=8 NWORK=10 ROUNDS=600):
  [dargwn6] FATAL sig: linux_in=4 bsd_out=4 si_code=2 si_addr=0x...3EF7F rip=0x...3EF7F
  -> linux_in=4 == Linux SIGILL, bsd_out=4 == BSD SIGILL: the server did NOT mis-tag/resend the signal. GENUINE SIGILL, faithfully delivered. (REFUTES the dar-gwn.1.5 'spurious resent signal' framing for THIS variant.) si_code=2 = ILL_ILLOPN; si_addr==rip == the faulting PC.

SYMBOLIZED (symbolize.sh vs the live boot's /proc maps): fault RIP = libsystem_platform.dylib __os_unfair_lock_lock_slow+0x13f = a ud2 (deliberate trap) reached via _os_unfair_lock_corruption_abort. The C-backtrace frame free_tiny+0x85 is the return addr right after 'callq _os_unfair_lock_lock_with_options' -> the thread was contending the malloc tiny-magazine os_unfair_lock.

MECHANISM: os_unfair_lock slow path calls __ulock_wait(UL_UNFAIR_LOCK|ULF_NO_ERRNO,...) and does : EINTR/EFAULT -> retry, EOWNERDEAD -> abort, DEFAULT -> __LIBPLATFORM_INTERNAL_CRASH__ (ud2). Darling's sys_ulock_wait (libsystem_kernel emulation/.../psynch/ulock_wait.c) under ULF_NO_ERRNO did:
   ret = errno_linux_to_bsd(ret);   // futex(FUTEX_WAIT) interrupted -> -EINTR(-4), correct
   if (no_errno) ret &= ~0x800;     // BUG: -4 & ~0x800 = -2052
-2052 is not EINTR/EFAULT/EOWNERDEAD -> default -> ud2 -> SIGILL. The fork/exec SIGCHLD storm is essential because it is what makes futex(FUTEX_WAIT) return -EINTR while a thread holds the magazine lock. The  is applied to an already-NEGATIVE value and corrupts EVERY futex error (verified: EINTR/EFAULT/EOWNERDEAD/ETIMEDOUT/EAGAIN all map to fatal values). There is no 0x800 errno tag in this ABI (the caller does plain switch(-ret)); upstream XNU just does *retval = -ret.

FIX: remove the bogus  (and the matching  early return) so sys_ulock_wait returns a clean negated BSD errno. Same bug+fix in ulock_wake.c. NOT a sigexc/darlingserver bug -- a libsystem_kernel syscall-emulation bug. TDD GREEN in progress. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
GREEN VERIFIED (2026-06-24): The ulock SIGILL is the WRONG layer for a value-fix; the REAL mechanism + fix:

WHY the first fix (drop the 0x800 mask, return -EINTR) did NOT help -- live ulock trace showed sys_ulock_wait correctly returned bsd=-4 (EINTR), yet it still crashed at __os_unfair_lock_lock_slow+0x13f (ud2). Disassembling the ___ulock_wait libsyscall stub (libsystem_kernel 0x2677c) revealed: callq __darling_bsd_syscall; cmpq -0xfff,rax; jb pass; else negq + jmp cerror_nocancel. Darling's BSD-syscall stub treats ANY return in [-4095,-1] as a failed syscall and routes it through cerror -> returns -1. So -EINTR(-4) became -1. The caller does switch(-ret): -1 is not EINTR/EFAULT/EOWNERDEAD -> _os_unfair_lock_corruption_abort -> ud2 -> SIGILL. (The original 0x800 hack was ALSO broken for the same reason: -2052 is still in errno range.) There is no way to smuggle a small -errno past the generic cerror stub, and os_unfair_lock is unmodifiable vendored code expecting a raw -errno.

FINAL FIX (libsystem_kernel emulation .../psynch/ulock_wait.c): retry futex(FUTEX_WAIT) internally on -EINTR for untimed (timeout==0) UL_COMPARE_AND_WAIT/UL_UNFAIR_LOCK waits, instead of returning EINTR. Semantically identical to what os_unfair_lock would do (case EINTR: continue) and to macOS's internal ulock restart, but avoids handing a spurious errno to the corruption-abort path. ulock_wake.c: dropped the matching bogus 0x800 mask too.

VERIFICATION (RUNS=8 NWORK=10 ROUNDS=600 fork/exec storm, deployed libsystem_kernel cafca076): RUN1 ran PAST ROUND 250 (baseline died ~ROUND 200) with 0 Illegal-instruction, 0 Ruby-handler SIGILL, 0 ulock_wait failure. dar-gwn.6 SIGILL ELIMINATED.

NEW ORTHOGONAL FAILURE EXPOSED (belongs to dar-gwn.1.5, NOT this bead): with the SIGILL gone, the same storm now kills ruby at ROUND ~250 via SIGFPE(8) delivered with si_code=0 (SI_USER) + SIGCHLD-shaped payload si_addr=0x3E8000014C6=(uid1000<<32|childpid 5318) + handler=0x0 (SIG_DFL -> terminate, silent, no Ruby [BUG]). This is the dar-gwn.1.5 reaped-child-signal-mistagged-as-fatal-signal class surfacing directly once the ulock cascade is removed. Recording to dar-gwn.1.5. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: REGRESSION TRAP found 2026-06-25 while starting dar-gwn.1.5: a libsystem_kernel build off the xnu submodule's checked-out integration/homebrew (HEAD ac4e340, an E-UNION experiment tip) SILENTLY REGRESSES this fix — ulock_wait.c there is the ORIGINAL buggy version (no EINTR retry). Symptom: fresh build crashed at __os_unfair_lock_lock_slow+0x13f rax=-1 (pure dar-gwn.6). The fix lives ONLY on fix/ulock-wait-eintr-retry @9d5c222 + the patch; integration/homebrew HEAD does not carry it as a commit. Mitigation before building: git checkout 9d5c222 -- the two ulock files, OR west patch verify. Verify compiled in: _sys_ulock_wait untimed path must show cmpl $-0x4,%eax; je <back to futex>. Known-good clean fix dylib md5 8393f649; fix+dar-gwn.1.5-trace dylib md5 8cec5efd. Nothing pushed.

title: Rebuild+redeploy libsystem_kernel.dylib WITH dar-gwn.6 EINTR-retry fix (deploy drifted: 228d19a33a9c != 8393f649)
description:
The DEPLOYED libsystem_kernel.dylib appears to lack the dar-gwn.6 ulock-EINTR-retry fix, causing brew install to crash (0x3e8 SIGCHLD-mistag SEGV) / hang at the libunistring ./configure phase under the fork storm.

EVIDENCE OF DRIFT: source tree HAS the fix -- xnu .../libsystem_kernel/emulation/src/xnu_syscall/bsd/impl/psynch/ulock_wait.c contains the `do { ret = futex(WAIT) } while (ret == -LINUX_EINTR && timeout == 0)` retry loop (the dar-gwn.6 fix). But the deployed dylib is md5 228d19a33a9c, which does NOT match the memory's clean-fix build 8393f649. So the deployed binary was likely built from a tree/commit without the fix (same patchset-drift class as dar-gax / the dar-gwn.6 REGRESSION TRAP).

DO:
 1. Confirm via disasm: `_sys_ulock_wait` untimed path in the deployed dylib should show the EINTR(-4)/futex retry (`cmpl $-0x4,%eax; je <back to futex>`). If absent -> regressed.
 2. Rebuild libsystem_kernel WITH the fix (ensure ulock_wait.c/ulock_wake.c match fix/ulock-wait-eintr-retry @9d5c222; or `west patch verify` clean). See memory dar-gwn6-ulock-eintr-sigill for the exact build/deploy steps and the disasm check.
 3. Redeploy into the DPREFIX UPPER overlay layer (the guest loads from $DPREFIX/usr/lib/system, not the build copy -- deploy trap from memory dar-gwn16-pathnull-guard).
 4. Re-run brew reinstall wget (~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/brew-install-validate.sh) and confirm BOTH the crash and the hang clear (BREW_REINSTALL_RC=0, INSTALLED_OK, 0 signatures).

Reference: memory dar-gwn6-ulock-eintr-sigill, patchset-drift-integration-homebrew, dar-gwn15-was-dargwn6-regression. Constraint: nothing pushed without explicit approval.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
dar-gwn.6.1 done-as-deployed (drift was real but NOT the cause); recording on the bead.
Rebuilt+redeployed libsystem_kernel.dylib b66bb1b41d7b with EINTR(-4) retry confirmed live in BOTH prefixes (main + homebrew-test). brew reinstall wget STILL crashed at libunistring with the same 0x3e8 SEGV => the deployed-dylib drift was NOT the cause of dar-gwn.6.2. The drift fix is correct hygiene and is now deployed, but the libunistring crash is a separate server-side RPC race (setPendingCall 'overwritten while active'); see dar-gwn.6.2. Marking 6.1 done (deployment objective met); 6.2 stays open with the real root cause.

title: libunistring fork-storm chain: ingest-drop FIXED + pthread_canceled livelock FIXED (6.3 closed); now residual 0x3e8 SEGV -> dar-gwn.6.4
description:
brew reinstall wget HANGS at the libunistring dependency phase (a second face of the dar-gwn.6 fork-storm family; the first face is the crash). Captured 2026-06-26 on darlingserver 612f8cd096d3 (clean, no dar-gwn.1.11 patch) + deployed libsystem_kernel.dylib 228d19a33a9c.

SIGNATURE: after ~51 min stuck at "==> Installing wget dependency: libunistring", the guest process set is FROZEN (youngest mldr ~51 min old, ZERO new forks), 16 guest threads in __skb_wait_for_more_packets (recvmsg), server has only 2 threads (main ep_poll, one worker futex idle), dserver.log EMPTY, no DSERVER FAULT / no WAITQPANIC / no ETIMEDOUT. 1 guest thread on-CPU not forking. So it is a WEDGE, not a crash and not dar-gwn.1.11.

RELATION: in the immediately prior run the SAME libunistring phase CRASHED with the dar-gwn.6 0x3e8<<32 SIGCHLD-mistag SEGV (ulock_wait -EINTR -> os_unfair_lock ud2, cascade "SEGV received in SEGV handler"). Crash vs hang at the same point = the two faces of the intermittent fork-storm dar-gwn.6 bug. Likely root: the DEPLOYED libsystem_kernel.dylib drifted and lacks the dar-gwn.6 EINTR-retry fix (228d19a33a9c != clean-fix 8393f649); see the sibling redeploy task and dar-gax (patchset drift).

EVIDENCE: ~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/brew-wedge-snap-1782437687/ (server-threads.txt, guest-threads.txt). Harness: ~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/brew-install-validate.sh (hardened: verdict from stdout markers, not exit code; in-guest `timeout` removed).

NEXT: first redeploy the dar-gwn.6-fixed dylib (sibling task), then re-run brew install. If the hang persists with the fix present, dig as a distinct lost-RPC site (use darling-rpc-loss-watch D2). Constraint: nothing pushed without explicit approval.
close_reason: Completed for this bead: ingest-drop error replies were implemented and validated; residual libunistring failure is localized and tracked separately in dar-gwn.6.4 / dar-gwn.1.10.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
RE-ROOTED 2026-06-26: the libunistring crash is NOT the dar-gwn.6 ulock-EINTR drift. It is a server-side RPC race: "Thread's pending call overwritten while active".

WHAT WE DID (dar-gwn.6.1, the drift hypothesis): the deployed libsystem_kernel.dylib was indeed stale (228d19a33a9c; source ulock_wait.c was edited AFTER the last build). Rebuilt -> b66bb1b41d7b, confirmed the EINTR(-4) retry IS present in the deployed binary (llvm-objdump: futex callq @0x377eb immediately followed by `cmpl $-0x4,%eax` @0x377f0). Deployed into BOTH the main prefix and darling-prefix-homebrew-test (the prefix brew actually loads from; its usr/lib/system/ was also stale). No dyld_shared_cache to override. So the fix is provably live.

RESULT: brew reinstall wget STILL crashed at the SAME libunistring phase, same 0x000003e80000130c (0x3e8=uid 1000 SIGCHLD-mistag) SEGV. => the dylib drift was NOT the cause. dar-gwn.6.1 is done-as-deployed but does not fix this.

ACTUAL ROOT (from the crash frames + dserver.log, this run):
 - Ruby crash control frames: c:0031 CFUNC :wait  <- c:0030 formula.rb:3560 BLOCK <- c:0029 CFUNC :open  (formula.rb:3519). This is the IO.popen / Process.wait CHILD-REAPING path, not malloc/os_unfair_lock.
 - dserver.log (exactly once): "(server, Error) Dropping message: callFromMessage threw: Thread's pending call overwritten while active".
 - The server SURVIVED (RESULT=no-server-fault, srv stayed up) BECAUSE the processcall-exception-guard.patch is deployed (strings confirm both "pending call overwritten while active" and "Dropping message" in the 612f8cd096d3 binary). That guard is a SURVIVAL guard, not a fix: it converts "the throw kills the whole server" into "log + DROP just this one message". The dropped message is the reply the guest :wait/:open call was blocked on -> guest gets no/corrupt wakeup under the signal storm -> 0x3e8 SEGV death-rattle ("SEGV received in SEGV handler / ABRT ...").

THE BUG (thread.cpp:269 setPendingCall): a SECOND RPC arrives on a thread that still has _pendingCall set. Only Call::Number::InterruptEnter is buffered gracefully (pushed to _pendingInterrupts); ANY OTHER call in that window throws "overwritten while active". Under brew's fork+SIGCHLD storm a non-Interrupt call (the reaping-path RPC) races the still-pending call and is dropped. Same RPC-protocol FAMILY as dar-gwn.1.7/1.10 (lost reply -> guest recvmsg strand), but a DISTINCT site: the pending-call-overwrite race itself, which the guard only contains, never resolves.

NEXT (the real fix, needs design): make setPendingCall not LOSE the racing call. Options to weigh: (a) buffer the second call the way InterruptEnter is buffered (queue + drain when the active call suspends/exits) instead of throwing; (b) understand WHY a guest thread issues a 2nd RPC while one is still pending (protocol invariant violation? a signal-delivery path injecting a call onto a thread mid-RPC?) and stop the overwrite at the source. (a) is symptom-contain++ ; (b) is the true fix. Reproduces every brew reinstall wget at libunistring (crash) — deterministic enough to drive a fix.

EVIDENCE: /tmp/claude-1000/.../scratchpad/brew-validate-run.log (this run). Harness ~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/brew-install-validate.sh. Constraint: nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
PIVOT 2026-06-26 (investigation (b)): the libunistring crash is NOT (only) the pending-call-overwrite. Two runs, two DIFFERENT server-side lost-reply sites at the same crash:

 - RUN #1 (06:18): server.cpp drop -> "Dropping message: callFromMessage threw: Thread's pending call overwritten while active" (thread.cpp:279 setPendingCall, non-interrupt 2nd call while _pendingCall set).
 - RUN #2 (06:36, with the DARGWN62 diagnostic armed): NO overwrite, NO DARGWN62 line at all. The only fresh server entry was call.cpp:85 "Received call from non-existent process?" -> the call is SILENTLY IGNORED with NO reply (explicit TODO at call.cpp:88 admits it should return -ESRCH but returns nullptr). This fires when processRegistry().registerIfAbsent's factory throws std::system_error constructing Process from /proc/<pid> (line 76) because the forked child's pid already vanished (exited/reaped) by the time its RPC is processed.

BOTH are lost-reply sites: guest sends an RPC, server discards it (drop OR silent-ignore), guest recvmsg never completes -> under the SIGCHLD/fork storm the parked thread becomes the 0x3e8<<32 SEGV death-rattle ("SEGV received in SEGV handler"). Same crash signature (0x000003e8...130c / ...1315), same libunistring phase, same :wait/:open reaping path -> these are TWO FACES of one root class: the server fails to reply when a fork-storm race makes the target process/thread momentarily inconsistent. The pending-call-overwrite is therefore NOT "the" bug; it's one symptom among >=2.

CONSEQUENCE for the fix: a fix that only addresses setPendingCall (mechanism (a)/(b1)) will NOT stop the crash -- run #2 proves the crash recurs via the non-existent-process path with zero overwrite. The unifying fix must guarantee EVERY ingest-time discard path sends an error reply (-ESRCH / -EINVAL) to the waiting guest instead of dropping silently, so the guest syscall returns an errno instead of hanging->SEGV. Sites to cover: call.cpp:85 (non-existent process), call.cpp:114 (non-existent thread, same silent-ignore TODO), server.cpp drop-on-throw (already logs, but drops with no reply), thread.cpp setPendingCall throw.

OPEN QUESTION before coding: is the lost reply the PRIMARY cause, or does the guest ALSO mis-handle a legitimately-absent process (i.e. should that child even be issuing an RPC)? Need frequency + ordering data: how many ignore/drop events per run, and does one deterministically precede every crash. Next instrument: count+timestamp all 4 discard sites and correlate with the crash, rather than reading single occurrences.

Diagnostic server 9113d3b6fd4e deployed (adds DARGWN62 line; otherwise == 612f8cd096d3). Nothing pushed. Backups: bin/darlingserver.bak-612f8cd-*, and the dylib .bak-228d19a-* from 6.1.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
FIX BUILT + VALIDATED 2026-06-26. Variant 1 (unifying error-reply) implemented and deployed.

THE FIX (clean branch fix/ingest-error-reply @ ddfc263, on top of fix/processcall-exception-guard a52a15e):
 - New Call::sendErrorReplyFromHeader(header, replyAddress, code): builds a minimal reply (dserver_rpc_replyhdr_t {number, code}) from the raw call header -- no constructed Call object needed, which is why the ingest sites couldn't reply before.
 - Wired at all 3 ingest discard sites in callFromMessage():
     * non-existent process (call.cpp:85)  -> reply -ESRCH
     * non-existent thread  (call.cpp:114) -> reply -ESRCH
     * pending-call-overwrite -> caught at its SOURCE (around the thread->setPendingCall call) -> reply -EAGAIN, instead of unwinding to the Server::start() guard which dropped silently.
   For a body-bearing call the guest wrapper sees a short reply and returns -ECOMM; for a body-less call it returns the code. Either way the parked guest recvmsg RETURNS instead of hanging into a SEGV.
 - Integrated: patches.yml entry darlingserver/ingest-error-reply.patch (sha256 5c6ce3ff..., west-format, bead dar-gwn.6.2, publication-status: blocked). west patch verify = 51 patches clean. Integration regenerated (integration/homebrew, darlingserver HEAD 0eef1d3). Built+deployed server 684904978871 (integration + this patch + fault tracer).

VALIDATION (brew reinstall wget, fault tracer armed):
 - The 0x3e8 SEGV / pending-call-overwrite crash DID NOT RECUR. No [BUG], no Segmentation, no DSERVER FAULT, server stayed alive. The crash that aborted runs #1/#2 in ~4-5 min at libunistring is GONE.
 - BUT the install now HANGS at the SAME libunistring phase (~60 min, no progress). State: the ruby build child (mldr 3043572) is parked in recvmsg (__skb_wait_for_more_packets) waiting for an RPC reply; the server is IDLE (main thread ep_poll + 1 worker futex_wait); dserver.log has ZERO fresh entries this run (epochs only 06:18/06:36 from prior runs) -> my -ESRCH/-EAGAIN paths NEVER FIRED. The build never started (log stops at "Installing wget dependency: libunistring", no configure output).

INTERPRETATION: the fix is CORRECT and load-bearing for the crash class it targets (the crash is cured, server no longer killed/cascaded). It UNMASKED a separate, pre-existing lost-RPC-reply stall (dar-gwn.1.7/1.10 family: guest waits forever for a reply the idle server never sends) that the fast crash was previously preempting. Crash -> hang is progress (no more memory corruption / SEGV cascade), but brew is still not green.

NEXT (distinct from the ingest fix): root-cause the lost-RPC stall at libunistring. The fix path did NOT fire, so it's not a dropped/ignored ingest message -- the call was accepted but its reply never produced/sent (the dar-gwn.1.10 "Call produced but never enqueued" / handler-hole shape, or a genuine in-server deadlock). Use darling-rpc-loss-watch D2 + DSERVER_LOG_LEVEL=debug to name the stuck call. Snapshot: ~/work/dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/fix-stall-snap-1782463594 (server-threads, guest-threads, dserver.log, harness-tail).

Nothing pushed. Backups: bin/darlingserver.bak-* (612f8cd, diag 9113d3b), dylib .bak-228d19a-*.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
DUG THE UNMASKED STALL 2026-06-26 (DSERVER_LOG_LEVEL=debug + rpc-loss-watch D2 on the fixed server 684904978871). The "hang" is primarily a LIVELOCK, with a nameable driver.

WHAT THE DEBUG LOG SHOWS (stalled brew driver = portable-ruby, nstid 124):
 - call recv-vs-reply is nearly balanced (207891 recv / 207887 reply, only ~4 outstanding) -> NOT a classic lost-reply.
 - ONE call dominates everything: #31 dserver_callnum_pthread_canceled = 167168 calls (next is psynch_cvwait 17578). The server handler Call::PthreadCanceled::processCall() is a TODO STUB: logs "TODO:" and returns -ENOSYS (-38), every time.
 - Live rate while "stalled": pthread_canceled ~670/sec, dserver.log growing ~950KB/sec, mldr count climbing -> the process is BURNING CPU in a tight loop on TID 124: Received #31 -> reply -38 -> Received #31 -> ... with nothing else interleaved. rpc-loss-watch flags it as a stall because threads momentarily park in recvmsg between RPC bursts, but it is a LIVELOCK, not a dead hang.

THE BUG (server stub vs real XNU semantics):
 - Real XNU __pthread_canceled(action) (bsd/kern/kern_sig.c:1037): action 1 -> clear UT_CANCELDISABLE, return 0; action 2 -> set UT_CANCELDISABLE, return 0; action 0 -> if (flags==UT_CANCEL) mark canceled return 0 else return EINVAL.
 - Darling darlingserver Call::PthreadCanceled::processCall() (src/call.cpp:698): returns -ENOSYS for ALL actions. So the guest's cancellation state machine never gets the 0 / EINVAL it expects.
 - Guest path: libpthread pthread_cancelable.c uses __pthread_canceled(0/1/2); _pthread_exit_if_canceled() (line 209) does `if ((error & 0xff) == EINTR && __pthread_canceled(0) == 0) pthread_exit(CANCELED)`. The CANCELATION_POINT() macro (cancelable.h) does `if (sys_pthread_canceled(0) == 0) return -EINTR`. With the stub returning -38 (never 0, never the EINVAL XNU returns), the cancellation handshake is broken; under brew's cancelable-syscall + fork storm the main thread spins re-issuing pthread_canceled forever.

RELATION TO THE 0x3e8 SEGV: the same run, under debug-log timing, EVENTUALLY still hit the guest [BUG] 0x3e8 SEGV at libunistring -- BUT with ZERO fresh server-side faults/drops this run (the verdict's RESULT=FAULT-server-crashed + Dropping-message=1 were FALSE POSITIVES from STALE appended logs: the only DSERVER FAULT is t=1782463601 pid=3041714 from the prior killed run; fresh epoch >=1782464700 has zero drop/overwrite/fault lines). So the pending-call-overwrite crash IS cured by the ingest-error-reply fix; the residual 0x3e8 SEGV is downstream of the pthread_canceled livelock's fork/signal churn (dar-gwn.6 SIGCHLD-mistag family).

CONCLUSION: the unmasked failure at libunistring is the pthread_canceled TODO-stub livelock. FIX CANDIDATE: implement Call::PthreadCanceled (and likely PthreadMarkcancel) to XNU semantics in darlingserver duct-tape -- per-thread cancel/canceldisable state: action1 clear-disable->0, action2 set-disable->0, action0 -> canceled?0:EINVAL. This needs the duct-tape thread to carry uu_flag-equivalent cancel bits. Distinct bead from the ingest-error-reply fix (that one stands: crash cured).

EVIDENCE: dserver debug log snapshot /tmp/.../scratchpad/dsl-snap.log (1.13M lines); rpc-loss-watch.log.stdout (D2 alert naming ruby nstid 124 multi-thread recvmsg + server idle). Server 684904978871 (integration + ingest-error-reply + fault tracer). Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
=== dar-gwn.6.2 CONSOLIDATED MAP (handoff 2026-06-26) ===

THE QUESTION THIS BEAD CHASED: brew reinstall wget reliably fails at the SAME phase ("Installing wget dependency: libunistring"). Established it is NOT one bug but a SEQUENCE of distinct server-side defects, each unmasked as the previous is fixed. Here is the full chain, what is fixed, and what remains.

------------------------------------------------------------------
LAYER 0 (RULED OUT): deployed dylib drift (dar-gwn.6.1)
  The deployed libsystem_kernel.dylib was stale (228d19a, missing the dar-gwn.6 ulock-EINTR retry). REBUILT b66bb1b41d7b WITH the EINTR(-4) retry (confirmed via llvm-objdump in the deployed binary) and redeployed to BOTH prefixes. brew STILL crashed -> drift was NOT the cause. dar-gwn.6.1 CLOSED (deploy hygiene done, but not the fix).

------------------------------------------------------------------
LAYER 1 (FIXED, load-bearing): ingest-time silent message drops
  Root: callFromMessage() had 3 ingest paths that discarded a managed RPC with NO reply to the parked guest thread:
    * non-existent process (call.cpp:85)
    * non-existent thread  (call.cpp:114)
    * pending-call-overwrite (Thread::setPendingCall throws -> unwinds to the server-loop guard, which logs "Dropping message" + drops)
  A guest thread blocks in recvmsg awaiting the reply; a dropped reply = permanent hang, which under the fork/signal storm degrades into the 0x3e8<<32 SIGCHLD-mistag SEGV (dar-gwn.6 family).
  FIX (clean branch fix/ingest-error-reply @ ddfc263, on top of fix/processcall-exception-guard a52a15e):
    new Call::sendErrorReplyFromHeader(header, replyAddr, code) builds a minimal {number,code} reply from the raw header (no Call object needed); wired at all 3 sites: non-existent process/thread -> -ESRCH, pending-overwrite -> -EAGAIN (caught at its source in callFromMessage, not unwound to the drop-guard).
    Integrated: patches.yml entry darlingserver/ingest-error-reply.patch (sha256 5c6ce3ff..., bead dar-gwn.6.2, publication-status: blocked). west patch verify = 51 patches clean. integration/homebrew regenerated (darlingserver tip incl. 0eef1d3). Built+deployed server 684904978871 (integration + this patch + dar-fwl fault tracer).
  VALIDATION: across 2 fixed-build runs, ZERO fresh pending-call-overwrite drops; the crash that aborted runs in ~5min is CURED. (One run's verdict showed RESULT=FAULT/Dropping=1 but those were FALSE POSITIVES on STALE appended logs -- the only DSERVER FAULT is t=1782463601 from a prior killed run; fresh epochs had zero.)

------------------------------------------------------------------
LAYER 2 (NAMED, NOT FIXED -- the current blocker): pthread_canceled TODO-stub LIVELOCK
  With Layer 1 fixed, brew no longer crashes fast; it LIVELOCKS at libunistring. Debug-log (DSERVER_LOG_LEVEL=debug) on the stalled brew driver (portable-ruby, nstid 124):
    - #31 dserver_callnum_pthread_canceled = 167168 calls (next: psynch_cvwait 17578). recv vs reply nearly balanced (207891/207887) -> NOT a lost reply.
    - Live rate ~670 pthread_canceled/sec, log +950KB/sec -> tight loop on TID 124: Received #31 -> reply -38 -> repeat, nothing interleaved.
  ROOT: Call::PthreadCanceled::processCall() (src/call.cpp:698) is a TODO stub returning -ENOSYS(-38) for EVERY action. Real XNU __pthread_canceled(action) (bsd/kern/kern_sig.c:1037):
    action 1 -> clear UT_CANCELDISABLE, return 0
    action 2 -> set   UT_CANCELDISABLE, return 0
    action 0 -> if (uu_flag & (CANCELDISABLE|CANCEL|CANCELED))==UT_CANCEL: set CANCELED, return 0; else return EINVAL
  Guest consumers (libpthread/src/pthread_cancelable.c): pthread_setcancelstate(ENABLE/DISABLE) -> __pthread_canceled(1)/(2); CANCELATION_POINT() macro (cancelable.h) -> `if (sys_pthread_canceled(0)==0) return -EINTR`; _pthread_exit_if_canceled (line 209) -> `if ((error&0xff)==EINTR && __pthread_canceled(0)==0) pthread_exit`. With the stub never returning the canonical 0/EINVAL, the cancellation handshake is broken; under the cancelable-syscall + fork storm the thread spins re-issuing pthread_canceled forever.

------------------------------------------------------------------
LAYER 3 (residual, downstream): the 0x3e8 SEGV tail
  The fixed-build run still eventually shows the guest [BUG] 0x3e8 SEGV at libunistring, but with ZERO fresh server faults/drops. It is downstream of the Layer-2 livelock's fork/signal churn (dar-gwn.6 SIGCHLD-mistag family). Expect it to recede once the livelock is fixed; re-evaluate AFTER Layer 2.

------------------------------------------------------------------
NET: 1 bug fixed (ingest drops, load-bearing, deployed, not pushed), 1 bug NAMED and ready to implement (pthread_canceled livelock), 1 residual to re-check after. brew is NOT yet green. Nothing pushed.

EVIDENCE: pthread-canceled-livelock-1782465106/ (call histogram, sample, D2 alert); dsl-snap.log (1.13M-line debug log); brew-wedge-snap-1782437687 (earlier hang). Server 684904978871 deployed; backups bin/darlingserver.bak-* + dylib .bak-228d19a-*.

Nothing pushed.

Remaining work = deliver the fatal EXC_BAD_ACCESS to the guest task handler (dar-gwn.6.4 task #77). The 0x3e8 SIGFPE/SI_USER faults are the sigexc kill(0,signum) cascade, not the cause. So the dar-gwn.6.2 chain's final blocker is now precisely localized to the gdtoa-OOM exception delivery. Nothing pushed.

title: Implement __pthread_canceled/markcancel to XNU semantics (fix libunistring livelock)
description:
# Implement __pthread_canceled (and pthread_markcancel) to XNU semantics in darlingserver

## Problem (the current brew blocker)
`brew reinstall wget` LIVELOCKS at "Installing wget dependency: libunistring". The brew driver (portable-ruby) spins issuing `#31 dserver_callnum_pthread_canceled` ~670 times/sec (167168 in one capture; recv≈reply, so NOT a lost reply -- a livelock). The server burns CPU forever and the build never starts.

## Root cause
`DarlingServer::Call::PthreadCanceled::processCall()` (darling/src/external/darlingserver/src/call.cpp:698) is a TODO STUB: it logs "TODO:" and returns `-ENOSYS` (-38) for EVERY action. That diverges from XNU and breaks the guest's pthread cancellation handshake, causing the spin.

Real XNU `__pthread_canceled(int action)` (darling/src/external/xnu/bsd/kern/kern_sig.c:1037) uses per-uthread flags (UT_CANCELDISABLE, UT_CANCEL, UT_CANCELED, UT_NO_SIGMASK):
  - action 1 -> `uu_flag &= ~UT_CANCELDISABLE;  return 0;`   (cancel-enable)
  - action 2 -> `uu_flag |=  UT_CANCELDISABLE;  return 0;`   (cancel-disable)
  - action 0 (or default) ->
        if ((uu_flag & (UT_CANCELDISABLE|UT_CANCEL|UT_CANCELED)) == UT_CANCEL) {
            uu_flag &= ~UT_CANCEL; uu_flag |= (UT_CANCELED | UT_NO_SIGMASK); return 0;
        }
        return EINVAL;

Guest consumers (darling/src/external/libpthread/src/pthread_cancelable.c):
  - `pthread_setcancelstate(ENABLE)`  -> `__pthread_canceled(1)` (line 128)
  - `pthread_setcancelstate(DISABLE)` -> `__pthread_canceled(2)` (line 131)
  - `_pthread_exit_if_canceled()`     -> `if ((error&0xff)==EINTR && __pthread_canceled(0)==0) pthread_exit(PTHREAD_CANCELED)` (line 209)
  - `CANCELATION_POINT()` macro (.../libsystem_kernel/.../bsdthread/cancelable.h) -> `if (sys_pthread_canceled(0)==0) return -EINTR;`
Guest syscall impl: .../libsystem_kernel/emulation/src/xnu_syscall/bsd/impl/bsdthread/pthread_canceled.c (already does `if (action==0 && !uses_threads()) return -EINVAL; else dserver_rpc_pthread_canceled(action)`).
The companion `__pthread_markcancel(port)` -> Call::PthreadMarkcancel::processCall() (call.cpp:707) is ALSO a -ENOSYS stub and must set UT_CANCEL on the TARGET thread (this is how one thread requests cancellation of another -- pthread_cancel).

## Fix plan (server-side, darlingserver duct-tape) -- clean fix/* branch, NOT pushed
1. Carry per-thread cancel state on the duct-tape thread. Add bits to struct dtape_thread (duct-tape/internal-include/darlingserver/duct-tape/thread.h) -- e.g. `bool cancel_disable; bool cancel_pending; bool canceled;` (mirror UT_CANCELDISABLE/UT_CANCEL/UT_CANCELED). Provide dtape accessors if call.cpp can't touch the struct directly (follow the fatal_exception_delivery precedent added for dar-gwn.1.10).
2. Implement Call::PthreadCanceled::processCall() per the action table above, operating on the CURRENT thread's bits, returning 0 / EINVAL (NOT -ENOSYS). NOTE the XNU return for action 0 not-canceled is +EINVAL (the guest macro/`==0` checks treat any non-zero as "not canceled", so returning -EINVAL or EINVAL both avoid the spin -- match XNU: action0 returns EINVAL (positive in-kernel, becomes the syscall errno path); verify the guest errno_linux_to_bsd handling so the wrapper sees a clean non-zero, never -ENOSYS).
3. Implement Call::PthreadMarkcancel::processCall(): resolve the target via Thread::threadForPort(_body.thread_port) (already partially scaffolded at call.cpp:710), set that thread's cancel_pending bit, return 0.
4. Keep PthreadKill (call.cpp:682) as-is unless evidence implicates it.

## Validation (REQUIRED before claiming fixed)
- TDD micro-repro FIRST (cheaper than brew): a guest program that loops a cancelable syscall under pthread_setcancelstate enable/disable + a pending cancel, on the deployed server. RED = pthread_canceled spin (watch the dserver.log #31 rate or rpc-loss-watch D2). GREEN = no spin, syscall returns cleanly. Model on ~/work/dar-gwn110-repro/ harness style.
- Then brew reinstall wget under DSERVER_LOG_LEVEL=debug: #31 pthread_canceled count must drop from ~167k to O(normal), libunistring must get PAST "Installing dependency" into configure/build. Use the brew-install-debuglog.sh harness + darling-rpc-loss-watch D2. Watch for the Layer-3 residual 0x3e8 SEGV (separate bead) re-surfacing once the livelock clears.

## Build/deploy/integrate (workspace rules)
- Edit on a clean fix/* branch off the same base as sibling darlingserver fixes (89751e6) OR stacked on integration tip if it depends on prior fixes; build in /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-build (ninja src/external/darlingserver/darlingserver); deploy to /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix/bin/darlingserver (launcher's binary, shared by the homebrew-test prefix).
- Package as a patch: `git format-patch --no-signature --no-numbered --subject-prefix=PATCH --full-index --binary --no-renames -1 <commit>` (west's exact format -- otherwise `west patch verify` reports "patch export drifted"); add entry to darling-workspace/patches/homebrew/patches.yml (bead = this one, publication-status: blocked); `west patch verify`; `west patch apply` to regenerate integration/homebrew; rebuild+redeploy. Re-add the dar-fwl fault tracer (darlingserver.cpp) to the test build via the saved diff if needed.
- NOTHING PUSHED without explicit approval.

## Context / siblings
Stacks on the ingest-error-reply fix (dar-gwn.6.2 Layer 1, branch fix/ingest-error-reply @ ddfc263, integrated). See the dar-gwn.6.2 CONSOLIDATED MAP comment for the full crash->livelock->SEGV chain. Reference memory: darling-rpc-loss-watch, dar-gwn6-ulock-eintr-sigill, patchset-drift-integration-homebrew, darling-build-deploy. Deployed server when this was named: 684904978871.
close_reason: pthread_canceled/markcancel implemented to XNU semantics; livelock eliminated, brew now runs the libunistring install (validated). Residual 0x3e8 SEGV is dar-gwn.6.4. Not pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-26 (NOT pushed). Fix landed exactly per spec.

CODE (clean branch fix/pthread-canceled-xnu-semantics, rebased onto integration darlingserver tip 25e1b6c so its context matches the post-all-patches tree; commit 6883a83 standalone / 455eaea in integration):
 - duct-tape/internal-include/.../thread.h: added per-thread cancel bits to struct dtape_thread -- bool cancel_disable / cancel_pending / canceled (mirror UT_CANCELDISABLE / UT_CANCEL / UT_CANCELED), placed after fatal_exception_delivery.
 - duct-tape/src/thread.c: init the 3 bits to false in dtape_thread_create; new dtape_thread_canceled(thread, action) implementing the XNU action table (1->clear-disable->0, 2->set-disable->0, 0-> (pending && !disable && !canceled) ? mark-canceled,return 0 : EINVAL); new dtape_thread_markcancel(thread) arming cancel_pending on the target (guarded by !pending && !canceled like XNU's (CANCEL|CANCELED)==0).
 - duct-tape/include/darlingserver/duct-tape.h: C declarations for both.
 - src/call.cpp: Call::PthreadCanceled::processCall() now does code = -dtape_thread_canceled(_thread.lock()->_dtapeThread, _body.action) (negate XNU code to guest BSD-errno: reply 0=canceled, -EINVAL=not-canceled); Call::PthreadMarkcancel::processCall() does code = -dtape_thread_markcancel(target->_dtapeThread), -ESRCH if no target. No more -ENOSYS stub.

INTEGRATE: patch darlingserver/pthread-canceled-xnu-semantics.patch (west format, sha256 37f20a7a..., source-commit 6883a83), patches.yml entry added after assert-wait-cancel-stale-timer (bead dar-gwn.6.3, publication-status blocked). `west patch verify` = 52 patches clean. `west patch apply` regenerated integration/homebrew (darlingserver tip 455eaea). Rebuilt from the FULL integration tree + dar-fwl fault tracer; deployed server md5 53e24e16 to darling-prefix/bin/darlingserver (backup .bak-integ-1782467459). NOTHING PUSHED.

LIVE PROOF the fix is active: in the run the handler now replies #31 with code -22 (=-EINVAL, the XNU action-0 not-canceled return) instead of the old -38 (-ENOSYS).

TRAP HIT + AVOIDED (recorded for the next agent): a first build off the bare fix branch (stacked only on ingest-error-reply) REGRESSED -- it was missing every sibling integration fix (cvwait-balanced, stale-wait-timer x2, fork-checkin-bound, sigexc, resume-race), so brew died early at download_queue.rb:184 sleep ETIMEDOUT (the dar-gwn.1.8/.1.9 signature). This is the patchset-drift trap from memory. Re-built from integration; that is the only valid test vehicle. Validation (brew reinstall wget under DSERVER_LOG_LEVEL=debug) running on the integration build now.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
VALIDATED GREEN 2026-06-26 (integration build, server md5 53e24e16, DSERVER_FAULT_TRACE=1 + DSERVER_LOG_LEVEL=debug, logs truncated at run start so no stale-log trap).

BEFORE (stub, server 684904978871): brew reinstall wget LIVELOCKED at "Installing wget dependency: libunistring" -- #31 pthread_canceled = 167168 calls from ONE stuck TID (124) at ~670/sec, build never started, eventually 0x3e8 SEGV.

AFTER (this fix): brew reinstall wget
  - downloaded all deps clean (libunistring/gettext/libidn2/openssl@3),
  - ENTERED and RAN "Installing wget dependency: libunistring" (reached libunistring.rb:27 'install', formula.rb:3560) -- the livelock is GONE,
  - #31 is now HEALTHY: spread across dozens of short-lived build-process PIDs (~200 calls each: 3160070/4075, 3160034/4056, 3160128/4112, ...), NOT one TID; replies are now -22 (-EINVAL, XNU action-0 not-canceled) / 0, never -38; a full variety of other RPCs flows alongside (mach_msg_overwrite, vchroot_path, set_thread_handles) = real forward progress, not a spin.

The run then hit a guest-side [BUG] Segmentation fault at 0x000003e800001309 during the libunistring install. 0x3e8 = uid 1000 = the dar-gwn.6 SIGCHLD-mistag death-rattle. ZERO fresh server faults (fault.log fresh-epoch >= 1782467480 = empty; WAITQPANIC=0 ETIMEDOUT/[BUG]=0 Dropping=0 in dserver.log). Env clean after (mldr=0 srv=0).

This is EXACTLY the predicted Layer-3 residual: clearing the livelock unmasks the downstream 0x3e8 SEGV. That is dar-gwn.6.4's job (now unblocked). dar-gwn.6.3 is DONE -- the pthread_canceled livelock is eliminated and brew advanced from "spins forever, build never starts" to "runs the install".

title: libunistring install fails: gnulib CHECK_PRINTF_SAFE conftest gdtoa-OOM SEGV not caught by its nocrash handler -> ruby build.rb RC=1 (SAME fault as dar-gwn.1.10; .1.10 bound cures deadlock but conftest still dies)
description:
# Re-check the residual 0x3e8 SEGV at libunistring AFTER the pthread_canceled livelock fix

## Why this bead exists
On the fixed build (server 684904978871 = integration + ingest-error-reply), brew reinstall wget at libunistring still eventually shows a guest Ruby `[BUG] Segmentation fault at 0x000003e8........` -- BUT with ZERO fresh server-side faults/drops (the verdict's RESULT=FAULT/Dropping=1 in one run were FALSE POSITIVES on stale appended logs). The 0x3e8 high-32 = uid 1000 = the dar-gwn.6 SIGCHLD-mistag death-rattle signature. It currently appears DOWNSTREAM of the pthread_canceled livelock's heavy fork/signal churn (dar-gwn.6.3).

## What to do (ONLY after dar-gwn.6.3 lands)
1. Re-run brew reinstall wget on the pthread_canceled-fixed server (fault tracer armed, DSERVER_LOG_LEVEL=debug). Determine whether the 0x3e8 SEGV STILL occurs once the livelock is gone.
2. If it RECEDES (libunistring builds, no SEGV): close this -- it was a symptom of the churn. Continue brew toward green (next dep gettext/libidn2/openssl@3).
3. If it PERSISTS: it is an independent dar-gwn.6-family SIGCHLD-mistag SEGV. Capture with the in-server fault tracer (DSERVER_FAULT_TRACE=1 -> dserver-fault.log) AND the guest-side faulting context. CAUTION: distinguish fresh vs stale fault-log entries by epoch (the harness verdict does NOT filter by run -- it false-positived once). Cross-reference the dar-gwn.6 ulock-EINTR fix (already deployed in the dylib b66bb1b41d7b) and the dar-gwn.1.5 SIGCHLD-mistag analysis (memory dar-gwn15-was-dargwn6-regression).

## Harness note (fix before trusting verdicts)
brew-install-validate.sh verdict scans the WHOLE dserver-fault.log / dserver.log, which are APPENDED across runs -> it reports stale crashes as if fresh. Filter by run-start epoch (record START epoch, grep only entries newer) before grading. This bit us this session.

## Context
Part of the dar-gwn.6.2 chain (Layer 3). See dar-gwn.6.2 CONSOLIDATED MAP comment. Blocked by dar-gwn.6.3 (do that first). Nothing pushed.

With the livelock gone, brew reinstall wget now ENTERS and RUNS "Installing wget dependency: libunistring" and crashes guest-side with:
  formula.rb:3560: [BUG] Segmentation fault at 0x000003e800001309
