title: E-UNION guest runtime: mkdir over rmdir-whiteouted lower dir returns EEXIST
description: While promoting dar-r7fc.4.3 to guest/runtime coverage, the eunion-16 delete/recreate contract did not hold through real Darling syscalls. Repro on /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix with E-UNION enabled: create a lower-only populated directory under /private/var/tmp/west-eunion-mutate/recreate, run a guest fixture that calls rmdir('/private/var/tmp/west-eunion-mutate/recreate') and then mkdir() for the same path. Observed: rmdir returns success, but the following mkdir fails with EEXIST. Earlier variant that unlinked the stale child first produced rmdir ENOTEMPTY. Root-cause hypothesis from source inspection: vchroot_prepare_create() copy-ups the parent before clearing the target whiteout; for mkdir-over-whiteout this can leave/recreate an upper object at the target, so the host mkdir sees EEXIST instead of creating a fresh opaque upper dir. Need a focused product fix and guest runtime regression test for xnu/eunion-16-mkdir-opaque.patch. Do not mark eunion-16 runtime-covered until this passes.
close_reason: Fixed by xnu/eunion-24-mkdir-upper-create.patch with guest-runtime RED/GREEN proof; west runtime-red builder fixed to inherit DARLING_EUNION.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Resolved. Root cause was not just opaque-dir marking: after rmdir created a whiteout for a lower-only populated dir, mkdir create preparation still let normal vchroot_expand resolve the target back to the lower template, so the host mkdirat hit the lower namesake and returned EEXIST. Product fix is xnu/eunion-24-mkdir-upper-create.patch from source commit a6bb8d7ab24ff9d5d1f9f1f337ed755ec19926a5: vchroot_prepare_create_path() prepares the parent/whiteout and returns the writable upper host path; mkdirat uses that path for absolute managed E-UNION creates, then still runs vchroot_post_mkdir() for opacity. Added real guest runtime test tests/eunion_mkdir_opaque_guest.c and metadata on xnu/eunion-24-mkdir-upper-create.patch. RED/GREEN proof: west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/eunion-24-mkdir-upper-create.patch --red-only --prove-red --env darling --prefix existing:/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs built current-minus-patch bad runtime, deployed libsystem_kernel.dylib + dyld, observed RED 'mkdir ... File exists' rc=1, restored runtime, then observed GREEN WEST_EUNION_MKDIR_OPAQUE_OK rc=0. While proving RED, fixed west test guest-runtime-deploy builder to inherit current Darling CMake feature toggles like DARLING_EUNION from CMakeCache; otherwise the bad runtime was built with E-UNION disabled and failed only the prefix preflight, which is not a valid RED. Added contract coverage in tests/run-west-test-runtime-red-contract.sh.

title: D16 per-thread lanes: postfork_reset closes fd 0 (128x close(0)) -> child stdin destroyed -> brew 'cannot duplicate fd 0'
close_reason: Fixed via RED-TDD; verified unit+fork/exec+brew; commits 8d554c2 (xnu) + 757bb94 (dserver), local only

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE (code-level, deterministically reproduced; Finding 2 from the #131 duplex re-measure).

Symptom: the integration/arch guest libsystem_kernel.dylib (0600ddd0, has D16/D17/D18a per-thread ring
lanes) reproducibly fails brew reinstall with "/bin/sh: cannot duplicate fd 0 to fd 0: Bad file descriptor"
in deep fork/exec. Old 6bd251c3 (07-02, pre-D16) ran brew fine. Reproduced with NO brew: a 3-line guest
shell (spawn a few bg subshells doing ring-eligible calls, then fork+exec /bin/sh) fails identically on
0600ddd0 and passes on 6bd251c3. => real REGRESSION in D16 (commit 76541a7), NOT stand contamination or a
build artifact.

Mechanism: g_lanes[] is `static gr_lane_t g_lanes[GR_MAX_LANES]` (dserver-ring.c:99) -> zero-initialized ->
every UN-attached lane has wake_fd == 0 (not -1). __dserver_ring_postfork_reset() (dserver-ring.c:188-212)
iterates ALL lanes and does `if (L->wake_fd >= 0) close(L->wake_fd)`. Since 0 >= 0, it executes close(0)
for every one of the 128 untouched lanes -> the fork child stdin is CLOSED. Next redirection/dup2(0,0)
in the child (bash) -> EBADF -> "cannot duplicate fd 0".

The pre-D16 code used a single SCALAR g_wake_fd explicitly initialized to -1, so its reset correctly
skipped the close when no ring was attached. The D16 refactor scalar->zero-init-table silently broke the
"-1 == no fd" sentinel invariant: the table cells are 0, not -1.

Empirical proof (fdleak_probe2, same clean workdir, only dylib swapped):
  OLD: parent fd0 -> /dev/null; child fd0 -> /dev/null (stdin survives fork). PASS.
  NEW: parent fd table MISSING fd0 (closed by postfork_reset); child fd0 recycled to a stray pipe;
       parent readlink /proc/self/fd/0 -> rc=1 (no such fd). /bin/sh: cannot duplicate fd 0. FAIL.

SECONDARY (same code, latent, not the trigger here but fix together): the lane wake_fd is NOT
close-on-exec. Server ::dup(ring->eventfd()) (call.cpp:2045/2049) STRIPS CLOEXEC; guest recv hook uses
plain recvmsg flags=0 (no MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC, dserver-rpc-defs.h:155/157); gr_attach_lane never re-sets
FD_CLOEXEC on the received wake_fd. So attached lane wake_fds leak across execve (execve.c:258 has no lane
drop). Old=1 leaked fd; new=up to 128 -> fd-table pressure. Not the fd0 trigger, but a real leak.

FIX (proper, not a patch): (1) establish + preserve the wake_fd == -1 sentinel for every lane at every
lifecycle point; gate postfork_reset close on a proper in-use predicate, never on the zero default.
(2) set FD_CLOEXEC on the received wake_fd in gr_attach_lane. (3) RED-first gate test: arm postfork_reset
over a zero-init table and assert NO close(0) -- the D16 gate covered active/generation races but NOT the
wake_fd sentinel (the coverage gap).

Blocks: the consistent-dylib real-workload duplex go/no-go re-measure (#131). Related: dar-1il.11 (D16),
dar-1il.12/.13 (D17/D18a raised lanes 64->128, widening the close(0) loop).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
FIXED (RED-TDD, local, separate fix branches). Verified at three levels.

Approach (architectural, per user: free by the OWNERSHIP MARKER not the resource value):
- postfork_reset frees map+wake_fd IFF active==1 (the publish marker acquire sets LAST),
  symmetric to release. A zero-init lane (active==0) owns nothing -> skipped -> no close(0).
- wake_fd=-1 restored at claim AND after reset -> the "-1 == no fd" invariant holds at every
  lifecycle point (defence in depth; the active gate alone already fixes the crash).
- FD_CLOEXEC on the received wake_fd in gr_attach_lane (secondary leak: SCM_RIGHTS recv has no
  MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC, server ::dup strips it; old leaked 1 fd, table up to GR_MAX_LANES).

Commits (LOCAL only, not pushed):
- xnu  fix/j7e7-lane-wakefd-sentinel  8d554c2  (dserver-ring.c: +27/-5)
- darlingserver  fix/perf18-server-ring  757bb94  (new gate ring_postfork_reset_gate_test.c
  + wired into run-ring-shm-validate.sh, after the multilane gate)

RED-TDD gate ring_postfork_reset_gate_test.c: GREEN passes (ownership-gated teardown, no
stdio fd closed, -1 sentinel); RED-A -DRESET_GATE_ON_WAKEFD_GE0 reproduces the shipped
close(0) bug (fails); RED-B -DRESET_LEAVES_WAKEFD_ZERO catches a dropped sentinel (fails).

Verification:
1. unit gate RED->GREEN; full perf#18 ring gate suite green ("all perf#18 ring gates: OK").
2. fork+exec fd probe: fixed dylib 1cf2496f child fd0 -> /dev/null, dup OK -- identical to
   the pre-D16 baseline 6bd251c3; buggy 0600ddd0 failed this same probe with close(0).
3. REAL WORKLOAD: brew reinstall xz completes rc=0 on 1cf2496f (configure/make check/make
   install, 96 files) -- the exact reproducible failure on 0600ddd0 is gone.

Fixed guest dylib md5: 1cf2496f (from the fix). Deploy baseline d81b5cf1 untouched; stand
restored to 6bd251c3. This unblocks the consistent-dylib real-workload duplex re-measure
(#131) once a fixed dylib is baked into the profile.

title: Guard west patch export against giant generated patch churn
description:
Problem: west patch export can dirty the workspace before discovering stale history metadata. After local history rewrite, running non-check export for the homebrew profile began writing patch files, then failed later on stale source-base d75d7cf6372aab62bd97951be313f5a257cdce3c for fix/mldr-stack-mmap-fallback. During this run it also produced multi-million-line patch diffs before failure.

Required fix: make export preflight the entire selected profile before writing any patch file. Validate every source-branch, source-base, and source-commit revision first; report exact stale entries and likely rewritten branch parent/head repairs. Add a guard for suspicious patch-size or line-count growth, requiring an explicit override before writing huge output.

Acceptance: export --check remains read-only; non-check export fails before writes when any selected entry has invalid revisions; huge patch churn is blocked by default; diagnostics point at the exact profile entry.
close_reason: Implemented export preflight and large-output guard. west patch export now plans the whole selected profile before writing, validates source-branch/source-base/source-commit availability, rejects suspicious patch-size growth without --allow-large-output, and reports exact patch metadata repairs. Added tests/run-west-patch-export-preflight-contract.sh covering missing source-base, missing source-commit, large-output no-write failure, and explicit override. Verified focused contracts, py_compile, patch check homebrew/perf/arch, and arch export --check.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Clean corrected problem statement: west patch export must preflight the whole profile before writing files. Repro: after local history rewrite, homebrew export can start writing many patch files, then fail later on stale source-base d75d7cf6372aab62bd97951be313f5a257cdce3c for fix/mldr-stack-mmap-fallback. It also produced multi-million-line patch diffs before failure. Acceptance: export --check remains read-only; non-check export validates all source-base/source-commit/source-branch revisions before writing; suspicious patch size or line-count growth fails unless an explicit override is passed; errors point to the exact patch entry and suggested rewritten branch parent/head repair.

title: Clean materialized profile worktrees after interrupted test runs
description: west test --prove-red materializes a profile in /tmp. SIGINT left west-profile-homebrew-* behind. Make profile worktree cleanup signal-safe, add an interruption contract, and ensure no worktree/process remains.
close_reason: Shielded profile worktree teardown from SIGINT and reproduced interruption with no leftover /tmp profile or git worktree entry.

title: Retain runtime proof source and build scratch as one evidence unit
description: west test --gc currently orders source and build scratch directories independently. A failed runtime proof has a materialized source forest and a separate west-runtime build directory; keep-last=1 can retain only one half. Design a run-scoped evidence manifest or grouping key so retention and pruning operate on the complete proof unit, while preserving safe dry-run and symlink guards.

title: rtk/find: document or wrap GNU find options
description: During cleanup/debug work, rtk find -printf ignored -printf because rtk/find is not GNU find-compatible. This produced misleading output while inspecting debug bundles. Decide whether to add an rtk passthrough/wrapper for GNU find options, document use of rtk proxy find for GNU-specific flags, or add an AGENTS.md rule. Acceptance: agents have an obvious command for find -printf style inspections and do not get silent ignored flags.

title: gettext build SLOW (NOT a stall): dar-6x4 perf — per-fork checkin RPC serialized through single-threaded server
description: Separate from dar-cps (dar-cps = abort_with_payload group-broadcast, FIXED). brew reinstall -s gettext intermittently STALLS at the stage/early-configure phase BEFORE any conftest aborts (abort_with_payload never reached). Signature: server idle in ep_poll (worker futex_wait), guest threads parked in recvmsg/do_select, run-log + dserver.log frozen for 6-12min, server CPU 20-50% (busy microthread). One 'procmem Error: Failed to write 92 bytes ... process <hostpid> (1): No such process' per stalled run, target nsid=1 (init) -> an S2C memory-write to a process that exited = lost-RPC-reply / S2C-to-dead-proc family (dar-gwn.1.7/1.10/dar-6x4 perf). Hit 2/2 gettext e2e attempts on the dar-cps-fixed build (dylib 5e4c4822, server fb027685). RED-run #1 of dar-cps got PAST this to configure, so it's intermittent. Evidence ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/cps-e2e-stall-diag{,2}.txt. NOT a dar-cps regression (fix code never ran).
notes: RE-DIAGNOSED 2026-06-27 (Opus): NOT a lost-RPC/S2C stall. It is THROUGHPUT SLOWNESS = dar-6x4 perf family. Live proof: (1) build PROGRESSES — guest ppid chain advanced brew->ruby->make->make->/bin/sh->gnulib gen (grep/sed/sort)->clang -cc1->libtool --mode=compile clang; (2) bounded resources — mldr oscillates 15<->54, zombies stable at 3 (no leak); (3) server GDB bt while at 37% CPU: main loop in MessageQueue::sendMany->sendmmsg (flushing replies), the single microthread worker IDLE in pthread_cond_wait (WorkQueue::worker) — no deadlock/spin/livelock; (4) ~2 process births/sec vs native 100s-1000s/s = ~100-1000x slowdown from the synchronous dserver_rpc_checkin round-trip per fork (fork.c:61) through the single-threaded server. The prior 'log frozen / idle ep_poll / procmem ESRCH' signature was misleading: default log level doesn't log checkins (flat log != stall); daemons park in recvmsg by design; procmem ESRCH = benign isDead() liveness race. FIX = perf epic dar-dar6x4-perf-5dq (esp .2 parallelize server RPC + .1 futex wait + .3 cheaper checkin). dar-cps fix unaffected. Evidence ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/l3a-REDIAGNOSIS.txt + server-bt.
close_reason: RE-DIAGNOSED + CLOSED: NOT a stall/lost-RPC/S2C bug. dar-l3a is THROUGHPUT SLOWNESS = dar-6x4 perf family. Live proof: brew reinstall -s gettext BUILT TO COMPLETION (🍺 gettext/1.0, 2499 files, configure->make->make install, 22m25s) with zero crash/wedge, zombies stable at 3 (no leak), mldr bounded (peak 51). Server GDB bt during the 'stall' showed main loop in sendmmsg (flushing replies) + the single microthread worker IDLE in pthread_cond_wait — no deadlock/spin. ~2 fork/s vs 100s-1000s native = the synchronous per-fork dserver_rpc_checkin round-trip through the single-threaded server (fork.c:61). Prior 'log frozen/idle ep_poll/procmem ESRCH' signature was misleading (default log silent on checkins; daemons recvmsg by design; procmem ESRCH = benign isDead race). Also CONFIRMS dar-cps fix correct: ./configure passed cleanly. Real work now lives in perf epic dar-dar6x4-perf-5dq (esp new perf #0 metrics .6, then .1 futex / .2 parallelize / .3 cheaper checkin). Evidence ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/l3a-REDIAGNOSIS.txt + server-bt.

title: xnu: fix getattrlist_pack_name_objtype_contract generated include path
description:
Audit 2026-07-09: xnu/getattrlist-name-objtype.patch --prove-red reached GREEN guest runtime, then host contract getattrlist_pack_name_objtype_contract failed to compile. The generated wrapper darling/emulation/xnu_syscall/bsd/helper/xattr/getattrlist_pack.h includes <xnu_syscall/bsd/helper/xattr/getattrlist_pack.h>, but the test include dirs only expose darling/src/libsystem_kernel/emulation/include, so the generated wrapper cannot resolve the production header.

Observed error:
fatal error: xnu_syscall/bsd/helper/xattr/getattrlist_pack.h: No such file or directory

Acceptance:
- Fix the c-fixture include dirs or generated wrapper so the contract compiles against the intended production shared packer.
- Re-run west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/getattrlist-name-objtype.patch --prefix-profile homebrew --prove-red.
- Ensure RED/GREEN behavior remains meaningful and not masked by compile infrastructure failure.
close_reason: fixed

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Fixed and verified. Root cause was broader than a missing include dir: source-base RED proofs used a bad worktree for RED, but ran GREEN against the live checkout. The live xnu checkout can be on an arbitrary fix branch, so the getattrlist host contract could not see the profile-current shared packer header even though the patch profile itself contained it. west test now materializes a GREEN profile source tree for source-base proofs when a profile is active, then runs the fixed side with the source-env pointing at that tree. Added runtime_red_contract coverage proving RED sees bad source and GREEN sees materialized profile source without mutating the live checkout. Also added the needed xnu include root for the generated getattrlist wrapper. Verified: west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/getattrlist-name-objtype.patch --prefix-profile homebrew --prove-red passes. Evidence: host contract RED fails on missing old header, GREEN prints 'GREEN: getattrlist shared packer contract'; runtime RED bundle 20260709T200811Z has ORACLE_RC=2 and 'getattrlist: Invalid argument (22)'; runtime GREEN bundle 20260709T201021Z has GETATTRLIST_NAME_OBJTYPE_GUEST_OK and ORACLE_RC=0. Gates passed: runtime_red_contract.py, west patch check --profile homebrew, west test --profile homebrew --red-audit, west patch export --profile homebrew --check, west patch verify --profile homebrew. Cleanup audit found no Darling processes, mounts, or west-red/green proof scratch tails.

title: Move historical Darling repro assets out of mega branch
description: The historical root branch contains Ruby regression scripts, DarlingServer resume/timer A/B reproducers, gdb_maloader.py, ruby-thread-watchdog.rb, and legacy shell debug helpers. The Rust darling-debug-runner itself is already extracted to its private repo and is newer than the embedded copy. Decide which remaining repro assets belong beside the runner versus diagnostics/archive, import them without source-tree pollution, and retain commit provenance from root.bundle.

title: perf/dar-6x4: darlingserver UAF race in process-death port teardown (notifyDead->dtape_task_destroy->ipc_port_release_send_and_unlock) under fork storms
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Discovered while validating perf #7-generalize (dar-dar6x4-perf-5dq.14). Two darlingserver lineages exist and were never merged:

  (A) perf lineage (branch perf/guest-per-callnum-sleep-account, server md5 716f958d): has per-call metrics (perf#9), NO_REPLY fire-and-forget (perf#10, CHANGES the RPC protocol for set_dyld_info/set_executable_path), guest sleep-account. Built on perf#2b inline-fastpath. LACKS the dar-6x4 checkin-suspend crash fix.

  (B) integration/homebrew (server md5 831a09d5): HAS the dar-6x4 checkin-suspend crash fix 5e5d688 (don't hold Process::_rwlock across suspending dtape calls in notifyCheckin) + perf#2b + progress-metrics(perf#0). LACKS the perf#9/#10 commits.

Consequence: the deployed prefix server 716f958d (lineage A) SEGFAULTS under fork storms -- dmesg shows repeated 'darlingserver segfault in libc.so.6 error 4 at addr 0x10', server log 'Uncaught exception from processCall (call checkin): Attempt to suspend thread other than current thread'. Every fork-heavy build (make -j) crashes the server partway, then all guest RPCs block forever on the dead socket and the build wedges at a partial .o count. This is what made fork-heavy e2e perf validation impossible.

Mixing lineages does NOT work: deploying integration server 831a09d5 against the perf-lineage dylib/mldr desyncs (server log 'Client-pushed reply overwriting existing saved reply') because NO_REPLY (perf#10) makes the client expect NO reply while the integration server still sends one -> guest launchd boot hangs.

TASK: produce ONE server that has BOTH the checkin-suspend crashfix 5e5d688 AND the perf-lineage protocol (at minimum perf#10 NO_REPLY, since the deployed dylib/mldr depend on it), so a matched mldr+dylib+dyld+server set boots AND survives fork storms. Then fork-heavy e2e perf (perf #7-generalize wall-clock A/B, perf #8 reaping) becomes measurable. Blocks meaningful wall-clock numbers for the whole perf epic.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
CORRECTION (Opus, 2026-06-28): the original framing of this bead was WRONG. The perf-server lineage and the dar-6x4 checkin-suspend crashfix were NOT unmerged. Verified: 5e5d688 (the crashfix) is the merge-base of perf/guest-per-callnum-sleep-account and integration/homebrew, AND is an ancestor of the perf branch. src/process.cpp is byte-identical between the two branches. The 3 perf commits on top (per-call metrics, NO_REPLY, sleep-account) only touch metrics.{hpp,cpp}, generate-rpc-wrappers.py, thread.cpp (+3 lines: _metrics.recordCall), and tests -- none touch checkin/suspend/rwlock. So the deployed perf server 716f958d ALREADY HAS the crashfix. No merge is needed. There is NO "missing 5e5d688" gap.

WHAT IS ACTUALLY TRUE:
1. The "Uncaught exception from processCall (call checkin): Attempt to suspend thread other than current thread" log line is CAUGHT (try/catch in thread.cpp microthreadWorker, since commit 0502449) -- the server LOGS it, sends an error reply, and SURVIVES. It is NOT a crash. It recurs occasionally under fork storms but does not kill the server.
2. There IS a separate, RARE server SEGFAULT under fork storms: dmesg "darlingserver segfault at 10 ... in libc.so.6[...] error 4", consistently at libc offset 0x837fc (addr2line on the stripped .so -> __fxprintf region, IMPRECISE -- needs a real gdb backtrace to confirm). error 4 = read fault, addr 0x10 = near-null deref. Observed ~5x across a full day of heavy build use (07:39, 09:05, 10:11, 10:41 etc), NOT every build. Could NOT be reproduced on demand: 6 consecutive fork-storm builds under gdb did not trigger it. darlingserver's own Log sink uses raw write() (no fprintf), so the __fxprintf attribution is suspect -- the real site is unknown until a live gdb bt is captured.
3. The "Client-pushed reply overwriting existing saved reply" errors at 10:41/10:48 were caused by ME deploying the MISMATCHED integration server 831a09d5 against the perf-lineage dylib/mldr: NO_REPLY (perf#10) makes the client expect no reply for set_dyld_info/set_executable_path while 831a09d5 (no NO_REPLY) still sends one -> desync. That is a lineage-MIXING artifact, not a standalone bug. Do NOT mix server/dylib/mldr lineages.

REVISED TASK: this is NOT a merge. It is "find + fix the rare server segfault under fork storms" (the real libc-region crash, item 2). Repro is hard (non-deterministic, didn't fire under gdb in 6 tries). Next step when picked up: attach gdb to the live server and leave it running across MANY builds / a real brew build until it catches the SIGSEGV with a backtrace; only then is the fix site known. Separately there is an intermittent GUEST-side failure where a build's make produces 0 .o even with a healthy server (also non-deterministic; likely shellspawn/connection flakiness on the 2nd darling-shell session). Both are pre-existing and independent of perf#7-generalize (guest-side recv-spin, which cannot crash the server and whose builds complete 200/200 and 250/250 when neither flake hits).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
PROGRESS 2026-06-28 (Opus): the crash is now well-characterized but not yet root-caused.

CONFIRMED 100%-consistent signature across 6 occurrences today (07:39, 09:05, 10:11, 10:41, 11:52 + earlier): dmesg "darlingserver[PID]: segfault at 10 ip <X> sp <Y> error 4 in libc.so.6[<base>+188000]", faulting at libc offset 0x837fc EVERY time (addr2line on stripped libc -> __fxprintf region, IMPRECISE). error 4 = userspace read fault; addr 0x10 = near-null+0x10 deref. RARE: ~1 in 3-6 fork-storm builds; did NOT fire in ~46 builds while gdb was ptrace-attached (attachment perturbs timing AND blocks the workload -> 0 .o builds).

REPRO: ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/build-correctness.sh (now fixed, see below) hits it ~1/3 runs at nfiles>=150 jobs=8. The crash leaves the stat socket unreachable + partial .o; the harness now classifies that as SERVER_CRASHED.

NOT root-caused: live gdb is incompatible with the workload (ptrace serializes the server -> builds make 0 progress -> crash never triggers). NEXT STEP = core dump, NOT live gdb: needs core_pattern set to a writable path + ulimit -c unlimited for the server (requires user approval -- overwriting host-wide /proc/sys/kernel/core_pattern was denied by the auto classifier). With a core, `gdb darlingserver core` gives the real faulting frame (the __fxprintf attribution is suspect since darlingserver's own Log sink uses raw write(), not fprintf -- the printf may be inside a libstdc++/exception/abort path or a duct-tape component). Alternative: a SIGSEGV handler in darlingserver that backtrace()s to the log before dying.

SEPARATE HARNESS BUG (FIXED, not a server issue): build-correctness.sh used TWO darling-shell sessions (a boot `darling shell /bin/echo` then a second `darling shell build.sh`). The 2nd session reliably failed -- 0 .o, server healthy, clients_blocked=0, make/clang children never progressed (shellspawn/re-connect handoff flake). FIX = ONE session that boots the guest as a side effect of its first exec and runs the build inline; detect liveness from the .c files it writes, completion from .o count. After the fix: DONE 150/150 reproducibly (when the rare server segfault doesn't hit). This two-session flake explains MANY of the earlier false "TIMEOUT"s, independent of the server crash.

So there are TWO independent pre-existing flakes, BOTH unrelated to perf#7-generalize (guest-side recv-spin): (1) the rare server libc-region SIGSEGV (this bead), (2) the two-session harness bug (now fixed in build-correctness.sh).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT-CAUSE LOCALIZED via core dump 2026-06-28 (Opus). User approved core dumps; set core_pattern to a writable path + suid_dumpable=2 + ulimit -c unlimited on the launcher, ran a fresh-boot-per-rep build soak, caught the crash on the 1st heavy rep. Restored core_pattern (apport) + suid_dumpable=0 after. Core + matching server binary + backtraces saved: ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/core.darlingserver.crash-12-18.716f958d, darlingserver.crash-binary.716f958d, CRASH-BT.txt.

THE CRASH (2 threads, server 716f958d):
  Thread 2 (kernelAsyncRunner) -- faulting:
    Process::notifyDead()::$_0  (the kernelAsync lambda)
      -> dtape_task_destroy            (duct-tape/src/task.c:140)
        -> ipc_task_terminate          (task.c:156)
          -> ipc_port_release_send_and_unlock
            -> dtape_mutex_unlock
              -> libsimple_lock_lock   -> SIGSEGV in libc (rdi=0; rip lands in libc padding 0x837fc, the __fxprintf-region red herring -- it is a corrupted/garbage control transfer, NOT a real printf).
  Thread 1 (main loop) -- concurrently:
    MessageQueue::receiveMany -> Message ctor -> vector<uint8_t>::resize  (reading/decoding an incoming RPC datagram)

DIAGNOSIS: a process-death TEARDOWN race under fork storms. notifyDead() schedules task release on the kernelAsyncRunner; the teardown chain dtape_task_destroy -> ipc_task_terminate -> ipc_port_release_send_and_unlock unlocks an IPC port whose dtape mutex/queue-lock memory is already garbage (use-after-free / double-destroy of the port or its lock). The fault is in dtape_mutex_unlock acquiring the lock's internal queue lock on freed memory -> jumps to a bogus address. This is DISTINCT from 5e5d688 (that was _rwlock-across-suspend on the CHECKIN path; this is the DEATH/port-teardown path). Under fork storms many processes die at once while the main loop keeps receiving messages, so the teardown runs constantly and races.

Relevant code: dtape_task_destroy task.c:140-167 (task_lock; ipc_task_disable; semaphore_destroy_all; ipc_space_terminate; ipc_task_terminate; dtape_vm_map_destroy; is_release; lck_mtx_destroy; free(task)). task_deallocate task.c:220-233 (os_ref_release -> on last ref dtape_task_destroy). notifyDead src/process.cpp:785 schedules dtape_task_release via Thread::kernelAsync. The likely bug: the os_ref refcount on the task/port is not actually serializing the final destroy against a concurrent ipc_port_release_send (port released twice, or the space terminated while a send-right release is in flight), so ipc_port_release_send_and_unlock touches a port already freed by ipc_space_terminate / a sibling release.

NEXT STEP (deliberate, not rushed -- delicate XNU duct-tape IPC refcounting): re-load the core with the duct-tape objects' line info (the server is RelWith-ish, has symbols) and inspect the port/mutex object at the unlock site to confirm freed-vs-live; then narrow the lock scope or fix the port-right refcount so the death-path port release cannot race the space terminate. A deterministic RED gate = the fresh-boot-per-rep heavy fork-storm soak (~/work/dar-6x4-repro/core-soak2.sh) which reproduced it within 1 rep with cores armed.

IMPACT: this rare server crash (~1 in 3-6 heavy fork-storm builds) is what intermittently wedges fork-heavy e2e builds (every guest RPC then blocks forever on the dead socket). Fixing it unblocks reliable fork-heavy e2e perf measurement for the whole epic. It is independent of perf#7-generalize (guest-side recv-spin).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
RED GATE BUILT + PROVEN 2026-06-28 (Opus). Per the red-tdd rule, the deterministic RED->GREEN gate for the dar-l8k UAF now exists, BEFORE the fix.

Test: tests/port_teardown_uaf_race.c + tests/run-port-teardown-uaf-race.sh (in the darlingserver module; backed up to ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/ since the module gets branch-switched). HOST test, plain glibc+pthreads, built with -fsanitize=address. Models the EXACT crash invariant from the core dump with the same primitives:
  - a refcounted object whose LOCK is the FIRST member (== ipc_object: io_lock_data, io_references), freed by io_release-style "decrement; if zero, io_free()".
  - a USER thread does ip_lock -> touch -> ip_unlock (as ipc_port_release_send_and_unlock and its callers do), while a death-path RELEASER thread does the final io_release (notifyDead -> dtape_task_release -> ... -> ip_release on the last ref -> io_free).
  - RED arm ("unsafe", the current shape): the user holds NO ref of its own across its lock section, so the releaser's io_free runs WHILE the user is inside ip_lock/ip_unlock -> the embedded lock + payload land on freed memory. ASan: heap-use-after-free at user_locker (deterministic via a g_done/g_freed handshake -- the releaser frees exactly while the user is in the critical section).
  - GREEN arm ("safe", the fix invariant): the user is HANDED its own counted reference up front (acquired while the object is provably alive, e.g. under the registry lock) and holds it across the whole lock/unlock section, so the refcount cannot reach 0 (and io_free cannot run) until no one holds the lock. Clean.
Verified deterministic 3/3 runs: RED trips ASan UAF every time, GREEN passes every time, script exit 0.

This is a host MODEL of the invariant (like the existing inline_suspend_rwlock_deadlock.c for 5e5d688), not a full-server link. It will be committed TOGETHER with the fix (red-tdd rule). The e2e companion remains ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/core-soak2.sh (fresh-boot-per-rep fork storm with cores armed) to prove the real server stops crashing.

REMAINING = THE FIX ITSELF (not yet written -- deliberately not attempted blind). The GREEN arm shows the shape: ensure every accessor of an ipc_port holds a counted reference across its ip_lock/ip_unlock critical section so the death-path final io_release cannot free the port (and destroy its embedded io_lock_data) underneath a concurrent lock/unlock. Pinning WHICH port + WHICH concurrent accessor in the real server needs a debug build (server -O0 -g) re-caught via core-soak2.sh, then inspect the port object at the unlock site. Likely area: dtape_task_destroy (task.c:140-167) doing ipc_space_terminate (:154) + ipc_task_terminate (:156) on task special/exception ports without serializing against a concurrent send-right release; or a missing ip_reference across an ip_lock section on the kernelAsyncRunner death path. Up to MAX_PERMANENT_KERNEL_RUNNERS=10 concurrent runners process notifyDead lambdas, so concurrency is real.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: RESOLVED + FIXED 2026-06-28 (Opus). Crash is NOT the notifyDead/ipc_port_release_send_and_unlock port-teardown lock UAF the title assumed, and NOT a lineage mismatch (perf server 716f958d already HAS crashfix 5e5d688). ROOT CAUSE pinned with an ASan server build: heap-use-after-free in Process::notifyCheckin exec/'replace task' branch. It released the OLD dtape thread + task BEFORE republishing the NEW dtape thread as current; the old task IPC-space teardown (dtape_task_release -> ipc_space_terminate -> ipc_right_terminate -> ipc_port_destroy) reads current_thread()->ith_assertions (IMPORTANCE_INHERITANCE=1), current_thread()=mainThread->_dtapeThread = STILL the just-freed old dtape_thread -> UAF. ASan: free at process.cpp dtape_thread_release(oldThread), use at ipc_port.c:936, alloc dtape_thread_create. Original prod core (SIGSEGV in main-loop _int_malloc on a corrupted freelist, ~1-in-3-6) = the delayed downstream of that heap corruption. METHOD NOTE: a naive ASan build first showed a misleading SEGV in WorkQueue::worker dropping a shared_ptr<Thread> across doWork()'s setcontext fiber switch -- an ASan fake-stack (detect_stack_use_after_return) ucontext artifact, NOT a bug; detect_stack_use_after_return=0 revealed the real heap UAF. Cross-thread fiber-resume hypothesis instrumented + DISPROVEN (0 hits) + reverted. FIX (branch fix/checkin-retask-current-thread-uaf @92049f05, base ee46485): publish _dtapeTask/_dtapeThread + new semaphores under _rwlock first, drop lock, then release old thread/task (releases stay unlocked -- dtape_task_release can suspend). RED->GREEN host gate committed WITH the fix: tests/checkin_retask_current_thread_uaf.c + run-checkin-retask-current-thread-uaf.sh (RED arm UAF, GREEN clean, 3/3). VALIDATED: ASan server boots clean 3/3 (UAF 3/3 pre-fix); prod server fork-heavy make -j8 6/6 DONE clean. Patch darlingserver/checkin-retask-current-thread-uaf.patch sha256 81c40b28, west patch verify=71, publication blocked. Obsolete wrong-model gate tests/port_teardown_uaf_race.c removed. NOTHING PUSHED. Deployed prod server a13ec2ae. UNBLOCKS fork-heavy e2e perf for the epic.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 correctness audit: focused prove-red and normal GREEN passed for checkin_retask_current_thread_runtime_trace. The host ASan model still trips heap-use-after-free in the release-before-republish arm and passes the republish-before-release arm. Runtime RED reaches the guest fixture (CHECKIN_EXEC_TRACE_GUEST_OK / ORACLE_RC=0) and fails for the intended old ordering marker process.checkin.exec_release_old_before_publish in /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T023554Z-west-test-checkin_retask_current_thread_runtime_trace. Runtime GREEN emits process.checkin.exec_publish_before_release and process.checkin.exec_release_old in /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T023626Z-west-test-checkin_retask_current_thread_runtime_trace and normal GREEN /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T023710Z-west-test-checkin_retask_current_thread_runtime_trace.

title: ATTR_CMN_ERROR getattrlist smoke returns EINVAL despite q95.29.1 support intent
description: Observed 2026-07-07 during runtime validation of dar-q95.29.1 after anchored patchset deploy. An ad-hoc Darling guest C probe for getattrlist/fgetattrlist/getattrlistat with commonattr = ATTR_CMN_NAME | ATTR_CMN_OBJTYPE passes and prints ATTRLIST_PROBE_OK. The same probe with ATTR_CMN_ERROR added to commonattr returns EINVAL for all three entrypoints before producing a packed result. Source-side helper xattr/getattrlist_pack.h includes DARLING_ATTR_CMN_ERROR in DARLING_ATTR_CMN_SUPPORTED and pack logic sets error=0, so this may be a userspace ABI/header/wrapper mismatch or a validation path not covered by the shared packer. Need turn the ad-hoc probe into a stable harness and decide whether ATTR_CMN_ERROR is meant to be accepted for single-entry getattrlist, or only in returned-attrs/bulk contexts.
close_reason: Resolved: stale DPREFIX deploy identified, ATTR_CMN_ERROR packer fixed, permanent runtime harness added and green

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Resolved investigation: initial EINVAL was from testing stale /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix-homebrew-test/usr/lib/system/libsystem_kernel.dylib while deploy updated /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix. After syncing DPREFIX and rebuilding from integration/perf, direct syscall getattrlist/fgetattrlist/getattrlistat with ATTR_CMN_ERROR returned 0. Added permanent runtime harness to dar-q95.29.1: darling/src/libsystem_kernel/tests/run-getattrlist-shared-packer.sh + C probe. Harness verdict: GETATTRLIST_SHARED_PACKER_OK / ORACLE_RC=0. Also fixed packer to always include requested ATTR_CMN_ERROR as synthetic error=0 instead of depending on entry.has_error.

title: beads: add command aliases for common agent misspellings
description: Add or document aliases in the beads/br CLI for common command forms agents keep trying first, especially comment/comments add and other high-frequency workflow commands. Acceptance: br robot-docs/help expose the preferred command plus accepted aliases; the alias path is covered by a small CLI test or documented blocker if beads upstream cannot support local aliasing cleanly.

title: Polish west test framework contracts into smaller idiomatic modules
description: Current state after dar-zhlt: product/patch tests moved to compact manifests, typed resources, and CTest-backed source-repo fixtures, but framework contract coverage is still partly expressed as large shell scripts with embedded Python. tests/run-west-test-metadata-contract.sh is ~937 lines and tests/run-west-test-runtime-red-contract.sh is ~786 lines; west_commands/test.py is also still ~3985 lines, though manifest/resources/ctest/cmake/runtime/prefix helpers are split out. This is acceptable for the migration closure, but not the final pretty shape. Goal: move framework-internal contracts toward smaller idiomatic Python contract modules or pytest-like tests with thin shell wrappers only for environment setup; keep behavior coverage real, not source matching; preserve red/green proof semantics; keep CTest/product tests colocated with patches. Acceptance: metadata contract split by domain (manifest normalization, ctest selection, source-contract policy, guest-command behavior, E-UNION resource, etc.); runtime-red contract split by helper/runtime deploy/prefix cleanup concerns; no giant embedded-Python shell file remains as the primary unit-test surface; docs/AGENTS updated with the preferred framework-contract style; focused checks remain green.
close_reason: Moved framework-internal Python contract bodies out of run-west-test shell wrappers into tests/west_test_contracts modules, including ctest backend, manifest normalization, resource providers, prefix cleanup, runtime RED, and E-UNION prerequisite checks. Shell wrappers are now thin compatibility entrypoints; run-west-test scripts no longer contain embedded Python heredocs. Updated AGENTS.md and docs/test-infra.md with the rule. Verified py_compile, all focused contracts, metadata integration contract, and git diff --check.

title: test-infra: colocate runtime RED fixtures outside current-minus source trees
description:
Audit 2026-07-09 found guest-runtime-deploy RED failures caused by missing fixture files after west rewrote repo paths into the current-minus source forest. Examples:
- xnu/psynch-cvsignal-args.patch / psynch_cvsignal_args_guest: RED failed at upload because darling/src/libsystem_kernel/tests/psynch_cvsignal_args_guest.c did not exist in current-minus source tree.
- libpthread/psynch-kernel-return-helper.patch / psynch_kernel_return_contract: RED failed at upload because tests/psynch_kernel_return_contract.c did not exist in current-minus libpthread source tree.
- xnu/getattrlist-shared-packer.patch / getattrlist_shared_packer: RED failed at upload because darling/src/libsystem_kernel/tests/getattrlist_shared_packer_test.c did not exist in current-minus source tree.

This is not a valid behavioral RED proof.

Acceptance:
- Move/copy runtime fixture inputs needed by patch metadata into darling-workspace/tests or an explicit testkit fixture area, or teach west a first-class fixture-source model.
- current-minus source forest is used for building bad runtime artifacts, not for losing the test input.
- RED for the listed tests fails because of the intended runtime behavior, with explicit expect-output-contains/lacks or equivalent structured oracle.
- GREEN still passes on current runtime.
- AGENTS/docs describe the fixture ownership rule.
close_reason: fixed

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Partial re-audit update: xnu/getattrlist-shared-packer no longer fails because the fixture is missing; the runner now uploads tests/getattrlist_shared_packer_test.c from darling-workspace as intended. The current failure moved to current-minus materialization before runtime: xnu/psynch-negative-errno.patch conflicts when applied after skipping getattrlist-shared-packer. That belongs to dar-st7m (stack cursor/non-linear current-minus), not fixture ownership. getattrlist-name-objtype was separately fixed and verified by dar-l52v: source-base GREEN now materializes profile source and the focused proof passes.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Verified fixed for the listed fixtures. Focused runs now reach behavioral RED/GREEN instead of missing-upload/source-setup failures: xnu/getattrlist-shared-packer.patch --prove-red (RED ORACLE_RC=1 + "getattrlist root: bad name '', want '/'", GREEN GETATTRLIST_SHARED_PACKER_OK/ORACLE_RC=0), libpthread/psynch-kernel-return-helper.patch --prove-red (RED PHASE cond_timedwait_eintr:start + WEST_GUEST_SHELL_RC=1, GREEN PSYNCH_KERNEL_RETURN_CONTRACT_OK), and xnu/psynch-cvsignal-args.patch --prove-red (source-base RED/GREEN plus runtime RED/GREEN trace oracle). Tooling also now rejects ordinary guest-c GREEN bundles missing ok-marker/ORACLE_RC=0 so this class cannot silently pass.

title: Clear stale CTest failure records before west test execution
description: CTest leaves testkit/build/Testing/Temporary/LastTestsFailed.log from a prior failed selection after a later successful selection. Repro 2026-07-10: full combined env=darling run passed fork_checkin_signal_storm_guest (bundle 20260710T140304Z contains ORACLE_RC=0 and FORK_CHECKIN_SIGNAL_STORM_OK), but LastTestsFailed.log still listed that test from the earlier timeout. west test must remove stale CTest failure records immediately before every non-list CTest invocation, preserve genuine current-run records, and have a focused lifecycle contract. No upstream push.
close_reason: Fixed with lifecycle cleanup and regression contract in 4fb1c5c.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented in local commit 4fb1c5c. west test now unlinks testkit/build/Testing/Temporary/LastTestsFailed.log immediately before each real CTest invocation. The focused lifecycle contract creates a stale record and proves it is absent at CTest execution; a real host west test via the job helper also removed the old fork_checkin_signal_storm_guest record after a successful run.

title: test infra: isolate Darling build-target patch tests from unrelated build graph failures
description: west darling-build --force --skip-doctor --targets darling_ec_tls_regress currently pulls a broad build graph and failed in unrelated src/external/objc4/runtime/objc-runtime.mm with #error mismatch in debug-ness macros before proving the LibreSSL regression target. Patch metadata needs a narrower build/test runner for source-module targets: materialize/apply the profile, build only the requested target and its true deps, capture build graph diagnostics, and distinguish unrelated global build breakage from target contract failure. Repro command: rtk west darling-build --force --skip-doctor --targets darling_ec_tls_regress from darling-workspace after homebrew profile materialization/current checkout.
close_reason: Isolated CMake target runner avoids the unrelated full Darling build graph for this blocker.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08: narrowed the LibreSSL build-target test path away from west darling-build/full graph. runner: darling-cmake-target-fixture builds the owning external subproject and target in isolation, captures configure/build/run failures, and avoids unrelated objc/xnu graph failures for darling_ec_tls_regress.

title: `darling shell` detaches stdout and returns before the guest finishes: no honest foreground/exit-code mode
description: Type: feature

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-09 DCC hard-fail gate evidence: guest dyld aborts print abort_with_payload / dyld: DCC2: cache path unset or cache invalid/stale, but darling shell still returns launcher rc 0. Added west guest-command-fixture expect.returncode=any so tests can honestly assert guest-visible output while this foreground/exit-code limitation remains open.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 implementation pending workspace commit: shellspawn now maps waitpid signal termination to POSIX 128+signal and preserves normal exits. Source-owned host CTest forks normal and SIGABRT children and passes in clean integration/arch (4/4 suite). Metadata adds a true guest-runtime RED->GREEN oracle: guest `kill -ABRT $$` must be rc 0 -> 134 when only usr/libexec/shellspawn is built/deployed. Runtime execution remains blocked by explicit 8 GiB capacity preflight and the separately tracked source-profile stack materialization conflict; neither is counted as RED/GREEN.

title: getattrlist: converge getattrlist/getattrlistbulk on shared Darwin packer
description: Architectural follow-up from dar-rpc-correctness-z27x.9. getattrlist and getattrlistbulk should converge on one Darwin attribute packing implementation rather than parallel encoders. Acceptance: audit both implementations; route common CMN/name/objtype/bulk packing through a shared helper where feasible; keep existing getattrlist/getattrlistbulk guest and host contracts green; add a contract that fails if the two paths diverge for a shared attribute case.

title: perf #18 P7: ring v3 stress + lost-wake invariants (gate for default-ON)
description: Gist 191a10db: do NOT flip default-ON until a stress/race suite is green. Required coverage: fork/exec storm (already 3/3 informally); process death while a request is half-written; server sleep race; guest waiter-bit lost-wake; ring full/backpressure; many idle clients + one hot; many hot clients; low-power mode idle CPU (must not burn a core); latency mode pinned; TSAN/ASAN where possible. TWO targeted lost-wake race tests: (1) REQUEST side -- guest publish -> server ACTIVE_POLLING->SLEEP_ARMED -> guest doorbell decision -> server critical recheck -> epoll sleep (must never sleep through a pending request); (2) RESPONSE side -- server publish response -> guest sets s2c_waiter -> guest futex_wait -> server conditional FUTEX_WAKE (must never skip a needed wake). RED->GREEN deterministic gates committed with any fix. This bead GATES default-ON; does NOT block P5/P6.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Gist 33355bf (P6.1-step2 verdict) sharpens P7 as the NEXT priority before default-ON, because the wake model + fast path are now so fast (~0.95us mean / ~740ns p50) that rare lost-wake/lifetime/ring-full bugs are the most expensive failure mode. REQUIRED deterministic RED->GREEN gates (commit each with its fix):
 (1) server ACTIVE_POLLING -> SLEEP_ARMED race (must never sleep through a pending request)
 (2) guest doorbell-predicate race
 (3) guest waiter-bit / FUTEX_WAIT race (must never skip a needed wake)
 (4) s2c ring full -> fallback (no double-mint, guest still gets reply)
 (5) client death mid-request (half-written slot)
 (6) server teardown with active rings
 (7) many idle clients + one hot client
 (8) many hot clients
 (9) low-power vs balanced vs latency: low-power must NOT burn a core idle; latency pinned
 (10) FAST_OPS=0 / transport OFF byte-identical behavior
 TSAN/ASAN where feasible. Per-op perf counters required: hit/fallback/fail/s2c_full/suspend (step-2 already added ring_fast_hit/ring_fast_fallback; extend with fail/s2c_full/suspend during P7). This bead GATES default-ON; does NOT block P5.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
P7 DONE (commit darlingserver @8707a80 on perf/shmem-ring-abi-validator). All 10 required gates covered.

DETERMINISTIC RED->GREEN GATE (committed, in run-ring-shm-validate.sh): tests/ring_wake_race_test.cpp.
 - EXHAUSTIVE ordering enumeration is the authoritative gate: models each side's race-window micro-ops over a real shared control block using the REAL predicates, enumerates EVERY interleaving of the two sequences, asserts ZERO lose the wake.
   REQUEST side (gates 1,2): guest publish+conditional-doorbell vs server ACTIVE_POLLING->SLEEP_ARMED->critical-recheck->SLEEPING_EPOLL. 0 lost interleavings GREEN.
   RESPONSE side (gate 3): server publishReply+conditional-FUTEX_WAKE vs guest set-waiter-bit->recheck->FUTEX_WAIT. 0 lost GREEN.
   RED arms: -DRACE_NO_SERVER_RECHECK -> request side finds 5 lost interleavings (FAILS); -DRACE_WAITERBIT_AFTER_RECHECK -> response side finds 1 (FAILS). Both RED arms exit 1.
 - concurrent two-thread stress (pinned to distinct cores) corroborates on real silicon; DIAGNOSTIC-ONLY (reports watchdog timeouts, never fails the gate) because a per-round-handshake stress is scheduling-sensitive and a flaky CI gate is the exact hazard the gist warns against. Verified GREEN exits 0 even under 12 CPU hogs; the RED->GREEN contract rests on the exact enumeration.

PER-OP COUNTERS (gist-required hit/fallback/fail/s2c_full/suspend): added ring_fast_fail/ring_s2c_full/ring_fast_suspend to stat JSON (alongside existing ring_fast_hit/ring_fast_fallback). Incremented at real sites: publishReply-full (s2c_full) in doMachReplyPortInline+pushCallReply; inline-suspend detection in doWorkInline+doMachReplyPortInline (must stay 0 = allowlist correct); fast-path fallback dispatch throw (fast_fail).

SYSTEM-LEVEL (booted guest, ring ON balanced, server b665508a; OPERATIONAL validation, scripts in guest prefix p7-stress.sh/p7-teardown.sh/p7-idle.sh):
 gate 8 many-hot (8 concurrent rpbench): all null=0.
 gate 7 idle+hot (6 idle + 1 hot): hot=981ns null=0 (idle clients don't perturb hot path).
 gate 5 client-death mid-request (5 SIGKILLs mid-flight): post-death sanity null=0 (server not wedged/corrupted).
 gate 6 server teardown with active ring client: guest cleanly errors (dserver_rpc_interrupt_enter -111 = ECONNREFUSED), no crash/hang -- ring doesn't strand guest in un-woken FUTEX_WAIT when server vanishes.
 gate 9 idle CPU per mode: low-power / balanced / LATENCY all 0% idle darlingserver CPU -- bounded pre-epoll spin only runs when rings have pending work; idle guest falls straight to epoll_wait(-1). Gist's 'low-power must not burn a core' satisfied (and latency too).
 COUNTERS after ~1M fast ops across the stress: ring_fast_hit=1000215, fast_fallback=0, fast_fail=0, s2c_full=0, fast_suspend=0, clients_blocked=0 -- cleanest possible.
 gate 10 FAST_OPS=0 / transport-OFF: OFF build compiles ring out (ON dylib +13KB, 4 ring strings; OFF dylib 0) = byte-identical UDS; FAST_OPS=0 -> ring_fast_hit=0 generic path identical results (P6.1-step2 A/B + unit hatch gate). Standing fork-storm 3/3 correctness holds.

VERDICT: default-ON gate is GREEN. The lost-wake invariants are proven deterministically; live stress + counters clean over ~1M ops + teardown/death/idle. Prod restored OFF (a13ec2ae/e5cd6b12/025aa772/ebd247fd), cmake OFF, nothing pushed. Whether to actually flip default-ON is a separate product call (the gist's order was P7 gate -> then decide); P5 hot-op migration (dar-1il) does not depend on this.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Gist 7e0ec6b (P7 review) confirms the gate approach is sound (exhaustive interleaving over the real predicates) and the safety prerequisites are met. Two follow-ups actioned:
 (1) STAGED-ROLLOUT startup NOTICE (commit darlingserver @adf4e1a): _resolveRingSpinBudget() emits a one-time [NOTICE] at error() level (default log cutoff is Error, so it shows WITHOUT raising the level) announcing ring ACTIVE + spin budget + fast_ops/fast_mach_reply_port state + the full disable matrix (rebuild w/o DSERVER_RING_TRANSPORT; DARLING_SERVER_FAST_OPS=0; FAST_MACH_REPLY_PORT=0; MODE/SPIN_US). Verified live in dserver.log on first ring attach. Existing safety valves confirmed present: ABI-mismatch auto-reject->UDS fallback (dserver_ring_reject_abi, abi_version!=DSERVER_RING_ABI_VERSION); env kill-switches; transport-OFF compiles ring out.
 (2) SLOW-PATH EXERCISE GAP (gist flag): the live counters showed fail=0 s2c_full=0 suspend=0 -- the gist correctly notes the slow/fallback paths got little NATURAL exercise. Honest accounting: these are BY-DESIGN near-zero in normal operation -- the SPSC protocol allows only ONE in-flight reply per ring thread (the guest blocks on its single reply before sending again), so s2c-full essentially never occurs naturally; suspend MUST be 0 (allowlist correctness); fail only on a dispatch throw. Their CORRECTNESS rests on: the retry-not-loss datapath gate (ring_datapath_test.cpp case 5 fills s2c and asserts the request stays unconsumed = no loss), the doMachReplyPortInline/pushCallReply publish-fail->UDS-fallback-no-double-mint code path (review), and the no-double-mint guarantee (the trap already minted, so fallback sends the SAME port via UDS). Not claimed as naturally-stress-exercised. STAGED ROLLOUT (gist): dev/nightly first w/ telemetry (the counters + NOTICE), kill-switches, ABI auto-fallback, clear startup logging+disable instructions -- all now in place. Bead stays CLOSED; flipping default-ON remains a product call.

title: west darling-build: make intentional dirty deploy feedback clearer
description: While iterating on a local test-only darlingserver runtime, west darling-build --deploy --deploy-darlingserver without --skip-post-doctor successfully copied the new runtime and then exited non-zero because post-deploy doctor compared deployed dyld/mldr/darlingserver against deploy-baseline.md5. The command output contains the real deploy but the final failure is easy for agents to misread as no deploy or broken build. Improve UX: when --deploy changes runtime artifacts intentionally, either suggest --skip-post-doctor/targeted deploy flags in the failure, distinguish deploy success from doctor failure in a machine-readable summary, or provide a safer focused local-test deploy mode. Acceptance: a contract or documented command makes local test-only darlingserver deploys obvious and does not mask real inconsistent deploys.

title: perf #18 P6.1: opcode-specialized server fast path (skip generic Call pipeline for hot ring ops)
description: P6/dar-aw2 measured the hot ring mach_reply_port floor: 48% is doWork() microthread/fiber dispatch + 14% callFromMessage, ~62% generic-pipeline overhead around an op that should cost tens of ns. The gist's prescription: switch(callnum){case OP_MACH_REPLY_PORT: fast_mach_reply_port(); ...; default: generic_rpc_dispatch();}. Implement a server-side fast path in ringServiceThread that, for a small set of pure self-contained ops (start: mach_reply_port -- a pure reply-port mint), computes the result DIRECTLY on the main loop without rebuilding a UDS Message, without callFromMessage registry lookup, and without the doWork() microthread setup; publish the reply inline. HARD/SAFETY: the generic path carries the dar-l8k UAF / dar-6x4 rwlock / dar-gwn reply-on-drop fixes -- a fast path must replicate exactly what the op needs and bypass ONLY what it provably doesn't (per-op audit; mach_reply_port mints a fresh receive right in the caller task -- no cross-task/registry/suspend, so safe). RED->GREEN: a gate asserting the fast path returns the IDENTICAL port-mint semantics as the generic path (same rights, same refcount) + the existing fork-storm/build correctness. TARGET: cut hot floor ~4.7us -> ~1.5-2us (transport 0.85 + wakeup 0.96 + lean body). Measure with the P6 phase-prof (already committed). BLOCKED-BY: none (P6 data in hand).
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
REFINED 2026-06-29 (Opus) per gist 9dc7bbb. Architecture principle (CRITICAL): do NOT write a new fast_mach_reply_port() with its own port-alloc code (would drift from generic). INSTEAD factor the existing server-side reply-port mint into ONE shared mach_reply_port_impl(task/thread/space context) with minimal EXPLICIT context, then:
  generic path: Call -> microthread -> trap wrapper -> mach_reply_port_impl(ctx-from-current-thread)
  fast path:    ring opcode -> mach_reply_port_impl(ctx-from-ring-client) directly on main loop
So both paths run byte-identical mint logic; the fast path only bypasses Message-rebuild + callFromMessage registry lookup + the doWork() microthread/fiber setup.

STAGED:
  P6a: fast opcode dispatch in ringServiceThread -- switch(callnum){case MACH_REPLY_PORT: if(can_fastpath) fast; else generic; default: generic}.
  P6b: direct mach_reply_port via the shared impl.
  P6c: generalize to other tiny non-blocking traps LATER.

HARD FAST-PATH GUARD (all must hold or fall to generic): no blocking/suspend; no OOL descriptors; no fd passing; no complex rights translation; no guest-thread emulation frame needed; identical error/status behavior; SAME port namespace/task/thread identity as generic. ESPECIALLY: must derive caller task/thread from the ring client/thread_id at least as strictly as generic (don't lose 'current thread' identity if the microthread layer currently ESTABLISHES identity, not just overhead -- open question being traced now).

ESCAPE HATCHES (ship behind these, default such that ring works WITHOUT fast semantics): DARLING_RING_FAST_OPS=0 and DARLING_RING_FAST_MACH_REPLY_PORT=0 so transport can be enabled without specialized semantics if a bug surfaces.

RED->GREEN: gate asserting fast vs generic return IDENTICAL port-mint semantics (same name in the right task namespace, same refcount, same receive/send-right semantics, repeated calls identical, same cleanup after thread/process death, fork/exec/container teardown leaves no dangling port, debug counters intact) + existing fork-storm/build correctness.

MEASURE AFTER (reuse P6 phase-prof): (1) fast body-only cost, (2) generic body-only cost, (3) port-alloc primitive cost w/o ring, (4) p50/p90/p99 under fork storm, (5) idle CPU balanced, (6) correctness diff generic-vs-fast. EXPECT (gist, slightly wider than my ~1.5-2us): remove Message-rebuild+lookup ~ -0.6us; remove microthread/trap wrapper ~ -1.5-2.0us; plausible new floor ~1.7-2.5us (some of doWork's 2260ns may be REAL port-table/refcount/cacheline work, not wrapper).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
STEP 1 DONE 2026-06-29 (Opus): no-fiber inline path. Server-only, NOT pushed. darlingserver @4596b92 (branch perf/shmem-ring-abi-validator).

RECON (Explore agent) confirmed the design is safe: MachReplyPort::processCall()=dtape_mach_reply_port()=mach_reply_port(NULL) (real XNU trap) -> ipc_port_alloc(current_task()->itk_space,...). current_task()/current_thread() resolve via dtape_hook_current_thread() -> Thread::currentThread() -> currentThreadVar (thread-local) -- NOT gs-register/fiber. mach_reply_port never blocks. So setting currentThreadVar + dtape_thread_entering (cheap) is enough; the fiber is pure overhead for non-blocking ops.

IMPL: Thread::doWorkInline() = doWork() with the fiber stripped -- same context-enter, same processCall (the SAME dtape_mach_reply_port, no reimplemented Mach = gist's 'good variant'), same exception/metrics as microthreadWorker, same essential doneWorking cleanup. Runs on the main-loop stack. CONTRACT: only for proven-non-blocking ops; suspend is detected+logged. ringServiceThread calls doWorkInline for allowlisted ops (mach_reply_port, task_self_trap), falls back to doWork() if it declines. Escape hatches DARLING_SERVER_FAST_OPS=0 (global) / DARLING_SERVER_FAST_MACH_REPLY_PORT=0 (per-op). RED->GREEN gate tests/ring_fastpath_gate_test.c (-DFASTPATH_NO_HATCH RED arm) wired into run-ring-shm-validate.sh.

MEASURED (live, same build, hatch-flipped A/B; phase-prof TSC 1.9962GHz):
  fast inline ON:  mach_reply_port ~3.2-3.9us  (median ~3.5)
  generic (FAST_OPS=0): ~4.2-5.0us
  => ~900ns / ~21% off the hot path, null=0 both (60k mint+free clean = identical port semantics).
  phase-prof: server BODY 2260ns -> 1589ns (the removed fiber). Residual 1589ns body = real ipc_port_alloc + dtape enter/exit + makePendingCallActive/cleanup locking, NOT wrapper.
CORRECTNESS: fork-storm build 3/3 DONE (doWorkInline cleanup mirrors doneWorking; dar-l8k/dar-6x4 unregressed).

STEP 2 (NOT done, keeps this bead open): full opcode fast path in ringServiceThread -- skip Message-rebuild (drain) + callFromMessage registry re-lookup (dispatch ~579ns) + the Call heap-alloc, calling dtape_mach_reply_port directly with thread context already in hand from the ring, publishReply inline. Target ~2-2.5us. The thread/process are ALREADY resolved in ringServiceThread before callFromMessage, so the re-lookup is pure waste for the fast path. Reuse the P6 phase-prof to measure. Prod restored OFF (a13ec2ae/e5cd6b12/025aa772).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
STEP 2 PLAN 2026-06-29 (Opus, per gist 72939ad7). Approved direction: SURGICAL one-op direct ring dispatch for EXACTLY mach_reply_port -- NOT a new general dispatcher. Reuse the pre-resolved Thread/Process already held in ringServiceThread; reuse the SAME lower primitive (dtape_mach_reply_port). Goal: drop the generic RPC framing (Message rebuild + callFromMessage registry re-lookup ~579-647ns + heap Call alloc + decode).

CURRENT (step 1, @4596b92): ringServiceThread builds a UDS-format Message, calls Call::callFromMessage (re-resolves thread/process via registry by nsid, heap-allocates a Call, setPendingCall), then doWorkInline() runs processCall()=dtape_mach_reply_port() without the fiber. ~3.5us median.

LIFETIME (gist's key safety point -- ALREADY SATISFIED, verify on edit): ringServiceThread receives `const std::shared_ptr<Thread>& thread`; `process = thread->process()` is also a shared_ptr. So the pre-resolved context is ref-held for the whole slot iteration; the fast path reuses `thread`/`process` directly (NO raw ptr, NO epoch issue). callFromMessage's registry lookup was redundant for the ring path -- it re-finds the SAME thread we already hold.

STEP 2 SHAPE:
  ring slot (mach_reply_port) -> shape guards -> Thread::doMachReplyPortInline(seq) -> [enter dtape ctx like doWorkInline] dtape_mach_reply_port() -> publish reply onto s2c directly -> [exit ctx] -> wakeGuest (conditional).
DESIGN DECISION for the next agent: the step-1 path published via Call::_sendReply -> Thread::pushCallReply (ring reply sink, one-shot beginRingReply). The fast path has NO Call object. Two options: (A) call RingBuffer::publishReply(seq, dserver_callnum_mach_reply_port, code=0, body={uint32 port}) DIRECTLY + ring->wakeGuest(), building the {dserver_ring_reply_hdr.code, uint32 port} body inline (this is what publishReply already expects) -- cleanest, no Call/Message at all; (B) keep beginRingReply + a minimal reply emit. RECOMMEND (A): the reply convention is already centralized in RingBuffer::publishReply; the fast path just supplies (seq, callnum, 0, &port, 4). Note dtape_mach_reply_port returns the uint32 port name; on the existing UDS reply it's {replyhdr{number,code=0}, port} -> ring body = {reply_hdr.code=0}{port}. Mirror exactly.

NEW Thread::doMachReplyPortInline(uint32_t seq) (gated DSERVER_RING_TRANSPORT): same guards as doWorkInline (deferral/running/terminating/dead -> return false to fall back); set _running + currentThreadVar + dtape_thread_entering; call dtape_mach_reply_port() DIRECTLY (no Call, no processCall object) on the main-loop stack (never blocks); publish via ring (option A); then the SAME essential cleanup as doWorkInline (dtape_thread_exiting, currentThreadVar=null, _running=false, interrupt scheduling, release). MUST reuse dtape_mach_reply_port -- do NOT reimplement ipc_port_alloc. The thread for the inline op is the ring-owner thread itself (it's its own caller), so currentThreadVar = that thread.

SAFETY RAILS (gist): fast dispatch ONLY when (DARLING_SERVER_FAST_OPS && DARLING_SERVER_FAST_MACH_REPLY_PORT) && callnum==mach_reply_port && reqlen==0 (no payload) && no descriptors/arena (ring slots carry none, assert arena_len==0) && ring ABI v2. ANY mismatch -> fall through to the EXISTING step-1 path (callFromMessage+doWorkInline), which itself falls to generic doWork. Preserve: same error metrics, same exception cleanup, same suspend-in-inline log, same process/thread/task accounting. DARLING_SERVER_FAST_MACH_REPLY_PORT=0 must give BYTE-equivalent old behavior (route through callFromMessage).

COUNTERS (gist): add fast_hit (direct dispatch taken), fast_fallback (shape mismatch -> generic), reuse the inline_suspend_detected log. Emit in stat JSON (ring-gated), like the existing ring_serviced_* counters.

RED->GREEN: extend ring_fastpath_gate_test.c OR add a gate asserting (a) shape predicate accepts only mach_reply_port w/ reqlen==0 + rejects payload/descriptors, (b) hatch off == route-to-generic. LIVE: 60k mint+free null=0 identical port lifecycle fast-on vs fast-off; phase-prof shows drain+dispatch collapse toward 0 for the fast op.

PERF TARGET (gist, slightly conservative): current ~3.2-3.9us median ~3.5 -> remove dispatch -0.5-0.7us -> expect ~2.7-3.1us median; stretch ~2.3-2.5us. Success bar: median < 3us. After this, decide whether to generalize a fast-op table to other tiny non-blocking traps (DEFER -- separate bead).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: STEP 2 AGENT BRIEF (self-contained handoff for another agent): darling-workspace/pr-drafts/perf18-p6.1-step2-agent-brief.md (also /home/ilyagulya/.claude-work/jobs/ae391f52/tmp/P6.1-STEP2-AGENT-BRIEF.md). Covers: surgical one-op direct dispatch design, Thread::doMachReplyPortInline + RingBuffer::publishReply option-A, lifetime guarantee (thread already shared_ptr in ringServiceThread), safety rails + counters (fast_hit/fast_fallback), RED->GREEN gate, 7-copy deploy map, the 'ninja emulation != relinks dylib' + fork-timeline staging gotchas, A/B + fork-storm protocol, target median <3us. Branch perf/shmem-ring-abi-validator @4596b92, server-only.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: CORRECTION on brief location: durable copy is at memory/dar-perf18-p6.1-step2-brief.md (the pr-drafts path does not exist in-tree). That memory file is the canonical self-contained handoff for the step-2 agent.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
P6.1 STEP 2 DONE (commit darlingserver @e9ce26d on perf/shmem-ring-abi-validator). Surgical direct ring dispatch for EXACTLY mach_reply_port -- skips Message-rebuild+callFromMessage registry re-lookup+Call alloc that step1 still paid. New Thread::doMachReplyPortInline(seq): same dtape context as doWorkInline (currentThreadVar+dtape_thread_entering), calls bare dtape_mach_reply_port() (real trap, zero reimpl), publishes {code=0}{uint32 port} straight via RingBuffer::publishReply (option A) + wakeGuest. NO Call/Message. Lifetime safe (thread shared_ptr-held in ringServiceThread). Shape guard reqlen==0 && arena_len==0 = security boundary for no-Call path; callnum mismatch/payload/arena/inline-decline -> falls back to generic step-1 path. Hatches: FAST_OPS=0 / FAST_MACH_REPLY_PORT=0 route to framed path (ring_fast_hit=0). Counters ring_fast_hit/ring_fast_fallback in stat JSON.

LIVE A/B (ring ON, 5x20000 isolated mach_reply_port, null=0 both): fast ON ~943-967ns/call median, fast_hit=101594 fast_fallback=0; fast OFF (FAST_OPS=0) ~4480-4800ns, fast_hit=0. ~4.7x over generic ring path, ~0.95us median -- BEAT the <3us target by 3x (bigger than projected ~2.7-3.1us: bare op also lets the server main-loop spin catch nearly every req with ~0 FUTEX_WAKE, so hot path is syscall-free AND alloc-free both sides). 60k+ clean mint+free null=0 both arms = identical semantics. Fork-storm correctness 3/3 DONE (.o=200/200, clients_blocked=0) -- no dar-l8k/dar-6x4 regress.

Progression: P3 ring 26us -> P4 wake 4.7us -> P6.1 step1 3.5us -> P6.1 step2 ~0.95us. RED->GREEN: ring_fastpath_gate_test.c gains shape predicate, -DNO_SHAPE_GUARD RED arm fails payload/arena-rejection asserts; both RED arms (NO_HATCH, NO_SHAPE_GUARD) correctly fail. Prod restored OFF (a13ec2ae/e5cd6b12/025aa772/ebd247fd), cmake OFF. Nothing pushed. Open follow-on (SEPARATE bead): generalize a fast-op table to other tiny non-blocking traps.

title: Package A0-ARCH stack into patch profiles (homebrew->perf->arch): git-am replay limitation to resolve
description:
Package the landed darlingserver work above the homebrew fix-tip into stacked patch profiles. ANALYSIS DONE (session ae391f52), packaging DEFERRED on a git-am limitation.

STRUCTURE (proven, clean sequential layers above integration/homebrew tip 92049f0):
  - 78 commits perf#18 SERVER ring   (92049f0..03cf98e)   bead dar-dar6x4-perf-5dq.30  -> perf profile
  - 41 commits A0-hang fix class     (03cf98e..f4530b5)    bead dar-a0-hang-fixes-gkev  -> perf profile
  - 40 commits A0-ARCH               (f4530b5..fix/a0-arch-redesign tip 93c7049)  bead dar-a0arch-redesign-253u -> NEW arch profile (base-profile: perf)
Ranges tile exactly (78+41+40=159). Profiles: homebrew -> perf (has guest ring; add SERVER ring + A0-hang) -> arch (A0-ARCH).

STATUS: perf profile with the 2 new ds patches PASSES west patch verify GREEN. arch profile FAILS west patch verify with a modify/delete on tests/a0-repro/a0-gate.sh + a src/thread.cpp content conflict.
ROOT: NOT a real base-mismatch -- a manual  of both patches onto the true tip 92049f0 applies 159 commits CLEAN (trial verified). west patch verify rebuilds the tree by replaying homebrew patches on the raw manifest base then am-ing the multi-commit mboxes commit-by-commit via --3way; on the 41+40-commit mboxes the 3-way merge loses a0-gate.sh at an intermediate step (31c2665 creates it inside a0-hang; A0-ARCH stage-0 2037e24 modifies it) -> modify/delete.

DECISION FOR NEXT ATTEMPT (pick one):
  (A) SQUASH each layer to a single commit before export -> mbox = 1 patch/layer, no commit-by-commit 3-way replay, the modify/delete edge disappears. Loses per-commit history but matches the 'one commit per layer' intent. RECOMMENDED.
  (B) Fix the source-base so west patch verify builds from an intermediate integration snapshot ref that matches the real tree (understand how verify picks the base for stacked base-profile chains first).
Do the whole thing in ONE pass (perf 2 patches + arch profile) and land only when BOTH profiles verify GREEN. Constraint: no push; local only. The reverted attempt's exact patch entries are in this session transcript.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: DIAGNOSIS CORRECTED (prior 'git-am --3way limitation' note was WRONG). Read west_commands/patch.py: a stacked profile takes its base from integration/<base-profile> (_base_revision l.179-181); if that branch is MISSING in the module it falls back to the raw manifest revision (l.182-183). arch verify was red purely because integration/perf did not exist -> A0-ARCH applied onto bare manifest -> a0-gate.sh modify/delete + thread.cpp conflict. The source-base values are CORRECT (92049f0/03cf98e/f4530b5; tile 78+41+40=159). NOTHING to fix in source-base. Completed this session: persistent fix/* layer branches (fix/perf18-server-ring @03cf98e, fix/a0-hang-fixes @f4530b5, fix/a0-arch-redesign @tip -- KEEP), full-SHA source-base, perf(+2 ds patches)+arch profiles built, export --check clean on both. REMAINING: run the stacked apply chain in order -- west patch apply homebrew -> apply perf (regenerates integration/perf WITH server-ring+a0-hang) -> apply/verify arch. verify --profile arch in isolation cannot pass by design. apply refuses unless every patched module is parked on its integration branch or detached at base (durability guard), so the chain moves the WHOLE multi-repo workspace onto integration/* branches -- deferred to a deliberate pass.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: APPLY-CHAIN RUN (session ae391f52): homebrew applied clean (integration/homebrew built, all modules). perf: the 2 NEW darlingserver patches (server-ring + a0-hang) APPLIED CLEAN on integration/homebrew ('applied 2 patches'), integration/perf built = homebrew + 119 commits, a0-gate.sh present -> the layer stacking is PROVEN correct. But apply hit TWO TOOLING bugs (not patch problems): (1) perf profile's integration-date was UNQUOTED yaml -> parsed as datetime -> _record_integration crashed ('expected str... not datetime'). FIXED: quoted to ISO string '2026-07-01T00:00:00Z' (homebrew uses the same quoted form; arch normalized too). (2) west patch apply on the SUPERPROJECT (darling module, perf's mldr-compact-fd patch) fails 'Dirty index: cannot apply' because parking submodules onto different branches dirties the superproject's submodule gitlink pointers, and git am (unlike apply's _ensure_clean which uses --ignore-submodules) refuses a dirty index. This is a real gap in west patch apply's superproject handling. Workspace fully RESTORED to pre-apply snapshot afterward (all 8 modules back on snapshot branches, deployed binary d81b5cf1 untouched, layer branches intact). REMAINING to finish the chain: fix bug (2) in west patch apply (stage/clean the superproject submodule pointers before git am, or apply the darling-module patches with a submodule-aware index reset), then re-run homebrew->perf->arch. The darlingserver-side stacking (the hard part) is proven; only the superproject mldr patch application is blocked by the tooling.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
RESOLVED (session ae391f52). Fixed tooling bug (2) in west_commands/patch.py: new _reset_submodule_index(repo) resets the superproject submodule gitlink INDEX entries to HEAD right before git am on the darling module. Parking submodules dirties those gitlinks; git am has its own dirty-index guard that (unlike _ensure_clean with --ignore-submodules=all) is NOT submodule-aware, so it refused. The darling-module patches only touch real files (mldr.c), never gitlinks, so restoring the index gitlinks to HEAD is a no-op on real content and lets git am proceed. Guarded to module=="darling". Bug (1) (unquoted integration-date) already fixed by ISO-quoting perf/arch dates.

FULL CHAIN NOW COMPLETES CLEAN: apply homebrew (8 parent patches) -> apply perf (2 ds patches server-ring+a0-hang; integration/perf 7115289e0) -> apply arch (1 ds patch a0-arch-redesign; integration/arch 790a74d63, ds tip d5cb486). DECISIVE PROOF: integration/arch darlingserver TREE d415bb9 is BYTE-IDENTICAL to canonical fix/a0-arch-redesign tip -> stacked profiles reconstruct the exact source state that built the deployed doctor-baseline gate-green binary d81b5cf1; rebuild redundant (identical tree => identical output). a0-gate.sh present. Neither DECISION option (A squash / B source-base) needed -- real fix was the superproject index reset in the applier. No push; local only. Layer branches intact. patch.py fix committed.

title: Make west test --gc remove historical guest runner output files
description: Post-fix hygiene audit found historical /tmp/west-ctest-guest-c.* files created before e7bc0e0. Current run-darling-c-test EXIT cleanup works under the real darling-debug-runner (a post-fix 10x mixed-queue run created none), but west test --gc does not discover/remove this known stale namespace. Extend GC with an ownership-safe cleanup rule for /tmp/west-ctest-guest-c.*, preserve active files, add a behavioral regression contract that creates controlled stale/current files and proves only stale output is removed, then run GC and complete a post-run hygiene check.

It only removes ordinary non-symlink files matching west-ctest-guest-c.* at or older than the configured age threshold, preserving fresh files and directories. ctest_lifecycle_contract.py covers stale-vs-fresh selection.
