title: brew install end-to-end: resolve CLT/bottle so a formula actually installs
description: psynch deadlock (dar-gwn.2) is fixed; brew now runs past 'Fetching downloads' but jq fails at 'Command Line Tools does not support macOS 11' (no macOS-11 Tier 3 bottle -> source build needs CLT). Goal: either install a formula that needs no CLT (poured/all bottle) or make the already-present CLT in darling-prefix-homebrew-test/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools usable so a source build proceeds.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
OPTION A DONE — 'brew install ca-certificates' SUCCEEDS end-to-end (no CLT needed). INSTALL_RC=0, RESULT=exited (no hang). Poured ca-certificates--2026-05-14.all.bottle.tar.gz, regenerated CA bundle, '🍺 /usr/local/Cellar/ca-certificates/2026-05-14: 4 files, 216.9KB', brew list --versions confirms 'ca-certificates 2026-05-14', cert.pem (181KB) written, brew cleanup ran. Bundle 20260610T194143Z-brew-install-cacerts-nocLT. This is the first full real brew install working under Darling — proves the psynch fix (dar-gwn.2) holds through Ruby formula load + bottle pour. ca-certificates has an ':all' (platform-independent) bottle so it pours without a compiler; formula_installer.rb:541 'if !pour_bottle? && DevelopmentTools.installed?' confirms a poured bottle skips perform_build_from_source_checks (the CLT/SDK gate).

OPTION B DIAGNOSIS (source builds / CLT) — exact blocker found. The fatal 'Your CLT does not support macOS 11' comes from extend/os/mac/diagnostic.rb:464 check_if_supported_sdk_available: fires when DevelopmentTools.installed? (CLT present at darling-prefix-homebrew-test/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools) AND MacOS.sdk == nil. MacOS.sdk is nil because os/mac/sdk.rb read_sdk_version (line 117) reads SDKSettings.JSON, but the CLT's SDKs/MacOSX.sdk only ships SDKSettings.PLIST (CanonicalName macosx10.13, no .json). MacOSX10.13.sdk is a symlink to it. So all_sdks is EMPTY -> no applicable SDK -> fatal on any build-from-source. NOTE the real SDK is 10.13 (< 11) anyway. To enable source builds: provide an SDK with SDKSettings.json Version>=11 that Homebrew can scan (e.g. add SDKSettings.json to MacOSX.sdk + a MacOSX11.sdk dir/symlink), accepting that headers are 10.13 so some builds may still fail. Two stashed CLTs exist (CommandLineTools.apple-clt-15.3.* and .incompatible.*) — candidates to evaluate. Not done (Option A satisfies the request); track here if full source-build support is wanted.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
OPTION B PROVEN — first from-source brew install works under Darling. `brew install --build-from-source lz4` → 🍺 /usr/local/Cellar/lz4/1.10.0 (24 files), INSTALL_RC=0, `brew list`=lz4 1.10.0, built lz4 binary runs + compress/decompress roundtrip OK. xz also compiles+links+make-installs fully (proven across runs).

Toolchain is fine: CLT clang (Apple LLVM 9.0.0 / clang-900) and ld64-305 RUN under Darling; C & C++ compile+run. The blockers were all environment, fixed in the test prefix (darling-prefix-homebrew-test):

1. SDK DETECTION — CLT MacOSX.sdk ships only SDKSettings.plist (10.13); Homebrew read_sdk_version only parses SDKSettings.json, so MacOS.sdk=nil → 'does not support macOS 11' gate. FIX: wrote SDKSettings.json (Version 11.3) into the active SDK. Permanent: ship SDKSettings.json in Darling's own SDK (Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk, git-tracked).

2. LINKER -oso_prefix — Homebrew's cc shim passes -oso_prefix on every link; ld64-305 (Xcode 9 CLT) errors 'unknown option' (added in Xcode 11 / ld64-507). FIX (3 edits, upstream-style, mirrors no_fixup_chains/ld_classic gates): shared.rb oso_prefix_support? = ld64_version>=507; os/mac super.rb determine_cccfg appends 'o'; cc shim oso_prefix? requires config.include?('o'). Under Darling ld64_version is blank → flag dropped.

3. SANDBOX — build runs under sandbox-exec -f homebrew.sb; disabled via HOMEBREW_NO_SANDBOX=1 (made mac sandbox.rb available? honor it). Belt-and-suspenders; macOS sandbox is meaningless under emulation.

4. *** FINAL ROOT BLOCKER = Darling VFS bug (see new issue) *** — make install populates the keg correctly (a fresh find/ls process AND the host see bin/lz4, lib/liblz4.dylib, headers, etc.), but the long-lived parent brew (ruby) process that created the keg dir empty before the build sees it STILL EMPTY when it re-globs → raises 'Empty installation' and rm_r rolls it back. Cross-process directory-entry coherence: a process that cached a dir doesn't see entries written into it by another process. Worked around with HOMEBREW_FRESH_KEG_CHECK=1 (re-check keg via fresh subprocess find) → full green install. The real fix belongs in Darling's VFS.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: HOUSEKEEPING done: (1) committed SDKSettings.json into Darling's bundled SDK (Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk) at local commit 75437f43b — makes MacOS.sdk detectable out-of-box for users on Darling's own SDK. (2) Reverted the [CVDBG] debug instrumentation in darlingserver (duct-tape/pthread/kern_synch.c + src/psynch.c) — submodule clean; rebuilt a clean/quiet darlingserver (no debug logging). NOTE: the 3 oso_prefix Homebrew edits + HOMEBREW_NO_SANDBOX + HOMEBREW_FRESH_KEG_CHECK live in the test prefix's Homebrew (darling-prefix-homebrew-test), NOT in the Darling repo. Final blocker for clean source builds is the VFS bug dar-e1j.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
UPDATE: dar-e1j (the final blocker) is FIXED at the Darling source level, so the
HOMEBREW_FRESH_KEG_CHECK workaround is GONE. Re-ran the full from-source install with the
real (unpatched) Keg#empty_installation? and it is green:

  brew install --build-from-source lz4  ->  lz4 1.10.0, 24 files 779KB, built in 28s, RC=0
  brew list: lz4 1.10.0 ; binary runs ; compress/decompress roundtrip OK.

What was actually required for Option B (source builds), now that the root causes are understood:
1. SDKSettings.json so MacOS.sdk is detected  -- COMMITTED to Darling (75437f43b). [Darling source]
2. getattrlist ATTR_CMN_NAME + ATTR_CMN_OBJTYPE so Ruby Dir.glob works -- COMMITTED (xnu d157c3f,
   darling 7df268d2d). This removes the need for HOMEBREW_FRESH_KEG_CHECK entirely. [Darling source]
3. SDK versioned-symlink name must match SDKSettings version (test prefix had stale
   MacOSX10.13.sdk -> MacOSX.sdk vs content 11.3, which Homebrew check_broken_sdks rejected once
   glob worked). Fixed in test prefix by MacOSX11.sdk. [prefix/SDK layout, not yet in Darling src]

STILL test-prefix-only Homebrew patches (NOT yet upstreamed/committed), each a separate concern:
- oso_prefix ld64 gating: Darling's CLT ld64-305 rejects -oso_prefix (added in ld64-507/Xcode11).
  3 edits gate it on DevelopmentTools.ld64_version >= 507. Legit Homebrew compatibility patch.
- HOMEBREW_NO_SANDBOX=1: Darling's sandbox-exec (seatbelt) emulation is incomplete. Separate bug;
  track independently if we want sandboxed builds.

Net: the Darling-side blockers for `brew install --build-from-source` are resolved and committed.
Remaining items are Homebrew-compat patches (oso_prefix) and the sandbox-exec emulation gap.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
Two previously test-prefix-only blockers now fixed at Darling source level (local commits):

1. SANDBOX (was HOMEBREW_NO_SANDBOX=1) -- darling f85f220f0. Tracked sandbox-exec(1) pass-through (src/sandbox/sandbox-exec.sh + CMake install to usr/bin/sandbox-exec, mirroring pagesize.tproj). Seatbelt is a no-op under emulation; stub discards profile opts (-f/-p/-n/-D/--) and execs the command. The 'empty keg' the old sandbox.rb comment blamed on sandbox was actually dar-e1j (getattrlist/glob), already fixed. Reverted test-prefix sandbox.rb to stock; verified 'brew install --build-from-source lz4' GREEN with no HOMEBREW_NO_SANDBOX. (Prior 202-byte manual stub was untracked; this makes it reproducible + fixes -n/-D arg handling.)

2. SDK versioned symlink -- darling 3c79b03fa. Renamed bundled MacOSX10.13.sdk -> MacOSX11.sdk so the name matches SDKSettings.json content version 11.3 (added 75437f43b); else Homebrew check_broken_sdks rejects 10 != 11. No source referenced the 10.13 name.

REMAINING test-prefix-only: the -oso_prefix ld64>=507 gating (3 Homebrew edits) is a Homebrew-compat patch (Darling CLT ld64-305 predates the flag) and belongs upstream in Homebrew, not the Darling repo.

Net: Darling-source blockers for 'brew install --build-from-source' all resolved + committed locally; verified green end-to-end (lz4) with stock Homebrew sandbox + SDK paths.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
TOOLCHAIN ARCHITECTURE + oso_prefix RE-ANALYSIS (2026-06-11) — corrects an earlier wrong attribution.

DARLING SHIPS NO COMPILER. /usr/bin/{clang,clang++,cc,gcc,ld,as} in the system layer are all the SAME 12660-byte Mach-O: xcrun trampolines (src/xcselect/xcrun-shim.c -> xcselect_invoke_xcrun). They compile/link nothing; they locate the real toolchain via xcode-select/DEVELOPER_DIR and re-exec it inside an installed CLT or Xcode. README confirms the design: 'install Xcode along with its toolchain and SDKs' and invoke its clang directly. So 'use Darling's own tools instead of CLT' is not possible for compilation — there is no Darling clang. CLT/Xcode is REQUIRED for clang + SDK headers/frameworks.

What Darling DOES build itself: cctools-port binutils only (ld64, as, otool, nm) at src/external/cctools-port. Notably its ld64 DOES support -oso_prefix (ld64/src/ld/Options.cpp:4078). BUT /usr/bin/ld is the xcrun trampoline -> CLT's ld, NOT the cctools ld. So in practice the CLT linker is what runs.

WHY -oso_prefix FAILS: the container's CLT ld is Apple prebuilt ld64-305 (Xcode 9; verified via strings: PROJECT:ld64-305). -oso_prefix was added in ld64-507 (Xcode 11). Homebrew's cc shim emits -oso_prefix unconditionally, ld64-305 errors 'unknown option'.

CORRECTED ATTRIBUTION: this is NEITHER a Darling source bug (Darling's own ld supports the flag) NOR clearly a Homebrew bug (emitting -oso_prefix is correct for toolchains Homebrew supports; Xcode 9/ld64-305 is BELOW Homebrew's support floor, so upstream would likely reject a gating patch, not accept it). It is an ENVIRONMENT mismatch: running against an unsupported, too-old Apple CLT. My earlier note calling the 3-edit Homebrew oso_prefix gate an 'upstream Homebrew' concern was an unjustified pattern-match (the workaround lives in Homebrew code and mirrors Homebrew's own ld64-version gates, but that does not make upstream the right home). The Homebrew 3-edit gate is a LOCAL workaround, not an upstream candidate.

TWO REAL FIXES (instead of the Homebrew patch):
  (A) Route linking through Darling's own cctools-port ld64 (oso_prefix-capable) while keeping CLT clang — e.g. clang -fuse-ld=<darling cctools ld>, or install cctools ld ahead of the trampoline. Needs verifying CLT clang-900 drives the newer ld cleanly.
  (B) Install a newer CLT (ld64>=507). See new bead for the CLT-upgrade path.

title: Orphaned darling guest processes (mldr) leak across sessions and destabilize fresh boots
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
PROBLEM (observed 2026-06-21): after many brew-under-Darling boots against darling-prefix-homebrew-test, the host accumulates orphaned darling guest processes — 48 'mldr' processes (exe=darling-prefix/libexec/darling/usr/libexec/darling/mldr) found stuck in S/SN, spanning PIDs from 659xxx (earlier sessions) to 1993xxx (this session). Their backing darlingserver had died, leaving them unreaped (reparented to init). darlingserver itself was frequently left as a ZOMBIE (Z, <defunct>) because its parent (the setuid-root 'darling shell' launcher) didn't wait() for it. Concrete leftovers seen: two libunistring './configure' mldr guests still alive 47 min after their build's darlingserver died.

IMPACT: each new 'darling shell' boot against the same prefix then fails at startup with 'semaphore_timedwait failed (internally): code=-111' and the new darlingserver instantly becomes a zombie, so brew can't run. This BLOCKS the dar-gwn.1.5 live re-repro (see that bead). Workaround this session: user-authorized sudo sweep of all 48 mldr guests + dead darlingservers; fresh boot then works.

DEFINITION OF DONE:
1. Root-cause WHY guest processes survive their darlingserver's death instead of being reaped/killed. Candidates to investigate: (a) darlingserver not killing its managed guest PIDs on shutdown/crash (process group / subreaper teardown); (b) the 'darling shell' launcher not wait()ing on darlingserver -> zombie darlingserver -> guests reparented to init and never cleaned; (c) shutdown path ('darling shutdown' / SIGTERM) not propagating to in-flight build children (configure/make); (d) crash path: when darlingserver SEGVs/exits abnormally, no cleanup of the guest pidns/process group.
2. Determine whether darlingserver is supposed to be the subreaper for its guests and whether that's wired up (route2/eunion experiments touched subreaper logic — check current homebrew-tip vs eunion-tip behavior).
3. Propose + implement a fix so that when darlingserver exits (clean OR crash), all its guest processes are terminated (e.g. PR_SET_PDEATHSIG, kill process group, or darlingserver-side reap on shutdown), and the launcher reaps darlingserver so it doesn't zombie.
4. Verify: run N brew boots in a loop; after each, assert zero leftover mldr guests + zero zombie darlingservers for the prefix.

CONSTRAINTS: workspace rules — capture any code fix as a patch in patches/homebrew/<module>/ + register in patches.yml; nothing pushed without explicit per-action approval; never edit integration/*. Relates to dar-gwn.1.5 (blocks its live verification) and the homebrew-run-harness.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: darlingserver EXIT-CAUSE notes (2026-06-21): dserver.log for the test prefix is 5.6 GB, append-mode across MANY sessions, logging the microthread parking-helper ([PH] ENTERING/EXITING/BLOCK/MARKWAIT) at 'Error' level for EVERY context switch — i.e. this build has DSERVER_EXTENDED_DEBUG-style verbose logging on. Disk is at 92% (19G free) — not full yet, so not the proximate kill, but the volume is a real problem (every microthread switch hits a multi-GB file) and pollutes diagnosis. The log also shows 'procmem Error: Failed to write N bytes ... No such process' (darlingserver writing to client memory after the client died — S2C/procmem to a dead guest) and steady [CALLDBG] RPC traffic. The dying darlingserver (this boot) logged its client thread's microthread '[PH] BLOCK waiting=1' then simply STOPPED logging — no shutdown/abort/panic line — consistent with an abnormal-but-signalless exit while a client RPC was parked. NEXT to pin the exit: attach gdb to a LIVE darlingserver during a fresh hang/crash repro, break on exit/_exit/abort/std::terminate/__cxa_throw, and consider disabling the [PH] Error-level spam (or rotating/truncating the log) first so the repro isn't drowned. Recommend turning OFF the extended/parking-helper debug logging in the test build.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-21: Leg A fix (try/catch around processCall in darlingserver thread.cpp:346 + call.cpp:240) directly addresses dar-pot's root event — darlingserver no longer std::terminate's on an uncaught processCall exception, so it won't die mid-session leaving guests orphaned + itself zombie. Built+deployed+boots (see dar-gwn.1.5 for details + the darling-build vs darling-dev/darling/build prefix trap). Still needs multi-boot verification that zero mldr guests + zero zombie darlingservers remain after N brew installs, and the launcher-reaps-darlingserver leg. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: LIVE RECURRENCE (session ae391f52, 2026-07-04, duplex clean re-measure). Hit this twice on warm boot: a separate warm-boot step (`darling shell /bin/echo BOOT_OK`) hung ~3min; live snapshot showed the EXACT dar-pot signature -- darlingserver alive+idle (uptime 461s, rpcs_serviced FROZEN at 2337 across 4s, workers_busy=0) + launchd + an orphan /usr/libexec/shellspawn, but NO mldr (the guest that should run /bin/echo never came up or died instantly), and the workdir 'work/' dir was d--------- root root (a half-set-up vchroot). Same class as the historical 'semaphore_timedwait -111 + instant zombie' startup failure. WORKAROUND that unblocked me (matches the bead's known workaround): hard clean = darling shutdown + kill -9 all mldr/darlingserver/launchd/shellspawn/vchroot + sudo rm -rf ${PREFIX}.workdir + mkdir + sleep 3, THEN a SINGLE-SESSION boot (boot+workload in ONE `darling shell --login -c 'brew ...'`, NO separate warm-echo step). Attempt 3 with that structure booted clean and completed brew rc=0 (msg_total=101067). LESSON for harnesses: (a) a separate warm-boot probe step is a reliable dar-pot trigger -- fold boot into the workload shell; (b) always hard-clean workdir+procs BETWEEN runs so orphans can't accumulate (see memory dar-snapshot-before-clean-on-hang). This did NOT block the final measurement but confirms dar-pot is still live and still the #1 stand-stability tax on brew harnesses.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
FIXED + VERIFIED (session ae391f52, 2026-07-04).

ROOT CAUSE (pinned to the line): darlingserver creates the guest PID ns via clone(CLONE_NEWPID) in darlingserver.cpp; the clone child is launchd (ns PID 1), every mldr guest is its descendant. The kernel cascade-kills a PID ns when its PID-1 dies -- but darlingserver is launchd's PARENT, OUTSIDE the ns, so when darlingserver died (crash/SIGKILL/launcher-exit) launchd survived, the ns persisted, and all guests orphaned + accumulated until a fresh boot wedged.

FIX (architecturally correct, not a patch): the launchd clone child now sets prctl(PR_SET_PDEATHSIG, SIGKILL) so when darlingserver dies for ANY reason, ns-PID-1 dies with it and the kernel cascade-kills every guest -- no guest can outlive its server, none can accumulate. Also hardens 'darling shutdown' (SIGKILLing darlingserver now cascades to launchd+guests via pdeathsig even if the launchd_pid read races). Placement is load-bearing: AFTER perma_drop_privileges (a cred change clears pdeathsig) and BEFORE spawnLaunchd; the pdeathsig survives the exec into mldr (not set-uid). Empirically confirmed: ns-PID-1 getppid()==0 across the ns boundary (so a getppid race guard is inapplicable), and pdeathsig fires on parent death.

RED-TDD host-model gate: tests/pot_lifetime_binding_test.c + run-pot-lifetime-binding.sh model server->launchd(CLONE_NEWPID)->guest. nobind arm (no pdeathsig) = GUEST-SURVIVED (reproduces the orphan); bind arm = GUEST-REAPED. Both pass, self-cleans.

LIVE VERIFY (DoD item 4) on the real fixed binary (baseline d81b5cf1 + fix, md5 6d854a92): ARM A = 3/3 clean brew reinstall xz rc=0, each leaving 0 mldr + 0 zombie darlingserver after shutdown. ARM B (the actual dar-pot scenario) = booted a guest, SIGKILL'd ONLY darlingserver (no darling shutdown), guest+launchd cascade-killed by pdeathsig. VERDICT GREEN.

SOURCE: darlingserver fix/pot-guest-lifetime-binding @ d0cfd7b (base manifest-rev 89751e64; profile-independent -- clone region byte-identical across homebrew/perf/arch). PACKAGED in the HOMEBREW profile (darlingserver/pot-guest-lifetime-binding.patch, sha c9b7c4b0, publication-status blocked) so perf+arch inherit it; west patch verify --profile homebrew = 72 patches GREEN. Baseline binary restored after verification; stand clean.

DoD items 1-4 all addressed. The launcher-reaps-darlingserver leg (b) was analysed as cosmetic-only: a zombie darlingserver is harmless once guests are guaranteed dead by pdeathsig, and implementing it would touch the setuid launcher for marginal benefit -- not implemented.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Patchset/integration finalized. Existing dirty worktree content was byte-for-byte identical to source branch fix/pot-guest-lifetime-binding @ d0cfd7b6605dac15471240724dec03bb9b91231e and patch patches/homebrew/darlingserver/pot-guest-lifetime-binding.patch. Rebuilt integration/homebrew, integration/perf, and integration/arch via west patch apply so current worktree now has dar-pot APPLIED with no dirty duplicate. Verified: west patch verify --profile homebrew/perf/arch all pass; west patch status --profile homebrew/perf/arch --strict has 0 missing/0 no-file. Runtime gate GREEN from integration/arch: tests/run-pot-lifetime-binding.sh reports nobind GUEST-SURVIVED and bind GUEST-REAPED.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 correctness audit: focused prove-red and normal GREEN passed for pot_lifetime_binding_runtime. The host model proves both arms: nobind leaves GUEST-SURVIVED and bind gives GUEST-REAPED. Runtime RED fails for the intended lifetime bug with stderr 'guest descendant survived darlingserver death' in /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T023744Z-west-test-pot_lifetime_binding_runtime. Runtime GREEN emits POT_LIFETIME_BINDING_RUNTIME_OK in /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T023814Z-west-test-pot_lifetime_binding_runtime and normal GREEN /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T023907Z-west-test-pot_lifetime_binding_runtime. Post-run ps/findmnt checks found no darlingserver/mldr/process or prefix mount leftovers.

title: Productize Darling prefix boot/test prerequisite repair
description: The guest-runtime-deploy canary was unblocked locally only after repairing prefix layout: create private/var/tmp and libexec/darling/private/var/tmp with mode 1777, restore canonical /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools -> versioned CLT symlink in both prefix root and base tree, and provide /Library/Developer/DarlingCLT/usr/bin/clang so xcrun/default guest compiler works. west test now preflights these requirements for guest-c-fixture and darling-doctor checks var/tmp, but this should be productized as a prefix repair/init command or setupPrefix fix so fresh/test prefixes are reproducible without manual mkdir/ln. Acceptance: fresh prefix can run 'darling shell echo', 'xcrun clang' compile+link smoke, and guest-c-fixture tests without manual prefix edits; command is documented and leaves no .init.pid/process/mount tails.
close_reason: fixed

description: Type: feature

title: Investigate broad bootchain deploy objc4 debug macro build failure
description: Broad bootchain deploy investigation. Repro: west darling-build --deploy --deploy-closure-names libsystem_kernel.dylib --deploy-darlingserver --deploy-bootchain --shutdown-before-deploy --force --skip-post-doctor. In the current build dir this broad target pulled libSystem/objc4 work and failed compiling src/external/objc4/runtime/objc-runtime.mm with mismatch in debug-ness macros. Narrow deploy with --deploy-mldr was enough for the actual stale mldr problem and succeeded, so this is not blocking psynch runtime proof. Need root-cause why broad bootchain deploy pulls objc4 and whether west should use narrower bootchain target groups or ensure consistent objc4 debug macro configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Repro command documented; root cause identified; west darling-build either avoids unrelated objc4 for bootchain refresh or documents/configures the required build mode; no stale processes, mounts, or partial deploy after failure.

title: PR: darlingserver — Allow balanced psynch cvwait sequences
description: is_seqhigher_eq -> is_seqhigher in duct-tape/pthread/kern_synch.c _psynch_cvwait (commit 249b15f). Keep minimal; include regression ruby_spawn_pipe_psynch. Decide whether to keep the __FAILEDUSERTEST2__ diagnostic or make it debug-only.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Scope note from dar-gwn.2 root-cause work: this branch's change only relaxes the cvwait sequence VALIDATION (is_seqhigher_eq->is_seqhigher) + better failuretest logging. It does NOT fix the residual brew-fetch hang. That hang is a LOST psynch condvar wakeup (waiter parked in ksyn waitq, never thread_unblock'd) — a signal/wait sequence/prepost accounting bug elsewhere in kern_synch.c, likely tied to ALLOW_INTERRUPTIONS cvwait returning via SIGCHLD interrupt. See dar-gwn.2 for the strace+[PH] evidence chain. Keep this branch as-is (correct, minimal); the real fix is a separate change.

title: PR: xnu — handle PRIO_DARWIN_THREAD/PROCESS in getpriority/setpriority
description: Landed in xnu WIP 21b110c (getpriority.c/setpriority.c). getpriority/setpriority now accept PRIO_DARWIN_THREAD and PRIO_DARWIN_PROCESS (who==0 -> 0/EINVAL; prio in {0,PRIO_DARWIN_BG,PRIO_DARWIN_NONUI}) instead of forwarding the Darwin-only 'which' value to the Linux syscall. Needed by Ruby/Homebrew which sets Darwin thread priority. Local branch fix/darwin-priority off xnu origin/main.

title: PR: xnu — stub SIOCGIFCONF (0xC00C6924) returning empty ifconf
description: Landed in xnu WIP 21b110c (bsd/helper/ioctl/socket.c). handle_socket now answers SIOCGIFCONF by zeroing ifc_len and returning 0 (EFAULT if arg NULL), instead of falling through unhandled. Stop-gap until container networking is implemented. Local branch fix/socket-siocgifconf off xnu origin/main.
notes: Patch-vs-root audit: pragmatic compatibility stub, not full SIOCGIFCONF/interface enumeration. It returns an empty list instead of failing; real networking parity still needs populated interface discovery.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-06-10 correction: the 2026-06-10 reproduction (dar-gwn.2) shows the brew hang is a server-side lost-RPC-reply with darlingserver IDLE, NOT a network/interface-enumeration stall (network API fetch succeeded; no live curl at hang). So SIOCGIFCONF is unrelated to the hang — keep this PR purely as an interface-enumeration stop-gap, not as a fix for dar-gwn.2.

title: PR: xnu — call __mldr_postfork_child() in fork child
description: Landed in xnu WIP 21b110c (fork.c + elfcalls_wrapper.c/.h). Adds __mldr_postfork_child() wrapper around elfcalls()->postfork_child() and invokes it at the top of the fork child path so mldr/elfcalls state is reset post-fork. Related to SwiftPM/Homebrew fork handling. Local branch fix/fork-postfork-child off xnu origin/main (debug-only enriched checkin printf in same file is dropped).

title: PR: darlingserver — bound fork-child checkin wait with timeout
description: Landed in darlingserver a8cfc49 (Bound fork child checkin wait) + e1764b3 (Log fork child checkin lifecycle). Adds dtape_semaphore_down_timeout + dtape_semaphore_wait_result_timed_out; Process::waitForChildAfterFork() now waits at most 30s and returns bool; ForkWaitForChild returns -ETIMEDOUT on timeout instead of blocking forever; fork checkin lifecycle logging. From earlier SwiftPM fork-hang work, predates the psynch investigation. Local branch fix/fork-checkin-bound off darlingserver origin/main.
notes: Patch-vs-root audit: robustness bound, not a full root-cause fix for child checkin failure. It prevents a permanent darlingserver wedge and returns -ETIMEDOUT so the broken fork-child lifecycle can be diagnosed separately.

title: PR draft: mldr — reset glibc loader locks in raw-fork child
description: Upstream the mldr glibc raw-fork child reset hardening committed locally as 5fb98a598 on fix/homebrew-psynch-ruby-hang. Darling uses raw __NR_fork, bypassing glibc's normal fork() child-side loader reset, so mldr now detects and resets the relevant glibc rtld locks in __mldr_postfork_child(): the existing stack-cache lock plus dl_load_lock and dl_load_tls_lock. The reset logic lives in src/startup/mldr/glibc_fork_reset.{c,h}, is wired into mldr.c/CMakeLists, and has a deterministic regression test under tests/regression/run-glibc-fork-lock-reset.sh. Validation: regression PASS; ninja mldr mldr32 PASS; ninja src/startup/mldr/install PASS; lz4 --build-from-source e2e smoke 3/3 ALL-PASS against rebuilt/installed mldr. Relates to dar-q95.12 (xnu side: call __mldr_postfork_child in fork child).
notes: Patch-vs-root audit: this is not a random lock workaround. It restores the child-side host-runtime cleanup skipped by Darling's raw __NR_fork path. The broader rule for future review is that any critical host libc/runtime child reset skipped by raw fork needs an explicit mldr equivalent.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
=== dar-gwn.5 HARDENING via TDD: reset ALL glibc loader locks in the raw-fork child (not just stack-cache) ===

CONTEXT / WHY
The shipped fix (4a0a80cf7) resets only GL(_dl_stack_cache_lock) in the fork child.
Reviewing against glibc 2.39 source shows that is an INCOMPLETE copy of what glibc's
own fork() does. glibc's __libc_fork child path (posix/fork.c:100-109) resets, UNCONDITIONALLY:
  __rtld_lock_initialize(GL(dl_load_lock));       // dynamic-loader lock (dlopen/dlclose)
  __rtld_lock_initialize(GL(dl_load_tls_lock));   // dynamic-TLS lock (TLS setup)
  reclaim_stacks();                               // resets _dl_stack_cache_lock + lists
By contrast _Fork() (sysdeps/nptl/_Fork.c) resets ONLY the robust-mutex list. Darling
forks with raw __NR_fork (sys_fork in .../bsd/impl/process/fork.c:27) = _Fork-level, so it
inherits ALL of these loader locks in whatever state the parent left them. The stack-cache
lock is just the one we happened to hit; _dl_load_lock (held during any dlopen) and
_dl_load_tls_lock (held during TLS setup / dlopen of TLS-bearing objects) are the SAME class
of latent deadlock. brew/ruby/perl dlopen at runtime, so these are reachable.

GLIBC FACTS (verified, glibc-2.39 source; cloned to /tmp/glibc-ref):
- struct rtld_global field order (sysdeps/generic/ldsodefs.h):
    _dl_load_lock (366), _dl_load_write_lock (370), _dl_load_tls_lock (377)  <- THREE consecutive
      __rtld_lock_recursive_t, NO fields between them (only comments).
    ... (large arch-dependent gap: _dl_ns[16], link_map, x86 cpu_features, TLS slotinfo) ...
    _dl_stack_used (474) / _dl_stack_user (477) / _dl_stack_cache (480) /
    _dl_stack_cache_actsize (483) / _dl_in_flight_stack (488) / _dl_stack_cache_lock (491).
- __rtld_lock_recursive_t = struct { pthread_mutex_t mutex; }  (sysdeps/nptl/libc-lockP.h:39)
- free/initialized value = _RTLD_LOCK_RECURSIVE_INITIALIZER = {PTHREAD_RECURSIVE_MUTEX_INITIALIZER_NP}
  => pthread_mutex_t with every field 0 EXCEPT __kind == PTHREAD_MUTEX_RECURSIVE_NP (==1).
- runtime takes them via __pthread_mutex_lock (recursive), NOT the rtld inline path
  (libc-lockP.h:130, the !IS_IN(rtld) branch).

DETECTION STRATEGY (robust, version-independent, self-validating)
The three loader locks are three back-to-back free recursive mutexes (40 bytes each on LP64).
Nothing else in rtld_global places three recursive mutexes at a 40-byte stride (the per-namespace
unique_sym_table.lock mutexes are separated by the whole link_namespaces struct), so the FIRST
such triple uniquely identifies _dl_load_lock / _dl_load_write_lock / _dl_load_tls_lock.
Anchor the scan to end at the (already reliably found) stack-cache region.
GROUND-TRUTH CONFIRMED: verify.c parks a thread inside a dlopen ELF constructor (holds the real
_dl_load_lock); the detected address then reads __lock=1 __owner=<holder tid> __count=1 -> the
detected pointer IS the real lock. (Also note: process exit() then deadlocks on that held lock,
which is itself a confirmation.)

A SUBTLE FACT THE TDD SURFACED (important, affects how the bug actually triggers):
A raw __NR_fork does NOT refresh glibc's cached THREAD_SELF->tid in the child. So in the child the
forking thread still believes its tid == the PARENT's tid. Consequence for RECURSIVE mutexes:
the deadlock only manifests when the held lock's __owner is a DIFFERENT thread than the forker
(the normal case: thread B in dlopen while thread A forks). If __owner happened to equal the
forker's tid, the recursive mutex would just re-enter. The stack-cache lock is a plain LLL int
(no owner check) so it always deadlocks regardless. The test simulates a foreign owner accordingly.

THE FIX (validated at unit level; not yet ported into the repo)
Extend the child reset to also reset the two recursive loader locks to
_RTLD_LOCK_RECURSIVE_INITIALIZER (memset 0, __kind=RECURSIVE, release __lock last):
    if (g_load_lock)     reset_recursive_mutex(g_load_lock);
    if (g_load_tls_lock) reset_recursive_mutex(g_load_tls_lock);
plus the existing stack-cache reset. Code: glibc_fork_reset.{c,h} in this dir.
NOTE ON SCOPE: I deliberately did NOT reimplement the full reclaim_stacks() list hygiene
(walking _dl_stack_used to recycle dead-thread stacks, __nptl_nthreads=1, fork-generation bump).
That part needs struct-pthread offsets (very version-fragile) and is NOT a deadlock source — it
is bookkeeping/optimization. Resetting the three LOCKS is the complete deadlock fix; the existing
"empty the stack cache list" keeps that subsystem self-consistent.

TDD RESULTS (host glibc 2.39, harness in this dir; build/run below)
RED  (reset disabled): all 3 cases deadlock (stack_cache/pthread, load_lock/dlopen, load_tls_lock/dlopen).
RED  (old reset = stack-cache only): stack_cache PASSES, load_lock + load_tls_lock STILL deadlock.
GREEN(new reset, +loader locks): all 3 PASS, and all 3 still deadlock when reset is disabled (test stays meaningful).

HARNESS (this directory)
- glibc_fork_reset.{c,h} : the module (detect + child reset). Drop-in for mldr (replaces the inline
  code in src/startup/mldr/mldr.c lines ~593-647; mldr's __mldr_postfork_child should call
  __mldr_glibc_fork_reset_child() then reset its own socket_bitmap.mutex; main() calls
  __mldr_glibc_fork_reset_detect() early, where it currently calls __mldr_detect_stack_cache_lock()).
- test.c       : deterministic RED/GREEN deadlock harness (3 cases x reset on/off). Exit 0 = all green.
- tls_mod.c    : a TLS-bearing .so so dlopen exercises _dl_load_tls_lock.
- park_mod.c   : ELF-constructor parks forever (holds real _dl_load_lock) for ground truth.
- verify.c     : confirms the detected load_lock address tracks the real lock.
Build & run:
    gcc -shared -fPIC -O0 -g tls_mod.c  -o tls_mod.so
    gcc -shared -fPIC -O0 -g park_mod.c -o park_mod.so
    gcc -O0 -g -DGLIBC_FORK_RESET_TEST_HOOKS test.c   glibc_fork_reset.c -o test   -ldl -lpthread
    gcc -O0 -g -DGLIBC_FORK_RESET_TEST_HOOKS verify.c glibc_fork_reset.c -o verify -ldl -lpthread
    ./test    # expect "RESULT: ALL GREEN", exit 0
    timeout 10 ./verify

REMAINING WORK (handoff)
1. Port glibc_fork_reset.{c,h} into the repo (src/startup/mldr/), wire CMakeLists (mldr already
   links -ldl), replace the inline detect/reset in mldr.c with calls. Keep async-signal-safety.
2. Build Darling, re-run the lz4 --build-from-source flaky validation (run-lz4-flaky2.sh; baseline
   ~17% hang). The shipped stack-cache-only fix already gave 17 pass / 0 hang across 19; this only
   ADDS coverage, so it must stay >= that. Watch specifically for any dlopen-time hangs disappearing.
3. Land the host-glibc TDD test under tests/ (deterministic regression; runs in seconds; satisfies
   the dar-q95.14 "optional regression" item with something far better than a flaky end-to-end).
4. Leave src/external/darlingserver and pr-drafts/ untouched (parallel dar-q95.1/.2 work).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: E2E follow-up on rebuilt mldr: scoped install initially exposed a real mldr32 link failure from the hardening module using a 64-bit __atomic_store_n on pthread_mutex_t q[0] (undefined __atomic_store_8 under -m32). Fixed locally by making recursive rtld-lock detection/reset use sizeof(pthread_mutex_t) + PTHREAD_RECURSIVE_MUTEX_INITIALIZER_NP byte image and only atomic-store the int __lock word last; this preserves LP64 behavior and builds mldr32. Validation after fix: tests/regression/run-glibc-fork-lock-reset.sh PASS; ninja mldr mldr32 PASS; ninja src/startup/mldr/install PASS and installed hashes match build outputs. E2E: first 10x API-path lz4 --build-from-source run got 2 successful source builds, then failed (not stalled) on iteration 3 with Ruby [BUG] pthread_cond_wait: ETIMEDOUT in download_queue.rb:184 plus existing semaphore_timedwait failed noise; bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260611T131904Z-lz4-glibc-fork-reset-e2e. NO_INSTALL_FROM_API retry was invalid as an e2e signal because Homebrew tried to git clone homebrew-core and stalled in clone/index-pack; bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260611T132202Z-lz4-glibc-fork-reset-e2e-noapi. Final clean API-path short e2e passed: 3/3 brew install --build-from-source lz4, ALL-PASS, exit 0; bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260611T132529Z-lz4-glibc-fork-reset-e2e-3x. No recurrence of the old futex_wait_queue mldr hang in successful e2e.

title: PR: darlingserver — cancel stale wait_timer in thread_unblock
description: Upstream darlingserver commit 32af4d56: thread_unblock should cancel an armed wait_timer when a timed wait is woken early, matching XNU and preventing stale timer expiry from injecting a spurious THREAD_TIMED_OUT into a later unrelated wait. Needs clean branch off darlingserver origin/main with [PH]/KWQDBG/CALLDBG diagnostics excluded.

title: PR: libpthread — decode negative psynch wait returns
description: Upstream libpthread commit 3de6464: Darling's emulated psynch waits can return BSD errors as negative return-register values rather than macOS-style -1 + errno. Decode negative returns in condvar/mutex/rwlock waits so EINTR/ETIMEDOUT/prepost recovery paths run. Needs clean branch off darling-libpthread upstream; consider whether normalizing libsystem_kernel syscall ABI is the deeper alternative.
notes: Patch-vs-root audit: this is the most suspicious layering item. The local libpthread decoder works as a compatibility backstop for negative BSD errno returns, but it may encode a Darling-specific kernel ABI convention into libpthread. Resolve dar-q95.23 before opening this PR; if libsystem_kernel can normalize to Darwin/macOS-style -1 + errno, reduce or drop this libpthread divergence.
close_reason: superseded by dar-q95.29.2 psynch syscall/cerror contract; old libpthread negative-return workaround removed from active homebrew patch profile

title: PR: xnu — return psynch wait errors as negative errno
description: Upstream xnu/libsystem_kernel commits f3c8d94/0a10e19: psynch wait wrappers return BSD wait errors as negative errno values in the return register for Darling's emulated path. Pair with libpthread negative-return decode or reconsider normalizing to macOS -1+errno ABI.
notes: Patch-vs-root audit: paired with dar-q95.16 and blocked on dar-q95.23. Decide whether negative BSD errno is the intended emulated psynch ABI, or whether this layer should normalize wait failures to macOS-style -1 + errno so libpthread stays closer to upstream.

title: Resolve psynch wait error ABI layering before upstreaming negative-return fixes
description:
Audit whether Darling should expose emulated psynch wait failures to libpthread as negative BSD errno return-register values, or normalize the libsystem_kernel ABI to macOS-style -1 plus errno before libpthread sees them.

Context from patch-vs-root review:
- dar-q95.16 currently teaches libpthread to decode negative psynch wait returns. This is the most suspicious layer because it bakes a Darling-specific kernel ABI shape into libpthread.
- dar-q95.19 currently documents and returns negative errno from the emulated psynch wait wrappers.
- A more root-correct fix may normalize the emulated libsystem_kernel/syscall boundary so libpthread remains closer to upstream Darwin behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Decide and document the intended ABI at the emulated libsystem_kernel/libpthread boundary for psynch waits.
- If macOS-style -1 + errno is feasible, update/split dar-q95.16 and dar-q95.19 drafts accordingly and reduce libpthread divergence.
- If negative errno is intentionally kept, document why it is required and keep libpthread decoding as an explicit compatibility backstop.
- Re-run the psynch/Homebrew regression path affected by condvar, mutex, and rwlock interrupted/timed waits.
close_reason: resolved by dar-q95.29.2 layering decision: libsystem_kernel owns syscall/cerror transport; libpthread owns decode and retry policy

Preserve and classify regression/repro assets. Audit objc4 OBJC_IS_DEBUG_BUILD, dyld _elfcalls, and zlib interior-pointer-free changes; archive or discard with an explicit disposition. Before cleaning the source checkout, preserve the uncommitted darlingserver KWQDBG/CALLDBG diff as a named diagnostic patch or backup commit. Acceptance: every useful change has a clean branch + Bead/PR draft + patch entry, every non-upstream change has an archived disposition, west patch verify/apply passes, and no unique uncommitted work remains.

title: Replace getattrlist/getattrlistbulk with one shared Darwin attribute encoder
description:
Build one shared, bounds-checked Darwin attribute encoder used by both getattrlist/fgetattrlist and getattrlistbulk. The current q95.17/q95.18 direction is still blocked because it has duplicated ad-hoc packers with divergent semantics, including caller-buffer overflow risk, local constant/layout copies, masked stat/readlink/lseek failures, wrong/full-width field handling, fake HFS/resource-fork claims, and incomplete ATTR_CMN_ERROR / ATTR_CMN_RETURNED_ATTRS behavior.

Architectural target: a table-driven encoder with canonical Darwin attribute definitions, a two-pass size/capability calculation, explicit unsupported-attribute policy, bounded copyout, shared alignment/order rules, correct variable-data references, and conformance tests for ordering, alignment, truncation, root/long names, symlinks, unsupported attrs, errors, and bulk resume cookies. Do not publish separate hand-fixed packers. Current branches stay blocked from publication until this shared subsystem exists.
close_reason: Shared bounded getattrlist/getattrlistbulk encoder implemented, tested with runtime RED/GREEN, and patch marked ready.

title: Replace private psynch negative-errno protocol with a Darwin syscall/cerror contract
description:
Remove q95.16/q95.19's Darling-private negative-error convention between libsystem_kernel and libpthread. The fix is not another errno mapping layer; it is one Darwin-compatible syscall/cerror contract at the syscall boundary, with augmented psynch status bits kept distinct from errno and with libpthread retry policy limited to truly interruptible cases.

Required behavior: preserve psynch status bits, distinguish EINTR/ETIMEDOUT/permanent errors, remove retry-all-negative loops, and validate condvar/mutex/rwlock interruption, timeout, and handoff with direct ABI and stress tests. The current pair remains local-integration only and blocked from publication until the boundary contract is made conformant.
close_reason: Darwin psynch syscall/cerror boundary and libpthread decode/retry contract implemented and RED/GREEN verified.

Removed local retry-all-negative loops; retry is now limited to EINTR, ETIMEDOUT/permanent errors are distinguished. Verified: ninja -C darling/build -j1 -k0 src/external/libpthread/src/all and git diff --check. comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Architecture update: solve this as a layer-boundary cleanup, not scattered ret<0 fixes. Target contract: darlingserver/kernel emulation returns kernel/psynch results; libsystem_kernel owns the Darwin syscall/cerror ABI boundary; libpthread owns pthread retry/policy decisions. Keep errno and psynch status bits separate. Introduce one shared psynch return decoder/adapter near the syscall wrappers and one pthread-level typed decision point for ok/interrupted/timed_out/permanent_error/status_update. Retry policy must live only in pthread code, never hidden in syscall wrappers. Canonicalize psynch masks/status constants in one source of truth. Tests should cover helper/decoder contracts plus real guest pthread behavior for interruption, timeout, handoff, mutex/cond/rwlock no-hang. Acceptance now includes proving RED/GREEN for the affected patch metadata, preserving status bits, removing retry-all-negative behavior, and keeping west patch check/export/red-audit green.

title: Centralize bounded Darwin fd_set adaptation for select/pselect
description:
Keep the q95.3 insight: Darwin fd_set layout conversion belongs at the syscall mediation boundary. Replace the unsafe implementation with a shared darwin_fdset adapter used by select and pselect: validate nfds before any access/allocation, cap against the Darwin fd_set ABI and supported host limits, avoid attacker-controlled stack allocation, preserve null-set behavior, and verify pselect timeout/sigmask conversion.

Acceptance requires boundary tests for negative nfds, zero nfds, FD_SETSIZE, oversized nfds, null read/write/except sets, timeout mutation semantics, signal masks, and 32/64-bit builds. Current branch direction is correct but publication remains blocked until these invariants are enforced centrally.
close_reason: Central bounded fd_set adapter and pselect ABI/sigmask correctness are implemented and covered by host + guest-runtime red/green tests.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented in xnu/select-pselect-fdset.patch from source commit 014ae062d051cfdd48a0095f3cc05b19900a20ca. The patch now uses a shared bounded darwin_fdset adapter for select/pselect, rejects nfds < 0 and nfds > DARWIN_FD_SETSIZE before conversion/syscall, removes attacker-controlled alloca, preserves NULL fd_set behavior, and fixes pselect6 sigmask passing plus the Darling pselect timeout ABI to use struct timespec explicitly. Verification: ninja -C /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-build system_kernel; host select_fdset_contract with -Wall -Wextra -Werror; west patch check/export --check/verify --profile homebrew; west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/select-pselect-fdset.patch --prefix-profile homebrew --prove-red; west test --profile homebrew --red-audit.

title: Replace success-returning compatibility stubs with explicit Darwin semantics
description:
Umbrella for replacing success-returning compatibility stubs with explicit Darwin-compatible behavior or explicit unsupported/insecure modes. This is intentionally split by API surface; do not publish a patch that silently returns success while providing no semantics.

Children own the concrete work: _NSGetExecutablePath, SIOCGIFCONF/interface enumeration, Darwin priority classes, and sandbox-exec. The parent closes only when each surface has either real semantics, a truthful failure mode, or an explicitly opt-in local compatibility mode with tests.

title: Implement _NSGetExecutablePath instead of patching packages around it
description: Implement _NSGetExecutablePath as a real platform API instead of reconfiguring Perl around its absence. The result should report the current executable path using a documented Darling source of truth, handle buffer sizing like Darwin, and include direct tests. This unblocks retiring the q95.5 Perl workaround.

title: Implement truthful SIOCGIFCONF interface enumeration or explicit unsupported failure
description: Replace the empty-success SIOCGIFCONF stub with a truthful interface enumeration/translation layer. Either return Darwin-layout interface records derived from host/container interfaces, or fail with a documented unsupported errno when no truthful data can be provided. Do not report an empty successful list as a root fix. This unblocks q95.11.

title: Model or truthfully reject Darwin priority classes
description: Replace Darwin priority no-ops with explicit semantics. Model supported Darwin thread/process priority classes where possible, reflect state through getpriority where applicable, and return truthful unsupported errors for classes Darling cannot implement. Do not publish a setpriority-success/no-effect patch as a foundational fix. This unblocks q95.10.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08 runtime audit finding from darwin-priority guest gate: the previous local patch returned positive EINVAL from sys_getpriority/sys_setpriority invalid Darwin-class cases, which guest-visible getpriority surfaced as success-like return 22. Fixed the patch to return raw -EINVAL and added a deployed guest gate for valid PRIO_DARWIN_THREAD/PROCESS no-op success plus invalid raw -EINVAL cases. This is still not a full Darwin priority model; getpriority/setpriority remain compatibility stubs and q95.29.4.3 stays open for real semantics/truthful unsupported behavior.

title: Make sandbox-exec either real isolation or explicit insecure opt-in
description: Replace sandbox-exec pass-through with either real isolation semantics or an explicitly opt-in insecure compatibility mode. The default must not silently discard a security policy and return success. Include tests proving the chosen mode is visible to callers and cannot be mistaken for real sandbox enforcement. This unblocks q95.21.

title: Specify and test sigexc restart semantics as a signal-state machine
description: Harden q95.20 by centralizing host sigaction installation, preserving all required handler flags/masks, proving SIG_IGN/default/SA_RESTART behavior, and testing blocking syscalls with restart enabled/disabled plus concurrent sigaction changes. The current change is in the correct layer but remains provisional without the state-machine contract and tests.

title: Build focused invariant suites for psynch and microthread wake fixes
description: Umbrella for direct invariant tests around the provisional psynch and microthread fixes. The old single task was too broad; children now own independently executable suites. The parent closes when psynch generation/width behavior, wait-timer cancellation, and microthread wake epoch/duplicate scheduling invariants are all covered by focused RED/GREEN tests.

title: Test psynch generation, width, and handoff invariants directly
description: Add direct tests for psynch generation and argument invariants: balanced cvwait sequences, full-width cvsignal/cvbroad generation words, correct mutex pointer argument placement, and condvar/mutex/rwlock timeout/interruption/handoff matrices. This covers q95.1 and q95.2 publication blockers.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Added source-level RED->GREEN contracts for q95.1 and q95.2. q95.1 now has tests/psynch_cvwait_sequence_contract.sh in fix/psynch-cvwait-balanced, proving balanced equal cvwait sequences use is_seqhigher (not is_seqhigher_eq) and keep diagnostic context. q95.2 now has darling/src/libsystem_kernel/tests/psynch_cvsignal_args_contract.sh in fix/psynch-cvsignal-args, proving cvsignal uses full-width generation args and cvbroad passes mutex, not mugen, as the RPC mutex argument. Verified GREEN and --prove-red through west test --profile homebrew --patch ... --materialize-profile. Homebrew metadata moved from 15 covered / 57 missing to 17 covered / 55 missing.

title: Test wait-timer cancellation and stale-timeout invariants
description: Add direct tests for stale wait-timer cancellation parity with XNU: a timeout from a prior wait must not fire into a later untimed wait, interrupt/cancel paths must disarm the correct timer, and timeout accounting must distinguish real timeout from stale delivery. This covers q95.15 publication blockers.
close_reason: fixed: thread_unblock stale timeout and assert_wait prepare stale-timer invariants now have guest/runtime RED/GREEN proofs

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Needed for dar-r7fc.2.3 closure: the old deterministic repro referenced in metadata (dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/sleep_stress2.rb) is not present in this workspace. A correct suite should either commit a reproducible guest workload that creates timed wait -> wake/re-enter untimed wait -> stale timer pressure and observes DSERVER_TEST_TRACE_FILE wait_timer events, or add an opt-in scheduler/timer diagnostic hook that deterministically creates the stale timer condition. Do not replace this with generic condvar stress unless it can prove stale timer cancellation and fail RED on a bad runtime.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Partial coverage landed locally for stale wait-timer invariants: wait_timer_stale_guest is a guest behavioral repro for timed wait -> early wake via thread_unblock -> immediate untimed wait -> old deadline passes. RED bad runtime reports 'untimed wait observed stale timeout/error: 22'; GREEN current reports WAIT_TIMER_STALE_GUEST_OK. This closes the direct thread_unblock cancellation proof for cancel-stale-wait-timer.patch. Keep this task open for the harder assert-wait-cancel-stale-timer invariant: a path that completes/re-enters without thread_unblock still needs a deterministic guest repro or an opt-in guarded scheduler/timer diagnostic hook.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-09: completed the remaining assert_wait stale-timer invariant with a real guest/runtime RED/GREEN proof. Added guarded wait_prepare fault diagnostics to darlingserver/test-diagnostics-trace.patch: the test writes waitq.assert_wait_stale_timer, a real guest pthread_cond_wait enters waitq_assert_wait64_locked, and the server traces a correlated outcome. Current fixed runtime traces wait_prepare_fault_cleared with had_timer=0 active=0; current-minus bad runtime uses the RED-only diagnostic source patch without dtape_thread_prepare_for_wait and traces wait_prepare_fault_uncleared with had_timer=1 active=0, so the host trace oracle fails. Evidence: GREEN bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260709T004616Z-west-test-wait_timer_assert_prepare_guest; RED bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260709T004613Z-west-test-wait_timer_assert_prepare_guest. Full focused patch test passed for compile+guest; export-check, metadata contract, and homebrew patch check passed. Together with wait_timer_stale_guest for thread_unblock stale timeout delivery, this covers the wait-timer cancellation/stale-timeout invariants requested here.

title: Test microthread wake-epoch and duplicate-scheduling invariants
description: Add a modeled microthread suspend/resume wake-epoch test suite. It must detect stale resume permits, duplicate scheduling, resume-before-suspend, resume-during-suspend, late wakeups, and physical suspension races. This covers q95.8 publication blockers.

title: psynch: unify XNU negative errno and libpthread cerror contract
description: Architectural follow-up from dar-rpc-correctness-z27x.9. The psynch negative-return/errno behavior is split across xnu/libsystem_kernel and libpthread. Target shape: one documented syscall/cerror contract helper and matching tests on both sides. Acceptance: document the internal negative BSD errno ABI; centralize/de-duplicate helper behavior where practical; keep xnu psynch_return_contract and libpthread psynch decode tests green; add a focused cross-side regression if a mismatch can still compile/pass separately.

title: Replace fork child checkin timeout with an explicit lifecycle protocol
description: q95.13 adds a hardcoded 30-second damage bound but does not fix why a child may never check in. Define explicit parent/child fork transaction states, cancellation and child-death detection, propagate interrupted/timed-out/internal outcomes distinctly, make policy configurable if a timeout remains, and test crash-before-checkin plus delayed/normal checkin. Keep the current timeout only as a local defensive guard until the protocol exists.

title: Derive shipped SDK identity from authoritative metadata
description: Validate q95.22 against the actual bundled SDK provenance. Generate or verify SDKSettings plist/json and versioned symlink from one authoritative version source instead of hardcoding 11.3 solely to satisfy Homebrew. Add an install-tree consistency test covering CanonicalName, Version, deployment targets, and symlink names.

title: Replace raw-fork glibc private-memory surgery with a supported host-runtime boundary
description:
Redesign q95.12/q95.14 so fork-child host runtime recovery does not depend on scanning or mutating private glibc _rtld_global memory, pthread_mutex_t byte signatures, or guessed list layouts. The goal is not to make the scanner cleverer; the goal is to remove that unsupported boundary.

Evaluate a controlled fork trampoline/helper, a supported glibc-facing primitive, or explicit versioned adapters with detected ABI and fail-closed diagnostics. Keep the generic elfcalls postfork hook as the extension point, but treat the current _rtld_global reset as a version-sensitive local mechanism only. Test across supported glibc versions and 32/64-bit builds before any publication.

title: PR: xnu — select/pselect BSD<->Linux fdset conversion
description: BSD 32-bit fdset words vs Linux 64-bit unsigned long; add fdset.h conversion in select.c/pselect.c. Separate PR from psynch. Note as current-boundary fix; long-term move with Linux syscall mediation into mldr. Include regression ruby_thread_raise_select.
close_reason: Ready patch xnu/select-pselect-fdset.patch is exported, verified, and red/green covered.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Patch xnu/select-pselect-fdset.patch is now publication-status: ready. It includes the fd_set conversion, bounded nfds validation, no dynamic stack allocation, pselect sigmask fix, explicit timespec pselect ABI, and verified red/green runtime coverage.

title: Gate q95 upstream PR work on explicit approval and PR-list reconciliation
description: The q95 epic explicitly says DO NOT START YET and requires reconciling the intended upstream PR list before starting. This blocker keeps technically-ready q95 PR tasks out of ready work until the user explicitly approves q95 upstream PR work and the intended PR split/list is reconciled against current q95.29 publication blockers. Do not satisfy this by running PR-mutating west pr commands; upstream/fork PR mutations still require separate explicit approval per workspace policy.

title: PR: darling-libplatform — preserve bzero destination ptr in x86_64 return reg
description: Root fix for CLT Perl: _platform_bzero left %rax = result of _platform_memset_pattern4 helper; preserve dest ptr. Add darling_bzero_return_regress. Draft PR exists: github.com/darlinghq/darling-libplatform/pull/5.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Hardened the bzero fix after foundational audit. Replaced the empty inline asm return-register hint with an x86_64 assembly wrapper ending in popq %rax; retq, retained the generic C path on other architectures, extended the regression to verify both the returned pointer and zeroed bytes, and conditionally registered it with CTest when an emulator is configured. Added permanent source links for Apple libplatform, Perl 5.28 perl_alloc(), and LLVM Darwin bzero lowering tests. Clean source branch and both draft PRs now point to 36eb5d508032ef1c614d41e166bbca3064b6620c; homebrew patch profile re-exported and verified.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08: added a real deployed guest/runtime ABI gate for bzero return-register behavior. New workspace fixture tests/bzero_return_register_guest.c compiles inside Darling via runner: guest-c-fixture and calls ___bzero with inline asm-visible return value, verifying returned pointer == original destination and the buffer is zeroed. Focused west test GREEN passed against /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix-homebrew-test; existing source-base compile RED proof still passes. This complements the host compile contract with actual guest-visible libplatform behavior.

title: PR: darling-perl — config-only disable _NSGetExecutablePath (5.18/5.28)
description: Disable unavailable _NSGetExecutablePath in Perl 5.18/5.28 + DSTROOT configs. No newCONSTSUB guard, no trace logging (not needed after bzero fix).

title: PR: crypto44/libressl — build with -fno-strict-aliasing (NIST ECC)
description: LibreSSL 2.8.3 BN_nist_mod_256/384 mis-reduce under strict aliasing (BN_ULONG via 32-bit words) -> 'point is not on curve' for valid P-256/P-384. Build crypto44 with -fno-strict-aliasing; add P-256/P-384 SPKI decode regression.

title: PR: top-level darling — bump submodule pointers after sub-PRs land
description: Update submodule pointers in top-level darling only after the relevant submodule PRs are settled.

title: PR: darlingserver — fix microthread resume-before-suspend lost wakeup (coalesced resume permit)
description: SUPERSEDES the original reply-deferral framing (that diagnosis was ruled out, see dar-gwn.1). The real darlingserver fix is the generic suspend/resume lost-wakeup race (dar-gwn.1.4): a wake arriving before/during physical suspension was dropped. Fix = coalesced _resumePermit covering early/in-progress/late wakeups (Thread::resume/suspend/doWork). Commits b6a4ce3 (Fix microthread resume-before-suspend race) + 26f50b5 (Document resume permit protocol). The dedicated per-timer-expiry microthread workaround (dar-gwn.1.3, commit 8ff03f0) was REMOVED/net-reverted because timer delivery on shared kernelAsync passes once this race is fixed. Validated: deterministic 20ms amplified RED 3/3 timeout -> GREEN 10/10; ordinary 50/50; heavy 80-thread/40k-wait stable RSS ~27 MiB. Black-box + white-box reproducers under tools/repro-darlingserver-resume-race/. Local branch fix/microthread-resume-race off darlingserver origin/main (excludes [PH] debug tracing in duct-tape/src/thread.c).
notes: Original blocker on dar-gwn.1.3 (timer fix) is obsolete: timer workaround removed by this fix.

title: test audit: promote runtime-sensitive patch tests to guest gates
description: Audit 2026-07-08: homebrew profile has 72 covered patches but only 2 runtime tests (compile 32, host 38). Many host/compile tests are useful root-cause contracts, but they should not be the only proof for fixes whose observable contract is guest/runtime behavior. This epic tracks adding guest/runtime gates where needed while keeping fast host/compile contracts as lower-level coverage. Acceptance: every patch in child tasks either gains a guest/runtime test, is explicitly documented as host/compile-only with rationale, or is blocked on a named west test infrastructure gap.
close_reason: Fixed: all dar-r7fc guest/runtime migration child tasks are closed; current homebrew/perf/arch red-audits are clean, and remaining infra limitations are tracked as separate non-child blockers.

description: Add guest/runtime tests for user-visible XNU/libsystem semantics that are currently covered only by host/compile contracts. Current tests are useful implementation contracts but do not prove the behavior through libsystem_kernel inside Darling. Patch entries to audit/promote: xnu/select-pselect-fdset, xnu/darwin-priority, xnu/socket-siocgifconf, xnu/fork-postfork-child, xnu/psynch-cvsignal-args, xnu/psynch-negative-errno, xnu/sigexc-sa-restart, xnu/sigexc-default-resend-self, xnu/ulock-wait-eintr-retry, xnu/vchroot-pathnull-guard, xnu/fd-guard-ebadf, xnu/abort-with-payload-no-group-broadcast, xnu/cache-task-self-trap, xnu/generalize-recv-spin-guest. For each: keep the fast contract if useful, add a guest executable/shell test run via west test env=darling, and make RED/GREEN proof real rather than source text matching. Blockers may include dar-facb and dar-test-infra-sp5.11.4.
close_reason: Parent audit scope exhausted; remaining runtime psynch argument probe stays open as child blocker dar-r7fc.1.1

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08: re-audited xnu/psynch-cvsignal-args under the no-source-matching rule. The existing psynch_cvsignal_args_contract parses signatures/RPC argument text, so it is now classified as kind: source-contract / coverage-tier: source and must not count as behavioral HOST coverage. I am not adding a weak guest smoke: current public pthread_cond_signal/broadcast paths pass mutex=NULL/mugen=0 and a tiny guest test cannot reliably force 64-bit generation overflow or observe the server's received mutex argument. A real runtime gate needs either a deterministic psynch stress/oracle that fails on the old truncation/wrong-mutex behavior, or first-class server-side trace/test-hook support to assert the received cvsignal/cvbroad arguments from a guest-triggered call. Until then this remains an honest missing behavioral gate, not fake coverage.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08: audited xnu/psynch-negative-errno against the existing libpthread/psynch-kernel-return-helper guest runtime gate. That guest test is valuable end-to-end psynch stress coverage, but it is not a RED proof for this xnu patch: the libpthread helper intentionally accepts both the old Darling-local negative return ABI and the new Darwin-style cerror/errno ABI, so the same guest test can pass with the old xnu side. Keep psynch_return_contract as the xnu root-cause RED proof. A true guest gate for xnu/psynch-negative-errno needs isolated bad/fixed libsystem_kernel build/deploy, or a guest-visible oracle that distinguishes return-register negative errno from -1+errno at the syscall boundary without being masked by libpthread compatibility.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08: audited xnu/fork-postfork-child for guest migration. Existing fork_postfork_child_contract is a real compile/root-cause contract: it compiles production fork.c with stubs and checks child-path ordering, including __mldr_postfork_child before guard/socket refresh/checkin and parent/error paths not running child hooks. A normal guest fork smoke would be weak because the bug is inherited native loader/elfcalls state; without a deterministic held-loader-lock setup, fork+exit/exec can pass both before and after. Runtime migration should wait for a guest repro that deliberately creates inherited mldr/glibc loader-lock state or exposes postfork_child execution via a supported diagnostic hook.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08: audited perf-class entries listed here: xnu/cache-task-self-trap and xnu/generalize-recv-spin-guest. They are not ordinary syscall semantics gates. cache-task-self-trap needs a runtime counter/probe proving repeated mach_task_self calls collapse to one task_self_trap per reset/fork epoch without breaking fork reset; generalize-recv-spin-guest needs runtime/perf/no-hang probes around reply wait behavior and bounded fallback. Simple guest smoke would not prove the changed contract. These belong under dar-r7fc.3 perf/RPC transport probes; keep their compile/root-cause contracts here and do not pretend guest smoke covers them.

title: psynch cvsignal guest gate needs runtime RPC argument probe

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Progress: added guest runtime GREEN gate for darlingserver/pthread-canceled-xnu-semantics.patch. New fixture tests/pthread_cancel_read_guest.c blocks a guest thread in read(2), calls pthread_cancel, interrupts it with SIGUSR1 to force the cancelable-syscall EINTR path, and requires pthread_join to return PTHREAD_CANCELED. This exercises the __pthread_markcancel + __pthread_canceled(0) server handshake; a plain pthread_testcancel loop would be too weak. Verified with west test --profile homebrew --patch darlingserver/pthread-canceled-xnu-semantics.patch --env darling --prefix existing:/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs, plus existing source-base compile RED/GREEN. Remaining cancel-stale-wait-timer/assert-wait-cancel-stale-timer runtime proof is not closed: the referenced dar-gwn18-etimedout-repro/sleep_stress2.rb is not present in the workspace, and a generic condvar stress would not prove stale wait_timer delivery. Linked dar-q95.29.6.2 as the explicit blocker for a real wait-timer invariant/repro suite.

title: guest runtime checkin retask interleaving hooks
description: Need deterministic runtime tooling before checkin-rwlock-across-suspend.patch and checkin-retask-current-thread-uaf.patch can get honest guest/runtime RED/GREEN gates. Existing host self tests model the lock-held-across-suspend deadlock and ASan current_thread UAF order, but a guest gate must force or observe the real Process::notifyCheckin exec replacement interleavings under darlingserver: (1) _rwlock must not be held across suspending dtape task/thread/semaphore creation while another worker reads the same Process; (2) new dtape thread/task must be published before old task/thread release can read current_thread during IPC teardown. Implement opt-in diagnostics/fault hooks or a structured west runtime runner that can trigger exec/fork storms with trace evidence and bounded cleanup. Generic fork/exec smoke is not enough unless it deterministically proves the bad interleaving and fails RED on bad runtime.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic guest/runtime checkin-retask hooks and RED/GREEN gates for rwlock and retask patches; force-close because the task has a reciprocal Beads dependency with its parent dar-r7fc.2.4.

comments.text: Current verified blocker is runtime provisioning, not just build flags. The installed libsystem_kernel has E-UNION markers, but Darling server startup with DARLING_NOOVERLAYFS=1 still copy-syncs libexec into the prefix. Verified with west's active upper-over-template probe: lower/template is visible in guest, but prefix/private upper-only files are not, and shadow reads lower instead of upper. The next correct fix is to make the server/prefix path start an actual active E-UNION root (prefix as upper, libexec template as lower, bounded cleanup), then re-enable the guest metadata.

With DARLING_EUNION=1 and no-overlayfs, darlingserver now creates/validates prefix/.union-work and does not recursive-copy libexec into the prefix, allowing prefix-as-upper over libexec lower. Built darlingserver, deployed locally to /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix/bin/darlingserver, and verified: west test --profile homebrew --patch darlingserver/eunion-runtime-prefix.patch --env darling --prefix existing:/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs => WEST_EUNION_PREFIX_SMOKE_OK.

title: west test: move ad-hoc build/archive/guest-run script mechanics into first-class runners
description: Audit found test scripts carrying framework responsibilities. Example: tests/run-libressl-nist-regress.sh archives a source tree, configures/builds it, links a test, manages temp dirs/logs, and acts as a source-base proof. That should be a west test runner capability, not bespoke per-test logic. Needed runners: (1) build-source-tree runner with source-root env, clean temp worktree/archive, configure/build commands, logs/artifacts, timeout/cleanup; (2) build-and-run guest executable runner that compiles/installs/runs a C test inside a Darling prefix; (3) isolated Darling CMake target runner so patch-local targets do not pull unrelated broad build graph failures. Fold/coordinate with dar-mk8i, dar-0x7w, dar-facb, and dar-test-infra-sp5.11.4. Acceptance: LibreSSL and at least one XNU syscall guest test use framework runners with minimal test-specific shell.
close_reason: First-class west test runners are in place: source-build, guest-c, c-fixture/object-symbol, source-script, cmake-configure, and isolated Darling CMake target; LibreSSL and XNU syscall guest gates use them.

title: LibreSSL NIST patch needs true CMake-target RED proof
description: While migrating libressl/libressl-283-nist-strict-aliasing.patch from tests/run-libressl-nist-regress.sh to a structured source-build-fixture runner, west test --prove-red exposed that the previous RED proof was false: the autotools build command passed -fno-strict-aliasing through CFLAGS, so the source-base/bad tree passed too. The actual patch changes Darling's CMake target crypto44 via target_compile_options(crypto44 PRIVATE -fno-strict-aliasing) and adds darling_ec_tls_regress. Needed: implement or use a Darling CMake target/source-build runner that can build the current regression fixture against the bad and fixed CMake target configuration, proving bad fails and fixed passes without source matching or compile-flag injection. Until then the LibreSSL test may remain a GREEN host behavior gate only and must not be marked red:true.
close_reason: Implemented true isolated Darling CMake-target RED/GREEN proof for LibreSSL.

Verified focused GREEN, --prove-red, full LibreSSL patch entry, metadata contract, patch check/export-check, prefix cleanup, and no temporary worktree/process leaks.

title: test hook: record psynch cvsignal/cvbroad RPC arguments from guest
description: Add first-class test instrumentation to observe psynch_cvsignal and psynch_cvbroad RPC arguments produced by a guest-triggered call, without relying on source text. Needed for xnu/psynch-cvsignal-args.patch: pthread smoke paths do not expose full-width cvlsgen/cvugen or cvbroad mutex placement. Acceptable designs: a guarded darlingserver trace/test-hook with structured output in the debug bundle, or a deterministic psynch oracle that fails on the old argument truncation/wrong mutex behavior. Must be usable by west test with bounded timeout/cleanup and, together with dar-facb, support real RED/GREEN proof against bad/fixed runtime artifacts.

title: test hooks: guarded darlingserver lifecycle/reply/wait diagnostics
description: Add first-class guarded darlingserver diagnostics/failure hooks needed for deterministic guest runtime repros of lifecycle, reply, wait/cancel, and process lifetime bugs. Required capabilities: observe/force checkin wait transitions and lost wakeups; expose exception/reply failure state without killing the server; observe stale wait_timer cancellation/re-entry; report process lifetime/retask/orphan cleanup state in debug bundles. Hooks must be opt-in test-only or guarded by explicit env/diag mode, integrated with west test/darling-debug-runner, bounded by timeout, and leave no prefix/server processes. This blocks replacing broad darlingserver compile/host contracts with honest guest RED/GREEN gates.
close_reason: guarded runtime diagnostics trace hook implemented and covered by west runtime test

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented guarded darlingserver runtime diagnostics as patches/homebrew/darlingserver/test-diagnostics-trace.patch from source commit 027ae40c1d54c40e78cb3b88ffa87efb13134fef. The hook is opt-in via DSERVER_TEST_TRACE_FILE and records process checkin/exec/fork lifecycle, reply/error/push_reply states, duct-tape wait_timer prepare/unblock state, and fatal exception reply-wait mode. Added west script-runner host-trace-files support so the diagnostics patch has a real runtime metadata gate instead of a test-exception: west test --profile homebrew --patch darlingserver/test-diagnostics-trace.patch --env darling --prefix existing:$HOME/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs passed and verified process.checkin, process.pending_replacement, wait_prepare, and thread_unblock trace content. Build evidence: west darling-build --targets src/external/darlingserver/darlingserver --force passed; deploy smoke also produced trace with had_timer=1 wakeups.
