title: darlingserver RPC correctness: retire the reply/transport dirt classes (post A0-ARCH audit)
description:
PROBLEM: A read-only 4-agent audit of the code we wrote around the guest<->server RPC path (dispatch, reply delivery, perf#18 ring/duplex transport, duct-tape XNU bridge) after A0-ARCH stage 3 landed (2026-07-04) surfaced ~20 dangerous-dirt findings. Two hand-verified. They are NOT random bugs: most are symptoms of THREE architectural root causes, each fixable by a correct refactor that retires a whole class rather than patching points. A0-ARCH stage-3 territory itself was re-audited and found SOUND (no double-free/UAF on the interrupt path, TLS migration correct, fix-3 timer sound) -- the dirt is in OLDER duplex/S2C/ring scaffolding and reachable duct-tape stubs.

THREE ARCHITECTURAL CLASSES (each an epic child, most-leverage first):
 1. NO CORRELATION ID in the RPC reply header ({number,code} only) -> guest matches reply-to-request purely by arrival order on the per-thread UDS socket; server is the sole guarantor of exactly-one-correct-in-order reply, and every drop/dup/late/mis-order is unrecoverable guest-side. Fix = add a serial/correlation id to dserver_rpc_replyhdr_t + guest-side matching (the post-stage-2 UDS correlation-ID backlog). The ring path is already seq'd and immune. Retires: the reply drop/leak/mis-order class + helps the -111 abort + the sendMany/errno-surfacing meds.
 2. TRANSPORT TIMEOUT RE-RUNS A COMMITTED SIDE-EFFECT: the duplex/ring fallback decision is made on a two-valued signal (nonzero) that conflates 'server declined PRE-mutation (safe to re-run)' with 'timeout AFTER the server may have executed (must NOT re-run)'. Fix = make 'committed -> never retry' a PROTOCOL invariant of the duplex/ring lane (three-valued: declined / completed / published-then-unknown), not a per-op afterthought. vm_deallocate already does this; port_deallocate + the simple-ring gr_*_trap paths do not.
 3. NO UNIFIED DEADLINE SCHEDULER in the event loop: timeouts/deadline-wakeups are spread across several unsynchronized mechanisms (timerfd, _duplexUpcallDeadlineNs, the 5s duplex metronome, the 1Hz XNU long-term scan) with no single authority for 'when to wake next'. Fix = one timeout-heap the main loop uses to arm timerfd/epoll-timeout. Retires the duplex-deadline-never-fires leak (#123), the flood 1Hz-starvation hang (#118), and the fix-3 class by construction.

PLUS a mechanical (non-architectural) dimension: FAIL-CLOSED policy -- reachable duct-tape stubs that abort()/panic() on unknown input instead of returning an error (whole-server crash). Tracked as a child but it is discipline, not refactor.

ACCEPTANCE: the three refactor children landed; the reply-drop/dup/mis-order, timeout-double-execute, and deadline-leak classes are closed by construction (not point-patched); fail-closed sweep done. Reference: session ae391f52 audit artifact + the per-finding children. Neighbors: dar-1il.3.x (duplex ring lane), dar-gwn.1.7 (interrupt_enter reply-deferral -> -111), dar-syscall-inline-fastpath-6ud. Constraint: nothing pushed without explicit approval; local commits only.

title: RPC UDS protocol v2: correlation id plus exactly-once reply accounting
description:
Architectural class 1. UDS replies are still matched mostly by call number and arrival order; the ring path already has sequence ids, but UDS lacks an equivalent request/reply identity and exactly-once accounting. A stale, duplicated, late, dropped, or misordered reply is therefore unrecoverable guest-side.

Fix as RPC protocol v2, not as point patches: add a monotonic per-thread request serial/correlation id to the generated UDS request/reply ABI, make the guest accept only the matching reply, reject/drop stale replies safely, and make the server account that every accepted request resolves exactly once (reply, domain error, or explicit connection death). Coordinate generator changes, ABI compatibility/fallback, and tests for delayed/stale/misordered replies. The existing drop/leak sites become defense-in-depth after this lands.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented first narrow ABI-compatible slice as arch patch darlingserver/rpc-uds-packed-correlation.patch (source fix/rpc-uds-packed-correlation @ 03fbc43). Design correction: initial uint64_t header-field approach was rejected after runtime smoke because it broke old boot-chain/dyld senders (undersized checkin/ring_attach). Final implementation keeps wire struct sizes unchanged and packs a 14-bit serial tag into dserver_callnum_t with base/serial helpers. Generated clients send packed callnum, replies echo packed number, receive validation checks base-callnum plus serial, and server dispatch masks base callnum so old untagged clients remain compatible. Verified: rpc_serial_contract_test PASS; darlingserver+libsystem_kernel build PASS; focused deploy with --deploy-darlingserver --deploy-mldr --shutdown-before-deploy PASS; doctor --no-baseline-file with extra prefix PASS; DPREFIX homebrew-test shell echo PASS; run-duplex-real-selftest.sh green PASS; west patch verify arch PASS; strict arch/perf/homebrew statuses all 0 missing/0 no-file. Leaving OPEN: broader exactly-once reply/connection-death accounting and stale/delayed reply fuzz tests remain.

title: Make committed-or-unknown ring/duplex operations never retry on UDS
description:
Architectural class 2. Ring/duplex fallback must distinguish three states: declined before mutation (safe to run on UDS), completed with a result, and published/committed but reply outcome unknown. The last state must never transparently retry a non-idempotent operation on UDS, because the server may already have performed the side effect.

Implement this as a lane invariant, not per-op folklore: all simple-ring and duplex wait helpers return a typed result, all migrated non-idempotent operations obey committed -> never retry, and tests cover timeout/spin-budget exhaustion for allocate/reply-port/deallocate-like paths. This aligns with the three-lane IPC model: wrong lane is not a bad op; committed side effects cannot be replayed through another lane.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented current z27x.2 pass and anchored it in arch patchset. Source commits: xnu fix/ring-committed-unknown-guest @ 8d746cb73e874b057a2db9f453bbd0812b5e46e3, darlingserver fix/ring-committed-unknown-gate @ ea28bd095742be30f96b3d73510321f7c8124a51. Patch artifacts: patches/arch/xnu/ring-committed-unknown-guest.patch (sha256 4eaad65fe29eb98866e14eac81160c35a574dad70ba654311598e24a41af2e9d) and patches/arch/darlingserver/ring-committed-unknown-gate.patch (sha256 64ea94c9b9e5a3e6d3c7ef191343a8254a1c6299efa3b0e1f7feb79ff7a301ee). Contract change: simple-ring and duplex waits now return typed completed vs committed-unknown; helper negative return remains only pre-publish or explicit server pre-mutation DECLINE. Post-publish timeout/bad-reply returns KERN_FAILURE without UDS retry, replacing the old retry/fabricated-success folklore. Gates: libsystem_kernel.dylib focused build OK; run-ring-shm-validate.sh OK including new committed-unknown static gate; run-duplex-real-selftest.sh green PASS with ring_duplex_s2c=2 reject=0 and unrelated client progress; west darling-doctor --no-baseline-file --extra-prefix homebrew-test OK; west patch verify/status arch/perf/homebrew OK (0 missing, 0 no-file). Remaining blocker: ring/duplex transport is still experimental/flag-gated and should publish only with perf#18 ABI review.

title: Unify event-loop deadline authority for duplex/timerfd deadlines
description:
Architectural class 3, narrowed after later investigation. The old explanation that flood progress was gated by the 1Hz long-term timer scan was wrong for the current config; serverperfmode disables that long-term path, and z27x.7 is now known to be a signal-delivery architecture problem tracked by z27x.8.

This task remains valid as a preventive deadline-authority cleanup for real duplex/deadline users: collapse timerfd deadlines, ad-hoc duplex upcall deadlines, and future timeout sources into one scheduler/heap that the main loop uses to arm timerfd or epoll timeout. Acceptance should target deterministic duplex/deadline RED tests, not the z27x.7 pthread_kill flood. Do not claim this fixes z27x.7.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT-CAUSE CORRECTION (session ae391f52, from CODE + the captured flood auxlog s3_flood_tt_auxlog.txt): the "1Hz XNU long-term-timer scan gates flood progress" narrative is WRONG for the current config. duct-tape/src/timer.c:41 sets serverperfmode=1 -> timer_call.c:1481 sets tlp->threshold.interval = TIMER_LONGTERM_NONE -> timer_longterm_enqueue_unlocked (timer_call.c:1204-1207) returns NULL for interval==NONE, so EVERY timer lands on the LOCAL queue and arms timerfd directly via timer_queue_assign -> dtape_hooks->timer_arm. The long-term queue + its ~1s rescan are DISABLED. There is also NO separate "5s duplex metronome" armed timer — the only 5s value is the _duplexUpcallDeadlineNs SCALAR (thread.cpp:2427) checked ad-hoc in _drainDuplexReply, never arming timerfd.

REAL ROOT (from the auxlog): the "perfect 1.000s cadence" is a ~1s GUEST timer being re-armed every second (TIMER-FIRED exp=1 at .573->.574->.575->.514, exactly 1.000s apart, all ovr=0). Forward progress is gated to it because dtape_hook_timer_arm (server.cpp:147) SKIPS any non-override arm whose deadline >= the single _currentTimerDeadline scalar. While a longer deadline (e.g. a guest 30s cond timeout, dl=...132...) sits armed, competing SHORTER-but-still-farther arms are dropped, and the loop only makes a step when the current scalar fires. So the true fragmentation is the SINGLE-SCALAR _currentTimerDeadline arming discipline, not a longterm scan. THE FIX (min-heap of all pending deadlines, arm timerfd from the min) is still correct and still subsumes #123 (the ad-hoc _duplexUpcallDeadlineNs becomes a real heap entry that provably arms the loop). But the RED repro + rationale must target scalar-skip starvation, NOT a longterm-scan fiction. Two mechanisms only: (A) dtape XNU timers -> timer_arm hook -> timerfd (already unified through one hook, but gated by the _currentTimerDeadline scalar); (B) _duplexUpcallDeadlineNs scalar, ad-hoc-checked, never arms timerfd. The scheduler collapses (A)+(B) into one authority.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: REACHABILITY FINDING (session ae391f52): #123 (the _duplexUpcallDeadlineNs never-arms-timerfd leak) is CURRENTLY LATENT / UNREACHABLE IN PRODUCTION. The only site that arms _duplexUpcallDeadlineNs is the duplex munmap S2C upcall (thread.cpp:2424-2428), reachable only via the duplex-PARENT path (call.cpp:1754/1821), which is gated by d5VmDeallocProofArmed() (call.cpp:1584-1585) = DARLING_SERVER_D5_VMDEALLOC_PROOF>0. That budget is a BOOT-SCOPED, one-shot, <=3-cap, auto-disarming proof armed ONLY by an env var ON THE SERVER PROCESS ITSELF (call.cpp:1559-1583); default 0 (off). In steady-state prod the deadline is NEVER armed -> #123 cannot leak today. And even when armed, the D5 proof guest DOES pump, so the deadline does not trip. CONSEQUENCE for RED-TDD: #123 is a poor first target -- no deterministic RED fails on the prod binary without launching the server with the proof flag, and the proof path pumps. #123 is a real ARCHITECTURAL wart (an ad-hoc CLOCK_MONOTONIC deadline that bypasses the timerfd, invisible to epoll_wait(-1)) that WILL bite when the duplex lane is enabled for real ops (perf#18 Phase E) -- but it is not a live hang now. RECOMMENDATION: reprioritize. The live reproducible hang is #118 (z27x.7, flood dispatch-starvation). Build the unified deadline-scheduler as the ARCHITECTURAL cure for #123 ONLY when Phase E turns the duplex lane on, OR treat z27x.3 as a preventive refactor gated by a server-launch-flag RED (arm the D5 proof + a deliberately-non-pumping test guest). Do not front-run #118 with a latent-only fix.

title: duct-tape: fail-closed policy for reachable stubs (stop abort()/panic() crashing the whole server)
description:
MECHANICAL (discipline, not a refactor) but CRITICAL severity. Reachable duct-tape stubs choose abort()/panic() on unknown input instead of returning an error -> one guest operation takes down darlingserver and with it every process in the prefix. Verified sites:
 - duct-tape/src/task.c:240-247 proc_get_effective_task_policy: panic() for any flavor != TASK_POLICY_ROLE. Any guest task_policy_get with another flavor crashes the server.
 - duct-tape/src/thread.c:467-480 handle_ux_exception: dtape_stub_unsafe (safety<0 -> abort) on the ux_exception->signal 'introduce signal' path when thread->processing_signal is false -> a routine guest hardware fault (SIGSEGV/SIGBUS/SIGILL/SIGFPE) crashes the server. VERIFY reachability of the processing_signal==false arm on the common fault path first (may be partly covered by dtape_thread_sigexc_enter).
Lower-severity fake-success stubs in the same class (fold in): psynch.c:173/383 _sleep synthesizes EINTR; thread.c:1468 thread_abort_safely no-op returns KERN_SUCCESS; task.c:240 fabricated TASK_UNSPECIFIED role; stubs.c:207 IOTaskHasEntitlement returns TRUE unconditionally (permissive-by-default).

FIX: policy sweep -- reachable stub => fail-closed (return error / best-effort default), never fail-abort; audit all dtape_stub_unsafe/panic on live paths. Was session tasklist #120.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented current z27x.4 pass in source branch fix/duct-tape-fail-closed-stubs @ e8ad65c9feb96b95551b9eb3ce24daacaad42b83, stacked after z27x.2 gate @ ea28bd0. Patch artifact: patches/arch/darlingserver/duct-tape-fail-closed-stubs.patch sha256 1e3ea456f90debbb3fdac5061600ccf798d292913d9ed5a396256c04423753d1. Changes: proc_get_effective_task_policy no longer panics on reachable non-role flavors; known non-role policies fail closed to neutral 0, role remains TASK_UNSPECIFIED. handle_ux_exception no longer dtape_stub_unsafe/aborts on processing_signal=false; it queues the translated pending signal when hook/context exist or returns KERN_FAILURE. IOTaskHasEntitlement now denies by default instead of granting all entitlements. thread_abort_safely now returns KERN_FAILURE instead of fake KERN_SUCCESS. Added tests/duct_tape_fail_closed_stubs_test.sh static gate to pin no panic/dtape_stub_unsafe/fake-success for these reachable stubs. Verification: static gate OK; git diff --check OK; west darling-build --targets src/external/darlingserver/darlingserver --force OK; west patch verify --profile arch OK; strict patch status arch/perf/homebrew OK (0 missing, 0 no-file). Remaining blocker: broader Darwin-policy parity/security review before proposing upstream; psynch _sleep signal-specific TODOs intentionally not swept in this pass because q95 psynch work is already active and needs dedicated runtime semantics.

title: RPC cleanup backlog (med/low audit findings: errno surfacing, MSG_CTRUNC, dead scaffolding, correctness TODOs)
description:
Consolidated med/low from the 2026-07-04 RPC audit (session tasklist #124). Not architectural -- individual fixes.
MED: call.cpp:502-514 sendErrorReply surfaces -ECOMM where server meant -ESRCH/-EAGAIN on the failure paths (call.cpp:185/216/433); message.cpp:587-605 recvmmsg ignores MSG_CTRUNC on the SCM_RIGHTS descriptor path -> dropped fds -> bad fd in checkin/push_reply pipe -> leak/hang; dserver-rpc-defs.c:12-15 DSERVER_RPC_USE_LOG_FILE defaults to 1 ('CHANGE TO 0 BEFORE COMMITTING') -> every guest RPC error appends to world-shared /tmp/dserver-client-rpc.log (EASY WIN: flip default to 0).
LOW: compiled-in experiment scaffolding hot only under env knobs (DSERVER_SCHED_FUZZ thread.cpp:80-131; census/heatmap metrics.cpp+server.cpp:507-561; D5 vm_dealloc proof call.cpp:1545-1586; half-wired P4 ring arena ring.cpp:111-152); correctness TODOs shipping wrong values (process.cpp:603 regionCount=0; process.cpp:853 NOTE_EXIT status=0; kqchan.cpp:561 no NOTE_REAP/NOTE_SIGNAL); narrow latent (server.cpp:993-1005 timerfd spurious-read continue skips sibling epoll events; async-writer.cpp:115 ~Stream() can throw -> std::terminate; kqchan.cpp:129-137 setup-error leaks guest-end socket fd; thread.cpp:1582 impersonate() native-block FIXME).
VERIFIED SOUND (do NOT re-investigate): stage-3 interrupt-frame stack lifetime, fiber-migration TLS, fix-3 dtape timer, psynch mutex/cv grant asymmetry, registry unregister/orphan path.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 update: implemented the MSG_CTRUNC/SCM_RIGHTS cleanup slice as arch/darlingserver/message-ctrunc-reject.patch. Source branch fix/message-ctrunc-reject and integration/arch now point at darlingserver d231a7cdacd1b643e1941b6dcde9f83f135484dc. Behavior: MessageQueue::receiveMany now rejects MSG_TRUNC/MSG_CTRUNC datagrams instead of accepting a partially received descriptor set, and default Message descriptor capacity is 8 so the current max RPC reply plus push_reply sync pipe survives SO_PASSCRED control overhead. Added real host behavior fixture tests/message_ctrunc_test.cpp using socketpair(AF_UNIX, SOCK_DGRAM), SO_PASSCRED, SCM_RIGHTS, and the real MessageQueue path. RED proof is source-base against fcb9f33 and now matches the intended failure reason: "message with maximum push_reply fd count was truncated". GREEN/current passes. Tooling improvement made on the way: west source-base RED proofs now capture stdout/stderr and enforce expect-output-contains/lacks, and source-base GREEN uses the current checkout when the selected profile is already applied instead of trying to rematerialize conflicting historical stacked patch files. Verification passed: focused west test --profile arch --patch darlingserver/message-ctrunc-reject.patch --prove-red; focused normal GREEN; tests/run-west-test-metadata-contract.sh; west patch check --profile arch; west test --profile arch --red-audit; west patch export --profile arch --check; git diff --check. Remaining z27x.5 backlog still open: log default, errno surfacing, and lower-priority cleanup/TODO items.

title: nestwait semaphore_timedwait/mach_msg -111 (-ECONNREFUSED) rare abort (~2%, pre-existing)
description: Session tasklist #119. Guest semaphore_timedwait (RPC #62) / mach_msg (RPC #38) trap emulation receives a raw transport negative (-ECONNREFUSED/-111, -ECOMM/-70, -EPIPE) on the reply recvmsg and __simple_abort()s, because the wrapper tolerates only -LINUX_EINTR (xnu .../mach/impl/mach_traps.c:203-219 and :129-131; generate-rpc-wrappers.py:1559-1570). ALLOW_INTERRUPTIONS fixes only the SEND side; the receive path is raw. PRE-EXISTING (proven not a stage-3 regression: A/B 2c vs stage3 = 0 on both over 8 iters; 20-iter stage3 repro = 0; ~1/44 nestwait runs; historically seen pre-stage-3 in PERF25A census line 604). Two audit agents converged here independently. RELATED: dar-gwn.1.7 (same '-111' signature from the interrupt_enter reply-deferral angle). Fix = guest-side: during a cancelable wait, map a receive-path transport errno to a domain result (retry / KERN_ABORTED / KERN_OPERATION_TIMED_OUT), not __simple_abort(). Partially subsumed by the correlation-id child (dar-rpc-correctness-z27x.1) which lets the guest discard a stale/wrong reply. Repro harness: job ae391f52 tmp s3_nw_repro.sh.

The actual reproducible symptom is storm-on nestwait losing progress/no RESULT with client RPC logs full of send-side -111 on pthread_canceled/pthread_kill/interrupt_enter after/around the stall. Next correct path is to correlate server dserver.log/rpctrace and per-leg client RPC logs around the first progress stop; do not solve by blindly mapping transport errors in generated wrappers or by ignoring sigexc disconnects.

title: RED gate: pthread_kill flood stalls on per-signal server RPC delivery
description:
RED symptom/gate for the pthread_kill flood forward-progress failure. Historical explanations involving the 1Hz long-term timer, workqueue FIFO starvation, psynch lost wakeups, and reply-stash deadlock were all refuted by later live measurement. The current root is architectural: Darling delivers each signal through multiple synchronous server RPCs, while macOS delivers the common signal path as a kernel trap with no per-signal server round trip.

Keep this bead as the reproducible failure and acceptance gate: NCONS=8 unbounded pthread_kill flood must go GREEN without crash/hang and with done making macOS-like forward progress. The implementation cure lives in dar-rpc-correctness-z27x.8. Do not reattempt deadline-scheduler, dispatch-fairness, psynch-protocol, or reply-stash tweaks as the primary fix.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: FLOOD ROOT NARROWED (session ae391f52, from cvstorm2.c source + 8 captured flood run logs): the workload uses PLAIN pthread_cond_wait with NO TIMEOUT (cvstorm2.c:32 `while(work==0 && !stop) pthread_cond_wait(&cv,&mtx)`). done_count (cvstorm2.c:34) advances ONLY on a real cond_signal/broadcast wake (producer at :43/:45) -- there is NO cond-timeout fallback. So the HANG is NOT gated by any guest ~1s cond timeout, and the unified deadline scheduler (z27x.3) would NOT fix it. Captured evidence across runs: during a `done` stall, (a) work= (guest busy-loop counter) keeps climbing ~50-70M/s -> guest is CPU-RUNNING not blocked; (b) storm_hits= (pthread_kill deliveries) keep climbing -> signals ARE arriving, not lost; (c) only done= (completed signal->wake->dispatch->resume cycles) latches, in ~1s-quantized steps. => DISPATCH-STARVATION: cond_signal marks a consumer fiber runnable but its resumption is DEFERRED under the pthread_kill AST/interrupt flood; forward progress resumes only when the storm briefly relents. CONSEQUENCE: #118/z27x.7 is a SEPARATE root from z27x.3 (deadline scheduler). z27x.3 cleanly owns #123 (duplex-deadline leak) only. #118 needs its own fix targeting the signal-wake dispatch path (AST/interrupt processing convoying ahead of ready-fiber dispatch). Do NOT bend the deadline-scheduler to cover it. Dispatch-path recon in progress.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: ROOT PROVEN TO THE LINE (session ae391f52, 2-agent code map + workload source + 8 captured flood logs). #118 = DISPATCH-STARVATION of signal-woken fibers through a SINGLE-WORKER queue. Chain: (WAKE ARRIVES reliably) guest cond_signal/pthread_kill -> psynch.c:596/604 shim_psynch_wait_wakeup -> thread_go -> thread_unblock (thread.c:767, resume hook at thread.c:818) -> dtape_hook_thread_resume (server.cpp:85) -> Thread::wake (thread.cpp:1460) marks Parked->Ready + scheduleThread (thread.cpp:1519). Typed wake-gen tokens (thread.cpp:1379/1416) + _rerunPending (thread.cpp:572, re-enqueued :851) are engineered to PREVENT lost wakeups -> the wake is not lost. (DISPATCH SERIALIZED) scheduleThread (server.cpp:1099) = _workQueue.push; build defaults DSERVER_SINGLE_THREADED=ON (CMakeLists.txt:59) => WorkQueue min=max=1 (workers.hpp:47), a single FIFO drained ONE item/iteration (workers.hpp:87-92). Every resumed parked cond-waiter goes through this one worker (the inline fastpath doWorkInline BAILS on _isSuspendedLocked, thread.cpp:882, so resumes never inline). (STORM AMPLIFIER) each pthread_kill -> InterruptEnter RPC -> _pendingInterrupts (thread.cpp:335) drained one-per-doneWorking (thread.cpp:838) + _handleInterruptEnter -> dtape_thread_sigexc_enter -> clear_wait_internal(THREAD_INTERRUPTED) (thread.c:593) aborting/re-parking every signalled waiter; contended dtape lck_mtx SUSPENDS the fiber (locks.c:110) instead of blocking a thread -> each contended lock = one more park+re-dispatch through the SAME single queue. CONVOY LOCKS: WorkQueue::_queueMutex (workers.hpp:39, the true global convoy), per-thread _rwlock (same-thread waker vs worker), threadRegistry _rwlock exclusive in threadForPort (thread.cpp:1774, serializes port-targeted signal storm). EMPIRICAL MATCH: during a done-stall work= climbs 50-70M/s (guest CPU-live), storm_hits= climbs (signals arriving), only done= latches. FIX DIRECTION (separate fix/* + this bead, NOT the deadline scheduler): break the single-worker serialization for signal-woken resumes and/or stop each pthread_kill forcing a full abort+re-park+re-dispatch cycle. Matches stage-4 memory (workers_busy=0 normally, but under flood the lone worker IS the serialization point). Root file:line list above is the fix surface.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT-CAUSE CORRECTED (session ae391f52) via LIVE MEASUREMENT + REAL macOS REFERENCE. Two prior narratives were WRONG for the current ring+inline binary:

1. LIVE darling-stat probe during the -DNCONS=1 unbounded flood (d81b5cf1): workqueue_depth=0, workers_busy=0, workers_available=1 the ENTIRE run; rpcs_serviced climbs steadily ~70-75k/s. So the 'single-worker FIFO dispatch-starvation / scheduleThread convoy' root is NOT the operative bottleneck -- the worker is de-facto idle, everything runs inline on the main loop. A dispatch-coalescing fix (dedup _queuedForDispatch bit on scheduleThread, built+deployed md5 198c64ee) gave 5/8 HANG = NO IMPROVEMENT, confirming the queue is never contended. REVERTED. A priority-lane on the workqueue would likewise do nothing.

2. REAL macOS REFERENCE (host 100.100.204.16, macOS 26.5.1 arm64, clang -O2 -DNCONS=1, IDENTICAL cvstorm2.c): done climbs 2.0M->60.9M over 20s, stall=0s throughout, storm_hits->1.1M, RESULT=OK. Darling latches done at ~48,710 under the same flood (work climbs 70M/s = guest CPU-live; storm_hits 15k/s = signals delivered). ~1250x forward-progress deficit. => #118 IS A DARLING DEFECT, NOT a POSIX/THREAD_ABORTSAFE workload pathology. (A code-only analysis had concluded 'expected/wontfix' from psynch being THREAD_ABORTSAFE; the real-macOS run REFUTES it -- per macos-reference-host, run the reference, don't argue semantics.)

REAL ROOT (matches PERF25A-CENSUS Update 11, the 42:1 wait:signal lost-wakeup signature): a LOST CONDVAR WAKEUP in the psynch cvwait/cvsignal PREPOST path under signal-forced EINTR aborts. A cvsignal grant races the sigexc clear_wait abort and lands on an already-aborted waiter -> orphaned prepost -> the wakeup is lost -> the consumer re-cvwaits forever (done latches EXACTLY, not merely slow). Same FAMILY as the perf#25a A0 fixes (signal-abort races a wakeup handoff) but a RESIDUAL face they did not fully close -- now proven distinct from and MORE than a single-abort grant-clobber (that one IS fixed; this survives the storm). FIX LOCUS: guest psynch cvwait/cvsignal prepost handling (libsystem_kernel psynch_cvwait.c prepost-orphan-on-EINTR) and/or server duct-tape _psynch_cvwait wait interaction with clear_wait_internal (dtape_thread_sigexc_enter). Evidence: job ae391f52 tmp z27x7_stat_probe_baseline.out, z27x7_macos_reference.log. NOT the workqueue, NOT the deadline scheduler (z27x.3). Next: pin the exact orphaned-prepost site vs macOS and fix surgically.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
PROGRESS + TWO MORE ELIMINATIONS (session ae391f52, live-instrumented). The lost-cvsignal-prepost hypothesis is ALSO wrong for this binary. Built a CV fix (mirror the mutex KERN_NOT_WAITING protection: _kwq_mark_interruped_wakeup(KWQ_INTR_WRITE) in ksyn_cvsignal @992, consume in _psynch_cvwait before prepost lookup; branch fix/z27x7-cv-lost-wakeup, md5 0997792a) -> 5/8 HANG, NO improvement. Then instrumented the psynch layer (ZTRACE via dtape_log_error at ksyn_signal/cvsignal/cvwait/mtxwait/mtxdrop) and captured a live flood at DSERVER_LOG_LEVEL=info:

DECISIVE: during the actual latch (10s flood, storm_hits climbing ~15k/s, work climbing 70M/s), the server psynch RPCs are essentially FLAT -- only ~3 ksyn_signal (ALL ret=0 SUCCESS, never NOT_WAITING), ~4 cvsignal, ~4 cvwait, ~10 mtxwait, 0 mtxdrop_signal -- and ALL of those are the startup/launchd-era handshake (pids 25/28), NOT the flood consumer. Total dserver.log = 1486 lines for a 10s flood. => the consumer is NOT re-cvwaiting and NOT in server-side psynch mutex/cond at all during the latch. The lost-wakeup narratives (mine + PERF25A Update 11) do NOT apply to the current ring+inline binary.

GUEST-STATE SNAPSHOT (busiest mldr, 4 flood threads, during a partial-progress window): tids in sigsuspend (x2), futex_wait_queue (x1), ep_poll (x1). Points at GUEST-SIDE SIGNAL-DELIVERY machinery (sigsuspend / the sigprocess RPC + handler + sigreturn per SIGUSR1) and/or a host-futex interaction -- NOT the psynch lock protocol. The consumer's work>0 fast path means it does uncontended userspace mutex lock/unlock + work--/done++, so a hard latch there means it never returns from signal handling to complete the cycle, OR blocks on a host futex for a signal-delivery RPC reply.

NEXT (not yet done): capture the guest USERSPACE backtrace (rip via server-side readMemory or gdb-namespace runner) of the stuck consumer during a HARD latch (stall>=3) to pin the exact frame -- signal delivery (sigexc/sigreturn), the mldr signal thread, or a futex. Only then design a fix. State: baseline d81b5cf1 restored+deployed, doctor GREEN, all fix branches deleted (dispatch-fairness + cv-lost-wakeup both refuted), source clean. Evidence: job ae391f52 tmp z27x7_ztrace*, z27x7_gueststate_KEEP.txt, z27x7_stat_probe_baseline.out, z27x7_macos_reference.log.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
ROOT PROVEN + fold implemented (NCONS=1 GREEN) but BLOCKED on NCONS=8 panic; reverted to baseline (session ae391f52 cont).

ROOT (definitive, via guest-rip tracer DSERVER_Z27X7 + per-tid RPC census DARLING_SERVER_AUXLOG): #118 is SIGNAL-DELIVERY RPC COST, not psynch. Under the -DNCONS=1 pthread_kill(SIGUSR1) flood the stormed consumer spins a 4-RPC cycle forever (sigprocess + interrupt_enter + interrupt_exit + thread_self_trap, ~127k each in 22s) and essentially never reaches its mutex/cond critical section (1 cvwait / 6 mutexwait total). Real macOS delivers signals as a kernel trap (0 RPC) -> the ~1250x forward-progress deficit. A genuine Darling defect.

FIX #1 (safe, correct, marginal): removed the DEAD mach_thread_self() in sigexc_handler+sigrt_handler -- unused result + a per-signal thread_self_trap RPC + a leaked mach send-right. Cuts ~25% of per-signal RPCs. Too marginal/high-variance alone to close #118.

FIX #2 (the closer, WIP): FOLD interrupt_enter INTO sigprocess (server runs the enter logic in Sigprocess::processCall; guest sends 1 RPC not 2; sigprocess gets PUSH_UNKNOWN_REPLIES; doWork stacks the frame for a pending Sigprocess; deferred fold reply carries new_bsd_signal_number via Call::sendDeferredReply). PROVEN: NCONS=1 flood gate 12/12 OK, ZERO hang, done 38k-149k, maxstall<=3 (baseline ~50% coin-flip hang, done 2-18k). BLOCKED: NCONS=8 (contended mutex + storm) PANICS -- processSignal runs inline after the folded enter's clear_wait on the SAME fiber, but the old two-RPC path dispatched the cancellation continuation on a SEPARATE fiber across the RPC boundary; folding re-asserts a wait mid-signal (perf#25a Part-2 / #114 fiber-reentry class). Panic: Sigprocess::processCall -> _handleInterruptEnterForCurrentThread -> dtape_thread_sigexc_enter -> clear_wait_internal -> assert_wait_deadline.

STATE: reverted BOTH fixes to PRISTINE baseline (dserver d81b5cf1, kernel 6bd251c3; doctor ALL GREEN; source clean). Fold saved as LOCAL WIP branches wip/z27x7-sigprocess-fold in darlingserver (5e0e61b) + xnu (fd3c4a4). NEXT: make the fold NCONS=8-safe -- settle the cancellation dispatch before processSignal, OR only fold when !frame.interruptedCall (keep the 2-RPC path when a real in-flight wait is interrupted). RED gates: job tmp flood-gate.sh.keep (NCONS=1), flood-gate-n8.sh (NCONS=8).

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
SAFE FOLD (fiber-boundary preserving) — NCONS=8 PANIC ELIMINATED; NCONS=8 hang is orthogonal + remains.

Directive "делай безопасным": made the sigprocess fold NCONS=8-safe without the second guest RPC.

WHAT CHANGED (server-only; guest xnu fold fd3c4a4 unchanged):
- Sigprocess::processCall is now TWO-PHASE. Phase 1 runs _handleInterruptEnterForCurrentThread().
  * replyNow==true (nothing in flight — the common flood case; NO clear_wait fired): process the
    signal INLINE and reply. Fast, panic-free.
  * replyNow==false (a real in-flight RPC was interrupted → enter fired clear_wait + armed replyOwed):
    do NOT touch processSignal on this fiber. Stash the 6 sigprocess params on the InterruptContext
    frame (foldPhase2Pending + foldSigCall + fold{Bsd,Linux,Code,SignalAddress,ThreadState,FloatState}),
    keep the replyOwed arming, return.
- pushCallReply's owed-enter flush — which fires exactly when the interrupted call stashes its reply
  (the post-repark-interrupt-cancel moment the old 2-RPC guest would have sent its separate sigprocess) —
  calls the new Thread::requeueFoldedSigprocess(): pushes the Sigprocess call onto _pendingInterrupts so
  doneWorking re-dispatches it (PHASE 2) on a FRESH fiber to run processSignal + _sendReply. This
  reproduces the fiber boundary the two separate RPCs used to provide, WITHOUT the extra guest RPC.
- doWork frame-stacking gained a foldPhase2Redispatch guard so the phase-2 re-run reuses the existing
  frame instead of double-stacking.
- Removed the dead sendDeferredReply machinery (base virtual + generated per-class override +
  owedReplyValue) from the prior panic-fold — the safe path sends its own reply in phase 2, so the
  flush reverts to plain sendBasicReply(0).

MEASURED (flood-gate-n8.sh, N=8 SECS=22, cvstorm2 -DNCONS=8 unbounded pthread_kill flood):
- SAFE FOLD (dserver 1fbf64d2 + guest 39765bb9): 8/8 — ZERO panic / ZERO crash / ZERO "already waiting".
  Prior panic-fold (38d42f48): 2 panics (waitq.c:2837 "thread already waiting") + hangs. Crash class GONE.
- BUT 8/8 RESULT=HANG (done latched 14..69829; work climbs to 4e8 = flood spinning). The residual is the
  ORTHOGONAL #118 dispatch-starvation (single-worker FIFO under 8 storming consumers). The fold halves
  signal-delivery RPC cost and removes the crash, but does NOT by itself cure NCONS=8 starvation.
- NO REGRESSION vs baseline (same harness, same session): baseline d81b5cf1 NCONS=8 tally = OK=1 / HANG=1 /
  NO_RESULT=6 — i.e. 6 of 8 iters wedged so hard the whole run was Killed at timeout (no `done` survives).
  Safe fold = 8/8 clean RESULT=HANG, 0 NO_RESULT, 0 crash: it eliminates BOTH the crash class AND the
  catastrophic NO_RESULT wedge, staying responsive enough to report progress and exit every run. NCONS=8
  remains a coin-flip-to-hang for both (baseline's single OK is the workload's stochasticity), so the fold
  does not by itself make NCONS=8 green — but it is strictly safer (no panic, no wedge).
- NCONS=1 (the fold's target case) still closes as before.

STATE: committed darlingserver 4e602cf on wip/z27x7-sigprocess-fold (parent 5e0e61b = old panic-fold).
NOT deployed as the doctor baseline (still d81b5cf1). The safe fold is strictly better than the
panic-fold (no crash + fixes the fix#1 per-signal mach_thread_self leak + halves RPCs + NCONS=1 closes),
but NCONS=8 is not green. Turning NCONS=8 green requires the SEPARATE dispatch-starvation fix (the
scheduleThread-coalescing / single-worker-serialization family), not more folding.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
STARVATION CURE attempted on top of the safe fold — root RE-CONFIRMED architectural; three stacked surgical fixes refuted by live instrumentation.

Directive: "делай starvation cure поверх safe fold." RED = NCONS=8 8/8 HANG on the safe fold.

REFUTED (each built, deployed, gated):
1. Fair round-robin dispatch (main-loop drain reordered by source tid so the stormer's 41k
   pthread_kill RPCs can't bury other threads' forward-progress RPCs) — 3/3 HANG. Starvation is
   NOT FIFO position.
2. psynch mutex grant-vs-abort reconciliation in psynch_mtxcontinue (a REAL race — a mutexdrop
   grant that races the signal-abort was discarded + returned EINTR; the CV path already
   reconciles this, the mutex path did not). Correct bug, but 3/3 HANG — not the dominant root.
3. Reply-stash deadlock — REFUTED by REPLY-DISP census: 264,247 disp=SEND vs 5 STASH-SAVED / 0
   FLUSH-SAVED. The server IS replying (264k replies/run).

DECISIVE INSTRUMENTATION (instrument-first):
- CVTRACE (cvsignal/cvwait/cvcontinue outcomes): only 33 cv events all-run, ALL in the first
  ~50ms (working fine), then the psynch layer goes SILENT for the rest of the hang. Consumers are
  NOT in the server psynch layer; the protocol is verbatim-Apple-correct.
- GUEST-STATE snapshot of the 8 consumer threads during the latch: ALL 8 are state=R SPINNERS
  burning ~330ms CPU each, stuck in __skb_wait_for_more_packets -> __unix_dgram_recvmsg -> recvmsg
  on the darlingserver UDS socket (perf#3 adaptive recv-spin) waiting for the next signal-RPC reply.
- RPC census: ~99% of ~205-264k RPCs are signal machinery (pthread_kill / sigprocess /
  interrupt_enter / interrupt_exit / thread_self_trap ~41k each); psynch_cvwait ~23.
- THROTTLE: STORM_THROTTLE_US=200 (~5k sig/s, realistic-heavy) STILL 6/6 HANG — not pure rate.

ROOT (architectural): Darling delivers EACH signal via multiple synchronous server RPCs; macOS
delivers signals as a 0-RPC kernel trap. Under the flood the consumer is 100% consumed cycling
signal-machinery RPCs (issue RPC -> spin in recvmsg for reply -> handle -> repeat) and never runs
its `work--; done++` critical section — done advances briefly then latches. A cure requires
kernel-trap-style signal delivery that does not round-trip the server per signal (or an
SA_RESTART-equivalent auto-restart that keeps the wait) — an A0-ARCH-grade rework, NOT a stacked
surgical fix.

STATE: no starvation fix landed. The safe fold (darlingserver 4e602cf on wip/z27x7-sigprocess-fold)
remains the shippable win (eliminates the NCONS=8 panic, halves per-signal RPCs, closes NCONS=1).
Deployed = pristine baseline (d81b5cf1 / 6bd251c3), doctor GREEN. Reusable probes in job tmp:
z27x7_cvtrace.sh, z27x7_gueststate_n8.sh, z27x7_replystash.sh, flood-gate-n8-throttle200.sh.

The safe fold (darlingserver wip/z27x7-sigprocess-fold @ 4e602cf) is the shippable partial win baked into z27x.8's starting baseline (kills the NCONS=8 panic, halves per-signal RPCs, closes NCONS=1). Surgical fixes are exhausted (see prior comments); do NOT re-attempt dispatch-fairness / psynch-protocol / reply-stash tweaks.

title: Re-architect signal delivery: no per-signal darlingserver RPC (kernel-trap-style) — the architectural cure for z27x.7/#118
description:
Architectural cure for z27x.7: redesign guest signal delivery so the common path does not perform a synchronous darlingserver round trip per delivered signal. The safe sigprocess fold is only a partial starting point; it reduces RPC count and removes one panic class, but NCONS=8 still hangs because the guest spends its time cycling signal machinery RPCs instead of running useful work.

Design/prototype directions: kernel-trap-style delivery for common cases, standard-signal coalescing while a signal is already pending/in flight, and restart/resume semantics for handlers that return without requiring full guest return-to-userspace plus reissue. Acceptance: NCONS=8 flood gate goes RED->GREEN, NCONS=1 stays green, doctor/brew smokes do not regress, and per-signal RPC census drops by construction rather than by another local scheduling tweak.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented a bounded first step toward the no-per-signal-RPC architecture: darlingserver now coalesces repeated Linux standard signals (1..31) per target thread while that signal is already pending/in flight, leaving realtime/S2C signals untouched. The pending bit is set before tgkill, cleared by RAII after processSignal completes, and cleared on tgkill/ESRCH failure. Verified: ninja -C darling/build src/external/darlingserver/darlingserver and git diff --check. Remaining before close: deploy locally and run the cvstorm2/a0-gate NCONS=8 flood acceptance; this is not yet a full kernel-trap-style signal delivery redesign.

title: Architectural follow-ups from full patchset audit
description:
Patchset audit 2026-07-08 found several places where existing fixes are probably correct as emergency patches but should be reworked into cleaner architectural units before upstream-quality publication.

Findings to track:
1. E-UNION is spread through vchroot/syscalls as a proof-of-concept; should become a clear union/resolver layer with a portable committed harness.
2. Fork/checkin/wait fixes rely on timeouts/sticky flags; should converge into an explicit lifecycle/state-machine contract.
3. darlingserver RPC reply/error containment is scattered across point fixes; should become a typed/RAII reply lifecycle with exactly-once accounting.
4. psynch negative-return/errno behavior is split across xnu/libpthread; should be one documented syscall/cerror contract helper plus tests.
5. Compatibility no-ops/stubs (_NSGetExecutablePath workaround, sandbox-exec pass-through, priority, SIOCGIFCONF, SDK detection) need real implementation or explicit emulation-policy failures.
6. getattrlist/getattrlistbulk should converge on the shared Darwin attribute packer rather than parallel encoders.
7. perf18/ring stack needs a stable transport interface, ABI contract tests, and separation between production path and experiments.
8. dyld DCC2 reader needs hermetic fixture/regression coverage, not only runtime fail-fast gates.
9. libunwind static no-jump-tables workaround needs target-specific dyld/static-runtime codegen policy and red test.
10. build-drift gate belongs primarily in west/workspace tooling, with CMake as an opt-in guard rather than workspace-aware policy.

Definition of done: each item is either represented by a child task under the owning epic/profile, folded into an existing open task with explicit acceptance criteria, or documented as an intentional exception with rationale.
close_reason: All ten architectural findings from the patchset audit are now mapped to owning follow-up tasks or existing epics with explicit acceptance criteria; see the 2026-07-10 matrix comment.

4 psynch negative errno/cerror cross-side contract -> dar-q95.29.6.4.
5 compatibility stubs/no-ops policy -> existing dar-q95.29.4.1/.2/.3/.4 plus dar-q95.29.8 and dar-q95.29.9 for SDK/raw-fork boundary.
6 getattrlist/getattrlistbulk shared packer convergence -> dar-mxss.

This satisfies the task's definition of done: every finding is now represented by an owning task/epic or documented as folded into an existing task with acceptance criteria.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Fixed. Replaced the stale shell-based RED path with a launch-free direct dserverdbg RED runner. The RED source forest now applies shared launch-free diagnostics plus a small Thread::suspend hook and a dserverdbg microthread-resume-before-suspend oracle; the runner starts only darlingserver, writes the microthread.resume_before_suspend fault, and calls dserver_rpc_thread_suspended through the test oracle. Bad current-minus runtime consumes the fault and reaches microthread.suspend.inject_resume_before_suspend, then the old lost wakeup leaves the RPC without a reply, producing DSERVERDBG_THREAD_SUSPENDED_RC=-11 / ORACLE_RC=1. Fixed runtime still runs the normal guest C fixture and prints MICROTHREAD_RESUME_BEFORE_SUSPEND_OK / ORACLE_RC=0. title: Teach current-minus materialization to respect patch stack cursor
description: guest-runtime-deploy current-minus currently starts from patch source-base/source-commit^ and then reapplies every earlier patch in the profile module. For patches whose source-base is already inside the local patch stack, this replays patches that are already present and can conflict before RED runtime is built. Repro: west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/eunion-24-mkdir-upper-create.patch --prefix-profile homebrew --prove-red; it fails applying xnu/select-pselect-fdset.patch with conflicts before runtime. Acceptance: materialization determines which profile patches are already included in the chosen bad revision (or records an explicit stack cursor), applies only the missing patches needed for current-minus, has contract tests for already-applied earlier patches, and eunion-24 reaches runtime RED/GREEN with a reason matcher instead of failing in source setup.
close_reason: fixed

title: dar-6x4 REAL blocker: mldr main-stack MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE EEXIST fails libunistring make check
description:
THE ACTUAL libunistring `make check` failure (live-captured 2026-06-27 on pristine fb027685/5f01b70f). brew reinstall -s libunistring RAN configure->make->make check WITHOUT hanging, then FAILED: `Failed to allocate stack of 65536 bytes: 17 (File exists)` -> test-fcntl-h -> make[4] Error 1 -> check-am Error 2 -> BREW_EXIT=1. This is NOT a hang/lost-reply (the original dar-6x4 framing is disproven).

ROOT CAUSE: mldr setup_space (darling/src/startup/mldr/mldr.c:830-855) allocates each guest process main stack via mmap at a FIXED address = commpage_address - size, i.e. [_COMM_PAGE64_BASE_ADDRESS - PAGE_SIZE*16, _COMM_PAGE64_BASE_ADDRESS) = [0x7fffffdf0000, 0x7fffffe00000), using MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE|MAP_GROWSDOWN. MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE returns EEXIST(17) if ANYTHING is already mapped in that exact range; mldr then exit(1)s, so the fork+exec test program dies and make check fails. Under make check fork/exec storm this collides intermittently (some prior loader artifact / dyld_shared_cache / ASLR placement occupies the slot).

SAME CLASS as dar-gwn.6.6 (commpage was a fixed address colliding under ASLR). The 6.6 commpage MAP_FIXED (now always pinning 0x7fffffe00000) likely exposes/worsens this adjacent-stack collision. Order in setup_space: commpage_setup() THEN stack mmap.

CANDIDATE FIXES (decide via TDD): (a) mirror the 6.6 approach — use MAP_FIXED (force, clobber the stray mapping) for the stack like the commpage now does, after auditing it is safe to displace whatever sits there at that early setup point; (b) retry/fall back to a NON-fixed stack address if MAP_FIXED_NOREPLACE returns EEXIST (stack does not strictly need the canonical address the way the commpage does — only must avoid colliding with the commpage); (c) reserve the stack range together with the commpage in one reservation. Need to confirm WHAT occupies [0x7fffffdf0000,0x7fffffe00000) at failure (strace -f the failing test-* exec; or add a one-shot /proc/self/maps dump on the EEXIST path).

RED gate: a guest fork/exec storm that reproduces the EEXIST (lighter than full make check); or run `brew reinstall -s libunistring` to the make check phase. Build mldr from darling-build; deploy to darling-prefix (kill server before cp). FINDING: ~/work/dar-6x4-repro/reframe-noDeadlock-1782557989 (brew log: brew-real-1782557989.out). Relates dar-6x4 (re-rooted) and dar-gwn.6.6. Nothing pushed.
close_reason: Fixed in mldr.c setup_space: try preferred fixed stack addr, fall back to kernel-chosen mmap on EEXIST. Collider live-captured = process's own native [stack] overlapping the fixed range; MAP_FIXED-evict rejected (would unmap mldr's stack). Validated GREEN x2 via brew reinstall -s libunistring (EEXIST gone; make check builds+runs full gnulib suite 674/636 PASS; 4 fd-test FAILs split to dar-6x4.1). Unit-proven fixtest.c. Not pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: COLLIDER NAILED + FIX IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-27. Diagnostic mldr dumped /proc/self/maps on EEXIST; live capture (~/work/dar-6x4-repro/diag-1782559420/02.make.log:2520-2522): wanted [0x7fffffdf0000,0x7fffffe00000); COLLIDER 7fffffde0000-7fffffe00000 rw-p [stack]. => The collider is the process's OWN NATIVE LINUX [stack] (top==commpage base, 128K); wanted 64K range is its top half => total overlap => EEXIST. NOT a random ASLR neighbor (plain setarch -R: [stack] at 0x7ffffffde000, no overlap). DECISION: candidate (a) MAP_FIXED-evict REJECTED (would unmap mldr's own running stack). Chose (b): try preferred fixed spot, on EEXIST retry same-size mmap WITHOUT MAP_FIXED* (kernel picks); set lr->stack_top=actual_base+size. Safe: stack_top is only initial %rsp (mldr.c:950) + KERN_USRSTACK (sysctl_kern.c:315/324). Implemented in mldr.c setup_space; built clean; deployed; green-validation brew reinstall in progress. Nothing pushed.

The stack EEXIST blocker is ELIMINATED. The remaining BREW_EXIT=1 is solely the 4 fd-semantics test failures (test-cloexec/dup2/fcntl/getdtablesize) -> filed separately as dar-6x4.1; NOT this bug.
Fix logic also unit-proven (~/work/dar-6x4-repro/fixtest.c: pre-occupy preferred range -> fallback relocates, writable, correct stack_top). mldr.c setup_space committed-to-tree, deployed md5 84cf2fbe. Marking CLOSED. Nothing pushed.

title: Product tests should converge on CTest-first colocated registrations with compact proof/resource metadata
description:
Problem: after dar-zhlt, product tests can be expressed through compact manifests and CTest-backed source-repo fixtures, but the long-term ideal shape must be explicit so future tests do not drift back into bespoke manifest runners or shell harnesses. We want an industry-grade pattern: source repos own their test targets; build systems register runnable tests; west test selects those registrations, prepares Darling-specific runtime context, and proves RED/GREEN.

Industry target shape:
1. Product regression tests live next to the product code they exercise whenever practical. For a LibreSSL crypto regression, the C file belongs under libressl/tests/ and registration belongs in the source repo tests/CMakeLists.txt or equivalent, not as a workspace-only harness script. For XNU/dyld/mldr/darlingserver runtime regressions, prefer source-repo CTest/add_darling_test registration where the test can be compiled by the product build.
2. The product build system registers runnable tests. CMake/CTest is the preferred backend: add_executable/add_darling_executable as appropriate, target_link_libraries, add_test(NAME ... COMMAND ...), set_tests_properties(... LABELS ...). Labels carry bead, submodule, env/runs, diag, tier, fuzz/stress/manual where relevant. west test should select CTest labels, not manually describe compilation for normal product cases.
3. CTest-first does not mean module-only. Host/source-local tests can be mostly module-local. Guest/system tests still need the full Darling runtime envelope: prefix, launcher, dyld/mldr/xnu/darlingserver artifacts, resource providers, timeout/debug runner, cleanup, and restore. CTest is the registry/selector; west test remains the system orchestrator.
4. RED proof belongs to orchestration, not to test code. The test asserts fixed behavior unconditionally; west test materializes source-base/current-minus-patch/runtime artifacts and proves that the same selected test fails before the fix and passes after it. The green expectation is separate and belongs in expect when a test needs explicit exit/output/status checks.
5. Compact manifests should use explicit axes, not a broad catch-all field:
   - ctest: <label> selects the registered CTest test(s).
   - runs: host | guest | macos declares the execution environment. runs: guest implies the Darling guest envelope; it is not hidden in another field.
   - red-proof: source | runtime | self | none declares how west test proves that the selected test is actually red without the fix. This is separate from expect, which describes the expected green result on the current tree.
   - expect: optional green-result expectations such as exit status, output markers, timeout policy, or domain-specific verdicts. Do not encode RED proof mechanics here.
   - build-target: <cmake-target> names the target to build before ctest when CTest will not build it implicitly. Prefer this spelling over ambiguous target.
   - artifacts: [...] names build outputs that west test builds/deploys/restores during runtime proof, e.g. xnu-kernel, dyld-runtime, mldr-runtime.
   - resources: [...] names runtime/test harness resources, e.g. DCC cache, host trace files, host stat deltas, allocated devices, external services, env-injected caches.
   - fixtures: [...] names setup/cleanup state, e.g. E-UNION overlay, seeded prefix state, service topology, filesystem templates.
6. Do not introduce needs as target syntax. It is too broad and can hide runtime semantics. New compact manifests should use artifacts/resources/fixtures explicitly.
7. Escape hatches stay explicit. script/guest-command/source-fixture runners are allowed for weird multi-process gates, stress/fuzz harnesses, transitional tests, or cases the product build cannot express. They should carry a reason and should not become the default shape for product regressions.

Ideal examples:
Host/source-local:
  ctest: bead:dar-q95.6
  runs: host
  red-proof: source
  build-target: darling_ec_tls_regress

Guest/runtime artifact proof:
  ctest: bead:dar-xnu.fd_guard
  runs: guest
  red-proof: runtime
  artifacts: [xnu-kernel]

Guest with cache/oracle:
  ctest: bead:dar-dyld.dcc
  runs: guest
  red-proof: runtime
  artifacts: [dyld-runtime]
  resources: [dcc6-smoke-cache, dyld-trace-oracle]
  expect:
    output-contains: DCC2: using cache

E-UNION:
  ctest: bead:dar-eunion.copyup
  runs: guest
  red-proof: runtime
  artifacts: [xnu-kernel]
  fixtures: [eunion-overlay]

Acceptance:
- Document this target architecture in docs/test-infra.md and AGENTS.md with examples.
- Implement parser aliases for ctest, runs, red-proof, expect, build-target, artifacts, resources, and fixtures while preserving existing old manifest fields.
- Do not implement needs as primary syntax or as a new shorthand for target manifests.
- Rename or alias target -> build-target for darling-cmake-target-fixture and expose expanded output in west test --list/--expanded so shorthand remains transparent.
- Migrate the LibreSSL representative test to the more upstream-like placement: test source under libressl/tests and registration in tests/CMakeLists.txt when feasible; use add_executable/add_test style unless Darling-specific add_darling_executable is required by the isolated build.
- Add focused manifest contract coverage for aliases and deterministic expansion.
- west patch check/export and west test --red-audit remain green.

Non-goals:
- Do not force every existing test to CTest immediately.
- Do not remove existing runner escape hatches.
- Do not push anything upstream; this is local/fork workflow design first.
close_reason: CTest-first compact manifest architecture implemented and representative LibreSSL placement verified.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Clarification: the CTest-first target shape is not meant to reduce every product regression to a single module-only unit test. Darling has at least three product-test scopes. (1) Host/source-local tests: a module can prove a pure compile/library behavior by itself, e.g. LibreSSL crypto regression, and then source-local CMake/CTest is the primary shape. (2) Guest/system tests: the test still belongs in product/source test registration when practical, but execution requires the whole Darling guest runtime: prefix, launcher, dyld/mldr/xnu/darlingserver artifacts, environment, resource providers, timeout/debug runner, and cleanup. In this scope CTest is the registry/selector for the testcase, while west test remains the orchestrator that materializes the full guest system and RED/GREEN runtime proof. (3) Cross-module/runtime-artifact tests: a patch in xnu/dyld/mldr/darlingserver may require building and deploying one or more artifacts into a prefix before executing a guest-visible CTest/testcase. The manifest 'needs' list is specifically for this: it names typed system dependencies, not just module dependencies. Example target shape: ctest: bead:dar-xnu.fd_guard; proves: runtime-red; needs: [darling-prefix, xnu-kernel]. That means: build/select the registered testcase by CTest label, build/deploy xnu-kernel from current-minus for RED, prepare a Darling prefix, run in guest Darling, restore artifacts, then run GREEN. So the ideal architecture is CTest-first for discovery/registration, but not CTest-only for orchestration. west test owns the system-level Darling runtime envelope.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Design correction: do not make the Darling runtime implicit through needs. The compact manifest should separate execution environment from additional dependencies. Preferred shape: runs: host|guest|macos says where/how the test executes; proves says the RED/GREEN proof mode; needs lists only extra typed artifacts/resources beyond the execution environment. Examples: (1) ctest: bead:dar-q95.6; runs: host; proves: source-red; build-target: darling_ec_tls_regress. (2) ctest: bead:dar-xnu.fd_guard; runs: guest; proves: runtime-red; needs: [xnu-kernel]. Here runs: guest implies the Darling guest envelope: prefix, launcher, debug runner/timeout, guest env, cleanup/restore. needs: [xnu-kernel] only says which runtime artifact participates in the proof. (3) ctest: bead:dar-dyld.dcc; runs: guest; proves: runtime-red; needs: [dyld-runtime, dcc6-smoke-cache]. Avoid using needs: [darling-prefix] in the target DSL except as a legacy/expanded low-level form. west test --list --expanded should show the implied guest envelope explicitly so the shorthand is not obscure.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Design correction: remove needs from the target DSL entirely. It is too broad and risks hiding execution/runtime semantics. Target compact manifests should use explicit axes only: runs: host|guest|macos for execution environment; proves: source-red|runtime-red|self|none for proof model; artifacts: [...] for build outputs that west test builds/deploys/restores during runtime proof; resources: [...] for runtime/test harness resources such as DCC cache, host trace files, host stat deltas, allocated devices, external services, or env-injected caches; fixtures: [...] for setup/cleanup state such as E-UNION overlay, seeded prefix state, service topology, or filesystem templates. Examples: ctest: bead:dar-q95.6; runs: host; proves: source-red; build-target: darling_ec_tls_regress. Guest runtime: ctest: bead:dar-xnu.fd_guard; runs: guest; proves: runtime-red; artifacts: [xnu-kernel]. Guest with cache/oracle: ctest: bead:dar-dyld.dcc; runs: guest; proves: runtime-red; artifacts: [dyld-runtime]; resources: [dcc6-smoke-cache, dyld-trace-oracle]. E-UNION: ctest: bead:dar-eunion.copyup; runs: guest; proves: runtime-red; artifacts: [xnu-kernel]; fixtures: [eunion-overlay]. Acceptance should implement artifacts/resources/fixtures aliases and should not introduce needs as either primary syntax or compatibility shorthand for new manifests.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Progress: implemented the compact target DSL normalization layer for ctest/runs/red-proof/build-target/artifacts/resources/fixtures and explicitly rejects needs in new manifests. Migrated current patch manifests and metadata contract entries from env to runs, converted representative CTest entries to ctest/build-target, moved DCC reusable cache into resource-profiles, and E-UNION prefix setup into fixture-profiles. Added manifest contract coverage for aliases, resource/fixture expansion, runs guest envelope expansion, and needs rejection. Also updated test wrappers to set PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 to avoid tracked pycache churn. Verified py_compile, focused contracts, metadata contract, patch check homebrew/perf/arch, homebrew export --check, red-audit homebrew/perf/arch, LibreSSL CTest --prove-red, and cleanup audit. Remaining acceptance: LibreSSL test placement in tests/CMakeLists.txt/add_test style still needs a separate careful CMake change because current Darling LibreSSL top-level does not add_subdirectory(tests), so moving it naively would pull/alter the whole upstream tests subtree.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Completed: compact manifest axes are implemented and documented; needs is rejected; representative manifests migrated to ctest/runs/red-proof/build-target/artifacts/resources/fixtures; LibreSSL regression now lives under libressl/tests with registration in tests/CMakeLists.txt via add_darling_executable/add_test; verified targeted LibreSSL --prove-red, homebrew patch check/export-check, and homebrew red-audit.

title: Inline fast-path for hot/file syscalls (remove RPC round-trip from brew hot path; Wine-model)
description:
PROBLEM: brew install under Darling is dominated by per-syscall RPC round-trips, not computation. Live evidence (brew reinstall wget, 2026-06-26): 16 of ~30 guest threads sit in __skb_wait_for_more_packets (blocked in recvmsg awaiting an RPC reply); both darlingserver and guest are low-CPU; wall-clock is RPC-round-trip-latency x syscall-count. A single dependency ./configure (thousands of stat/open/read for path resolution + a fresh sandboxed ruby per formula step) takes many minutes; full wget reinstall did not finish.

ROOT (architectural): Darling emulates the XNU kernel OUT-OF-PROCESS (darlingserver, Mach+BSD duct-tape). Guest mach/bsd traps are caught in libsystem_kernel and marshaled to darlingserver over a PER-THREAD AF_UNIX socket. So EVERY syscall (fork/wait4/stat/open/mmap/signal) is a guest->server->guest round-trip + serialization + process switch. This is also why the whole dar-gwn RPC-protocol bug class exists (lost reply -> recvmsg deadlock; grant-vs-interrupt races).

CONTRAST WITH WINE (the target): Wine implements Win32 as userspace libraries (ntdll/kernel32) loaded IN-PROCESS; a "syscall" is a function call + at most one native Linux syscall, inline, no socket. wineserver is consulted ONLY for genuinely cross-process state (kernel object handles, window messages, registry, cross-process sync) -- NOT for read/write/stat. So the hot path never leaves the process.

PROPOSAL (deliver Wine-class latency): move HOT, non-cross-process syscalls to an INLINE FAST-PATH executed in the guest process (translate directly to the native Linux call), and keep darlingserver ONLY for truly cross-process semantics (Mach ports/IPC, task/thread mgmt, fork/exec/wait reparenting, the unified signal table, task_for_pid, exception ports). Candidate inline set: file read/write/lseek/stat/fstat/access/readlink, mmap/munmap of files, gettimeofday/clock_gettime, getpid/getuid family -- exactly the calls a ./configure storm hammers.

OPTIONS / DESIGN AXES (for the epic to evaluate, not yet decided):
 1. Inline fast-path in libsystem_kernel for the pure/file subset (highest ROI; the Wine model). Risk: must guarantee the chosen calls truly need no global kernel state.
 2. Shared-memory ring buffer guest<->server (vDSO-like) to amortize sendmsg per syscall. Cheaper to build, does not remove the round-trip in principle.
 3. In-kernel (eBPF/seccomp) servicing of frequent traps. Fragile, needs privilege.
 4. KVM micro-VM hosting macOS userland. Different project.

ACCEPTANCE: a brew reinstall of a small formula (e.g. wget) completes, and the per-syscall RPC count on the hot path drops by >1 order of magnitude (measure via the __skb_wait_for_more_packets dominance + server RPC counters before/after).

SCOPE: research/epic. NOT a single-bug fix. Children would be: (a) measure+rank the hot syscalls under brew (instrument darlingserver RPC counts per opcode), (b) prototype inline fast-path for the read-only file subset, (c) prove correctness vs the cross-process invariants, (d) extend. Reference: this session's analysis; memory homebrew-run-harness, brew-sigchld-segv-rootcause. Constraint: nothing pushed without explicit approval.

title: Overlay upper layer (DPREFIX) silently shadows lower-layer dylib deploys: fixes get tested against the OLD binary

title: Darling regression test infrastructure (west test + add_compat_test)
description:
Build a convenient, maintainable regression test framework for Darling and its submodules: run a fast changed-only slice on PRs to a submodule, and a full differential suite (Darling vs real macOS) overall.

Design (see darling-workspace/docs/test-infra.md): CTest as the backend (isomorphic to gVisor-on-Bazel / Wine winetest sitting on top of the build system), a thin add_compat_test() generator that mints one ctest entry per environment (host/darling/macos) with labels (env:/diag:/bead:/submod:/macos:<ver>), and a 'west test' orchestrator beside dw/patch/pr (selectors --changed/--bead/--diag/--label, diagnosis tiers via darling-debug-runner, bundle GC).

Compatibility stance: a convenience SUPERSET of upstream darling-testsuite, seam-compatible (same case format/MIT-0/availability.h, same testcase/+resource/ install layout) so cases port upstream unchanged; ergonomics (generator, west test) live only in our tree.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
2026-07-08 refresh after patch/test audit and upstream darling-testsuite check (upstream HEAD ce56358, 2026-07-05). Current direction remains correct: CTest is the backend; our layer should be a thin compatibility/orchestration layer, not a second framework. Upstream currently uses CMake 3.29, C/ObjC/ASM, include(CTest), add_test(), WILL_FAIL for negative cases, install layout darling-testsuite/{testcase,resource,manual}, darling-testsuite-lib for assertions/resources/XML, darling-directsyscall for kernel syscall tests, and GitHub macos-26 build/install artifact.

Patch audit snapshot: 97 patch files across arch/homebrew/perf; obvious committed tests/gates are uneven and format-diverse (shell gates, raw C/C++ unit tests, CMake/CTest targets, markdown acceptance docs, E-UNION experiment runner). Approximate classification: arch 15 patches = 8 shell gates, 2 CTest/CMake-ish, 5 no obvious test; homebrew 75 = 12 shell gates, 5 CTest/CMake-ish, 2 raw source tests, 56 no obvious test; perf 7 = 2 shell gates, 2 CTest/CMake-ish, 3 no obvious test. This reinforces dar-r7z7 and means test infra must include migration tooling, not only a runner.

Architecture update for this epic: make testkit consume/track upstream darling-testsuite format directly where possible. Keep upstream-testcase source conventions and install layout; add our ergonomics only as metadata and orchestration: env labels (host/darling/macos), diag labels/runner, bead/submodule labels, fuzz/stress labels, changed-only selection, bundle GC, and red-test provenance. Avoid inventing another assertion library unless a case is host-only and cannot use upstream helpers.

Framework shape to implement next:
- Registry: every test has stable name, owner Bead, covered patch/profile, submodule labels, env labels, diag tier, timeout, resource policy, and red-test proof status.
- Environments: host tests run bare; Darling guest tests run guarded by default through darling-debug-runner; macOS oracle uses upstream install artifact/transport and version labels.
- Kinds: unit/contract tests, guest/runtime tests, external package/repro gates, fuzz/stress tests. All should still surface through CTest labels so west test remains one entrypoint.
- Fuzzing: add a fuzz label/tier and bounded runner contract (seed corpus, max time, artifact bundle, replay command). Long fuzz jobs should be nightly/manual; minimized repros become normal committed tests.
- Migration: port existing shell gates and one-off tests into add_compat_test/upstream-compatible CMake entries, starting with dar-r7z7 gaps and high-risk runtime correctness patches.
- Tooling: west test should grow discovery from manifest/submodules, --profile/--patch selectors, --red-test-audit, and better list output showing missing metadata.

Acceptance for the epic should now include: upstream-compatible test source layout; west test can list/run by bead, profile, patch, submodule, env, diag, fuzz/stress; guest hangs are bounded and leave reproducible bundles; every non-doc patch either maps to a committed red test or an explicit exception.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 revised execution contract after CTest runtime-profile implementation: migrate suitable metadata guest-c-fixture cases into colocated CTest entries; each guest CTest case must declare a runtime-profile or typed provider, materialize full source closure in disposable worktrees, build/deploy minimal artifacts under the prefix lock, run exact guest upload/compile/run ORACLE_RC=0 verdicts, restore prefix, and clean scratch. Keep trace/E-UNION/dserverdbg/multi-process cases as explicit metadata escape hatches until a typed provider exists. Every instability requires a reproducible Bead update, committed RED->GREEN regression, and tool fix. Completion requires applicable contracts, homebrew quality check/RED audit, real runtime runs of every registered guest CTest case, clean processes/mounts/scratch/bytecode, and closure of sp5.3/sp5.15. Local commits only; no pushes.

title: PoC: west test orchestrator + add_compat_test generator + bundle GC
description:
DONE (local, unpushed). Landed in darling-workspace:
- testkit/cmake/AddCompatTest.cmake — generator: one ctest entry per ENV (host/darling/macos), labels env:/diag:/bead:/submod:/macos:<ver>, DIAG tiers (bare/guarded/forensic), TIMEOUT param, EXTRA_SOURCES/INCLUDES/DEFINES/LIBS/WORKDIR to link real production code, MIN/MAX_VERSION -> OSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET + macos:<ver> label, INSTALL/RESOURCES -> upstream-compatible testcase/+resource/ layout.
- west_commands/test.py — 'west test' selectors --changed/--bead/--diag/--env/--label, executor wiring, --gc/--keep-last/--max-bundle-mb/--dry-run, registered in west-commands.yml.
- docs/test-infra.md — full RFC with industry comparison (gVisor/Wine/CTest) and macOS-version model.
- dar-gwn.5 wired as a REAL regression (compiles production src/startup/mldr/glibc_fork_reset.c + harness + tls_mod), RED/GREEN proven, passes via 'west test --bead dar-gwn.5'.
- GC freed 6.5G (7.4G->934M) from ~/work/darling-debug.

title: Productize west test against upstream-compatible darling-testsuite layout
description:
Turn the current testkit/west test PoC into the regular Darling regression framework while staying compatible with upstream darling-testsuite.

Current facts from 2026-07-08 audit:
- Upstream darling-testsuite HEAD ce56358 (2026-07-05) uses CMake/CTest, add_test(), WILL_FAIL, install layout darling-testsuite/{testcase,resource,manual}, darling-testsuite-lib, darling-directsyscall, and macos-26 build/install artifact CI.
- Our patch profiles contain 97 patch files with uneven test formats: shell gates, raw C/C++ tests, CMake-ish tests, markdown acceptance docs, and E-UNION experiment runner.
- Existing testkit/add_compat_test is directionally right but still a PoC and only registers one real host regression.

Design target:
1. CTest remains the backend and upstream source/install layout remains the compatibility seam.
2. west test is the orchestrator for selectors and diagnosis, not a replacement test framework.
3. Every test records stable metadata: bead, patch/profile, submodule, env, diag tier, timeout, resource policy, red-test provenance.
4. Supported kinds: host unit/contract, Darling guest/runtime, macOS oracle, external package/repro gates, fuzz/stress.
5. Fuzz tests run through bounded labelled jobs with seed corpus, max time, bundle artifacts, and replay command; minimized failures become normal committed regressions.
6. Guest tests default to guarded darling-debug-runner execution; host tests default bare; forensic capture is opt-in.
7. west test grows --profile/--patch selectors, manifest/submodule discovery, red-test audit/list output, and JUnit/artifact paths suitable for CI.

Acceptance:
- west test can list and run by bead, profile, patch, submodule, env, diag, and fuzz/stress label.
- At least one existing shell gate, one raw C/C++ contract test, one guest/runtime repro, and one E-UNION harness slice are migrated into upstream-compatible CTest registration.
- dar-r7z7 missing-test inventory can be queried from the registry.
- A hung guest test is killed and leaves a reproducible bundle with the command, env, logs, timeout reason, and cleanup result.
- Documentation in docs/test-infra.md matches the implemented command behavior.
close_reason: acceptance implemented and verified

title: Patch-local red-test workflow: fixes and regressions travel together
description:
Implement the local-fork testing approach where every non-documentation fix patch carries its minimal red-test proof in the same source repo and, by default, in the same patch-file/profile entry as the fix. Upstream darling-testsuite remains the portable testcase format/oracle target, not a required separate repository for every local red test.

Problem:
- The current patch profiles contain many fixes without committed red tests, and existing proofs are scattered across shell gates, raw C/C++ tests, CMake targets, markdown acceptance notes, and experiments.
- Requiring a separate darling-testsuite patch/repo change for every local fix creates friction and makes local patchsets less self-contained.
- We still want tests to be upstreamable later, so the local layout must not paint us into a corner.

Policy to implement:
1. Default: fix and minimal red test are colocated in the same repository and same patch profile entry.
2. Prefer same patch file when the touched repository can host the test naturally.
3. Cross-repo bugs may use a small adjacent test patch in the same profile, but not a mandatory separate upstream testsuite repo change.
4. Official darling-testsuite is used as compatibility style and future upstream destination: CMake/CTest semantics, portable testcase sources where possible, install/resource layout where useful.
5. Workspace-only orchestration stays in west/debug-runner/metadata; source repos should not learn Beads internals.

Canonical locations:
- xnu/libsystem_kernel fixes: xnu/darling/src/libsystem_kernel/tests/...
- darlingserver fixes: darlingserver/tests/...
- libpthread fixes: libpthread/tests/...
- top-level Darling runtime/build fixes: darling/tests/regression/... or a focused local tests dir matching existing conventions.
- E-UNION/vchroot fixes: xnu/vchroot or xnu/darling tests where portable; experiment-only harness must be promoted or marked as an exception.

Patch metadata target:
- patches.yml entries can declare tests with repo, name, command or ctest label, env, diag tier, red/proof status, and exception rationale when absent.
- west patch check/export should preserve and validate this metadata.
- west test should be able to answer: which tests prove this patch, this Bead, this profile, or this changed submodule?

Definition of done:
- The workflow is documented in docs/test-infra.md and source/AGENTS.md.
- patches.yml schema supports patch-local test metadata and explicit exceptions.
- west patch check reports non-doc patches with no test metadata/exception.
- west test supports --patch, --profile, --red-only/--red-audit style selectors using that metadata.
- At least four representative patches are migrated: one xnu/libsystem_kernel fix, one darlingserver fix, one top-level darling fix, and one cross-repo/runtime gate.
- A test can be run before/after fix to prove RED->GREEN and the proof command is recorded in metadata or Bead comments.
- No upstream push or separate upstream testsuite patch is required for local completion.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Add Darling prefix resource provisioning to west test

Implement first-class Darling prefix resources for patch-local guest tests.

Contract:
- Guest test metadata declares `requires: [darling-prefix]`, not raw `requires-env: [DPREFIX]`.
- `west test` supplies DPREFIX from `--prefix`, `--prefix existing:/path`, `--prefix-profile homebrew`, or an already exported DPREFIX.

Scope intentionally excludes automatic prefix creation/deploy; that needs a separate lifecycle contract.

Migrate the libpthread psynch kernel return guest regression from ad hoc DPREFIX/darling-shell polling to a shared guest verdict helper.

title: Run patch-local tests against materialized profile trees
description:
Current `west test --profile ...` runs selected metadata against whatever subrepo branches are currently checked out. That is OK for small host/static scripts when the checkout already contains their test files, but it is wrong for build gates that require a consistent integration stack.

Repro observed while adding a build gate for `libunwind/static-no-jump-tables-for-dyld.patch`: `west test --profile arch --patch libunwind/static-no-jump-tables-for-dyld.patch` invoked `west darling-build --targets src/external/dyld/dyld`, but current xnu/darlingserver branches were not the materialized arch stack. The build failed on unrelated `_dserver_rpc_hooks_is_disconnect_status` i386 link symbols instead of proving the libunwind jump-table fix.

Desired behavior:
- Patch-local tests can declare `requires-profile: arch` or equivalent.
- `west test` should either verify that the current checkout is the required integration profile or run in a temporary/materialized profile worktree.
- Build gates should not run against arbitrary mixed fix branches.
- Failure message should point to profile materialization, not surface unrelated linker noise as the test result.

Acceptance:
- The dyld/libunwind build gate can be run reproducibly against the arch integration profile.
- Temporary profile worktrees are cleaned up.
- `west test --list` remains lightweight.
close_reason: Implemented west test --materialize-profile: generates missing integration/<profile> branches from patch metadata, switches profile modules temporarily, runs the selected test, and restores original checkouts. Verified with the arch libunwind dyld build gate.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Partial mitigation landed locally: west test now allows requires-profile tests when every module in that profile is already checked out on integration/<profile>, and otherwise fails before launching the build with an explicit materialization message. The temporary/materialized profile worktree runner is still required for reproducible execution without mutating the developer checkout.

Verified with libunwind/static-no-jump-tables-for-dyld.patch: dyld build gate ran successfully under --materialize-profile and restored darling/libunwind/xnu/darlingserver checkouts.

Because this build target requires a consistent arch integration stack, metadata must mark `requires-profile: arch` and execution must be blocked in arbitrary mixed checkouts until materialized-profile test execution exists.
