title: Review XNU compile-only runtime-sensitive patch tests for guest coverage
description:
2026-07-10 test-quality audit found two XNU runtime-sensitive patches that are currently covered only by compile/build-style contracts: xnu/fork-postfork-child.patch and xnu/sigexc-debug-flood.patch. They may be defensible as narrow compile contracts, but that needs explicit review rather than accidental acceptance.

Acceptance:
- For xnu/fork-postfork-child.patch, decide whether a real guest fork/postfork behavior gate is possible and stable. If yes, add guest runtime coverage with RED/GREEN proof; if no, document why compile evidence is the correct tier and what broader fork lifecycle task owns runtime validation.
- For xnu/sigexc-debug-flood.patch, decide whether runtime log-flood/no-debug behavior can be observed in a stable guest test. If yes, add guest coverage; if no, document why object-symbol/build evidence is sufficient for this compile-time debug-gating patch.
- Ensure any added RED proof matches the intended failure reason and does not rely on source text matching.
- Update audits/test-quality-2026-07-10.md or successor audit with the decision.
close_reason: Reviewed both runtime-sensitive compile-only XNU patches. fork-postfork-child remains compile-tier because the direct invariant is exact sys_fork child-path ordering; broader deployed fork lifecycle validation belongs to dar-q95.29.9/dar-r7fc.2.1. sigexc-debug-flood remains object-symbol compile-tier because the direct regression is the default __simple_kprintf dependency; deployed log/perf runtime coverage is tracked separately by dar-sigexc-hotpath-kprintf-runtime-gate-9mpv. Metadata notes and test-quality audit updated.

Also support explicit exception entries with rationale for doc-only patches or cases where a deterministic red test is impossible.

Acceptance:
- Schema is documented in docs/test-infra.md with examples.
- Existing patches.yml parser tolerates and preserves the new fields.
- At least one real patch entry uses the schema.
- Exceptions are machine-readable enough for west patch check to distinguish them from missing tests.

title: Add shared patch-local test runners/helpers
description:
Context: after reading the scripts referenced by patch-local test metadata, the metadata shape is better than raw command strings, but the scripts themselves still duplicate runner logic.

Findings:
- Static contract gates repeatedly define fail(), repo-root discovery, grep-required/grep-forbidden assertions, and ordering checks.
- Guest runtime tests repeatedly copy C fixtures into DPREFIX/tmp, launch darling shell, poll for an ORACLE_RC verdict, kill/wait the shell, and print filtered runner logs.
- A0 gate has its own mature cleanup/snapshot logic that should inform the common guest runner contract.
- `west patch check` must not treat script existence in the current checkout as profile invalid: profile metadata can refer to tests added by another patch in the same stack, and subrepos may currently be on unrelated fix branches. Script existence belongs in `west test` at execution time unless/until we validate against a materialized integration tree.

Goal:
- Add small shared shell helpers for static contract gates and guest verdict tests.
- Keep per-test scripts thin: declare fixture, expected verdict marker, timeout, env requirements, and assertions.
- Preserve command/script overrides only for corner cases such as long bespoke A0 gates.
- Validate metadata shape and repo references in `west patch check`; validate actual scripts in `west test` when running/listing against the current checkout.

Acceptance:
- At least one static contract gate and one DPREFIX guest test migrated to shared helpers.
- `west patch check` stays stable across arbitrary fix-branch checkouts and validates repo/schema without false script-existence invalids.
- `west test` still lists/runs existing structured metadata and fails clearly if a selected script is absent or DPREFIX is missing.
- Docs and AGENTS mention that new tests should prefer shared helpers before ad hoc shell.
design:
Patch-local regression tests have two distinct contracts:

1. GREEN regression run (default): `west test --profile/--patch` runs against the current checkout and must pass. This is what developers and CI should use as the normal latest-state guard.
2. RED proof run (explicit): `west test --profile/--patch --prove-red` verifies that a test is not blind and really distinguishes the old/bad behavior from the fixed behavior.

Metadata semantics:
- `red: true` means "this is intended to be a RED->GREEN regression proof", not "this should fail on latest".
- `red-proof: {mode: self}` means the test script/binary carries its own bad-path oracle and can prove RED without changing the checkout. Example: run old algorithm/model and require failure, then run fixed algorithm/model and require success.
- `red-proof: {mode: source-base}` is the long-term normal mode for tests added by a fix patch: the runner should take the current test asset and run it against a temporary worktree at the patch source-base, with explicit source-root/fixture redirection. This must be implemented by shared runners, not guessed from arbitrary shell scripts.

Resource model direction:
- Env vars such as DPREFIX are compatibility plumbing. Metadata should evolve toward resource requirements (`requires: [darling-prefix]`) and west should provision or select resources (`--prefix`, `--prefix-profile`, eventually `--prefix auto`).

Implementation order:
1. Implement `--prove-red` for `mode: self` so existing self-discriminating tests are first-class.
2. Validate `red-proof` metadata in `west patch check`.
3. Use shared static/guest runners to enable `mode: source-base` without each script hand-rolling checkout/prefix behavior.
4. Migrate ordinary regression tests from notes/ad hoc shell to this model.
acceptance_criteria:
`west test --profile ...` expects GREEN on the current checkout.
`west test --profile ... --prove-red` exists and runs `red-proof: {mode: self}` tests.
`west patch check` validates `red-proof` metadata.
At least two existing self-discriminating tests are annotated and runnable through `--prove-red`.
Docs state clearly that `red: true` does not mean fail-on-latest.
Follow-up remains for source-base/guest-prefix resource provisioning.
close_reason: fixed

title: Preserve patches.yml formatting in west patch export
description:
`west patch export` rewrites entire patches.yml files through PyYAML. On the homebrew profile this converts existing block-scalar publication-blocker text into quoted strings with doubled apostrophes and blank-line churn, even when the semantic update is only source-commit/sha256sum for one patch.

Impact:
- Patchset commits get noisy and harder to review.
- Agents may accidentally commit serialization churn while only trying to refresh one patch.

Desired behavior:
- Preserve human-authored scalar style and unrelated entries when exporting.
- Either perform targeted in-place updates for source-commit/sha256sum or use a YAML library/round-trip approach that preserves style.
- Add an export check that reports unrelated metadata churn before commit.

Acceptance:
- Re-exporting a profile with one changed source commit produces a diff limited to the changed patch file and that patch entry metadata.
- Existing block scalar publication-blocker text remains block scalar after export.
- Document the no-churn expectation in AGENTS/docs.
close_reason: fixed

title: Implement source-base RED proof runner
description:
Implement explicit `west test --prove-red` support for `red-proof: {mode: source-base}`.

Contract:
- Normal `west test` remains GREEN-on-current.
- `--prove-red` takes the current test asset, creates a temporary git worktree at the patch bad revision (`source-base`, or `source-commit^` when no explicit base exists), points a declared source-root env var at that worktree, expects the test to fail there, then expects the same test to pass on the current checkout.
- This must require explicit metadata such as `source-env: DSERVER_SRC_ROOT`; no implicit checkout mutation or arbitrary shell guessing.

Acceptance:
- `west test --profile arch --patch darlingserver/standard-signal-coalescing.patch --prove-red` shows RED on bad tree and GREEN on current.
- Temporary worktrees are removed after the run.
- `west patch check` validates source-base proof metadata.
close_reason: fixed

title: west test: prune stale runtime proof scratch directories
description: During the 2026-07-10 runtime-red audit a stale /tmp/west-red-proof-runtime-* directory was found after contract/failure-path testing. It was small and manually removed, but west doctor/cleanup should explicitly discover and optionally prune stale west-red-proof-runtime-* and west-green-proof-runtime-* scratch dirs, separately from source worktrees/prefixes, and the cleanup contract should assert these runtime scratch patterns too. Acceptance: west doctor or a focused west test cleanup command reports stale runtime proof scratch dirs with age/size, can prune them safely, and tests cover both RED and GREEN runtime scratch patterns without racing active west test processes.

Source-only entries are explicit scoped exceptions; behavioral entries report runtime, compile, or host coverage.

title: Unify guest test execution and lifecycle guarantees
description: Create one reusable guest C execution path for metadata and CTest with fixed exact verdict matching, unique files, guaranteed guest cleanup, bounded process groups, diagnostics, prefix locking/shutdown/mount cleanup. Make timeout-seconds apply to all runners and remove misleading local macOS execution fallback. Add RED->GREEN contracts.
close_reason: Completed: metadata guest-C and CTest guest-C share darling-guest-shell transport plus resolved prefix/launcher semantics; both use unique guest files, exact markers, staged ORACLE_RC, bounded deadlines, and cleanup. west test owns CTest prefix lock/shutdown/mount cleanup. Verified runtime RED, metadata, guest command, direct guest-C, CTest lifecycle, CMake, worktree cleanup, and full arch/homebrew/perf strict check + RED audit + patch verify. Final cleanup found no temporary worktrees, gc.log, runtime/Git processes, or bytecode.

title: Decompose west test orchestration and complete CTest-first migration

Remove tracked Python bytecode and enforce generated artifact hygiene. Close only after full regression and audit.

title: west test leaks profile and green source worktrees after successful prove-red
description: Repro 2026-07-10: run west test --profile homebrew --prove-red --env host. It exits 0 but leaves /tmp/west-profile-homebrew-* and /tmp/west-green-proof-source-*; Git lists them as detached worktrees. Fix must remove all temporary directories and Git worktrees on success and prove it with a behavioral regression test.
close_reason: Investigated: normal completion is clean. The apparent leak came from an output-limited foreground transport terminating a noisy prove-red run before Python finally handlers ran. Added checked worktree-removal diagnostics, a focused worktree cleanup contract, and AGENTS guidance for nohup setsid log/PID runs; controlled full and targeted RED runs left no temporary worktrees or processes.

title: patch verify leaves Git auto-gc residue in temporary worktrees
description: Repro 2026-07-10: run west patch verify for arch, homebrew, perf. Verify succeeds, but Git emits gc.log and too-many-unreachable-loose-objects warnings under Darling nested module worktree metadata. Investigate temporary verify worktree lifecycle and disable or isolate auto-gc for disposable apply/verify worktrees; add a regression check that verify leaves no temporary worktree or Git auto-gc residue.
close_reason: Root cause fixed: patch applicability used git am in disposable worktrees without disabling modern maintenance. The dedicated temporary-patch operation now disables gc.auto and maintenance.auto only for that apply; behavioral contract applies a real patch in an auto-gc-prone repo. Full arch, homebrew, perf verify passed with no gc.log warnings, temporary worktrees, or live Git processes.

title: Disable Git auto-gc during west patch apply integration
description: Observed 2026-07-11 while running west patch apply --profile arch: every controlled git am in the generated integration branches may trigger background auto-gc and print noisy warnings. This is separate from closed dar-yefz, which fixed temporary west test materialization only. Repro: run west patch apply --profile arch after adding darling/ci-host-regression-tests.patch; output reports Auto packing the repository in background for xnu and darlingserver modules. Fix west_commands/patch.py integration apply path to invoke git am with auto-gc disabled, preserve explicit/manual housekeeping, and add a focused behavioural contract that proves the command carries the config without weakening patch applicability or cleanup. Acceptance: normal patch apply produces no auto-gc noise/residue, generated lock/profile state remains correct, and a follow-up apply/status is clean.
close_reason: Implemented fecab6f: all west patch-apply git am calls explicitly disable auto-gc and auto-maintenance. Behavioural fixture contract passes; strict arch patch status has no missing patches; post-fix workspace has no gc.log residue.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented controlled patch-application Git options in west_commands/patch.py: both temporary verification and generated integration git am now use gc.auto=0 and maintenance.auto=false. Added a behavioural contract that invokes DarlingPatch._apply on a real fixture repository under Trace2, proving the integration path applies the patch, leaves no gc.log, and launches no auto-maintenance. The pre-fix real arch apply produced Auto packing and gc.log warnings; the post-fix repeat applied all 17 patches with no Auto packing or maintenance output. Existing gc.log files produced by the earlier broken run are intentionally not silently deleted: historical object cleanup remains explicit.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Closure evidence 2026-07-11: tests/run-west-patch-verify-contract.sh passed (real fixture patch application under Trace2, no gc.log or maintenance run); west patch status --profile arch --strict reported 14 applied, 0 missing; a workspace gc.log scan returned none. Historic gc.log files from the pre-fix run were not deleted implicitly.

title: Run Darling guest tests without SUID (container-friendly execution)
description:
BLOCKER for guest-environment CI. The installed launcher must be setuid root (it needs mount + PID/mount-namespace creation); we hit this live — ~/work/darling-build/.../darling refused to run ('not setuid root, which is mandatory'). The prefix launcher IS setuid (works locally), but a CI docker container won't have that, and CuriousTommy already flagged SUID as a container concern.

Investigate: can darlingserver/launcher create the needed namespaces via user namespaces / CAP_SYS_ADMIN in unprivileged containers, rootless approaches, or a --privileged Jenkins agent as a stopgap. Outcome: a documented way to run 'darling shell' in CI without host setuid. Decouples Tier 1/2 CI from the SUID dependency.
design: RESOLVED (investigation): see docs/suid-investigation.md. The 'mandatory setuid' gate is just geteuid()!=0 in darling.c:64; the real need is EFFECTIVE ROOT for one-time prefix bring-up (overlayfs/mount/PID-ns), then privileges are dropped before guest code runs. Setuid is only one way to reach euid==0. VERIFIED on this host (kernel 6.8): (1) setuid launcher -> 'darling shell echo' rc=0; (2) NON-setuid build-tree launcher run via sudo (euid==0) -> guest exec rc=0. So CI does NOT need host setuid -- it needs a root/privileged container. Unprivileged-userns rootless is blocked by Ubuntu 24.04 AppArmor (unprivileged_userns profile: 'audit deny capability'), NOT the kernel; finishing it = Darling-core change (darling.c:831 commented uid_map path), out of harness scope. DECISION: run env=darling CI in a --privileged root container (docker { args '--privileged -u 0:0' }); existing ci/Jenkinsfile only builds pkgs, never runs darling shell, so guest stage is net-new. No add_compat_test change needed (DARLING_SHELL launch already works). Bonus: prefix at ~/.darling is currently healthy (de-risks sp5.3).
close_reason: Investigation complete; deliverable met: docs/suid-investigation.md documents how to run darling shell in CI without host setuid (privileged root container; effective-root, not setuid-bit, is the real requirement). Both setuid and non-setuid-as-root paths verified live. Rootless-userns deferred to Darling-core (out of harness scope).

title: Reject incompatible runtime source profiles before materialization
description: west test accepts a patch profile and prefix profile whose source patch series cannot coexist, then creates a disposable runtime source forest and fails during git am on submodule conflicts. Repro 2026-07-11: --profile arch plus --prefix-profile homebrew for darling/shellspawn-exit-status.patch attempted homebrew rootless patches in an arch source forest and conflicted in xnu/darlingserver. Resolve profile compatibility before creating scratch or shutting down/deploying a prefix; error must name selected patch profile, runtime/prefix profile, conflicting source profile or patch, and remediation. Add a behavioural contract. Do not classify this as a runtime RED failure.
close_reason: Runtime profile applicability now has a cached pre-materialization preflight with behavioral contract coverage.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Root-cause refinement: arch inherits perf/homebrew, and runtime source materialization reconstructs that full profile stack from manifest revisions. During reconstruction, homebrew/darling/rootless-runtime-components.patch no longer applies to the xnu/darlingserver gitlinks after rootless-no-mount-launch was applied, yielding a 3-way submodule conflict. The runner must validate the full resolved profile stack against its declared source bases/gitles before source worktree creation; do not treat failed git am as a guest RED outcome or recover by silently choosing arbitrary submodule revisions.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Immediate source-series defect fixed: rootless-runtime-components was rebased onto rootless-no-mount-launch and `west patch verify --profile homebrew` now passes 85/85. Keep this task open for the runner-level preflight that reports an invalid future profile stack before scratch/prefix side effects; current resolution removes the reproduced conflict.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 resolved in the workspace: west test now runs a cached applicability preflight for every layer of the selected runtime base-profile stack before any runtime source forest, build, prefix shutdown, deploy, or RED/GREEN execution. It delegates patch integrity/applicability to west patch verify, names the failing layer and remediation, and classifies it explicitly as non-runtime failure. Contracts prove ordered homebrew->arch verification, cache behavior, exact failure diagnostics, and no RED/GREEN scratch allocation on preflight failure. Verified targeted runner/prefix contracts, testkit contracts, patch quality (homebrew 77 covered/8 explicit exceptions; arch 6 covered/12 explicit exceptions), and profile verification.

title: Rootless runner: cancel bootstrap descendants and enforce timeout
description: Repro 2026-07-11: west test --prefix-profile homebrew --bootstrap-runtime-profile homebrew-prefix-baseline --bootstrap-syscall-trace <dir> exceeded bootstrap-smoke-timeout-seconds=20 while waiting for shellspawn. scripts/west-job.sh cancel correctly ended the west job with rc=143 but five rootless /sbin/launchd descendants survived as PPID 1 (session 1070819, one distinct process group 1070884), all marked by DARLING_PREFIX. Add a process-group/session ownership contract from runtime bootstrap through cancel/timeout, make timeout produce a bounded diagnostic verdict, terminate all owned descendants, restore prefix, remove scratch, and add a reproducible behavioral contract proving no surviving Darling process after cancellation. Do not kill unrelated processes by name.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 reproduced before implementation: rootless guest smoke exceeded 20s; run_bounded killed its direct process group but then communicate() waited forever because an escaped session retained stdout/stderr. The old implementation was proven under an outer 5s guard: a 1s bounded command returned external timeout rc=124 rather than its internal timeout verdict. Rootless descendants carried exact DARLING_PREFIX plus DARLING_ROOTLESS=1 and survived west-job cancel as PPID 1. Fix in progress: bound post-timeout drain and reclaim only env-owned rootless descendants; contracts use a real fork+setsid escaped pipe and real tagged process.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 timeout-path GREEN: full materialized rootless bootstrap reached the shellspawn launch path, then returned the bounded domain verdict after exactly 20s instead of hanging. Runner cleanup found six processes by exact DARLING_PREFIX plus DARLING_ROOTLESS ownership, sent TERM then KILL where needed, and final audit found no rootless env owners, no Darling process, and no prefix mount. Repro: west test --prefix-profile homebrew --bootstrap-runtime-profile homebrew-prefix-baseline --bootstrap-syscall-trace /tmp/west-rootless-lifecycle-202607111230. A second full run reproduced the same bounded cleanup. west-job now has a cooperative SIGINT command-pid path covered by tests/run-west-job-contract.sh, but an actual manual cancel during the live rootless guest phase has not yet been captured because its 20s smoke deadline completed first; keep task open until that direct proof exists.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 post-RED/GREEN cleanup audit: deterministic rootless readiness proof reclaimed all prefix-owned processes and left no mounts, but west runner left stale prefix .darlingserver.sock after both RED and GREEN. west darling-prefix-repair --prefix /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix-homebrew-test --cleanup-mounts removed it with .init.pid absent. Keep this task open: runner cleanup must remove stale server/socket state itself and still needs direct live-cancel proof.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 direct live cancel repro (job /tmp/west-job-rootless-shellspawn-cancel-20260711-final): framework-owned readiness file proved the rootless launcher was live; west-job cancel sent SIGINT then force-killed the command after 5 seconds, job ended rc=143, but rootless darlingserver PID 2465629 and prefix .darlingserver.sock survived. Root cause: the 5s west-job escalation deadline is shorter than West prefix cleanup (shutdown plus rootless TERM/KILL passes), so it kills Python before its finally/resource cleanup completes. Fix must give the cooperative command a declared cleanup grace sufficient for its bounded shutdown and prove direct cancel reclaims process, socket, mount and host-temp file.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 tooling regression during contract verification: west-job launched its supervised command as an asynchronous Bash child, which inherited SIGINT ignored; therefore cooperative cancel could only reach the forced escalation. A first attempted repair also exposed unsafe nested quoting and caused the test trap to remove its state directory while a detached job survived. The contract now needs runner-level SIGINT disposition restoration plus EXIT cleanup of every state directory. One old orphaned contract runner (PID 1134863, exact deleted state path /tmp/tmp.3SbXsIG6k8/cancelled) remains from that failed harness run; it cannot be recovered through state-based west-job cancellation. Reclaim its verified dedicated session/process group, then prove the corrected harness leaves no processes.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 final direct live-cancel proof: job /tmp/west-job-rootless-shellspawn-cancel-20260711-proved-cancel caught the framework-owned marker shellspawn-pending, written by the live rootless shellspawn test hook before socket creation. west-job cooperative cancel completed rc=143. Post-cancel audit found the host-temp marker absent, .darlingserver.sock absent, no process carrying DARLING_PREFIX=/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix-homebrew-test, and west darling-prefix-repair --cleanup-mounts reported no mounted filesystems. Root causes fixed: background Bash inherited ignored SIGINT; setsid outer PID race; premature contract state deletion; pre-run prefix cleanup omitted rootless env-owned descendants; GC omitted Git worktree metadata; runtime resources began too late; and host-temp paths required guest-visible representation for rootless execution.

title: Tooling: prevent detached polling sleep processes
description: Repro 2026-07-11: in this execution transport, a command shaped as rtk bash -c "sleep N; scripts/west-job.sh status ..." can return control before the shell completes, leaving the sleep and its bash parent running after the job is finished. This polluted process audits during rootless lifecycle work. Do not use the pattern; AGENTS now requires short status-only polls. Investigate whether the job helper or transport wrapper can make waiting observable/cancellable, add a reproducible contract where the local tool can own it, and ensure monitoring itself never leaks processes.
close_reason: Replaced detached sleep polling with PID-tied completion waits, bounded cancellation, and a behavioral delayed-job contract; exact old monitor tail was reclaimed and no post-fix monitor process remains after job completion.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 reproduced directly: west-job.sh wait returned while its recorded job PID and command PID were still alive. Root cause was polling through sleep in wait_job (and cancel); this execution transport can detach those monitors. Replaced polling with tail --pid completion waits, kept cancellation bounded with timeout --foreground, and added a real delayed-command contract that fails if wait returns before the command exits. The old detached wait monitor was terminated by exact PID; post-fix contract passed without west-job/tail residue.

title: Tooling: make west-job waiting safe under agent execution transport
description: Observed on 2026-07-11: scripts/west-job.sh wait was invoked for /tmp/west-job-rootless-dyld-main-20260711. The agent execution transport reported the wait cell complete while the state file still said running and the job continued materializing/building. The shell-level no-tail contract passes, so it does not model this controller boundary. Define and implement an explicit agent-safe wait policy: either make wait robust in this transport or detect it and reject it with a clear status-polling instruction. Cover normal shell wait, agent-mode behavior, cancellation, and documentation; status must remain the authoritative observable state.
close_reason: Agent-safe wait contract implemented and verified

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented and verified 2026-07-11. RED contract added an agent-mode wait case: previous wait blocked on a 30-second job instead of rejecting the unsafe controller boundary. GREEN: scripts/west-job.sh now rejects wait only when CODEX_CI=1 with an exact status-polling instruction; ordinary attached shells retain pidwait-based wait. Contract covers normal completion, no-tail operation, agent rejection while status remains running, agent cancellation, and cooperative cancellation. AGENTS now makes status authoritative in agent transport. Verified tests/run-west-job-contract.sh and bash -n.

title: Rootless runner: capture stack frames in bootstrap syscall traces
description: The rootless bootstrap trace proves launchd forks a shellspawn child but system-call-only evidence cannot identify the exact guest/library frame before posix_spawn. Add an explicit opt-in bootstrap stack-trace mode that augments existing strace output with native stack frames, preserves the same bounded execution and cleanup semantics, and is covered by a runner contract plus one real rootless evidence run. Do not enable it by default or change guest transport behavior.
close_reason: Perf stack sampling implemented and verified on rootless bootstrap

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented and runtime-verified 2026-07-11. Initial strace -k prototype was rejected because it changed the 20s bootstrap boundary; full syscall interception plus unwinding is not a valid diagnostic. Final design is a separate --bootstrap-stack-sample DIR mode using perf record --all-user --call-graph fp, mutually exclusive with --bootstrap-syscall-trace. It preserves the normal bootstrap boundary, writes bootstrap.perf.data and rendered bootstrap.perf.txt, and uses the same fresh DSERVER_TEST_TRACE_FILE lifecycle as syscall mode. RED->GREEN lifecycle contract covers command construction, rendering, timeout evidence, and stale server-trace clearing. Fresh rootless run /tmp/west-rootless-perf-stack-fresh-rpc-20260711 reached dyld main-entry-ready, captured launchd and shellspawn mappings, and cleaned all owned processes/mounts. The resulting mapping localized the product fault to the launcher client socket address rather than shellspawn startup.

title: West test: clarify patch profile selection
description: west test --patch with --prefix-profile fails with the ambiguous error '--patch requires --profile'. Make the CLI explain that --prefix-profile only selects a prefix and that --profile selects patch metadata; add a contract test and close once verified.
close_reason: CLI now distinguishes metadata --profile from --prefix-profile and the loader contract covers it.

title: West runtime RED: hold prefix resource context through invocation
description: _run_guest_runtime_deploy_proof enters _resource_context only to obtain env, then runs the RED invocation after the context has closed. This can release the prefix lock and run cleanup before the runtime oracle. Keep the context open across _run_invocation_captured, add a behavioral contract, and close only after the actual runtime invocation exits.
close_reason: RED invocation now remains inside the prefix resource context; runtime contract proves it.

title: Repair/stabilize a Darling prefix for guest test runs
description: Guest (env=darling) tests cannot run today: a bare 'darling shell echo' on ~/work/darling-prefix hung indefinitely (darlingserver stuck Sl 12+ min) during the test-infra session. Need a known-good, reproducible prefix that runs 'darling shell' reliably, plus a documented reset/rebuild procedure (and DARLING_SHELL wiring for 'west test --env darling'). Prereq for landing any guest test case.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Observed on 2026-07-08 while validating xnu/getattrlist-shared-packer.patch with --prefix-profile homebrew: basic darling shell echo works, but guest clang is unstable. Command: timeout --kill-after=5 20 env DPREFIX=/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix-homebrew-test /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix/bin/darling shell /bin/bash --login -c "which clang; clang --version | head -2" prints /usr/bin/clang and then hangs until host timeout kills it with SIGKILL. This blocks guest C tests that compile inside the prefix; test framework should either stabilize the prefix/toolchain or support a host-compiled fixture path.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08 update: root-caused guest C verdict instability. /usr/bin/clang wrapper can hang under darling shell; direct CLT clang works when invoked as /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/bin/clang with -isysroot /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk. Updated guest-verdict helpers to copy fixtures through guest shell, use unique /tmp names, default to CLT clang+SDK, and preserve compiler/runtime rc in ORACLE_RC. Also made west test own darling-prefix lifecycle for metadata tests: after requires: [darling-prefix] runs it calls darling shutdown and kills matching leftover darlingserver unless --keep-prefix-running is set. Verified psynch and getattrlist guest contracts green and no homebrew-test prefix processes left.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 update: during focused pushreply guest-runtime-deploy RED proof, west failed before deploy because /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix-homebrew-test/proc was busy even though .init.pid was absent. fuser showed an orphan guest launchd tree holding the mount: host PIDs 444209 launchd plus 444214 opendirectoryd, 444215 memberd, 444216 securityd, 444217 shellspawn, 444218 iokitd, all COMMAND=mldr and parented under PID 1. west darling-prefix-repair --cleanup-mounts only removed stale .darlingserver.sock and then failed to unmount proc; it did not discover/kill the orphan tree. Manual kill of those PIDs followed by west darling-prefix-repair --cleanup-mounts unmounted proc successfully. Tooling gap: prefix repair/cleanup should detect busy prefix mounts via fuser/findmnt or namespace ownership and terminate matching orphan guest processes even when .init.pid is missing, then retry unmount.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 update: fixed the specific prefix-cleanup tooling gap from comment #635. west prefix cleanup now handles the "busy mount but .init.pid absent" case by running fuser -m on mounted prefix targets, filtering holders to Darling runtime executables (mldr/darlingserver/vchroot/darling), terminating only those processes, and retrying umount. Added regression coverage in tests/run-west-prefix-repair-contract.sh for a busy proc mount held by an orphan mldr PID, plus a non-Darling holder guard to prove ordinary processes are not killed. Verified: python3 tests/west_test_contracts/prefix_cleanup_contract.py; tests/run-west-prefix-repair-contract.sh; tests/run-west-test-metadata-contract.sh; west patch check --profile arch; west test --profile arch --red-audit; git diff --check. This closes the orphan mldr busy-mount cleanup subproblem, but the broader stable-prefix task remains open until guest prefix reliability is proven end-to-end.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 repro: new CTest source-driven dar-q95.3 case ran with west test --bead dar-q95.3 --env darling --prefix-profile homebrew. Prefix lifecycle/compile succeeded, but runtime failed in 3s: pselect high fd failed rc=-1 isset=64 errno=14; ORACLE_RC=1. Bundle: /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T120803Z-darling_select_fdset_guest. This indicates the homebrew-test prefix has stale/mismatched deployed libsystem_kernel (or launcher/prefix artifact mismatch), not a CTest registration failure. Need compare metadata guest-c-fixture on the same prefix and make profile-to-runtime deployment reproducible.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 follow-up: metadata runtime-deploy test for xnu/select-pselect-fdset passed rc=0 after rebuilding/deploying libsystem_kernel into homebrew-test prefix. Retried new CTest dar-q95.3 case after propagating DARLING_NOOVERLAYFS=1; it still failed with the same errno=14 (bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T121432Z-darling_select_fdset_guest). Therefore CTest bridge has the expected prefix/copy-mode env, but launcher runtime resolution differs from metadata deploy path or deployment target. Need inspect launcher/prefix library resolution and make deployed artifact selection observable/reproducible.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 further isolation: CTest guest runner now uses separate upload/compile/run guest sessions and west prefix context now forces pre-run shutdown. Both changes were contract-tested. Retried dar-q95.3 CTest case: it still failed errno=14 after confirmed pre-run shutdown (log /tmp/west-select-ctest-fresh-20260710T122320Z.log, bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T122320Z-darling_select_fdset_guest). Excluded stale live server and shared compile/run session as causes. Metadata runtime-deploy remains rc=0. Remaining root cause is effective runtime artifact selection/loading beyond visible prefix path/hash.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 CTest runtime-profile provider now reaches disposable profile source forest and fresh system_kernel build before any prefix deployment. Repro: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 west test --bead dar-q95.3 --env darling --prefix-profile homebrew --ctest-timeout-seconds 120. The first run failed during the fresh CTest homebrew system_kernel build in cctools/ld64, before deployment; prefix libsystem_kernel SHA stayed c81ac5897cf794c6ff6ff0ca3b83567f6a640f6101f92cf74f104f9ac95a1ca4. Investigation found a tooling bug: failure preservation caught Exception but west die raises SystemExit, deleting promised scratch. Fixed with a regression contract; next repro retains source/build scratch to capture the exact compiler failure. Blocker remains open until the isolated profile build and guest CTest gate pass.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 resolved the standalone CTest runtime-profile defect for dar-q95.3. The final repro passed: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 west test --bead dar-q95.3 --env darling --prefix-profile homebrew --ctest-timeout-seconds 120. west selected runtime-profile:homebrew from CTest JSON, materialized darling + darlingserver + xnu in disposable worktrees, built system_kernel, atomically deployed it under the prefix lock, ran darling/select_fdset_guest green in 3.00s, restored the original libsystem_kernel SHA c81ac5897cf794c6ff6ff0ca3b83567f6a640f6101f92cf74f104f9ac95a1ca4, and left no processes or scratch. The provider validates the full system_kernel source closure; GC now prunes failed source/profile scratch through west test --gc. Keep this blocker open for migration of remaining CTest guest runtime cases and profile stamp/preflight work.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 new end-to-end result: composed homebrew+libplatform+darlingserver run built/deployed all three artifacts and ran 12 guest CTests. 11 passed; vchroot_pathnull_guard_guest hung in guest CLT compile after eight preceding successes and timed out. Bundle 20260710T141739Z-darling_vchroot_pathnull_guard_guest has upload+compile only and runtime hashes. A single vchroot run under the identical three-provider deployment passed in 3s, proving a sequence-dependent guest compiler/lifecycle flake. Filed dar-dcsv with both repro commands. Prefix restore, mount cleanup, and scratch removal succeeded; keep this stability blocker open.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 closure evidence: end-to-end CTest guest lifecycle is now stable under the full repeat control: 120 real guest upload/compile/run executions (110 homebrew/libplatform plus 10 perf darlingserver), all green, with explicit runtime deployment, restore, proc unmount, no processes, and no remaining source/build scratch. west test --gc also pruned retained failed scratch safely. This proves the test prefix workflow, including the former mixed-queue timeout path, is reproducible and clean.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 reproducible regression: after the full isolated homebrew-rootless-no-mount CTest completes and cleans up,  is absent. A direct guest probe therefore returns HOST_RC=127 before it reaches Darling. The runtime provider temporarily deployed its built launcher, ran CTest, then restored the original missing-file state. Named prefix profiles must either preflight a usable baseline launcher or own an explicit bootstrap/rebuild contract; a provider must not make a named prefix appear healthy only during its own lifecycle. This blocks meaningful standalone completion tests and invalidates the earlier stable-prefix closure evidence. No process or mount tail was present.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Reopened: Named homebrew prefix currently lacks bin/darling after a runtime-provider restore; closure no longer holds.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Correction: the immediately preceding comment contains an accidental shell-substitution fragment and should be read only for its first sentence. Authoritative repro: the named homebrew prefix has no bin/darling after runtime restore, so a direct launch cannot begin; provider lifecycle must preserve or bootstrap a usable baseline launcher.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 fixed the immediate false-identity defect: explicit DPREFIX now resolves only its own bin/darling and never falls back to the default prefix launcher. A behavioral contract proves explicit-missing returns None while implicit discovery still finds the default launcher. CLI repro now fails before CTest/runtime deployment with the exact missing path /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix-homebrew-test/bin/darling. This prevents cross-prefix execution; the remaining task is to bootstrap or preserve a genuine named-prefix baseline.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 implementation started: named homebrew prefix has no launcher and is not paired with a prefix-matched build directory. The fix will add an explicit doctor-gated bootstrap from a materialized runtime profile, rather than copying a launcher or allowing cross-prefix fallback. Bootstrap must install a coherent launcher plus declared boot artifacts, verify the named prefix, preserve lifecycle cleanup, and carry behavioral contracts.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 named-prefix bootstrap investigation: the new transactional provider materialized and built a full baseline, deployed launcher, bootchain, dyld, server, and kernel, then correctly rolled all files back when guest smoke failed. Exact cause was structural: a launcher copied from a normal build tree is not setuid, so privileged startup rejects it. The bootstrap API is now constrained to an explicitly declared rootless prefix-baseline with doctor plus guest-smoke verification; ordinary runtime profiles are rejected before build. This aligns prefix provisioning with the unprivileged test-infra contract. The rootless product bootstrap task remains the blocker for a GREEN baseline.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 rootless baseline bootstrap path now has a real bounded RED proof. It builds/deploys the full declared baseline, runs doctor, then real guest smoke; current failure is the existing rootless mldr-to-launchd pre-main stall rather than a missing launcher, SUID capability error, prefix mismatch, or cleanup leak. Provider metadata now gives this smoke a 20-second explicit deadline for future runs.

title: Author guest (env=darling) regression cases for closed runtime bugs
description: Convert the closed runtime bugs whose repro lives only in bead notes / /tmp scripts into guest cases via add_compat_test(ENVS darling, DIAG guarded). Candidates with documented repros: dar-e1j (getattrlist ATTR_CMN_NAME -> Ruby Dir.glob), dar-gwn.1.4 (Thread suspend/resume lost wakeup), dar-gwn.2 (mutex handoff deadlock after cvwait EINTR), dar-gwn.3 (curl HEAD stalls in select). Each runs inside the prefix via DARLING_SHELL; guarded tier so a runtime hang is captured, not wedged. Blocked on a working prefix and (for CI) on SUID-less execution.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Migration audit 2026-07-11: dar-e1j now has a colocated add_compat_test(ENVS darling) registration backed by tests/getattrlist_name_objtype_guest.c, with explicit homebrew runtime provider, guest compile/run, and strict marker. The fixture is behavioural: it creates regular/directory/symlink entries and validates the public getattrlist NAME|OBJTYPE ABI; it is a current GREEN regression gate, not a fabricated RED proof for the historic unprofiled fix. dar-gwn.5 was already a real host CTest regression. dar-gwn.3 was resolved as an external unresponsive GNU mirror, not a Darling select defect, so no Darling regression should be invented. dar-gwn.1.4 and dar-gwn.2 still need direct guest/model coverage through existing focused tasks dar-q95.29.6.3 (wake epochs) and dar-q95.29.6.1 (psynch invariants); their broad historical Ruby stress scripts are not suitable default CTest fixtures. Verified add-compat and testkit contracts plus full host CTest. Local commit: test: register getattrlist guest regression.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Clarification after metadata audit: the new CTest bead:dar-e1j registration is intentionally a current-tree guest regression selector. The corresponding maintained patch xnu/getattrlist-name-objtype.patch (dar-q95.17) already owns the real RED->GREEN proof: red-proof mode guest-runtime-deploy rebuilds/deploys current-minus-patch system_kernel and requires getattrlist Invalid argument (22) plus ORACLE_RC=2 before restoring and proving GREEN. west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/getattrlist-name-objtype.patch --prove-red --list confirms that exact plan. Runtime execution remains pending normal-capacity scratch, not replaced by CTest listing.

title: Port closed-bug repros into colocated regression cases
description: 5 closed bugs carry repro evidence as prose/scripts that are not discoverable or re-runnable: dar-e1j, dar-gwn.1.4, dar-gwn.2, dar-gwn.3, dar-gwn.5. dar-gwn.5 is DONE (host case landed). Port the rest into add_compat_test cases (host where possible, else guest) so closed bugs gain a live RED/GREEN guard. Convention: a closed bug should have a case. Overlaps the guest-cases task for the runtime ones; this task tracks the host/portable ones and the convention itself.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Migration audit 2026-07-11: dar-e1j is now discoverable through CTest by its closed-bug Bead and runs the real guest getattrlist NAME|OBJTYPE fixture. dar-gwn.5 remains covered by its host CTest regression. dar-gwn.3 is a false positive caused by an external GNU mirror and is explicitly excluded from Darling coverage. Remaining dar-gwn.1.4/dar-gwn.2 coverage must land through their focused direct invariants (dar-q95.29.6.3 / dar-q95.29.6.1), not copied unbounded Ruby stress scripts.

title: CI Tier 0: run host regressions + reuse lint in darling Jenkinsfile
description: Add a test stage to darling/ci/Jenkinsfile (Jenkins, githubPush trigger, ccache already wired) after the build: run 'west test --env host' (or ctest -L env:host) + reuse/lint. Host regressions need no prefix and no SUID, so this is the only CI tier shippable without resolving the SUID blocker. Gate: land once >=3 stable host cases exist so the stage is meaningful. Tier 1 (full-darling build at submodule pointer, ctest -L submod:<changed>) and Tier 2 (macOS-version farm) are separate, blocked on SUID.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: CI architecture audit 2026-07-11: current upstream darling ci/Jenkinsfile checks out only Darling into source/, runs Debian/RPM packaging, and its top Alpine agent contains no west workspace or local testkit. Therefore adding west test --env host directly would be a guaranteed broken stage, not Tier 0. The portable host suite currently lives in the private workspace and references the sibling source checkout through DARLING_SRC. Before changing Jenkins, provide a transportable testkit artifact/submodule checkout plus a documented bootstrap command that works from a clean upstream clone and its CI image; then wire that command after build. Docker is irrelevant for Tier 0. Verified the current local Tier 0 substitute: west test --env host passed 4/4, and framework/manifest/patch-quality gates passed. Keep this task open until the actual Jenkins stage is executable from its own checkout.

title: Make Tier 0 host regressions source-owned CTest targets
description: The upstream Darling Jenkinsfile checks out only Darling, while the current host regression registrations live in private darling-workspace/testkit. Do not make Jenkins clone an unspecified private workspace or call west. Move the stable source-owned host regressions into the Darling CMake graph behind an explicit opt-in option, using CTest labels that distinguish env:host and diagnostic policy. Start with tests/regression/thread_create_checkin_wait.c and the glibc fork-lock reset harness plus its TLS helper, preserving their existing behavioral GREEN and explicit RED arm where applicable. Add a source-owned CMake/CTest contract or focused configure/build/run test that proves the targets are buildable and selectable from a clean source checkout. Then add a Jenkins Tier 0 stage after Prepare that configures only these host targets, runs ctest by the host label, and runs the repositorys actual REUSE/lint command only when its tool is available and required by project policy; do not invent a lint command. Keep package stages independent; guest runtime/Docker are out of scope. Export a normal patch from a clean fix branch, add patch metadata and RED/GREEN evidence, verify patch application, and leave all work local. Acceptance: a clean Darling checkout can configure/build/run the host CTest selection without west/testkit; Jenkins invokes that exact command; tests have stable labels and no root/prefix/Docker prerequisite; patch verification and focused CTest pass.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented source-owned Tier 0 suite on clean fix/ci-host-regression-tests: commits 85bfc3a, 3d22c6f, b41159e. tests/regression is a standalone CMake/CTest root that builds the real mldr glibc reset source and existing thread-checkin harness, labels all cases env:host, and uses a shared exact failure-marker runner for the explicit busy-spin RED arm. Jenkins Host Regression Tests stage calls the same run-host-regressions.sh command from its own checkout, with no west, prefix, root, or guest Docker capability. Direct CTest passed 3/3. Patch arch/darling/ci-host-regression-tests.patch applied and west test --profile arch --patch darling/ci-host-regression-tests.patch --prove-red gave real RED rc=1 on source-base followed by GREEN 3/3. Remaining acceptance is an external clean Jenkins execution; no push performed.

title: Wire macos differential oracle: install transport + version matrix
description: Make env=macos real (currently aliases host launch path; TODO in AddCompatTest.cmake). Build-once-run-many: low OSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET universal binary, install via the upstream-compatible testcase/+resource/ layout, ship to a real-macOS host and run there, diff vs Darling (a single fixed version, SystemVersion.plist 11.7.4). CI matrix rows: macos-14/15/26 hosted runners (+ self-hosted VM farm a la WineTestBot for <13). MIN/MAX_VERSION + macos:<ver> labels + 'west test --label macos:15' already in place; this task wires the runner/transport. Long-horizon (Tier 2).

title: Experiment: rootless/no-privilege Darling startup (userns + userxattr / fuse-overlayfs / copy-mode)
description:
Explore making Darling prefix bring-up work in a container with MINIMAL privileges (ideal: plain 'docker run', no --privileged, no --cap-add, no SUID). Follows the SUID investigation (docs/suid-investigation.md, dar-test-infra-sp5.2 closed).

ESTABLISHED (verified live, kernel 6.8, this host + docker):
- The 'mandatory setuid' is just geteuid()==0; Darling needs EFFECTIVE ROOT only for one-time bring-up mounts (overlay, tmpfs /dev/shm, proc), then drops privs.
- mount(2) needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN; only non-root source is a user namespace mapped to uid 0.
- '--cap-add SYS_ADMIN' (no --privileged) is enough for ALL Darling mounts (overlay/proc/tmpfs/pidns) -> proven. This is the practical CI minimum TODAY.
- 'Fully unprivileged' (no caps) is NOT a single docker flag: rootful Docker + seccomp=unconfined still gives EACCES on tmpfs/overlay inside a nested userns, because the container rootfs does not permit nested mounts. It requires the ENGINE itself to be rootless (Podman / rootless Docker delegating subuid maps) AND Darling to be userns-aware. Neither alone suffices -- proven by 3 failed probes.
- darling.c:831 has a COMMENTED-OUT uid_map/gid_map block (disabled since 2017) -- the seed of the userns path.
- darlingserver.cpp:305 shouldUseOverlayFs(): DARLING_NOOVERLAYFS=1 forces COPY-MODE (copyAndSetAttributes) instead of overlay -- already in code.

EXPERIMENTS TO RUN (record results in docs/suid-investigation.md):
E1. copy-mode reach: run Darling with DARLING_NOOVERLAYFS=1 as a normal user (no root) and see how far bring-up gets without the overlay mount. Quantifies what the existing code already removes.
E2. userns path (Variant A): prototype uncommenting darling.c:831 uid_map + create CLONE_NEWUSER before mountns + add ',userxattr' to overlay opts; test under Podman/rootless (NOT rootful docker). Assess kernel >=5.11 viability + AppArmor caveat on stock Ubuntu 24.04.
E3. fuse-overlayfs path (Variant B): add as a third backend in shouldUseOverlayFs() branch; needs fuse-overlayfs pkg + /dev/fuse. The Podman/rootless standard.
E4. trim mounts (Variant D): can bring-up skip the separate PID-ns (=> no mount proc) and reuse existing /dev/shm? Reduces the privileged surface.

GOAL: a documented matrix of (environment x Darling change) -> works/doesn't, and a recommendation. This is DARLING-CORE work (darlingserver/darling.c), not a test-harness change; tracked here because it unblocks fully-unprivileged guest CI (dar-test-infra-sp5.4/.7).

CONSTRAINT: all work local, nothing pushed anywhere.
notes:
E1 DONE + #3 RECON DONE (probe reverted, nothing pushed).

#3: Softened the geteuid()!=0 gate in darling.c behind DARLING_ROOTLESS=1 on a throwaway branch exp/rootless-userns-probe (built incrementally, ran as normal user, then fully reverted + rebuilt stock). Findings:
- Gate softening works: with DARLING_ROOTLESS=1 the launcher entered CLONE_NEWUSER and proceeded past the 'mandatory setuid' wall. NEXT WALL: 'You do not own the prefix directory' because euid ended at 65534, not 0 -- the SELF-written uid_map was rejected.
- Root cause isolated in clean C probes:
  * SELF-map pattern (unshare in same proc, write own /proc/self/uid_map): FAILS on this host -- 'close uid_map: Operation not permitted', uid_map empty, setuid(0)=EINVAL. This is Ubuntu 24.04 AppArmor unprivileged_userns lockdown (audit deny capability).
  * FORK pattern (child unshares, PARENT writes child's /proc/<pid>/uid_map): mapping SUCCEEDS -- 'child after map: uid=0 euid=0'. BUT the subsequent mount('tmpfs') still returns EACCES, because AppArmor strips CAP_SYS_ADMIN inside the unpriv userns.
- STRUCTURAL LESSONS for the real darling-core change:
  1. uid_map MUST be written by a parent after fork (NOT self-map). darling.c:831's commented block is already in the parent -> correct shape.
  2. Even correctly mapped, mount needs an env WITHOUT the AppArmor userns lockdown: non-Ubuntu, or apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns=0, or a rootless container engine (Podman/rootless Docker) that pre-arranges the userns. Cannot be validated on stock Ubuntu 24.04 without weakening host AppArmor (declined).
- CONCLUSION: the userns code path is structurally viable and small (finish darling.c:831 + fork-first ordering + userxattr overlay/copy-mode), but its payoff depends entirely on the runtime environment's userns policy. Recommend next real step E2 be done either on a non-Ubuntu host or under rootless Podman, NOT here.

E2(full userns impl)/E3(fuse-overlayfs)/E4(trim pidns) still pending. Full log in docs/suid-investigation.md.

title: E-B2: replace CLONE_NEWPID with PR_SET_CHILD_SUBREAPER (drop the proc mount + pidns)
description:
Hypothesis (from systems-challenge subagent): darlingserver.cpp:693 uses clone(CLONE_NEWPID) so launchd is pid 1 in a fresh pidns; this forces the privileged mount('proc') at darlingserver.cpp:707. The ONLY load-bearing reason found is reaping orphaned guest processes (no host getpid()==1 assumption anywhere; reaping is explicit waitpid in thread.cpp:142). prctl(PR_SET_CHILD_SUBREAPER,1) does orphan-reaping WITHOUT privilege.

VERIFIED LIVE (this host, normal user uid=1000): PR_SET_CHILD_SUBREAPER set OK with no privilege; forked child->grandchild->child-exits; we REAPED the orphaned grandchild (pid, exit=42). So subreaper replaces the reaping role of pidns.

CAVEAT: subreaper does NOT give launchd a fresh pid-1 VIEW. Must confirm no guest/launchd code depends on being literal pid 1 in its namespace (the <prefix>/proc mount serves the GUEST filesystem view, a separate concern -- guest /proc may be synthesized by the emulation layer, needs check). If a guest pid-1 view is needed, this is partial.

PAYOFF: removes CLONE_NEWPID + the mount('proc') call = one fewer namespace AND one fewer privileged mount. darling-core change ~10 lines. CONSTRAINT: local only, no push.
notes: PARTIAL RESULT from route2 prototype: PR_SET_CHILD_SUBREAPER correctly handles orphan REAPING (probe + prototype). BUT removing CLONE_NEWPID breaks launchd boot: launchd.c:163 requires getpid()==1 OR getppid()==1, which only the PID namespace provides. So subreaper canNOT fully replace the pidns -- the pidns is load-bearing for launchd's pid-1 identity, not just reaping. To drop the host-root pidns we must EITHER patch launchd's guard OR get CLONE_NEWPID via an unprivileged userns. So this bead stays OPEN: 'subreaper replaces reaping, but pidns still needed for launchd pid-1'.

title: E-B1: skip the /dev/shm tmpfs mount when one already exists (let the engine provide it)
description: darlingserver.cpp:613 unconditionally mounts a fresh tmpfs on /dev/shm (with MS_NOSUID|MS_NODEV|MS_NOEXEC). VERIFIED LIVE: /dev/shm is ALREADY a tmpfs on the host (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec) and in a default docker container (tmpfs, writable). So Darling can make this mount CONDITIONAL (skip if /dev/shm is already tmpfs, or behind DARLING_NO_SHM_MOUNT) and rely on the engine-provided shm. Tradeoff: engine shm flags may differ (size, exec); guest expecting NOEXEC could be affected -- minor. PAYOFF: removes one privileged mount. ~5 lines. Local only.
close_reason: Confirmed in route2 prototype: skipping the /dev/shm mount and relying on host/engine-provided tmpfs works (bring-up proceeded with no /dev/shm mount). ~5-line conditional. See sp5.8.4 notes + docs/suid-investigation.md.

title: E-NS: audit whether CLONE_NEWUTS/NEWIPC/NEWNS are load-bearing (drop or make conditional)
description: VERIFIED LIVE: a normal user CANNOT unshare(NEWUTS|NEWIPC) nor unshare(NEWNS) -- both EPERM (need CAP_SYS_ADMIN or a userns). So these are part of the privilege requirement even after all mounts are removed. AUDIT (code grep done): (1) NEWNS at darlingserver.cpp:600 exists ONLY to scope the overlay/proc/shm mounts (the MS_SLAVE remount at :623 is about not leaking the overlay) -- if copy-mode + E-B1 + E-B2 remove all mounts, NEWNS is no longer needed. (2) NEWUTS|NEWIPC at darling.c:792: grep finds NO sethostname/setdomainname/shmget/semget/msgget anywhere -- appears to be isolation-for-cleanliness, not load-bearing; likely droppable or deferrable. NEEDS maintainer confirmation but evidence is strong. PAYOFF: if all three drop, NO namespace unshare remains -> combined with no-mounts, Darling needs NEITHER CAP_SYS_ADMIN NOR a userns -> runs in a plain unprivileged container. This is the alternative to finishing the userns code. Local only.
close_reason: Confirmed in route2 prototype: dropping unshare(NEWNS) and unshare(NEWUTS|NEWIPC) caused NO failure during bring-up or copy-mode -- they are not load-bearing once the mounts they scoped are gone. (NEWNS only scoped the overlay/proc/shm mounts; no sethostname/SysV-IPC use.) The remaining hard namespace is NEWPID, tracked in sp5.8.1.

title: E-NOMOUNT: prove the 'no privileged op left' path (copy-mode + E-B1 + E-B2 + E-NS)
description:
STRATEGIC SYNTHESIS of the subagent challenge. KEY CODE FINDING (verified by reading process.cpp:147-167 + mldr.c:909): Darling's guest root is NOT a kernel mount/chroot -- vchroot is pure USERSPACE path indirection (readlink /proc/self/fd/<n> -> string prefix, sent to mldr via dserver_rpc_vchroot_path). No pivot_root/chroot anywhere. Therefore the overlay mount is ONLY a directory-merge tool, fully replaceable.

If we stack: copy-mode (DARLING_NOOVERLAYFS=1, removes overlay -- in code) + E-B1 (skip /dev/shm, engine provides) + E-B2 (subreaper instead of pidns, removes proc mount) + E-NS (drop NEWUTS/NEWIPC/NEWNS once no mounts need scoping) => ZERO privileged mounts AND zero required namespace unshares remain. That means Darling could run in a PLAIN unprivileged container with NO CAP_SYS_ADMIN and NO userns at all -- a simpler path than finishing the userns/uid_map code.

This bead = end-to-end prototype: a darling-core branch combining all four, built, run as normal user in a default 'docker run' (no caps, no privileged). Gate behind a single DARLING_ROOTLESS-style flag. Compare against the userns path (which is blocked by Ubuntu AppArmor anyway). Blocks on E-B1/E-B2/E-NS landing as prototypes. CONSTRAINT: local only, no push.
notes: ROUTE 2 (E-NOMOUNT) FUNCTIONALLY DEMONSTRATED end-to-end (2026-06-15, variant A, host, all local NOT pushed). Combining copy-mode + no-mount-ns + no-UTS/IPC + subreaper-pidns (variant A, server-anchored nsid 1) + the E-BOOTSTRAP fix (sp5.8.4.3, Fix 1 sys_fstatfs64), a rootless 'darling shell' runs a full guest bash pipeline: UID=1000, uname=Darwin, tr-pipeline, exit 0; shellspawn + opendirectoryd/securityd spawn. NO setuid, NO mount/PID/UTS/IPC namespace, NO overlay, NO /dev/shm. This is the 'no privileged op left' path proven. Remaining for full productization: pick/land Fix 1 on exp/route2-nomount, decide pidns story (variant A nsid-anchor vs variant B userns), and harden prefix-init (the E-SOCKET /var/tmp wall only bites when a prefix dir is pre-created empty, bypassing setupPrefix). Nothing pushed.

title: E-PID-A: rootless pidns-free — force init task to report nsid=1 (no userns)
description:
Route 2 variant A: make Darling fully unprivileged WITHOUT any namespace, by emulating the init task's nsid=1 instead of getting it from a PID namespace.

CONTEXT (proven in sp5.8.4): with DARLING_ROOTLESS route 2 removes overlay+/dev/shm+mount-ns+UTS/IPC+setuid and launchd boots (after the guard patch), but launchd then HANGS in recvmsg because darlingserver keys every task on its namespace-relative pid, and the client reports it via dserver_rpc_hooks_get_pid == bare getpid() (mldr/resources/dserver-rpc-defs.h:26). Without CLONE_NEWPID, launchd's getpid() is the host pid, not 1, so the init/bootstrap task is not nsid 1 and the Mach bootstrap port routing never matches.

TASK: when DARLING_ROOTLESS and this is the init process, force the reported pid to 1: (a) mldr's get_pid hook returns 1 for the init process under rootless; (b) darlingserver's call.cpp:74 accepts a host-pid != 1 mapping to nsid 1 for the first/init process; (c) check NSpid parsing in thread.cpp:88-113 / process.cpp doesn't re-derive a conflicting nsid from /proc (no NSpid namespace line exists without the pidns, so it must fall back to the reported pid). Verify guest boots (shellspawn socket appears, 'darling shell echo' returns) as a normal user, no userns, no caps.

PROS: the only path to TRULY unprivileged (no userns, no caps, no setuid) -> plain 'docker run'. Dodges Ubuntu AppArmor entirely. CONS: deeper change to darlingserver's task-identity model; risk that other code assumes real namespace pids; only the init task can be faked-1 (other guest procs still use host pids -> confirm collisions impossible). Local only, no push. Prototype lives on exp/route2-nomount.
notes: PROGRESS (impl + tested, local only on exp/route2-nomount, NOT pushed): implemented all three parts. (a) mldr get_pid hook returns 1 via __mldr_report_pid1, set from env __mldr_rootless_pid1 (set by darlingserver only for the init mldr, consumed in unset_special_env). Emulation layer learns it via a new dserver_report_pid1 elfcalls callback (cached); raw-fork child resets it. (b) Server-side AUTHORITATIVE anchor in call.cpp: darlingserver records the init host pid it fork()s (g_rootlessInitHostPid) and forces nsid=1 + registry key=1 for any checkin whose SO_PEERCRED host pid matches. This was necessary because a secondary launchd thread raced and registered a phantom duplicate process at the host pid. (c) process.cpp exec path: also accept main thread by host tid==host pid (its real nstid no longer equals forced nsid 1). RESULT: launchd registers as nsid 1, NO duplicate, no clock-port/ipc-send errors, no fork-wait timeout. Boots cleanly past the whole identity layer. REMAINING WALL (shared with variant B, see new bead): launchd forks '/bin/launchctl bootstrap -S System' which hangs in ep_poll on a Mach reply; shellspawn never starts. That is launchd service bring-up / Mach bootstrap-port routing, NOT a pidns/identity problem. So the pidns-closure goal of THIS bead is functionally met.

title: E-PID-B: rootless pidns via unprivileged userns (unshare(NEWUSER|NEWPID), no mounts)
description:
Route 2 variant B: keep the PID namespace (so launchd's getpid()==1 for free, no identity-model change) but acquire it WITHOUT root, via an unprivileged user namespace.

CONTEXT (proven in sp5.8.4): route 2 removes every privileged op except CLONE_NEWPID, which darlingserver needs so the init task is nsid 1. CLONE_NEWPID needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN OR a userns. unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER|CLONE_NEWPID) is allowed for an unprivileged user (no mounts needed) where the kernel/distro permits userns.

TASK: in darlingserver (rootless path), instead of plain fork(), do: create a user namespace (fork-first: child unshares CLONE_NEWUSER, parent writes single-id uid_map/gid_map per the E1/#3 findings) then CLONE_NEWPID inside it, so launchd is pid 1 again. NO overlay/mount/proc still (copy-mode + engine /dev/shm). Verify guest boots as normal user. Test BOTH: (1) bare host -- expected BLOCKED by Ubuntu 24.04 AppArmor unprivileged_userns (audit deny capability), confirming environment-dependence; (2) inside rootless Podman or a non-Ubuntu/knob-off env -- expected to WORK.

PROS: smaller, surgical; launchd unchanged; reuses the existing pidns code path; matches the Apptainer/Podman model (sp5.8.5). CONS: reintroduces a userns -> fails on stock Ubuntu 24.04 (AppArmor), so it's environment-dependent, NOT 'works anywhere'. Compare head-to-head with E-PID-A. Local only, no push.
notes: PROGRESS (impl + tested in ROOTLESS PODMAN, local only on exp/route2-nomount, NOT pushed): implemented via DARLING_ROOTLESS_USERNS=1 -> darlingserver creates launchd with clone(CLONE_NEWUSER|CLONE_NEWPID); child maps itself to uid 0 in the new userns (setup_userns_maps) so CLONE_NEWPID is legal unprivileged; launchd gets getpid()==1 natively. No mounts. Server parent stays in host namespaces. ENV MATRIX (verified): bare Ubuntu 24.04 host BLOCKS unpriv userns (apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns=1, uid_map write EPERM) as expected. 'docker run --security-opt seccomp=unconfined' ALONE enables it (no caps, no --privileged). Installed rootless Podman 4.9.3 + uidmap (subuid/subgid preconfigured) -> nests userns+pidns with ZERO host privilege. RAN end-to-end in rootless podman: copy-mode prefix set up, launchd booted to pid 1 cleanly (no identity errors, no clock spam). REMAINING WALL: identical to variant A -> launchd forks '/bin/launchctl bootstrap -S System' which hangs in ep_poll; shellspawn never appears. Since A (no userns) and B (userns) hit the SAME stall by independent routes, the pidns closure is solved both ways; the wall is launchd service bring-up (Mach bootstrap-port), tracked separately.

title: E-BOOTSTRAP: rootless launchd loads zero LaunchDaemons (copy-mode /proc missing -> fstatfs/opendir ENOENT)
description:
Converged blocker found while finishing route-2 PID-namespace closure (sp5.8.4.1 variant A + sp5.8.4.2 variant B, both prototyped on exp/route2-nomount, local only NOT pushed).

SYMPTOM: with launchd correctly at pid/nsid 1 (proven both via server-anchored nsid in variant A AND via unprivileged userns pidns in variant B), launchd boots cleanly (no identity errors, no clock-port spam, no fork-wait timeout) and forks exactly ONE child: /bin/launchctl bootstrap -S System. launchctl then HANGS in ep_poll waiting on a Mach reply that never arrives; launchd sits in skb_wait_for_more_packets on its darlingserver socket. shellspawn (and every other System LaunchDaemon) never starts, so the launcher times out after 15s connecting to var/run/shellspawn.sock (darling.c:196).

KEY POINT: variant A (NO userns) and variant B (userns pidns) reach the IDENTICAL stall by two independent routes -> this is NOT the PID-namespace/identity problem (both solved that); it is launchd's service bring-up, specifically launchctl bootstrap's Mach call to the init bootstrap port not completing in the rootless/copy-mode configuration.

INVESTIGATE: how the bootstrap (task special) port is wired for the init task and inherited by launchctl across fork; whether nsid-1 anchoring left the bootstrap port pointing at the right task; dtape Mach port routing for the host/bootstrap special ports under rootless. Compare against a normal (setuid/pidns) boot where launchctl bootstrap completes.

This is the narrow successor to sp5.8.4.1/.2: the pidns closure itself is functionally done; this is what stands between 'launchd at pid 1' and 'guest shell runs'.
notes: DECISION (2026-06-15): Variant 1 adopted. xnu exp/route2-nomount fast-forwarded onto commit 4ebed05 'sys_fstatfs64 tolerate missing /proc/self/mounts'; main repo exp/route2-nomount records the pointer bump (6624650). libc reset to clean route2 pointer 5a38c8d; Variant 2 preserved unused on exp/route2-opendir-fix. Re-verified end-to-end on final config (Fix1 only, clean libc, no instrumentation): rootless guest bash pipeline -> UID=1000, Darwin x86_64, exit 0. All local, NOTHING pushed.

title: E-UNION: research + prototype union-in-vchroot (cheap-disk rootless prefix without mount/fuse)
description:
GOAL: a prefix-assembly mechanism that works in ANY unprivileged container (incl. default root-docker, the most restrictive) without 7GB/prefix copying.

WHY (verified this session, variant A host + container probes):
- route 2 currently assembles the macOS-style guest root by COPYING the whole LIBEXEC template into the prefix (darlingserver.cpp:722 else-branch -> copyAndSetAttributes). ~7GB per prefix, slow first boot.
- the elegant alternatives all need privileges the target lacks:
  * native mount overlay -> needs CAP_SYS_ADMIN
  * fuse-overlayfs -> needs /dev/fuse (absent in default docker) AND mount capability; PROVEN fail in default docker, rootless podman w/o caps, even seccomp=unconfined (fusermount3: mount EPERM)
  * userns+overlay via newuidmap -> works on bare host and in rootless podman/rootless-docker (nested userns OK), but PROVEN blocked in DEFAULT root-docker (seccomp blocks unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER))
- container matrix (measured): default root-docker = ONLY syscall-based copy/hardlink survive; rootless podman & rootless docker = nested userns OK so fuse/overlay possible w/ /dev/fuse; SYS_ADMIN container = native overlay.
- => for 'any unprivileged container incl. default docker' the only cheap-disk option is doing the union IN USERSPACE inside Darling's path translator (vchroot), which needs no mount/fuse/userns at all.

IDEA (union-in-vchroot): vchroot_run (libsystem_kernel emulation/.../linux_premigration/vchroot_userspace.c) already resolves guest paths component-by-component on the CLIENT with ENOENT/unknown_component + case-insensitive + symlink handling. Extend the resolver to a two-layer lookup: writable subtrees -> $prefix; everything else -> shared read-only $LIBEXEC template (one template shared by ALL prefixes = max disk saving, zero copy, zero mount, zero privilege). Writable set is small+fixed (from setupPrefix darling.c:1011): /var, /private/var/{log,db}, /private/etc/{passwd,master.passwd,group}, /Users, /Applications, /Volumes, /usr/local, /tmp.

HARD PARTS (the real work):
1. copy-on-write: open(O_CREAT|O_WRONLY)/truncate of a file that exists only in $LIBEXEC must copy-up to $prefix first. Translator runs PER CLIENT PROCESS -> concurrent copy-up across launchd+daemons+shell => need race-safe copy-up (atomic rename/lock).
2. whiteout: unlink/rmdir of a lower-only path needs a deletion marker in $prefix + marker check on every resolve.
3. readdir-merge: opendir of a dir present in both layers must return merged listing minus whiteouts/dups (touches the same opendir/__fd_is_on_union_mount path we already patched for E-BOOTSTRAP).
4. spread across ~24 write-op emulation files (open/openat/mkdir*/rename*/link*/unlink*/symlink*/truncate*/shm_open/...), not one site.

REUSE (no off-the-shelf lib fits our model): there is NO drop-in 'union via path-translation' library — fuse-overlayfs/gVisor/PRoot all embed union logic inside their own intercept engine, not extractable. BUT two GPL references to PORT algorithmically (Darling is GPL-3, compatible):
  * PRoot (userspace chroot/bind/mount via syscall translation, no root) — closest analog; mine path/binding + copy-up logic.
  * gVisor rootfs-overlay (copy-up design writeup) — algorithmic model for copy-up/whiteout.

DELIVERABLES:
- [research] decide layering: pure union-in-vchroot (zero copy, shared template) vs hardlink-copy (cp -al; needs the SAME break-link CoW but gets whiteout/readdir free from the real fs; per-prefix inode dup). Note: hardlink + union share the copy-on-write core, so prototyping CoW first de-risks both.
- [research] template-protection: chmod RO does NOT protect (container runs as uid 0); must protect via break-hardlink / copy-up in Darling at open(O_WRONLY) when st_nlink>1 (or lower-layer hit), not via fs perms.
- [proto] read-only union resolve in vchroot_run ($prefix -> $LIBEXEC fallback); verify read-only part of route 2 boots.
- [proto] race-safe copy-up on first write; whiteout; readdir-merge.
- [verify] full route 2 (darling shell guest pipeline) in DEFAULT unprivileged docker with no /dev/fuse, no caps, no userns.

CONTEXT: builds on sp5.8.4.3 (E-BOOTSTRAP fixed, Variant 1 sys_fstatfs64 adopted on exp/route2-nomount) and sp5.8.4 (E-NOMOUNT). All prior work local, nothing pushed.
notes:
STEP 6+7 milestone: real build + route-2 boot in DEFAULT unprivileged docker (local only, NOTHING pushed).

BUILD SWITCH: top-level CMake option DARLING_EUNION (OFF=byte-identical baseline; shared by darlingserver + libsystem_kernel/emulation + vchroot + mldr). Built full tree Release+DARLING_EUNION=ON (26420 targets), staged, ran DARLING_ROOTLESS=1 darling shell in default docker run (ubuntu:24.04, NO --privileged/--cap-add/--device fuse/userns; template mounted :ro).

PROVEN E2E IN CONTAINER: (1) zero-copy -- prefix bootstrap = empty prefix + .union-work marker (~156KB) vs copy-mode ~524MB; :ro template never written. (2) activation via .union-work marker (absent=inert=baseline). (3) launchd boots THROUGH the union: dyld+libsystem dylibs load from RO template (verified /proc/maps), copies up /private/etc/passwd, creates /var/run/{utmpx,.systemStarterRunning} in upper. (4) readdir-merge on REAL data: 20/20 template LaunchDaemons via vchroot_getdents_merge from empty upper.

BOOT FIXES (all #ifdef, prod byte-identical), commits on the route2-nomount branches: objc4 #error = baseline -> -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release (4c4f7c3 covers loader fixes); vchroot.c init-binary access() -> template fallback; mldr.c load() raw open() dyld/binary -> template fallback via lr->root_path (mldr d7.. / 4c4f7c3); sys_bind AF_UNIX -> copy-up socket parent dir (xnu d7cd015); getdents_merge -> raw passthrough when union inactive (xnu dd0856b). xnu eunion fix branches still local.

OPEN WALL: zero-copy bootstrap -> launchd forks one child (launchctl bootstrap -S System) which HANGS in ep_poll on a Mach reply; shellspawn never starts -> launcher times out 15s on var/run/shellspawn.sock. IDENTICAL signature to pre-existing route-2 stall dar-test-infra-sp5.8.4.3, but reproduces on the EUNION zero-copy bootstrap branch where copy-mode bootstrap CLEARS it. Controlled isolation: copy-mode empty-prefix real bootstrap BOOTS (rc=0); EUNION zero-copy stalls. RULED OUT: union resolve/copy-up (full-copy prefix + active union still stalls), readdir-merge (20/20; passthrough fix no change), write-op site wirings (gated no-op when inactive), exec-path identity (seeding launchd/launchctl/dyld into upper did not clear). TEST HYGIENE: raw cp -a is NOT a bootable prefix (copy-mode cp -a also stalls) -- compare bootstrap-vs-bootstrap only.

NEXT: darlingserver-side Mach/dtape trace of the bootstrap-port reply path under the zero-copy prefix -- what launchctl bootstrap waits on and why the reply that arrives in copy-mode does not arrive here. Focused darlingserver Mach investigation, separate from the union mechanism (proven working up to this point). DESIGN.md updated with full step 6+7 record.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
BREAKTHROUGH: E-UNION zero-copy boots a FULL guest pipeline in DEFAULT unprivileged docker. `darling shell echo HELLO_EUNION` -> HELLO_EUNION, rc=0, prefix ~572KB (vs ~524MB copy-mode). No --privileged/caps/fuse/userns; template mounted :ro.

The launchctl-bootstrap stall (previously suspected Mach-reply path) was a path-DETRANSLATION bug in the union, found by stracing the guest tree: launchctl 'bootstrap -S System' opened System/Library/LaunchDaemons (union -> lower template), getdents-merged 52 entries OK, then opened ZERO plists and waited 60s -> exit. Cause: vchroot_fdpath()/vchroot_unexpand() only knew the prefix (UPPER); a fd/path into the read-only LOWER template lives outside the prefix, so both fell through to the EXIT_PATH escape hatch -> bogus /Volumes/SystemRoot/<host-template-path> guest path. launchctl F_GETPATHs the LaunchDaemons dir fd to build child plist paths; the escape-hatch base made every plist ENOENT -> no daemons registered -> bootstrap never completed.

Fix (xnu 35025e9, both functions, #ifdef EUNION): when readlink/path result is under libexec_path, strip it and return the bare guest path, exactly as the existing prefix_path branch. Out-of-both paths still escape via EXIT_PATH. Hermetic suite 70 -> 76/76 (F1-F5 pin lower/upper/out-of-both detranslation for fdpath+unexpand).

Test hygiene: harness default prefix /root/.darling-eunion gives EACCES (launcher stat()s prefix before setuid; /root is 0700). Use /tmp/... . Nothing pushed; all commits local.
