title: E-UNION hardening: unlink/rmdir lower-only must not touch the template
description: unlinkat.c/rmdir.c physically deleted the lower object from the SHARED template (not RO mount) then whiteout'd. FIXED: vchroot_prepare_unlink(guest) decides policy BEFORE syscall -- lower-only => whiteout + SKIP host delete; upper => PROCEED then post_unlink. Wired into unlinkat.c + rmdir.c. TDD U1-U3 (114-suite). xnu local, not pushed.

title: vchroot_run: strncasecmp_l(EXIT_PATH, LC_C_LOCALE=NULL) NULL-locale deref risk
description:
BASELINE (pre-E-UNION) concern surfaced by the hermetic harness under glibc.

vchroot_userspace.c:1676 (the EXIT_PATH check, present with or without EUNION):
    strncasecmp_l(ctxt->current_path + ctxt->current_root_len, EXIT_PATH,
                  sizeof(EXIT_PATH)-1, LC_C_LOCALE)
and line 52 defines  LC_C_LOCALE ((locale_t)NULL).

glibc's strncasecmp_l DEREFERENCES the locale argument once the compared bytes
match (it reaches the per-locale tolower table on the evex/AVX path), so passing
a NULL locale SEGVs precisely when a guest path equals EXIT_PATH
('/Volumes/SystemRoot'). The compiler even warns: 'argument 4 null where non-null
expected [-Wnonnull]'. Confirmed with a 3-line repro: strncasecmp_l(s, s, n, NULL)
on a matching string -> SIGSEGV.

In Darling's own libsystem_c a NULL 'C locale' MAY be tolerated (the *_l string
functions there may treat NULL as the C locale), which is why this has not been
observed at runtime -- but it is undefined and fragile. If a guest ever resolves
EXIT_PATH exactly, the translator could crash.

Surfaced because the hermetic E-UNION harness initially defined LC_C_LOCALE as
(locale_t)0 too and crashed non-deterministically in vchroot_run whenever an
expand()'d path's EXIT_PATH-length suffix matched. Harness now builds a real C
locale via newlocale(LC_ALL_MASK,'C',0); the suite is deterministic at 135/135.

FIX (real code): build a real C locale once (newlocale) and pass it, OR use
plain strncasecmp/memcmp for the EXIT_PATH compare (it is an ASCII literal,
case-fold is unnecessary). VERIFY on Darling's actual libsystem_c whether NULL
is tolerated before deciding severity.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
FIXED via TDD (RED->GREEN->mutation audit).

ROOT CAUSE confirmed with a 3-line glibc repro: strncasecmp_l(s, s, 19, (locale_t)NULL) on a string equal to EXIT_PATH SIGSEGVs (exit 139) + gcc -Wnonnull. glibc derefs the locale the instant the compared bytes match -> crash exactly when the /Volumes/SystemRoot escape hatch fires.

FIX: replaced strncasecmp_l(..., LC_C_LOCALE) at the EXIT_PATH check with a locale-free ASCII case-fold comparator eunion_ascii_ncasecmp(); removed the now-dead locale_t / LC_C_LOCALE / strncasecmp_l decls. EXIT_PATH is pure ASCII so case folding needs no locale; case-insensitive prefix match (HFS+-like) preserved. This is a BASELINE fix (outside any #ifdef EUNION) — the deref existed with or without the union.

TESTS (hermetic harness, runner.c EP1-EP4): EP1 identical compares equal (the case that crashed); EP2 case-folded /volumes/systemroot & /VOLUMES/systemROOT match; EP3 different bytes / digit-vs-letter don't match, only first n bytes compared; EP4 end-to-end vchroot_run escape-hatch resolves without crashing and leaves the union. Suite 204 -> 214, 0 failed.

MUTATION AUDIT (3/3 caught): always-equal -> EP3 RED; drop case-fold -> EP2 RED; asymmetric fold (one side only) -> EP1+EP2 RED.

HARNESS CLEANUP: removed the workaround that masked this (newlocale C-locale injection + #define LC_C_LOCALE g_c_locale in runner.c, and -Wno-nonnull in run.sh). Suite still green & warning-free for this site.

NOTHING PUSHED (exp/route2-nomount has no upstream).

title: E-UNION: ftruncate on a lower/RO fd corrupts the template (missed fd-write site)
description:
ftruncate.c calls __NR_ftruncate(fd, len) on a raw fd with NO copy-up. ftruncate mutates file CONTENT by descriptor -- structurally identical to the fd-metadata syscalls fixed in .4 (fchmod/fchmod_extended/futimes/fsetxattr/fremovexattr), but it was omitted from that set. If a writable fd references a lower-only (shared template) inode without having gone through the write-open copy-up, ftruncate truncates the SHARED template for ALL prefixes.

Reachability = the same residual class .4 closed: legal write-fds route through openat (O_WRONLY/O_RDWR/O_CREAT/O_TRUNC -> prepare_write copy-up; open.c and guarded_open_np.c both delegate to sys_openat_nocancel), so a normal open(O_RDWR)+ftruncate already lands on the upper copy. The hole is a write-fd on a lower inode obtained outside the protected write-open -- exactly what vchroot_fd_for_meta_write(fd) was built for in .4.

FIX (mirror fchmod.c): under #ifdef EUNION, wfd = vchroot_fd_for_meta_write(fd); ftruncate the upper fd; close wfd if != fd. OFF byte-identical. TDD: ftruncate via an RO/lower fd truncates only the upper copy; template inode size unchanged. Mutation-verified. Found by a full 216-file write-op audit of vchroot protection coverage.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
FIXED via TDD (RED->GREEN->mutation audit). Found by a full 216-file write-op audit of vchroot protection coverage — ftruncate was the ONLY unprotected mutating site (every other path/fd write op routes through a vchroot_prepare_* / fd_for_meta_write / post_mkdir; open-family all delegates to the protected sys_openat_nocancel).

ROOT CAUSE: sys_ftruncate hit __NR_ftruncate on the raw fd, no copy-up. Same class as the .4 fd-metadata fixes but omitted. KEY DIFFERENCE from .4: ftruncate REQUIRES a writable fd — the .4 helper re-opens the upper copy O_RDONLY (fine for fchmod/utimensat/fsetxattr which check inode perms), so reusing it makes ftruncate EINVAL. Surfaced by the RED test failing on ftruncate() itself.

FIX (option 1, chosen by user — do not touch the proven .4 helper): added sibling vchroot_fd_for_content_write(fd) — identical copy-up to vchroot_fd_for_meta_write but re-opens the upper copy O_RDWR. Wired sys_ftruncate to it under #ifdef EUNION; #else = original. Added LINUX_O_RDWR fallback def. New helper sits inside vchroot_userspace.c's existing #ifdef EUNION block -> OFF byte-identical (verified: guard-evaluated OFF diff = empty for vchroot_userspace.c; ftruncate.c EUNION-branch-only; vchroot_expand.h decl-only). ftruncate.c's unconditional vchroot_expand.h include matches the .4 fchmod.c/futimes.c pattern (decl-only header = zero object-code delta).

TEST FT (dedicated pristine lower-only non-empty fixture /var/log/ftrunc_lower): open lower inode -> content helper copies up + re-opens O_RDWR upper -> ftruncate(0) -> upper truncated to 0, TEMPLATE bytes preserved. Suite 214 -> 222, 0 failed.

MUTATION AUDIT: (a) content helper opens O_RDONLY (the original-bug shape) -> FT ftruncate EINVAL + upper-not-truncated RED; (b) stub helper returns original fd (no copy-up) -> different-fd + upper-materialized + template-safety RED.

NOTHING PUSHED.

title: E-UNION hardening: rename newParent copy-up + lower dest whiteout
description: renameat.c did not copy up the dest parent nor handle a lower-only dest -> rename moved INTO / overwrote the template. FIXED: vchroot_prepare_rename_dest(newpath) copies up dest parent + whiteouts a lower-only dest namesake + clears stale whiteout. Wired into renameat.c (replaced pre_mkdir). TDD RN1-RN2. xnu local.

title: E-UNION hardening: create sites must copy up a lower-only parent
description: mkdirat/mknod/mkfifo/symlinkat (+openat O_CREAT) created entries in lower-only parents -> landed inside the template. FIXED: vchroot_prepare_create(guest) copies up parent + clears whiteout; wired into all 5 sites. TDD CR1. xnu local.

title: E-UNION: fchmod/futimes/fsetxattr on a lower/RO fd corrupt the template
description: fchmod/fchmod_extended/futimes/fsetxattr/fremovexattr act on an fd with no path. If the fd was opened O_RDONLY on a lower object, the inode is the template's -> metadata mutation hits the SHARED template. Primitive vchroot_prepare_write_fd(fd,out,cap) DONE+tested (FD1): recovers guest path, copies up, returns path. REMAINING: in each site, after copy-up RE-OPEN the upper copy (old fd still on lower inode!) and apply to the new fd (or path via /proc/self/fd). TDD: fchmod via RO-fd on lower file changes only the upper copy; template inode mode unchanged. NOT triggered during the proven boot.
notes:
DONE (TDD + mutation-verified + patched).

Implemented vchroot_fd_for_meta_write(fd): copy up a lower-only object via prepare_write_fd, then RE-OPEN the upper copy (O_RDONLY|O_NOFOLLOW) and return a fresh fd. The original fd still points at the lower inode after copy-up, so the metadata op MUST target the re-opened fd. Returns the original fd when unmanaged.

Wired all 5 sites (#ifdef EUNION, OFF byte-identical): fchmod, fchmod_extended (chmod+chown both on the new fd), futimes (utimensat), fsetxattr, fremovexattr. Uniform caller rule: apply op to returned fd, close iff != original.

Bonus fix: vchroot_prepare_write_fd EXIT_PATH escape detection was wrong (guest[0]!='/' did not catch '/Volumes/SystemRoot/...'); a fd outside both layers would have triggered a host-path copy-up. Caught by the new FD4 test. Now recognized as unmanaged.

TDD: FD2 end-to-end (chmod via returned fd -> upper changes, TEMPLATE 0644 UNTOUCHED) proven RED under a re-open-disabling mutation; FD3 (upper-only) + FD4 (unmanaged /dev/null) pin the branches. Suite 147->149, deterministic.

Build: full -DDARLING_EUNION dylib compiles+links. Commit 167ec89, patch eunion-14-fd-metadata (verified).
close_reason: fd-metadata template protection: vchroot_fd_for_meta_write + 5 sites wired, prepare_write_fd escape fix, TDD 149/149 mutation-verified, build green, patch eunion-14 verified. Commit 167ec89.

title: E-UNION: setxattr/removexattr never expand the path (baseline) + no copy-up
description: xattr/setxattr.c + removexattr.c pass the raw GUEST path to the Linux syscall -- they never call vchroot_expand (pre-existing baseline bug; getxattr/listxattr do expand). Because unexpanded, they don't actually corrupt the template today, but they are broken. FIX: add vchroot_expand + vchroot_prepare_write(path) (fixes baseline AND template protection). CAUTION: must isolate our user.union.* marker namespace from guest setxattr/getxattr/listxattr (do not let the guest read/forge whiteout/opaque markers). TDD: setxattr on a lower object copies up, template untouched; guest write of user.union.* rejected/masked.
close_reason: Implemented (PoC, #ifdef EUNION; OFF byte-identical, verified per-file via gcc -E -fdirectives-only vs git baseline). vchroot_xattr_is_marker(name) predicate (reserved user.union.* namespace; trailing dot significant) in vchroot_userspace.c. All four path-based xattr sites wired: setxattr/removexattr expand (fixes pre-EUNION baseline bug) + vchroot_prepare_write copy-up + reject guest marker forging (EPERM) + operate on the upper path (never the shared template); getxattr returns ENOATTR for markers; listxattr uses the expanded path (baseline-bug fix) + filters user.union.* from the returned name list. Hermetic suite 166/166 green; new XM1-XM4 (predicate) + XS1 (path copy-up) mutation-verified (always-0, drop-the-dot nslen-1, and over-broad match-user. all RED). Local xnu dc27576 on exp/route2-nomount; fix/e-union-15-xattr-isolation; eunion-15-xattr-isolation.patch registered in patches.yml (publication-status: blocked); west patch verify EXIT 0. Nothing pushed.

title: E-UNION: mkdir over a removed lower dir must be opaque (delete-recreate)
description: mkdirat only calls prepare_create (copyup parent + unwhiteout), never vchroot_set_opaque. After rmdir+mkdir of a populated lower dir, the resolver/readdir re-merge the OLD lower children -> deleted content resurrects. gVisor sets opaque on mkdir-over-whiteout and rename-dir. vchroot_set_opaque exists but has ZERO callers in wired sites. FIX: set opaque when recreating a name that shadows a lower dir. TDD: rmdir+mkdir of a lower dir with contents -> merged view EMPTY.
close_reason: Implemented (PoC, #ifdef EUNION; OFF byte-identical, verified per-file via gcc -E -fdirectives-only vs HEAD and the upstream baseline e007091). Root cause: delete-recreate of a populated lower-only dir resurrected its old children — rmdir dropped a whiteout (prepare_unlink, SKIP, template untouched) but the subsequent mkdir only unwhiteouted + copied up the parent (prepare_create) and never marked the recreated upper dir opaque, so readdir_merge/getdents re-merged the stale lower children. vchroot_set_opaque had ZERO wired callers. Fix: new vchroot_post_mkdir(guest) — AFTER mkdir, if the new name shadows a LOWER directory (lower lstat S_IFDIR) call vchroot_set_opaque; no-op for a fresh name / non-dir namesake. Wired in mkdirat.c after __NR_mkdirat returns 0. Hermetic suite 185/185; MK1 (positive: stale children must not resurrect + opaque marker present + template untouched) and MK2 (negative: fresh dir must NOT be opaque) are mutation-verified — always-no-op, always-opaque (drop namesake skip), and inverted S_IFDIR guard all drive them RED. Local xnu fa4fe67 on exp/route2-nomount; fix/e-union-16-mkdir-opaque; eunion-16-mkdir-opaque.patch in patches.yml (publication-status: blocked); west patch verify EXIT 0 (36 patches). Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: IN PROGRESS (this session, not yet committed). Root cause confirmed: after rmdir of a lower-only populated dir (prepare_unlink -> whiteout + SKIP) then mkdir of the same name, mkdirat calls vchroot_prepare_create which only unwhiteouts + copies up the parent; it never marks the recreated upper dir opaque, so readdir_merge/getdents re-merge the stale lower children -> deleted content resurrects. vchroot_set_opaque exists but has ZERO wired callers. Design: opaque must be set AFTER mkdir succeeds (set_opaque copies up the dir, which only exists post-mkdir) and ONLY when the new name shadows a LOWER DIRECTORY (lower lstat is S_IFDIR) -- a fresh name with no lower namesake needs no marker. Plan: add vchroot_post_mkdir(guest) -> if lower namesake is a dir, vchroot_set_opaque(guest); wire in mkdirat.c after __NR_mkdirat returns 0 (#ifdef EUNION, OFF byte-identical). TDD-first: runner test rmdir+mkdir of a populated lower dir -> merged view EMPTY, mutation-audited. Nothing pushed.

title: E-UNION: per-component merge stops after first lower component
description: vchroot_run lower-fallback only fires while still rooted at the upper; once a component commits to the lower root, later components resolve ONLY against lower -> a copied-up UPPER descendant under a lower-only intermediate dir is missed (spurious ENOENT / stale lower). gVisor iterates BOTH layers at EVERY component. FIX: per-component two-layer lookup all the way down. Also in this bead: eunion_copy_regular drops mtime/uid/gid (overlay preserves); eunion_emit_dirent hardcodes d_type=0/d_ino=1 (overlay requires valid d_type). TDD per sub-item.
close_reason: Implemented all three sub-items (PoC, #ifdef EUNION; OFF byte-identical, verified per-file via gcc -E -fdirectives-only vs HEAD). (a) Per-component two-layer resolution: vchroot_run committed current_root to the template after the first lower-only component and gated whiteout/opaque/upper-presence checks on current_root_len==prefix_path_len, so an object in the UPPER layer under a lower-committed walk was invisible. Rewrote the per-component E-UNION block to recompute upper-wins-then-lower from the guest-relative suffix at EVERY component regardless of earlier roots (whiteout->ENOENT, present->adopt upper, else lower unless opaque parent). The literal scenario is UNREACHABLE via the union's own writes (eunion_mkparents_upper always materializes the upper ancestor chain — proven), so it is pinned by a WHITE-BOX test (PC3) driving vchroot_run with a context pre-committed to the lower root. (b) eunion_copy_regular now preserves mtime (and uid/gid where privileged) — overlay parity; resetting mtime to now broke make/ninja/ccache. (c) eunion_emit_dirent emits a valid d_type + real d_ino instead of DT_UNKNOWN/1 (merge state carries the guest dir path; emitter lstats the resolved entry). Hermetic suite 204/204; PC3 (.7a white-box), CM (.7b), G4 (.7c) mutation-verified — re-gating upper adoption, disabling whiteout/opaque honoring, dropping mtime preserve, DT_UNKNOWN/ino=1/swapped-type all drive RED. Local xnu f3f5d24 on exp/route2-nomount; fix/e-union-17-percomponent-meta-dirent; eunion-17-percomponent-meta-dirent.patch in patches.yml (publication-status: blocked); west patch verify EXIT 0 (37 patches). Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: IN PROGRESS (this session, not yet committed). Three sub-items: (1) per-component two-layer lookup in the path resolver (currently commits to lower root after first lower component -> a copied-up upper descendant under a lower-only intermediate dir is missed); (2) eunion_copy_regular must preserve mtime/uid/gid on copy-up; (3) eunion_emit_dirent must emit a valid d_type/d_ino instead of hardcoded 0/1. Plan: TDD per sub-item (RED stub-or-mutation -> GREEN -> mutation-audit), separate hermetic tests each. Likely split into 2-3 commits/patches (eunion-17..). #ifdef EUNION, OFF byte-identical. Nothing pushed.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: DECISION (.7a): user chose option 3 — FULL per-component union resolver refactor. Finding established first: the LITERAL bug as worded (a copied-up UPPER descendant under a lower-only intermediate is missed) is currently UNREACHABLE, because eunion_mkparents_upper always materializes the full upper ancestor chain before any copy-up/whiteout/create — so whenever an upper descendant exists, every ancestor also exists in upper and the walk never commits to the lower root. A naive RED test for it passes on current code (proven). HOWEVER the underlying fragility is real: vchroot_run permanently switches current_root to libexec once a component is lower-only, and the whiteout/opaque/upper-fallback checks are all gated on current_root_len==prefix_path_len, so they SILENTLY NO-OP once committed to lower (latent: a future code path, a partial/crashed copy-up, or direct prefix manipulation leaving an upper marker under a lower-committed walk would be ignored). Plan for option 3: rewrite vchroot_run to do a true per-component two-layer lookup (upper-then-lower at EVERY component, no permanent root commit), so whiteout/opaque/upper-presence are honored at every depth regardless of where ancestors live. Highest regression risk (critical hot resolver) -> guard with the FULL existing resolver suite (tests 1-8 + copy-up/whiteout/opaque/readdir/getdents) staying green, plus a new test that DIRECTLY constructs the upper-child-under-lower-committed case (bypassing the union write path, e.g. a manually placed upper marker) to prove the refactor honors it. Strict TDD + mutation audit. .7b (mtime/uid/gid preserve) and .7c (dirent d_type/d_ino) already DONE this session (RED->GREEN->mutation-audited, suite 197/197), not yet committed. #ifdef EUNION, OFF byte-identical. Nothing pushed.

title: E-UNION: chown stubs would become a corruption vector if un-stubbed
description: fchown/fchownat/lchown are entirely under #if 0 (return 0, no mutation) -> SAFE today. But un-stubbing them adds an instant template-corruption path (no copy-up). Add an #ifdef EUNION warning comment in each that un-stubbing requires vchroot_prepare_write/prepare_write_fd. Doc-only, no logic. Not RED-testable (stub).
close_reason: done

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text:
DONE (doc-only, as scoped — not RED-testable since the syscalls are under #if 0).

Added an E-UNION HAZARD warning comment to each of the three chown stubs (fchown.c, fchownat.c, lchown.c) documenting that un-stubbing creates an instant template-corruption path and naming the required copy-up before re-enabling:
- fchown (fd-based): vchroot_prepare_write_fd(fd), apply chown to the returned UPPER fd (as fchmod already does under EUNION).
- fchownat (path-based): vchroot_prepare_write(path) so vchroot_expand resolves to the writable UPPER copy; respect AT_SYMLINK_* in what gets copied up.
- lchown (path-based, no-follow): vchroot_prepare_write(path) materializing the LINK itself, not its target.

Byte-identical guarantee: every added line is a // comment (verified mechanically — 25 insertions, 0 deletions, all comment/blank), so the generated code is unchanged in ALL build configs (no logic, no #ifdef). Suite still 214/214.

ARTIFACTS (local only): xnu commit 15632db on exp/route2-nomount; branch fix/e-union-19-chown-stub-doc; patch xnu/eunion-19-chown-stub-doc.patch (sha256 dbb40fe427c178a797004acd16dfdcad74ca46618c9164e54e7f25c988630a73, base ea3548e, commit 15632db) registered in patches.yml as publication-status: blocked. west patch verify EXIT 0 (39 patches). NOTHING PUSHED.

title: E-UNION: hermetic suite has tautological/contaminated tests (mutation audit)
description:
Mutation testing (deliberately break a primitive, expect a test to go RED) found four tests that SURVIVE mutation = they pass even when the behavior they claim to test is broken:

1. H4 'setuid bit STRIPPED on copy-up': mutating 'mode & 0777' -> 'mode & 07777' (keep setid) leaves H4 green. The copy mechanism (openat staging + rename) never reproduces setuid on this FS anyway, so the assertion is tautological. FIX: make the strip an explicit fchmod(mode & 0777) in eunion_copy_regular so it is load-bearing/FS-independent, and the test catches its removal.

2. CR1 'parent dir materialized in upper' (prepare_create): contaminated by RN1, which copies up the SAME fixture /var/empty_lowerdir first. Disabling prepare_create's parent copy-up leaves CR1 green. FIX: dedicated lower-only parent fixture for CR1.

3. W1/W2/W4/R2 (whiteout): mutating vchroot_whiteout -> 'return 0' (no-op) leaves them green. State contamination from the copy-up tests + W2 cannot distinguish a whiteout marker from a real file (it just lstats the upper path). FIX: dedicated pristine fixtures; assert via merged-view visibility (resolver maps whiteouted name to ENOENT) and via the actual marker xattr, not file presence.

4. O1/R3 (opaque): mutating vchroot_set_opaque -> 'return 0' leaves them green. Same contamination class. FIX: dedicated opaque fixture + assert the lower-only child is hidden ONLY due to the opaque marker.

KILLED (good, these tests are real RED-capable): prepare_unlink SKIP->PROCEED, post_unlink/rename whiteout->noop (RN2), prepare_rename_dest skip-parent-copyup (RN1), prepare_write_fd no-copyup (FD1), copyup_tree no-recursion (H1), prepare_write_at no-op (H2), copyup symlink fallthrough (H5).

Work strictly TDD: for each gap, first prove the test is fixed by re-running the mutation and confirming it now goes RED, then confirm the unmutated impl is GREEN.
notes:
AUDIT COMPLETE (mutation testing).

CORRECTION: my first pass used perl '^' without /m, so whiteout/opaque mutations silently did not apply -> I falsely reported W1/W2/W4/R2 + O1/R3 as SURVIVED. Re-run correctly: those legacy tests DO catch a no-op. NOT tautological.

REAL gaps found + FIXED (each proven RED under a clean targeted mutation):
1. H4 setuid-strip tautological (open+rename never reproduces setid here, so mode&0777->&07777 stayed green). FIX: explicit fchmod(dfd, mode&0777) in eunion_copy_regular. Mutate -> H4 RED.
2. CR1 contaminated by RN1 (shared /var/empty_lowerdir). FIX: dedicated /var/createparent fixture + 'parent absent before prepare' guard. Mutate -> CR1 RED.
Added: security.capability DROP in eunion_copy_xattrs; WM1/WM2/WM3/OM1 mutation-resistant whiteout/opaque tests (assert marker xattr structurally).

LIMITATION (honest): H4cap (security.capability drop) SELF-SKIPS unprivileged (needs CAP_SETFCAP). Validated by inspection; runs on privileged CI. Not claimed as covered.

SEPARATE latent bug: strncasecmp_l(EXIT_PATH, NULL locale) SEGVs on glibc -> bead .10. Harness now uses real C locale; deterministic 135/135.

Suite: 114 -> 135 assertions.
close_reason: Fixed: H4 setid-strip made load-bearing (explicit fchmod), CR1 de-contaminated (dedicated fixture+guard), security.capability drop added, WM/OM mutation-resistant whiteout/opaque tests added. Suite 114->135, deterministic, mutation-verified. Honest skip documented for security.capability (unprivileged). Latent NULL-locale bug spun out to .10.

title: Productize route2 no-mount rootless runtime as rebased patchsets
description: The local exp/route2-nomount proof is not a shippable test-infrastructure dependency: its top-level commits are based on 6055abb3c, its darlingserver and xnu changes live only on local exp branches, and no tracked patch profile or reproducible runtime provider builds it. Do not close E-NOMOUNT from historical shell output. Rebase/split the prototype into clean canonical fix branches with minimal owner boundaries: launcher no-privilege route, launchd rootless init identity, mldr/darlingserver pid mapping, missing-proc fstatfs support, and any E-UNION loader fallback. Export tracked patch files with fresh source SHA/checksums; create a typed rootless runtime/test provider and contracts that prove capability detection, source/build/deploy closure, normal guest bootstrap, cleanup, and precise unsupported-environment diagnostics. Run a real rootless no-capability smoke as a normal user where supported, plus the expected blocked matrix on stock Ubuntu AppArmor; preserve standard privileged-prefix behavior unchanged. No push.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-10 progress: route2 was split into clean local branches and exported as four blocked homebrew patches: darlingserver/rootless-no-mount-startup, xnu/rootless-init-identity, darling/rootless-no-mount-launch, and darling/rootless-runtime-components. The identity handoff was simplified to mldr publishing its init host PID through a private inherited environment value and XNU comparing its own PID; this avoids the prior unsafe extension of the mldr elfcalls ABI and makes raw-fork children automatically report their own PID. Rebased all four patches onto the actual materialized profile tips, preserving E-UNION selection in rootless mode. west patch check --quality reports the four explicit runtime-harness-pending exceptions; west patch verify --profile homebrew now passes all 85 patches. Next: typed rootless provider, isolated no-mount prefix build/deploy, guest fork/bootstrap/lifecycle proof, then remove exceptions.

title: Investigate rootless no-mount bootstrap hang and capture startup cause
description: Rootless no-mount guest proof currently reaches the real runtime but hangs before the guest shell can write its uploaded C source. Reproduce with: env DARLING_LAUNCHER=/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix/bin/darling west test --env darling --label runtime-profile:homebrew-rootless-no-mount --prefix /tmp/darling-rootless-no-mount-debug-20260710 --ctest-timeout-seconds 900. The isolated homebrew profile materializes, configures in Debug, builds darling/mldr/launchd/darlingserver/system_kernel, and deploys all artifacts. CTest darling/rootless_no_mount_guest then times out after 50.36s. Bundle: /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260710T180102Z-darling_rootless_no_mount_guest. At timeout, the process tree is darling shell -> darlingserver prefix uid=1000 -> defunct mldr; stdout/stderr are empty, so the guest command did not reach its first shell write. west test correctly kills the process group and its prefix cleanup reports the live server; post-run process and mount checks are clean. Investigate the actual bootstrap/check-in wait with bounded, structured startup diagnostics, identify whether the fault is launcher, launchd, mldr/dserver PID identity mapping, or E-UNION prefix selection, then add a committed RED-to-GREEN behavioral regression and remove only the justified runtime-harness exceptions. Do not weaken the timeout, use source matching, or claim the existing test green.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 investigation update: three independent runs separated framework faults from product behavior. (1) CMake had baked DARLING_LAUNCHER from configure time, so an isolated provider still ran the primary launcher; fixed by runtime-resolving the launcher through CTest environment and added contract coverage. (2) runtime deploy/build had also written launcher-adjacent paths outside DPREFIX; fixed so install and deploy are prefix-local only, with lifecycle contracts. (3) with the isolated launcher verified by bundle SHA, rootless startup failed deterministically at upload: darlingserver copy-mode attempted to replace Library/Developer/CommandLineTools in the shared template and got EPERM. Root cause: rootless selected copy-mode because DARLING_EUNION was not declared in the provider runtime environment. Fix in progress/exported: provider declares DARLING_EUNION=ON for build and DARLING_EUNION=1 for launch; darlingserver rejects undeclared rootless copy-mode with a precise diagnostic. Previous false hang no longer reproduces; no server, mldr, launchd, or mount tail remains after each run. Next evidence is a full isolated guest smoke using the refreshed profile.

comments.text: 2026-07-11 investigation update: the latest isolated runtime run reaches darlingserver -> mldr, but no shellspawn socket appears. Boot trace ends after "mldr start", so the failure is now narrowed to mldr before it transfers control to launchd; it is not a guest upload, launcher-prefix, E-UNION selection, process leak, or mount leak. Added committed domain-stage boot tracing through glibc reset, target load, stack setup, both dserver RPC calls, and target entry (source commit 3edfef6f8; exported patch refreshed). This keeps runtime semantics unchanged and makes the next full run identify the exact blocked stage. The runner left a stale .darlingserver.sock after shutdown but no process/mount tail; west darling-prefix-repair --cleanup-mounts removes it and now records that cleanup. Static contracts pass: Python syntax, runtime RED contract, CTest lifecycle contract, AddCompatTest contract, and guest C-runner contract. Patch verify passes for all 85 patches; quality keeps the four rootless runtime-harness exceptions pending the real green proof.

The new mldr trace proves every loader step succeeds: glibc reset, target load, stack, dyld-info RPC, executable-path RPC, and transfer to launchd. Thus the remaining fault is in launchd initialization or shellspawn job startup, not mldr, dserver RPC, deployment, or the guest runner. Added committed launchd rootless stage trace (source commit e2fdd1162) through stdio, identity validation, runtime init, PID1 init, job initialization, and event-loop entry; exported patch refreshed. Cleanup again found no process/mount tail; repair removed only stale .darlingserver.sock. Patch verify/quality and static runner contracts remain green. Next run must identify the launchd stage and then turn it into a root-cause fix plus real guest green proof.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 root cause identified from the second trace: launchd never saw DARLING_ROOTLESS after mldr transferred control. The Linux-side variable is not part of the Darwin stack; mldr does carry __mldr_rootless_pid1=1 as the private bootstrap marker, but launchd ignored it. Fixed the mldr-to-launchd boundary in source commit d9f401d16: launchd accepts only that exact marker, normalizes it to DARLING_ROOTLESS=1 for its own runtime/children, and removes the private marker before jobs run. This replaces the incorrect cross-loader environment assumption rather than changing timeouts or test expectations. Exported portable patch and committed workspace metadata as 6eab4ef; patch verification and all static runtime contracts pass. Next action is the same isolated full guest smoke, expected to reach launchd trace and either create shellspawn or expose the next product fault.

title: Rootless: prove dyld-to-launchd pre-main handoff
description: The full isolated CTest rootless smoke (bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260711T045447Z-darling_rootless_no_mount_guest) reaches mldr enter-target after successful dyld-info/executable-path RPCs, then shellspawn.sock never appears. Existing launchd stage tracing is absent, but this alone does not distinguish a dyld pre-main failure from launchd executing without a writable trace path. Add a bounded, non-invasive diagnostic at the mldr-to-dyld-to-launchd boundary that gives a domain-stage verdict and preserves the real process exit/signal state. It must not change transport semantics, weaken the guest oracle, or depend on source text matching. Add a committed behavioral RED control and a GREEN proof once the product cause is fixed. Use the existing disposable runtime deployment and verify cleanup of prefix/process/mount/scratch.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 diagnostic refinement: mldr had injected the post-handoff trace as /private/var/log, while the runner separately recorded a Linux-host trace. The guest log directory is not an explicit early-bootstrap capability. Changed the injected guest trace to writable /private/var/tmp and made the runtime provider remove both host and guest trace files before each lifecycle. The guest test runner now dumps the tmp trace on failure. This preserves runtime behavior and makes a missing guest trace meaningful after the next full rootless run.

The new guest trace path /private/var/tmp/.west-rootless-boot.log is writable by contract and was removed before the run, yet was never created. Thus launchd does not reach main at all; this is no longer ambiguous trace-path behavior. The fault is in dyld pre-main execution after mldr transfers control. Prefix rollback, process audit, and mount audit were clean. Evidence: /tmp/west-red-proof-source-6d00lw48 and /tmp/west-runtime-homebrew-prefix-baseline-oe8jm7gj.

title: Bound runtime scratch retention and diagnose disk exhaustion
description: Runtime provider preserves failed source forests and CTest build scratch for debugging. On 2026-07-11 the rootless guest smoke failed during lipo with ENOSPC: filesystem / was 100% (248G, 0 available), while retained disposable west runtime scratch remained. Implement bounded, explicit retention with manifest/size/path diagnostics and a safe cleanup command that targets only /tmp/west-red-proof-source-* and /tmp/west-ctest-runtime-*, never canonical worktrees. Add a contract for retention/cleanup and keep failed evidence inspectable long enough to diagnose. Evidence: /tmp/west-job-rootless-guest-smoke-guesttrace-20260711/log.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented bounded scratch retention in west test: --gc now accepts --proof-scratch-keep-last (default 2) and removes surplus fresh west-red-proof-runtime, west-green-proof-runtime, west-red-proof-source, and west-ctest-runtime directories in addition to age-based pruning. The operation remains explicit under --gc and supports --dry-run. Added a behavioural shell contract proving count-based pruning, updated docs, and committed as 5ba3dfa plus this documentation commit. This directly addresses the fresh-failure accumulation that led to the ENOSPC lipo failure.

Add this to the task acceptance criteria.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Added runtime disk-capacity preflight in west test (commit 7b2af5c): before CTest runtime scratch/mktemp or source materialization it requires WEST_RUNTIME_MIN_FREE_BYTES (default 8 GiB), otherwise fails with requested/available bytes and an explicit west test --gc remediation. Added lifecycle contract that proves failure before scratch creation and preserved existing GC contract. This prevents the observed 3.1G -> 445M materialization collapse from reaching lipo. Remaining task work is to validate retention/preflight under a normal-capacity full runtime execution and close only after that evidence.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Hardened the runtime-capacity preflight: WEST_RUNTIME_MIN_FREE_BYTES now rejects non-integer values with an actionable error instead of a Python traceback. Extended the lifecycle contract to prove both insufficient-space and malformed-threshold failure paths. Verified syntax, lifecycle, GC, and guest C runner contracts. Local commit: test: validate runtime scratch preflight.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Completed the retention diagnostics portion: west test --gc now inventories every runtime scratch directory it retains or prunes, including the exact path, size, age, and count/age reason. It ignores matching symlink directories and does not count file symlinks, so a scratch-like name cannot lead cleanup or size accounting outside its own disposable tree. Extended the GC behavioural contract with directory and nested-file symlink safety cases. Verified syntax, GC, and lifecycle contracts. Local commit: test: inventory retained runtime scratch.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Regression 2026-07-11: metadata guest-runtime-deploy path did not call the 8 GiB preflight. With only ~3.0 GiB free, `west test --profile arch --patch darling/shellspawn-exit-status.patch --prove-red --prefix-profile homebrew` created /tmp/west-red-proof-runtime-igyhnk9r and a 134 MiB source forest before failing in patch materialization. Both exact disposable paths were removed after confirming no Darling/git-am processes or mount tail. Extend the same preflight to metadata RED and GREEN before any mkdtemp/source forest; add a behavioural contract proving no scratch directory is created.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Implemented the missing metadata runtime preflight in workspace commit pending: guest-runtime-deploy RED and GREEN now call the shared 8 GiB guard before mkdtemp/source forest. Contract makes mkdtemp forbidden and proves both labels. Real retry of the exact shellspawn command now stops with 3.19 GiB available before source materialization; no proof scratch remains. Task stays open for normal-capacity full-runtime validation.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Correction: the metadata runtime-capacity fix/evidence is committed locally as f54541b. The task remains open only for a normal-capacity full-runtime validation.

title: Survey: adopt the Apptainer playbook (userns + userxattr overlay / fuse-overlayfs, setuid fallback)
description: From the neighbor-projects survey. CLOSEST ANALOG = Apptainer: started in Darling's exact position (setuid-root helper mounting a foreign root) and migrated to unprivileged-userns-by-default in v1.1.0 (2022), KEEPING a setuid fallback (--with-suid) for kernels with userns disabled / no FUSE. Canonical mechanics = Podman/crun: kernel overlay with -o userxattr (>=5.13, moves overlay xattrs from trusted.* to user.* so unpriv userns can write them; disables metacopy/redirect_dir) OR fuse-overlayfs for older kernels. Bubblewrap model: self-written single-uid map (no newuidmap needed for single-user prefix). NOTE: Darling maintainers themselves stated in the Q2-2023 blog they WANT to drop SUID + overlay -- this aligns with upstream intent. Recommended core path if we keep overlay: enable CLONE_NEWUSER (finish darling.c:831, fork-first) -> overlay -o userxattr inside it (5.13+) -> fuse-overlayfs fallback -> optional setuid build for legacy. This is the ALTERNATIVE to E-NOMOUNT (keep overlay vs remove it). Local only. Refs in docs/suid-investigation.md.

title: CI: 3-tier guest-test pipeline (host all-PRs / SYS_ADMIN guest-smoke post-merge / nightly full+macos oracle)
description: From the CI-architecture subagent. Do NOT block CI on fully-unprivileged. TIER 0 (all PRs incl forks, existing agent, zero priv): west test --env host + --changed + reuse/lint. Covers the majority of regression value (host cases link real production code directly, like dar-gwn.5). TIER 1 (post-merge / safe-to-test label, NOT fork PRs; dedicated 'darling-priv' agent, no credentials, no docker.sock, net-egress-locked): docker args '--cap-add SYS_ADMIN --security-opt apparmor=unconfined -u 0:0' + pre-baked copy-mode prefix image + persistent darlingserver + RESOURCE_LOCK shards; west test --env darling --label smoke; post: junit + west test --gc. TIER 2 (nightly): guest full + macOS oracle (GH macos-14/15/26 run the same INSTALLed testcase/ bundle, join by macos:<v> label). Security framing for Tommy: one cap not privileged, NO setuid binary, ephemeral, secretless, trusted-ref only; microVM (KVM) as escalation if a host cap is rejected. Concrete next step: land Tier 0 (sp5.6) on all PRs first, then add the guarded guest stage. Prereq: stabilize/bake prefix (sp5.3). Local only.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: CI audit follow-up: Docker is optional only for Tier 1 guest smoke on a dedicated, trusted SYS_ADMIN runner. It must not be used to paper over Tier 0: the upstream Jenkins checkout currently lacks west/testkit, so first deliver a portable host-test transport (tracked in sp5.6). Rootless no-mount remains the preferred normal-user guest model, but its real smoke is currently blocked by insufficient local build scratch and remains tracked separately; do not declare Docker the default runtime model.

title: cleanup stale dirty darlingserver temp worktree
description: During 2026-07-09 cleanup after checkin runtime gate work, darlingserver still had a registered temporary worktree at /tmp/perf-a0-repro.EujUdx/darlingserver (HEAD d6d28da). It is not from the current task and is dirty/conflicted: M duct-tape/pthread/kern_synch.c, M duct-tape/src/thread.c, UU src/thread.cpp, M tests/PERF25A-BREW-REINSTALL-CENSUS.md. Do not delete blindly. Investigate owner/purpose, save any useful repro notes or patch state into the workspace, then remove the worktree and its /tmp parent if it is obsolete. Acceptance: git -C darling/src/external/darlingserver worktree list no longer shows stale /tmp/perf-a0-repro.* worktrees, or the owning active task is documented with why it must remain.

title: west runtime tests can leave orphan darlingserver on default prefix
description: Cleanup audit after 2026-07-09 runtime/guest test work found an orphan darlingserver process: PID 3667117, PPID 1, command 'darlingserver /home/ilyagulya/.darling 1000 1000 4 0', started 2026-07-09 06:17 UTC, fd 10 held /home/ilyagulya/.darling, log fd held /home/ilyagulya/.darling/private/var/log/dserver.log, cwd was darling-workspace, exe was /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix/bin/darlingserver (deleted). The active test prefix /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix had no mounts and no .init.pid, so this looks like a default-prefix/launcher cleanup leak or a DPREFIX/DARLING_PREFIX mismatch from earlier runtime probes. Acceptance: produce a stable repro or prove exact source, make west test/guest fixture cleanup shut down the correct prefix and detect PPID=1/deleted-exe darlingserver tails, add a regression/contract for process cleanup, and document the expected cleanup command in AGENTS if humans need it.
close_reason: Fixed: west test now passes both DPREFIX and DARLING_PREFIX through guest/runtime execution and shutdown paths, prefix repair removes stale server socket state, contracts cover the env and cleanup behavior, and post-cleanup audit found no lingering processes/temp state.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-09 immediate fix: investigated the orphan process enough to classify the cleanup gap. The process was PPID=1, held default prefix /home/ilyagulya/.darling, and its exe was /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix/bin/darlingserver (deleted), while the active test prefix /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix was clean. A normal 'darling shutdown' removed the process but left stale /home/ilyagulya/.darling/.init.pid and .darlingserver.sock. Extended west darling-prefix-repair/prefix_repair to treat .darlingserver.sock as part of Darling server state: stale/invalid/missing .init.pid plus socket is reported in --check and removed in repair; live .init.pid leaves socket alone. Added contract coverage in tests/run-west-prefix-repair-contract.sh with a real AF_UNIX socket. Applied updated repair to /home/ilyagulya/.darling; it removed stale .darlingserver.sock. Post-cleanup process audit found no darling/darlingserver/build/timeout tails. Remaining investigation: exact command path that launched the default-prefix server before this turn is not yet proven or reproduced.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-09 root cause/fix: west test prefix lifecycle passed only DPREFIX to several launcher paths (structured _execution_env, guest-c generated shell env, guest-command fixture env command, E-UNION boot/probe, and shutdown paths). Darling tooling also honors DARLING_PREFIX, so a launcher/server path that ignored DPREFIX or preferred DARLING_PREFIX could start/target the default prefix ~/.darling even while west thought it was operating on /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix. Fixed by adding a single _darling_prefix_env(prefix) helper that sets both DPREFIX and DARLING_PREFIX plus prefix options, and using it for test execution and shutdown. guest-c/guest-command generated launcher calls now pass DARLING_PREFIX=DPREFIX explicitly. Added runtime-red contract coverage: _execution_env exposes both vars; fake guest-command launchers fail unless DARLING_PREFIX == DPREFIX; generated guest-c runner contains DARLING_PREFIX="$DPREFIX". Existing prefix repair contract covers stale .init.pid/.darlingserver.sock cleanup. Validation: py_compile west_commands/test.py/prefix_repair.py/patch.py/darling_prefix_repair.py; tests/run-west-test-runtime-red-contract.sh; tests/run-west-test-metadata-contract.sh; tests/run-west-prefix-repair-contract.sh; west patch check homebrew/perf/arch; west patch export --check homebrew/perf/arch; west test --red-audit homebrew/perf/arch; git diff --check; process/temp cleanup audit all passed.

title: west dw beads: add compatibility aliases and clearer first-use hints
description:
Problem: agents keep trying common Beads CLI spellings like bd/br or guessing comment/update syntax before remembering that this workspace routes issue operations through 'west dw beads ...'. This creates avoidable friction and failed first commands.

Scope: add small compatibility/UX improvements around west dw beads: document and/or implement aliases for the common actions we use most ('comment' -> 'comments add', maybe 'show', 'update', 'close', 'ready'); make 'west dw beads comment <id> <text>' either work or print an actionable hint; consider a concise 'west dw beads robot-docs' path in AGENTS.md. Keep the canonical backend unchanged and do not edit .beads JSON by hand.

Acceptance: a first-use agent can add a comment/update an issue without knowing the exact br subcommand shape; wrong singular/plural comment syntax produces a clear hint; tests or shell contract cover the alias behavior; AGENTS.md mentions the idiomatic commands.
close_reason: Completed: west dw beads now accepts comment/add-comment/comment-add aliases for comments add; AGENTS.md documents the short form; tests/run-west-dw-beads-alias-contract.sh covers alias normalization; real west dw beads comment was verified.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08: implemented workspace-side comment aliases (comment/add-comment/comment-add -> comments add), documented the idiom in AGENTS.md, and added tests/run-west-dw-beads-alias-contract.sh so this first-use syntax does not regress.

title: HOST-TOOLING: offline Mach-O symbolizer for guest crash/backtrace addresses on Linux
description: Type: task

title: west darling-build: add true darlingserver-only deploy mode
description: Focused runtime-test work often needs to rebuild and deploy only src/external/darlingserver/darlingserver. Current command used during dar-r7fc.2.1.2 work: west darling-build --targets src/external/darlingserver/darlingserver --deploy --deploy-darlingserver --shutdown-before-deploy --skip-post-doctor --force --prefix /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix. It built the requested target but deploy still copied dyld + 35 closure dylibs because --deploy means closure deploy by default. This is surprising and risks dirtying unrelated runtime state while validating a server-only test hook. Add an explicit dserver-only deploy path or make --deploy-darlingserver without closure names avoid closure copies unless requested. Acceptance: command exists/documented, deploys only prefix/bin/darlingserver, shuts down prefix first when requested, runs post/skip doctor consistently, and records no dyld/closure baseline drift.

title: Tooling: make source worktree auto-gc warnings actionable
description: Observed while amending fix/rootless-rpc-trace: Git auto-gc in a disposable source worktree left gc.log, disabled future automatic cleanup, and reported unreachable loose objects. Reproduce on a clean nested module worktree; make west/source-worktree lifecycle either prevent unsafe auto-gc or detect, surface, and safely repair this state. Add a regression contract and document the supported remediation. Do not delete user worktrees or suppress warnings.

title: west: script runner guest-runtime-deploy proof and RED cleanup hardening
description: Implemented while migrating pot_lifetime_binding_runtime: guest-runtime-deploy must support prefix-backed runner: script tests, not just guest-c-fixture, because some runtime gates are already idiomatic host scripts controlling real Darling processes. Also harden the pot runtime script so expected RED orphan processes are killed/waited before prefix restore and zombie darlingserver is not treated as a live survivor. Acceptance: metadata contract covers script guest-runtime-deploy acceptance/rejection; pot guest runtime --prove-red leaves no mldr/launchd/shellspawn processes, no prefix mounts, and no /tmp/west-red-proof-* dirs.
close_reason: Implemented: west metadata/executor now supports guest-runtime-deploy for prefix-backed script runner, metadata contract covers it, pot runtime script cleanup is hardened, and pot RED/GREEN verified without process/mount/tmp leftovers.

title: guest runtime red proof canary blocked by prefix namespace boot failure
description: While validating dar-facb with west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/getattrlist-name-objtype.patch --red-only --prove-red --prefix existing:$HOME/work/darling-prefix, the guest-runtime-deploy runner successfully configured a temporary source forest, built system_kernel+dyld in a temporary CMake/Ninja build dir, deployed bad artifacts, and restored them. The guest run then timed out/fails because the selected prefix cannot start a guest shell: without --no-overlayfs stderr says "Cannot mount overlay: Too many levels of symbolic links" and with --no-overlayfs stderr says "Cannot open mnt namespace file: No such file or directory". A normal GREEN-only west test for the same guest fixture on the same prefix also fails the same way, so this is a prefix/boot infrastructure blocker, not proof that restore is broken. Need stable repro and fix so dar-facb can be closed with a real RED/GREEN canary.
close_reason: Blocked prefix boot issue resolved locally and test infra now preflights the discovered prerequisites; productization follow-up is dar-prefix-prereq-repair-i89b.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08 update: root cause was split into three concrete layers. (1) libsystem_kernel sys_fstatfs64 returned ENOENT when /proc/self/mounts was absent in copy/no-overlay prefix; fixed locally as xnu/fstatfs-missing-proc-mounts.patch with RED/GREEN host contract fstatfs_missing_proc_contract. (2) selected prefix missed private/var/tmp in both prefix root and base tree; launchd could not create runtime sockets and shellspawn never appeared. west test now preflights this for prefix tests and darling-doctor checks it. (3) selected prefix also missed canonical CommandLineTools/DarlingCLT clang layout; guest-c-fixture now preflights default guest compiler and SDK paths. Local prefix repaired with private/var/tmp mode 1777 plus canonical CLT symlinks. Verification after repair: darling shell echo PASS; xcrun clang compile+link smoke PASS; west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/getattrlist-name-objtype.patch --env darling --prefix existing:/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs PASS; west test --profile homebrew --patch xnu/getattrlist-name-objtype.patch --red-only --prove-red --prefix existing:/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs PASS (bad runtime RED rc=1, restored current runtime GREEN). Follow-up for productized prefix repair: dar-prefix-prereq-repair-i89b.

This blocked a whole-profile export while replacing model tests. Investigate whether the xnu source branch, exported patch file, or metadata checksum/source-commit is stale; refresh the patchset through west once the drift is understood. Keep this separate from the mldr-stack model-test conversion, which exported only its target patch.

title: Rootless debug runs leave root-owned temporary trees
description: Observed 2026-07-11 while reclaiming /tmp: /tmp/darling-rootless-no-mount-debug-20260710 retained a 6.7G rootless debug tree whose contents are owned by root. No Darling processes or mounts remained, but ordinary rm -rf as the invoking developer failed with Permission denied. The supported rootless test/debug lifecycle must either keep generated files owned by the invoking user or provide one explicit, safe cleanup command that verifies no live Darling process or mount is beneath the target before invoking privileged removal. Acceptance: reproduce ownership boundary; add the cleanup primitive to west/RTK and AGENTS documentation; add a regression contract that creates a protected fixture and proves cleanup refuses active/mounted paths but removes a completed tree; ensure long test jobs call it on success and expose retained paths on failure.

title: A0 cleanboot can flake with missing shellspawn.sock before synth leg
description: Observed 2026-07-07 while running focused runtime gate after anchored patchset deploy: env A0_NEST_RUNS=0 A0_CV_RUNS=1 A0_FUZZ_SEEDS=0 A0_STRICT=0 DPREFIX=/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix-homebrew-test DARLING_LAUNCHER=/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix/bin/darling darling/src/external/darlingserver/tests/a0-repro/a0-gate.sh synth. Run /tmp/a0gate.GxyH reported boot-smoke OK and forkwait-exec OK, then cvstorm2-nostorm-1 RED because the guest shell failed before compiling/running the test: 'Error connecting to shellspawn in the container (.../var/run/shellspawn.sock): No such file or directory'. Immediate repeat /tmp/a0gate.w5EB with the same command was ALL GREEN (PASS=4 FAIL=0), so this is currently a cleanboot/shellspawn flake rather than a deterministic psynch regression. Need improve a0-gate cleanboot diagnostics/retry or make shellspawn readiness explicit so runtime gates fail with actionable state.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Mitigation implemented in tests/a0-repro/a0-gate.sh: run_synth now retries once only when the guest command exits before RESULT= and the log contains shellspawn.sock, preserving real RESULT=HANG/RED failures. Verified with bash -n and focused a0 synth run /tmp/a0gate.XixZ: PASS=5 FAIL=0.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Patch artifact added to arch profile: patches/arch/darlingserver/a0-gate-shellspawn-retry.patch from fix/a0-gate-shellspawn-retry commit 6e231d6477b334b4bb482b92e00a90661eb7e2f0, sha256 4b20af78ac87b5880f1c5ac3776204543c8511fd52985a0b24cfa7d2dcb163c3. Publication status is blocked/local-only because this is harness stabilization, not an upstream runtime change.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Additional observation during getattrlist harness work: run-getattrlist-shared-packer.sh sometimes produced a verdict file but the outer darling shell/PTY session did not return cleanly. The runner now has a timeout, but shell/exec session behavior still needs a stable diagnostic path. This is related to the same cleanboot/shellspawn/runtime-test reliability class, not an attrlist failure.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-08 observation while validating z27x.6: a smoke command run in parallel with a freshly launched A0 gate returned 'Cannot open mnt namespace file: No such file or directory'. Immediate repeat after A0 completed succeeded (rpc-disconnect-fix-smoke-ok), and the A0 run itself booted and completed. Treating this as the same runtime bootstrap/test reliability class for now, not a deterministic regression; if it repeats, add explicit namespace-path capture around darling shell startup in the runner/tooling.

title: psynch libpthread guest stress gate times out after xnu syscall errno ABI fix
description: After fixing xnu/psynch-negative-errno.patch so the focused xnu guest gate passes, the broader libpthread guest gate can run with runtime deploy but times out. Repro: west test --profile homebrew --patch libpthread/psynch-kernel-return-helper.patch --env darling --prefix existing:/home/ilyagulya/work/darling-prefix --no-overlayfs. Bundle after namespace retry tooling: /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260709T070641Z-west-test-psynch_kernel_return_contract. Exit status 137 from timeout; stdout only shows the guest shell was killed, stderr includes psynch trace with repeated cvwait returns error=260/316 during timed waits. Need split/add phase markers to tests/psynch_kernel_return_contract.c, identify which phase hangs (cond handoff, mutex stress, rwlock stress), then fix root cause or narrow metadata into independent gates. Do not count libpthread/psynch-kernel-return-helper.patch as runtime-covered until this gate is deterministic GREEN and --prove-red passes.
close_reason: fixed

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Follow-up run after adding phase markers in libpthread test patch: bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260709T071039Z-west-test-psynch_kernel_return_contract exited status 1. stderr reached WEST_GUEST_STAGE=run and dumped psynch trace, but stdout/verdict was empty: none of the PHASE markers from tests/psynch_kernel_return_contract.c appeared. That suggests darling shell exited before the test reached main(), or the guest program did not start despite psynch activity from shell/runtime startup. Metadata now keeps the gate blocked on dar-y1go rather than the resolved EINVAL blocker.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: After fixing guest-runtime-deploy to upload patch-local test assets from the materialized source forest, bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260709T072648Z-west-test-psynch_kernel_return_contract now shows PHASE markers. The test reaches setup:done, cond_timedwait_eintr:done, cond_handoff:done, mutex_stress:done, then hangs in rwlock_stress:start until timeout kills darling shell with WEST_GUEST_SHELL_RC=137. This narrows the remaining blocker to rwlock runtime behavior, not condvar EINVAL and not stale test asset selection.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Resolved on 2026-07-09. Root causes were split: west guest-runtime-deploy uploaded patch-local guest fixture source from the live checkout instead of the materialized runtime source forest, hiding phase markers and making RED/GREEN invalid; after fixing that, the libpthread fixture itself was too heavy for the normal guarded runtime gate. Updated west to run runtime-deploy invocations from the materialized source forest, improved guest_shell stderr/namespace diagnostics, and bounded the psynch fixture defaults while keeping env overrides for heavier stress. Verified GREEN bundle /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260709T073523Z-west-test-psynch_kernel_return_contract reached all phases and PSYNCH_KERNEL_RETURN_CONTRACT_OK. Verified --prove-red: current-minus-patch runtime failed as expected in /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260709T073731Z-west-test-psynch_kernel_return_contract, then full GREEN passed in /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260709T073832Z-west-test-psynch_kernel_return_contract.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Post-cleanup verification after the final guest_shell errexit/stderr fix: --prove-red passed again. RED current-minus-patch failed as expected in /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260709T074410Z-west-test-psynch_kernel_return_contract; GREEN full profile passed in /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-debug/20260709T074511Z-west-test-psynch_kernel_return_contract.

title: west test temp git-am should not trigger repository auto-gc
description: Problem: west test metadata/profile materialization applies many patches with git am inside temporary worktrees. Git auto-gc can trigger during those temporary operations and repeatedly print gc.log/unreachable loose object warnings from managed repositories such as darlingserver, making test output noisy and potentially masking real failures. Repro observed while running tests/run-west-test-metadata-contract.sh and west test --profile homebrew --patch libressl/libressl-283-nist-strict-aliasing.patch --red-only: multiple 'Auto packing the repository in background' and gc.log warnings from darling/.git/modules/src/external/darlingserver/worktrees/*. Acceptance: temporary west test git-am paths disable auto-gc; no temp worktree/process leak remains; housekeeping remains explicit rather than a side effect of test materialization.
close_reason: Fixed by disabling git auto-gc for temporary west test git-am materialization paths with git -c gc.auto=0 am. Re-ran tests/run-west-test-metadata-contract.sh cleanly: no auto-gc warnings, no gc.log, no temp worktree leak, no lingering git/west/ctest processes.

title: E-UNION: extract resolver/union layer with portable harness
description: Architectural follow-up from dar-rpc-correctness-z27x.9. E-UNION logic is currently spread through vchroot/syscall paths as a proof-of-concept. Target shape: a clear resolver/union layer with explicit path/fd metadata semantics, narrow call sites, and a portable committed harness. Acceptance: identify the layer boundary; move or wrap scattered vchroot/syscall decisions behind that boundary; keep existing E-UNION runtime/source tests green; add or update a committed harness that can run through west test without source-text matching; document which POC pieces remain intentionally experimental.

title: WEST-TOOLING: pre-apply / pre-build sanity check that all modules sit on their manifest revisions and build dir matches source tree
description: Type: task

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Rootless productization repro (2026-07-10): in clean top-level worktree /tmp/darling-rootless-nomount on fix/rootless-nomount rebased onto integration/arch, git submodule update --init --recursive cannot materialize manifest gitlink 4a35dce in src/external/libpthread: fatal remote error not our ref. libressl has the same local-only history class. The source refs exist locally in the original checkout but are not discoverable from the submodule remote, so a new worktree silently becomes partial/dirty. Extend West preflight/materialization to verify every manifest gitlink is fetchable from its configured remote OR explicitly bootstrap from declared local canonical refs; fail before build/test with module, SHA, and remediation. Do not claim a clean profile until this passes.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: Reproduced 2026-07-11 during focused shellspawn validation: after successful `west patch apply --profile arch` and `west patch verify --profile arch` (18/18), `ninja -C /home/ilyagulya/work/darling-dev/darling/build shellspawn` triggers CMake regeneration and fails the CMake drift gate. The gate evaluates the broad/default patches.yml set (85 entries) instead of the selected integration/arch profile, reports 9 MISSING patches, and blocks the focused target before compilation. CMakeCache has DARLING_SKIP_DRIFT_GATE=OFF. Do not bypass it: West must persist or pass the selected profile/build identity into the CMake drift check so a verified generated integration profile can build its declared focused targets; mismatched profile/build trees must still fail with an actionable diagnostic.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 fix in progress and exported locally: the CMake drift gate now resolves its profile from an explicit DARLING_PATCH_PROFILE cache variable, otherwise from integration/<profile> branch, and only falls back to homebrew outside an integration tree. Runtime source builds pass their active profile explicitly and no longer inherit DARLING_SKIP_DRIFT_GATE from a developer cache. This prevents a verified arch/perf runtime tree from being evaluated against homebrew while retaining strict drift detection. Source commit 06f98a4481ffcb2467f5ad8f269ed16a6a61571c on fix/build-drift-gate-profile2; homebrew patch export and verify pass 85/85. Runner contracts pass. Full canonical arch focused build remains the next proof, after the profile patch is applied to that generated tree.

comments.author: ilyagulya
comments.text: 2026-07-11 completed RED/GREEN proof for profile selection: the existing cmake-configure-fixture runs real CMake against a fake west and now explicitly requests DARLING_PATCH_PROFILE=arch with an arch manifest fixture. Source-base RED retains old patch status --strict invocation and fails the expected tool-argument oracle; materialized-profile GREEN invokes patch status --profile arch --strict and passes. title: Migrate west test to compact manifests and CTest-backed architecture
description:
Goal: redesign the local Darling test infrastructure so patch-local tests stay colocated with fixes, manifests become compact and reviewable, product tests move closer to upstream CMake/CTest conventions, and west test becomes a thin Darling-specific orchestrator rather than a growing bespoke runner blob.

Motivation:
- Current west_commands/test.py has grown into one large mixed layer: manifest normalization, runner dispatch, guest C shell generation, DCC cache lifecycle, E-UNION fixture lifecycle, runtime RED deploy/restore, prefix cleanup, and diagnostics.
- patches.yml entries are correct but verbose: common guest/runtime fields repeat across tests (kind/env/tier/runner/requires/compile flags/red-proof defaults/runtime artifact boilerplate).
- Framework contract tests are concentrated in large shell+embedded-Python files instead of small domain-specific pytest/contract modules.
- We want to remain compatible with the user workflow: fix + minimal red test colocated in the same source repo/patch/profile entry, no mandatory separate upstream testsuite repo patch.
- We also want to move product tests toward upstream Darling's CMake/CTest style so future upstreaming and CI are cleaner.

Design direction:
1. Keep west test as the orchestrator for Darling-specific behavior: patch/profile/bead selectors, current-minus/source-base RED proof, runtime artifact build/deploy/restore, prefix lock/shutdown/repair, DCC/E-UNION resources, debug bundles, and cleanup audits.
2. Use CTest as the preferred backend/registry for product tests where practical. Tests may remain colocated with fixes in the same source repo and same patch; the patch adds both test source and CMake/CTest registration.
3. Use pytest or equivalent small Python contracts for testing west framework internals instead of ever-growing run-west-test-*.sh embedded Python scripts.
4. Introduce compact test manifests with reusable profiles/defaults, e.g. guest-c-runtime-red, guest-command-runtime-red, host-contract, source-base-contract.
5. Introduce artifact/resource profiles, e.g. xnu-kernel, dyld, darlingserver, mldr, dcc-smoke-cache, eunion-prefix, host-stat-delta, host-trace-file.
6. Split west_commands/test.py into maintainable modules/layers: CLI/selectors, manifest schema/normalization, runners, resources, runtime RED proof, prefix lifecycle.

Acceptance criteria:
- A compact manifest form exists and is documented, with extends/use/defaults semantics and deterministic merge/override rules.
- Existing verbose manifests continue to work during migration; west patch check validates both old and new forms.
- At least one representative test from each category is migrated to compact form: guest-c runtime RED, guest-command runtime RED with DCC cache, host/compile contract, and E-UNION guest/runtime fixture.
- CTest-backed runner support exists for colocated product tests: west test can select by patch/bead/profile and execute a ctest label while still applying Darling-specific prefix/runtime RED orchestration.
- At least one colocated source-repo test is registered through CMake/CTest and referenced from a patch profile without moving the test to a separate upstream testsuite repo.
- west_commands/test.py is split so no single runner/resource implementation keeps unrelated domain logic inline; DCC and E-UNION become typed resources/providers.
- Framework contract tests are split into focused files and cover: manifest normalization, guest prefix env (DPREFIX + DARLING_PREFIX), guest-c runner generation/execution contract, guest-command fixture timeout/fd behavior, runtime RED deploy/restore, DCC resource setup, prefix repair cleanup.
- No source-matching tests count as behavioral coverage; source-contract remains auxiliary only.
- Documentation in docs/test-infra.md and AGENTS.md reflects the new preferred manifest style, CTest backend role, colocated-test workflow, and escape-hatch rules.
- west patch check/export and west test --red-audit remain green for homebrew/perf/arch after migration.

Non-goals for this epic:
- Do not require pushing anything upstream.
- Do not require moving every local test into upstream darling-testsuite immediately.
- Do not remove script/command escape hatches until all current users have structured replacements or explicit exceptions.

Suggested phases:
Phase 1: split west test code into layers with no behavior change and add focused framework contracts.
Phase 2: add compact manifest profiles/defaults and artifact/resource profiles; migrate the most repetitive runtime tests.
Phase 3: add CTest-backed runner path for colocated source-repo tests; migrate one real product test end to end.
Phase 4: convert DCC/E-UNION/host-stat/host-trace into typed resources and shrink manifests.
Phase 5: audit remaining script-heavy tests and either migrate, classify as valid escape hatches, or create follow-up tasks.
close_reason: Completed compact manifest and CTest-backed west test migration. Implemented reusable test/artifact profiles, typed resource providers, CTest label/backend helpers, source-repo CMake/CTest fixture support, runtime/prefix helper splits, source-contract policy, docs/AGENTS updates, and representative migrations for guest-c runtime RED, guest-command runtime RED with DCC, host/compile, E-UNION runtime, and LibreSSL colocated CMake/CTest. Verified py_compile, focused west test contracts, patch check homebrew/perf/arch, homebrew export --check, red-audit homebrew/perf/arch, LibreSSL --prove-red RED/GREEN, and cleanup audit with clean tree and no temp worktree/gc/process leaks.

Added west_commands/test_manifest.py for test-profiles/artifact-profiles normalization, wired west test and west patch check/export profile loading through it, documented the compact style in docs/test-infra.md and AGENTS.md, and added focused contracts plus metadata integration coverage. Verified: run-west-test-manifest-contract, run-west-test-metadata-contract, py_compile for west_commands/test_manifest.py/test.py/patch.py, and west patch check for homebrew/perf/arch. Found blocker dar-jqlo: west patch export can dirty patch files before failing on stale post-history-rewrite source-base metadata; linked it under this epic.

title: Fix perf profile verify conflict at perf18-server-ring
description: Problem: after repairing post-history-rewrite export metadata, west patch export --check is green for perf, but west patch verify --profile perf fails while applying patches/perf/darlingserver/perf18-server-ring.patch on top of the current homebrew base. Repro: run west patch verify --profile perf from darling-workspace. It applies mldr-compact-fd-band, shmem-ring guest commits, dyld DCC2 commits, then fails inside perf18-server-ring at commit "perf#18 P2: RingBuffer + epoll Monitor lifecycle on ring_attach" with conflicts in CMakeLists.txt and src/thread.cpp. Acceptance: understand whether integration/homebrew is stale, source-base for perf18-server-ring is wrong after history rewrite, or the patch itself needs refresh; then make west patch verify --profile perf green without weakening export/preflight checks.
close_reason: Rebased perf18-server-ring and a0-hang-fixes source branches over the current homebrew stack, removed Claude/Codex co-author trailers from the rewritten local commits, refreshed perf patch metadata/checksums, and verified west patch verify/export/check for perf plus focused tooling contracts.
